f3c1005c5e28.source: I have a love/hate relationship with calculus: it demonstrates the beauty of math and the agony of math education.
f3c1005c5e28.target: 我对微积分(Calculus)有一种爱恨交织的感情：它既展现了数学之美，也展现了数学教育的痛苦。

51a42a16f7bc.source: Calculus relates topics in an elegant, brain-bending manner. My closest analogy is Darwin’s Theory of Evolution: once understood, you start seeing Nature in terms of survival. You understand why drugs lead to resistant germs (survival of the fittest). You know why sugar and fat taste sweet (encourage consumption of high-calorie foods in times of scarcity). It all fits together.
51a42a16f7bc.target: 微积分以优雅而烧脑的方式将各个主题联系起来。我最贴切的类比是达尔文的进化论：一旦理解，你就会开始用生存的角度来看待自然。你会明白为什么药物会导致耐药菌（适者生存）。你知道为什么糖和脂肪尝起来是甜的（在匮乏时期鼓励摄入高热量食物）。一切都环环相扣。

56f0d4708a33.source: Calculus is similarly enlightening. Don’t these formulas seem related in some way?
56f0d4708a33.target: 微积分同样具有启发意义。这些公式难道不像是在某些方面互有关联吗？

f24f1828fcc0.source: They are. But most of us learn these formulas independently. Calculus lets us start with $\text{circumference} = 2 \pi r$ and figure out the others — the Greeks would have appreciated this.
f24f1828fcc0.target: 它们确实有关联。但我们大多数人都是独立学习这些公式的。微积分让我们能从 $\text{circumference} = 2 \pi r$（周长 = 2πr）出发推导出其他公式——希腊人要是知道肯定会欣赏这一点。

2add58a524a4.source: . Most lessons feature contrived examples, arcane proofs, and memorization that body slam our intuition & enthusiasm.
2add58a524a4.target: 。大多数课程充斥着生搬硬套的例子、晦涩的证明，以及摧残我们直觉与热情的死记硬背。

cf8f56bd553c.source: It really shouldn’t be this way.

fd3150973bea.target: 我从学校里学到了一件事：

6ecd903e7077.target: 具体来说，在……之下依然保持斗志

d30635b65292.source: is an excellent essay on this issue that
d30635b65292.target: 是一篇关于此问题的精彩文章，文中

cf46ca13ef1d.source: “…if I had to design a mechanism for the express purpose of destroying a child’s natural curiosity and love of pattern-making, I couldn’t possibly do as good a job as is currently being done — I simply wouldn’t have the imagination to come up with the kind of senseless, soul-crushing ideas that constitute contemporary mathematics education.”
cf46ca13ef1d.target: “……如果让我专门设计一种机制，以摧毁孩子天生的好奇心和他们对模式制作的热爱为目的，我不可能做得比现在正在做的更好——我根本没那种想象力，想不出那些构成当代数学教育的、毫无意义且摧残灵魂的点子。”

69df08f7314b.source: Instead, let’s study paint chemistry, the physics of light, and the anatomy of the eye. After 12 years of this, if the kids (now teenagers) don’t hate art already, they may begin to start coloring on their own. After all, they have the “rigorous, testable” fundamentals to start appreciating art. Right?
69df08f7314b.target: 相反，让我们来学习颜料化学、光的物理学以及眼睛的解剖学。经过12年这样的学习，如果孩子们（如今已是青少年）还没有讨厌美术，他们或许会开始自己涂涂画画。毕竟，他们已经具备了“严谨、可检验”的基础去欣赏艺术。对吧？

553ab2b2b7d4.source: Poetry is similar. Imagine studying this quote (formula):
553ab2b2b7d4.target: 诗歌也是类似的。想象一下学习下面这句话（公式）：

d728a0f0de4c.source: It’s an elegant way of saying “be yourself” (and if that means writing irreverently about math, so be it). But if this were math class, we’d be counting the syllables, analyzing the iambic pentameter, and mapping out the subject, verb and object.
d728a0f0de4c.target: 这是一种表达“做你自己”的优雅方式（如果这意味着不羁地写数学，那也无妨）。但如果这是数学课，我们就会去数音节、分析抑扬格五音步，并标出主语、动词和宾语。

c5611259002e.source: , a way to express a mathematical truth.

9589ac6b4022.source: We’ve forgotten that math is about ideas, not robotically manipulating the formulas that express them.
9589ac6b4022.target: 我们忘了数学是关于思想的，而不是机械地摆弄表达这些思想的公式。

0298a41615ac.source: Feisty, are we? Well, here’s what I won’t do: recreate the existing textbooks. If you need answers
0298a41615ac.target: 很暴躁，是吧？好吧，这是我不会做的事：重造现有的教科书。如果你需要答案

271320e12171.source: for that big test, there’s plenty of
271320e12171.target: 来应付那场大考，有很多

c04e034bafda.source: . Equations aren’t enough — I want the “aha!” moments that make everything click.
c04e034bafda.target: 。光有方程是不够的——我要的是让一切豁然开朗的“aha！”时刻。

71d2d36af0a4.source: Formal mathematical language is one just one way to communicate. Diagrams, animations, and just plain talkin’ can often provide more insight than a page full of proofs.
71d2d36af0a4.target: 形式化的数学语言只是一种交流方式。图表、动画，还有 plain talkin'（ plain talkin'）往往比满页的证明更能提供洞见。

9a3be6ec5281.source: I think anyone can appreciate the core ideas of calculus. We don’t need to be writers to enjoy Shakespeare.
9a3be6ec5281.target: 我认为任何人都能领会微积分(Calculus)的核心思想。我们不必成为作家也能欣赏莎士比亚。

8322303dd539.source: It’s within your reach if you know algebra and have a general interest in math. Not long ago, reading and writing were the work of trained scribes. Yet today that can be handled by a 10-year old. Why?
8322303dd539.target: 如果你懂代数并对数学有普遍兴趣，它就在你够得着的地方。不久之前，读写还是专业抄写员的活儿。然而今天，这活儿一个10岁小孩就能干。为什么？

82ec24bf526e.source: Because we expect it. Expectations play a huge part in what’s possible. So
82ec24bf526e.target: 因为我们期待如此。期望在何为可能中扮演了巨大角色。所以

b9e7e9f63bf5.source: that calculus is just another subject. Some people get into the nitty-gritty (the writers/mathematicians). But the rest of us can still admire what’s happening, and expand our brain along the way.
b9e7e9f63bf5.target: 微积分只是另一门学科。有些人会钻进细枝末节（那些作家/数学家）。但我们其余的人仍可以欣赏正在发生的事，并在此过程中扩展我们的大脑。

0d96743f37e8.source: It’s about how far you want to go. I’d love for everyone to understand the core concepts of calculus and say “whoa”.
0d96743f37e8.target: 这取决于你想走多远。我很希望每个人都能理解微积分的核心概念并说出“whoa”。

818f1996ec32.source: as “the branch of mathematics that deals with limits and the differentiation and integration of functions of one or more variables”. It’s correct, but not helpful for beginners.
818f1996ec32.target: 为“研究极限以及一个或多个变量的函数(Function)的微分(Differentiation)和积分(Integration)的数学分支”。这没错，但对初学者没帮助。

d0afaf12f743.source: Here’s my take: Calculus does to algebra what algebra did to arithmetic.
d0afaf12f743.target: 这是我的看法：微积分对代数做的事，就像代数对算术做的事。

39b07b12564f.source: is about manipulating numbers (addition, multiplication, etc.).
39b07b12564f.target: 是关于摆弄数字（加法、乘法等）。

593a563fdbe6.source: : $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$ is a famous relationship, describing the sides of a right triangle. Algebra finds entire sets of numbers — if you know a and b, you can find c.
593a563fdbe6.target: ： $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$ 是一个著名的关系，描述直角三角形的边。代数找出整组数字——如果你知道 a 和 b，就能找到 c。

2747e3b9c4fb.source: : you can see how one equation ($\text{circumference} = 2 \pi r$) relates to a similar one ($\text{area} = \pi r^2$).
2747e3b9c4fb.target: ：你能看到一个方程（$\text{circumference} = 2 \pi r$）如何关联到类似的一个（$\text{area} = \pi r^2$）。

ee65ce696b15.source: Using calculus, we can ask all sorts of questions:
ee65ce696b15.target: 利用微积分(Calculus)，我们可以提出各种各样的问题：

949d7854d116.source: Algebra & calculus are a problem-solving duo: calculus finds new equations, and algebra solves them.
949d7854d116.target: 代数与微积分(Calculus)是一对解决问题的搭档：微积分(Calculus)找出新方程，代数去求解它们。

9a53ac1c2b0b.source: Let’s walk the walk. Suppose we know the equation for circumference ($2 \pi  r$) and want to find area. What to do?
9a53ac1c2b0b.target: 我们来实际走一遍。假设我们知道周长的方程（$2 \pi  r$）并想求面积(Area)。该怎么做？

fb1480b2d8fd.source: Here are two ways to draw a disc:
fb1480b2d8fd.target: 这里有两种画圆盘(Disc)的方法：

736db18f0873.source: The amount of “space” (area) should be the same in each case, right? And how much space does a ring use?
736db18f0873.target: 每种情况下“空间”（面积(Area)）的量应该是相同的，对吧？那么一个圆环(Ring)占用了多少空间？

8227e983ae68.source: Well, the very largest ring has radius “r” and a circumference $2 \pi r$. As the rings get smaller their circumference shrinks, but it keeps the pattern of $2 \pi \cdot \text{current radius}$. The final ring is more like a pinpoint, with no circumference at all.
8227e983ae68.target: 嗯，最大的圆环(Ring)半径(Radius)为“r”，周长(Circumference)为 $2 \pi r$。随着圆环(Ring)变小，它们的周长(Circumference)也缩小，但始终保持 $2 \pi \cdot \text{current radius}$ 的模式。最后一个圆环(Ring)更像一个针尖，根本没有周长(Circumference)。

43fa8638fbff.source: Now here’s where things get funky.
43fa8638fbff.target: 现在事情开始变得奇妙了。

83af71ebd3ca.source: Yowza! The combined area of the rings = the area of the triangle = area of circle!
83af71ebd3ca.target: 哇塞！圆环(Ring)的总面积(Area) = 三角形的面积(Area) = 圆面的面积(Area)！

d9f96fab6587.source: This was a quick example, but did you catch the key idea? We took a disc, split it up, and put the segments together in a different way. Calculus showed us that a disc and ring are intimately related: a disc is really just a bunch of rings.
d9f96fab6587.target: 这是个快速的示例，但你抓住核心思想了吗？我们把一个圆盘(Disc)拆分，然后用不同的方式把片段重新组合。微积分(Calculus)向我们表明，圆盘(Disc)和圆环(Ring)密切相关：圆盘(Disc)其实就是一堆圆环(Ring)。

93987d4db896.source: This is a recurring theme in calculus:
93987d4db896.target: 这是微积分(Calculus)中反复出现的主题：

91e3c537182c.source: And sometimes the little things are easier to work with.
91e3c537182c.target: 而且有时候，小东西更容易处理。

932200a4832d.source: Many calculus examples are based on physics. That’s great, but it can be hard to relate: honestly, how often do you know
932200a4832d.target: 许多微积分(Calculus)示例都基于物理学。那很棒，但可能难以产生共鸣：老实说，你多久会知道

82b1e355ae9c.source: for an object? Less than once a week, if that.
82b1e355ae9c.target: 一个物体的这个？就算有，一周也不到一次。

0f82a9b6a1eb.source: I prefer starting with physical, visual examples because it’s how our minds work. That ring/circle thing we made? You could build it out of several pipe cleaners, separate them, and straighten them into a crude triangle to see if the math really works. That’s just not happening with your velocity equation.
0f82a9b6a1eb.target: 我更倾向于从物理的、视觉化的示例入手，因为这是我们大脑运作的方式。我们做的那个圆环(Ring)/圆面东西？你可以用几根管状清洁条把它做出来，分开它们，拉直成一个粗糙的三角形，看看数学是否真的成立。而对于你的速度方程，那根本做不到。

0f6196f7455d.source: I can feel the math pedants firing up their keyboards. Just a few words on “rigor”.
0f6196f7455d.target: 我能感觉到数学原教旨主义者正点燃他们的键盘。只说几句关于“严谨性”的话。

3191a228cf8e.source: Did you know we don’t learn calculus the way Newton and Leibniz discovered it? They used intuitive ideas of “fluxions” and “infinitesimals” which were replaced with limits because
3191a228cf8e.target: 你知道吗，我们学习微积分(Calculus)的方式并不是牛顿和莱布尼茨发现它时的方式？他们使用了“流数”和“无穷小(Infinitesimal)”的直观概念，后来被极限(Limit)所取代，因为

b32c07bc1667.source: We’ve created complex mechanical constructs to “rigorously” prove calculus, but have lost our intuition in the process.
b32c07bc1667.target: 我们构建了复杂的机械式结构来“严谨地”证明微积分(Calculus)，却在这个过程中丢失了直觉。

6dd6e5edee79.source: We’re looking at the sweetness of sugar from the level of brain-chemistry, instead of recognizing it as Nature’s way of saying “This has lots of energy. Eat it.”
6dd6e5edee79.target: 我们正从脑化学的层面去看待糖的甜美，而不是把它认作大自然在说“这有很多能量。吃吧。”的一种方式。

ba1ff7d5029b.source: I don’t want to (and can’t) teach an analysis course or train researchers. Would it be so bad if everyone understood calculus to the “non-rigorous” level that Newton did? That it changed how they saw the world, as it did for him?
ba1ff7d5029b.target: 我不想（也没能力）教一门分析课程或培养研究者。如果每个人都能像牛顿那样以“非严谨”的水平理解微积分(Calculus)——就像他那样改变了看世界的方式——会有那么糟吗？

04aaaae08b51.source: A premature focus on rigor dissuades students and makes math hard to learn. Case in point: e is technically defined by a limit, but the
04aaaae08b51.target: 过早强调严谨性会劝退学生，并让数学变得难以学习。举个例子：e 从技术上讲是由一个极限定义的，但

b53d81d4c426.source: is how it was discovered. The natural log can be seen as an integral, or the
b53d81d4c426.target: 才是它被发现的途径。自然对数可以看作一个积分，或者

9147482edd91.source: Let’s fingerpaint a bit, and get into the chemistry along the way. Happy math.
9147482edd91.target: 让我们先随意涂鸦一番，在此过程中慢慢了解其内在原理。数学愉快。

e7f65e8fce3d.source: (PS: A kind reader has created an
e7f65e8fce3d.target: （附：一位好心的读者制作了一个

dddf6e3dc22c.source: that helps present this idea more visually (best viewed in PowerPoint, due to the animations). Thanks!)
dddf6e3dc22c.target: ，能更直观地展示这个想法（由于有动画，最好在 PowerPoint 中查看）。谢谢！）

31b9635f98d7.source: Ok bub, what’s your great idea?
31b9635f98d7.target: 好吧老兄，你有什么高见？

b0ac0c043b79.target: 那么微积分到底讲什么？

c0c6a69ca3d2.source: A note on rigor (for the math geeks)
c0c6a69ca3d2.target: 关于严谨性的说明（给数学极客）

765411b0a81e.source: Unfortunately, calculus can epitomize what’s wrong with math education
765411b0a81e.target: 不幸的是，微积分堪称数学教育弊端的缩影

dd41df9b84d0.source: Math isn’t the hard part of math; motivation is.
dd41df9b84d0.target: 数学本身的难度不是问题，动力才是。

d3042d46703c.target: 孩子们，幼儿园里不许用手指画画。

425545cd8c87.source: Math and poetry are fingers pointing at the moon. Don’t confuse the finger for the moon.
425545cd8c87.target: 数学和诗歌都是指向月亮的手指。别把手指当成了月亮。

c3caf65bd6a6.source: Instead, let’s share the core insights of calculus
c3caf65bd6a6.target: 相反，让我们分享微积分的核心洞见

bf28d110ab75.target: 代数(Algebra)寻找数字之间的规律

6002dec24895.target: 微积分(Calculus)寻找方程之间的规律

c9038f3ae7a3.source: Like evolution, calculus expands your understanding of how Nature works.
c9038f3ae7a3.target: 就像进化一样，微积分拓展了你对自然运作方式的理解。

bc2631a8ca43.source: Realize that a filled-in disc is like a set of Russian dolls.
bc2631a8ca43.target: 要意识到，一个实心圆盘就像一套俄罗斯套娃。

22d6d23dbe3e.source: Let’s unroll those rings and line them up.
22d6d23dbe3e.target: 让我们把这些圆环展开并排成一排。

2fef4522f6fd.source: Big things are made from little things.

4b22c212ba5a.source: “Sure, it works in practice. But does it work in theory?”
4b22c212ba5a.target: “没错，它在实践中行得通。但理论上行得通吗？”

a8bb4a93a5e6.source: Note: I’ve made an entire intuition-first calculus series in the style of this article:
a8bb4a93a5e6.target: 注：我已按照本文风格制作了整套以直觉为先的微积分系列：

6164a83ccd7a.source: Teachers focused more on publishing/perishing than teaching
6164a83ccd7a.target: 教师更关注发表论文/不发表就出局，而非教学

4329536685f8.source: Self-fulfilling prophecies that math is difficult, boring, unpopular or “not your subject”
4329536685f8.target: 数学很难、无聊、冷门或“不是你的菜”这类自我实现的预言

98dfa938fdc0.source: Textbooks and curriculums more concerned with

d3f580215124.target: 以及考试成绩，而非洞察力

01593a5f90eb.source: How does an equation grow and shrink? Accumulate over time?
01593a5f90eb.target: 一个方程如何增长和收缩？如何随时间累积？

a9882f2b5bf0.source: When does it reach its highest/lowest point?
a9882f2b5bf0.target: 它何时达到最高/最低点？

81e689863df2.source: How do we use variables that are constantly changing? (Heat, motion, populations, …).
81e689863df2.target: 我们如何使用不断变化的变量？（热量、运动、人口……）

787f702ae75c.target: 还有非常多，非常多的内容！

d179397d6180.source: Make a circle and fill it in

849806679b89.source: Draw a bunch of rings with a thick marker
849806679b89.target: 用粗记号笔画出一堆圆环

09c6ad226c31.source: We get a bunch of lines, making a jagged triangle. But if we take thinner rings, that triangle becomes less jagged (more on this in future articles).
09c6ad226c31.target: 我们得到一堆线条，构成一个锯齿状三角形。但如果我们取更薄的圆环，那个三角形就会变得不那么锯齿状（后续文章会详述）。

97ad497231a5.source: One side has the smallest ring (0) and the other side has the largest ring ($2 \pi r$)
97ad497231a5.target: 一侧有最小的圆环(0)，另一侧有最大的圆环($2 \pi r$)

d9f89065caa4.source: We have rings going from radius 0 to up to “r”. For each possible radius (0 to r), we just place the unrolled ring at that location.
d9f89065caa4.target: 我们有半径从 0 到“r”的圆环。对于每个可能的半径(0 到 r)，我们只需将展开的圆环放在那个位置。

1e9515487d9c.source: The total area of the “ring triangle” = $\frac{1}{2} \text{ base} \cdot \text{height} = \frac{1}{2}  (r) (2 \pi r) = \pi  r^2$, which is the formula for area!
1e9515487d9c.target: “圆环三角形”的总面积 = $\frac{1}{2} \text{ base} \cdot \text{height} = \frac{1}{2}  (r) (2 \pi r) = \pi  r^2$，这就是面积公式！
