5b73277a336e.target: 从倍角公式开始，求解 $\sin(a)$，它是 $\sin(2a)$ 中所用角的一半。《Trig without tears》（一个很棒的资料和名字）有更多细节：

3aed02253c27.source: A few other references I found helpful:
3aed02253c27.target: 我找到的其他几个有用参考：

82def5f0a18a.source: Einstein's backstory and how it influenced his work
82def5f0a18a.target: 爱因斯坦的背景故事及其对工作的影响

6836216d9661.source: The mechanics of the equation in a way that you can understand
6836216d9661.target: 以你能理解的方式解释方程的机理

fb2866da6ce7.source: The implications of the equation for our view of the Universe
fb2866da6ce7.target: 该方程对我们宇宙观的影响

c2ae4e65f75a.source: I had another great chat with Nasos Papadopoulos on the wonderful
c2ae4e65f75a.target: 我与 Nasos Papadopoulos 在精彩的

326ac1c1bd0e.source: Einstein’s Theory of Special Relativity has many consequences, including the famous equation E = MC² (Energy = Mass x Speed of Light²) and the notion that measurements of space and time are relative to the observer.
326ac1c1bd0e.target: 爱因斯坦的狭义相对论有很多推论，包括著名的方程 E = MC²（能量 = 质量 × 光速²）以及时空测量相对于观察者的概念。

e5124814e409.target: 在这期 30 分钟的节目中，我们讨论了：

335b03417799.source: It was a lot of fun -- hope you enjoy it.
335b03417799.target: 这非常有趣——希望你喜欢。

bd6b14a344bb.source: Have a mental gutcheck for learning.

82f2712da49e.source: Hold lessons to a higher standard.

0451ad5fc106.source: The goal of this lesson is for you to truly feel an insight in your bones. The words are just hints about how to get there.
0451ad5fc106.target: 本节课的目标是让你真正从骨子里感受到一种洞察。文字只是提示如何达到那种境界。

2e72f7b5099f.source: Imaginary numbers are the square root of negative numbers. We label the square root of -1 "i". Time for practice problems.
2e72f7b5099f.target: 虚数(Imaginary numbers)是负数的平方根。我们将-1的平方根标记为"i"。现在来做练习题。

6114fdba5bb1.source: "Aw, you weren't smart enough to rely solely on the technical description, here's a diagram."
6114fdba5bb1.target: "啊，你不够聪明，无法仅凭技术描述理解，给你一张图吧。"

ef61f9bea340.source: Let's say an alien visits Earth and wants to understand our Earthly ways. After a few hours of questioning, we resort to "Uh... just Google it." Back to playing Factorio.
ef61f9bea340.target: 假设一个外星人造访地球，想了解我们地球人的方式。经过几个小时的询问，我们只好说："呃……谷歌一下。"然后继续玩《异星工厂》。

f8afe2063ded.source: The alien starts looking up random words.

a15f4fab5802.source: "Red (adjective): of a color at the end of a spectrum next to orange..."
a15f4fab5802.target: "红色（形容词）：光谱中靠近橙色末端的一种颜色……"

2282d5294645.source: We Earthers know the dictionary is missing a huge caveat:
2282d5294645.target: 我们地球人都知道这个字典缺少一个重要的说明：

2a71436a66aa.source: Dear Reader: You can't truly understand 'red' by reading about it. You need to see it for yourself. The dictionary definition is an attempt with dry words. Even better is a metaphor: red is the sound of a blaring trumpet, the taste of a chili pepper, the feeling of stepping barefoot on a lego. But please, stop reading and find yourself a strawberry.
2a71436a66aa.target: 亲爱的读者：你无法通过阅读真正理解"红色"。你需要亲眼看到它。字典定义只是用枯燥的文字尝试描述。更好的方法是比喻：红色是吹响的小号的声音，是辣椒的味道，是赤脚踩到乐高积木的感觉。但请停止阅读，去找一颗草莓吧。

97cdfb77471e.source: We know reading gives a limited understanding of the topic. But if we weren't paying attention, the alien would have claimed mastery of the official definition, and gone back to teach generations of students about it.
97cdfb77471e.target: 我们知道阅读只能带来有限的理解。但如果我们不注意，外星人就会声称掌握了官方定义，然后回去教给一代又一代学生。

b960a5a7576a.source: Whoops, I'm getting late for math class. What was this week's topic again? Oh right, imaginary numbers:
b960a5a7576a.target: 哎呀，我数学课要迟到了。这周的话题是什么来着？哦对，虚数：

2a0870100ad1.source: There's a missing caveat to every math lesson:
2a0870100ad1.target: 每节数学课都缺少一个说明：

b704f1cdb288.source: Let's take the concept of imaginary numbers. The abstract definition trotted out in countless lessons is something like:
b704f1cdb288.target: 以虚数的概念为例。无数节课中抛出的抽象定义大致是：

bed02d577d76.source: Ugh. There's no acknowledgement that the words "square root of a negative" are baffling limited, and no way to truly understand the idea. Here's the missing "see the color" caveat (
bed02d577d76.target: 呃。没有人承认"负数的平方根"这几个字令人困惑且局限，根本无法真正理解这个概念。这里就是缺失的"看见颜色"的说明（

c0e21529da88.source: Hey. That technical definition is frustratingly lifeless. You're probably wondering how negatives can have square roots. Picture imaginary numbers as rotations, like this:
c0e21529da88.target: 嘿。那个技术定义枯燥得令人沮丧。你可能正纳闷负数怎么能有平方根。把虚数想象成旋转，就像这样：

43f799cda4a6.source: Whoa. The "square root of a negative" is really "halfway negative", or something pointing vertically. If positives are East, negatives are West, imaginary numbers let us go North/South. Now, back to your dictionary definition.
43f799cda4a6.target: 哇。“负数的平方根”实际上是“半负的”，或者说是指向垂直方向的东西。如果正数是东，负数是西，虚数则让我们走向北/南。现在，回到你的字典定义。

d38c8c26c8d5.source: There's a pernicious objection that getting an intuition for a concept is a "baby version" of the real thing. (
d38c8c26c8d5.target: 有一种有害的反对意见，认为对概念获得直觉是真实事物的“婴儿版”。（

ca125378c75d.source: ) That's like claiming seeing a color is the "baby version" of reading about it!
ca125378c75d.target: ）这好比声称看到颜色是阅读关于它的描述的“婴儿版”！

dca0b5725d4c.source: Experiencing an idea is our goal all along. If thermodynamics can be truly understood via an interpretative dance in a hula skirt... well, I'll bring the coconuts. I want an intuition.
dca0b5725d4c.target: 体验一个概念一直是我们追求的目标。如果热力学可以通过穿草裙的诠释舞蹈来真正理解……好吧，我会带上椰子。我想要直觉。

05e8a7dcfd36.source: We aren't hard drives trying to store the text of a novel without its meaning. (And for what it's worth: progress with imaginary numbers truly began after the 2d rotation interpretation was discovered.)
05e8a7dcfd36.target: 我们不是试图存储小说文本而不理解其意义的硬盘。（而且，值得一提的是：虚数的真正进展是在发现了二维旋转解释之后开始的。）

93c9f181d63c.source: Ok. This color analogy helps us look for an experience beyond the lesson. What can we do with this mindset?
93c9f181d63c.target: 好的。这个色彩类比帮助我们寻找超越课程本身的体验。有了这种心态，我们能做什么呢？

7665811f8968.source: When I'm in a lesson (video, textbook, lecture, etc.) I'm constantly asking "Am I seeing the color, or just getting the description?". A quick check is whether you can make analogies about what you're learning. Homer (the blind poet, that one) had red described as a blaring trumpet. That poetic description conveys more understanding than "the wavelength of light at 650 nanometers".
7665811f8968.target: 当我在上课（视频、教科书、讲座等）时，我不断问自己：“我是在看到颜色，还是仅仅得到描述？”一个快速检验法是，看你是否能够对你正在学习的内容做出类比。荷马（那位盲诗人）曾把红色描述为嘹亮的小号声。那种诗意的描述比“波长为650纳米的光”传达了更多的理解。

3c215adc7939.source: If a concept isn't clicking, the most likely cause is that the lesson wasn't helpful enough.  It may be throwing words at you when an experience is needed. Yes, some people can get by with words alone, just like some can glance at sheet music and think "That sounds beautiful". I'm not one of those people, I need to find the play button.
3c215adc7939.target: 如果一个概念无法理解，最可能的原因是课程不够有帮助。它可能在你需要体验时，只是扔给你一堆词汇。是的，有些人仅凭词汇就能应付，就像有些人看一眼乐谱就能想“这听起来很美”。我不是那种人，我需要找到播放按钮。

2e45e699d2cf.source: This mindset does lead to some potentially uncomfortable questions: Has the teacher viscerally experienced the idea being taught? Is that the goal of their lesson? Is this lesson part of a chain of dictionary definitions recited by aliens?
2e45e699d2cf.target: 这种心态确实会引发一些可能令人不安的问题：老师是否亲身深切体验过所教的概念？这是他们课程的目标吗？这节课是否是外星人背诵的一连串字典定义的一部分？

9c17a020c023.source: The counterpoint to having higher standards is compassion: teachers don't want to miss the point on purpose. If we're open to treating a lesson as an exploration, a potential experience, we should welcome any chance to have a "I finally see the color!" moment (teacher and student alike).
9c17a020c023.target: 高标准背后的另一面是同情：老师们并不想刻意错过要点。如果我们愿意把课程看作一次探索、一次潜在的体验，我们应当欢迎任何能够拥有“我终于看到颜色了！”时刻的机会（老师和学生都一样）。

a75d30ae800d.source: Are people naturally curious? Let's find out. Did you know a new shade of blue pigment has been discovered? No real-world object could have that color. Feel that growing itch of curiosity?
a75d30ae800d.target: 人们天生好奇吗？让我们来验证一下。你知道发现了一种新的蓝色颜料色调吗？现实中没有任何物体拥有那种颜色。感觉到那股愈加强烈的好奇心了吗？

54395eb50c72.source: Interesting, right? Shiny and shimmering? Now, maybe you don't want to paint your house that color, but you wanted to see it for yourself. How frustrated would you be if the
54395eb50c72.target: 很有趣，对吧？闪闪发光？现在，也许你不想把你的房子漆成那种颜色，但你想亲眼看看。如果

6c8f80a6a5a6.source: the curiosity we can unlock for new ideas if we know an experience is coming. Colors are natural enough that we're sure we'll experience
6c8f80a6a5a6.target: 如果我们知道一个体验即将到来，我们能激发对新鲜概念的好奇心。颜色足够自然，我们确信我们会体验到

05f7e63ee152.source: when we look. But with math, we may have forgotten (or never had) that eye-opening experience, so "a new math concept" is like having someone describe that awesome movie they saw. "Oh, there was this part, and this part. And then this happened. I loved it! Why don't you?". We have innate curiosity when a subject is approached with an experience in mind, built on the trust of having several previous Aha! moments.
05f7e63ee152.target: 当我们看的时候。但对于数学，我们可能已经忘记了（或者从未有过）那种开阔眼界的体验，所以“一个新数学概念”就像听别人描述他们看过的一部很棒的电影。“哦，有这部分，这部分，然后发生了这个。我喜欢它！你为什么不喜欢呢？”当我们以一种体验的心态去接触一门学科时，我们会拥有天生的好奇心，这种好奇心建立在之前多次“啊哈！”时刻的信任之上。

af2de778d2bb.source: Words and symbols have their place: they're compact, precise, and easily expressed. But they should come after the experience (show, then tell). Once the experience is understood, and enthusiasm fired up, words can act as placeholders for concepts in our mind's eye ("red sports car").
af2de778d2bb.target: 词汇和符号自有其位置：它们紧凑、精确且易于表达。但它们应该出现在体验之后（先展示，后讲述）。一旦体验被理解，热情被点燃，词汇就可以充当脑海中概念的代表（比如“红色跑车”）。

ec1f1224f68b.source: Ultimately, I don't learn because I want more entries in my mental dictionary. I want to see new colors.
ec1f1224f68b.target: 归根结底，我学习不是因为我想要在心理词典中添加更多词条。我想看到新的颜色。

060df8f9345d.source: How do you uncover the experience behind an idea? In the best case, your teacher had one which they can share, saving you the trouble of looking.
060df8f9345d.target: 如何揭示一个概念背后的体验？最佳情况是，你的老师有这种体验并且可以分享，从而省去你寻找的麻烦。

6092a64f86f2.source: But many times you're on your own. I use the
6092a64f86f2.target: 但很多时候你得靠自己。我使用

ceaf3011bfed.source: as a checklist for what helps a concept click:
ceaf3011bfed.target: 作为帮助概念理解的检查清单：

6cc56a171e1e.source: If a lesson isn't clicking, I run through that checklist: Do I have an analogy in place? A diagram? An example? A plain-English version? Can I find someone who as the above?
6cc56a171e1e.target: 如果一节课没听懂，我会逐条检查清单：我有类比吗？有图表吗？有例子吗？有简明英语版本吗？我能找到具备上述条件的人吗？

08e75bffb23b.source: These pieces aren't always easy to find, and it can take years. But I never want to stop looking. Just because I haven't had an experience
08e75bffb23b.target: 这些东西并不总是容易找到，可能需要数年时间。但我从未想停止寻找。仅仅因为我还没有经历过，

ca88a2420728.target: 并不意味着它不可能发生。

fb8410a7c119.source: What specific steps can we take to align our efforts?
fb8410a7c119.target: 我们可以采取哪些具体步骤来协调我们的努力？

e9b9651ece06.source: I usually avoid current events, but recent
e9b9651ece06.target: 我通常不关注时事，但最近

22a094cb0dea.source: in the math world prompted me to chime in. To recap, there’ve been heated discussions about math education and the role of online resources like Khan Academy.
22a094cb0dea.target: 在数学界发生的一些事情促使我也来插句话。回顾一下，关于数学教育和像Khan Academy这样的在线资源的作用，一直有激烈的讨论。

78444f1431e1.source: As fun as a good math showdown may appear, there’s a bigger threat: Apathy. And Justin Bieber.
78444f1431e1.target: 尽管一场精彩的数学对决看起来很有趣，但还有一个更大的威胁：冷漠。还有贾斯汀·比伯。

95b186129e97.source: Educators, online or not, don’t compete with each other. They struggle to be noticed in our math-phobic society, where we casually
95b186129e97.target: 教育工作者，无论是否在线，都不应该互相竞争。他们在我们这个害怕数学的社会中挣扎着获得关注，在这个社会里，人们随意地

a918d6ffcdc1.source: “Should algebra be taught at all?” not “Can algebra be taught better?”.
a918d6ffcdc1.target: “代数到底应不应该教？”而不是“代数能不能教得更好？”。

991c0dcb10d5.source: Entertainment is great; I love Starcraft. But it’s alarming when a prominent learning initiative gets less attention than a throwaway pop song (Super Bass: 268M views in a year; Khan Academy: 175M views in 5 years). Online learning is a rounding error next to Justin Bieber — “Baby” has 700M views alone.
991c0dcb10d5.target: 娱乐很精彩；我喜欢星际争霸。但当一个重要的学习计划受到的关注还不如一首随手创作的流行歌曲时，这令人担忧（Super Bass: 一年2.68亿次观看；Khan Academy: 五年1.75亿次观看）。在线学习在贾斯汀·比伯面前只是个零头——仅“Baby”就有7亿次观看。

bec6ee4b7d19.source: What do we need? The Math Avengers. Different heroes, different tactics, and not without differences… but everyone fighting on the same side. Against Bieber.
bec6ee4b7d19.target: 我们需要什么？数学复仇者(Math Avengers)。不同的英雄，不同的策略，有分歧也很正常……但每个人都站在同一战线。对抗比伯。

5de3f99048ad.source: I could be walking into a knife fight with an ice cream cone, but I’d like to approach each side with empathy and offer specific suggestions to bridge the gap.
5de3f99048ad.target: 我可能像是拿着冰淇淋蛋筒走进一场刀战，但我愿意带着同理心接近每一方，并提出弥合差距的具体建议。

2a05c504e698.source: Superheroes need a misunderstanding before working together. It’s inevitable, and here’s ours (as a math relationship, of course):
2a05c504e698.target: 超级英雄们在合作前都需要一个误解。这是不可避免的，而我们的误解是这样的（当然是数学关系方面的）：

10fde6bdc6ae.source: Bad Teacher < Online Learning < Good teacher
10fde6bdc6ae.target: 差老师 < 在线学习 < 好老师

0d1a63c2aed8.source: The problem is in considering each part separately.
0d1a63c2aed8.target: 问题在于把每个部分分开考虑。

c5d1b165a9f1.source: Is Khan Academy (free, friendly, always available) better than a mean, uninformed, or absent teacher? Yes!
c5d1b165a9f1.target: Khan Academy（免费、友好、随时可用）比一个刻薄、无知或缺席的老师好吗？是的！

194b1e0bb682.source: Is an engaging human experience better than learning from a computer? Yes!
194b1e0bb682.target: 引人入胜的人类体验比从电脑上学习更好吗？是的！

c08cefcd479b.source: But, really, the ultimate solution is Online learning + Good Teachers.
c08cefcd479b.target: 但说真的，最终的解决方案是在线学习 + 好老师。

a8d3e86e1bdd.source: Tactics differ, but we can agree on the mission: give students great online resources, and give teachers tools to augment their classroom.
a8d3e86e1bdd.target: 策略可能不同，但我们在使命上达成一致：为学生提供优秀的在线资源，为教师提供增强课堂的工具。

b5af84a65701.source: I love learning. Here’s my brief background so you can root out my biases.
b5af84a65701.target: 我热爱学习。以下是我的简要背景，以便你了解我的立场。

cd2ede1ec538.source: I was a good student. I was on the math team and hummed songs like “Life is a sine-wave, I want to de-rive it all night long…”. I drew comics about sine & cosine, the crimefighting duo. You might say I enjoyed math.
cd2ede1ec538.target: 我曾是个好学生。我参加了数学竞赛，哼着像“生活是正弦波，我想整夜求导……”这样的歌。我画了关于正弦和余弦（这对打击犯罪的搭档）的漫画。你可以说我喜欢数学。

abf552be5533.source: I entered college and was slapped in the face by my freshman year math class.
abf552be5533.target: 我进入大学，大一数学课给了我一记耳光。

65110f2d239e.source: Professors at big universities must know everything, right? If I didn’t get a concept, something must be wrong with me, right?
65110f2d239e.target: 大型大学的教授必须无所不知，对吗？如果我不理解某个概念，那一定是我有问题，对吗？

a0ba6a2f7e16.source: I had a WWII-era, finish-half-a-proof-in-class, grouch of a teacher. I bombed the midterm and was distressed. Math… I loved math! I didn’t mind difficulties in Physics or Spanish. But math? What I used to sing and draw cartoons about?
a0ba6a2f7e16.target: 我有一位二战时期、上课只讲半道证明、脾气暴躁的老师。期中考试我考砸了，非常沮丧。数学……我曾热爱的数学！我不介意物理或西班牙语的困难。但是数学？那个我曾经唱歌画漫画的数学？

9693727e0dc8.source: Finals came. While cramming, I found notes online, far more helpful than my book and teacher. I sent an email to the class, gingerly suggesting BY EUCLID YOU NEED TO READ THESE WEBSITES THEY ARE SO MUCH BETTER THAN THE PROFESSOR. The websites turned up on an index card in the computer lab that evening. How many of us were struggling?
9693727e0dc8.target: 期末考试来临。临时抱佛脚时，我在网上找到了笔记，比我的书和老师有用得多。我给班级发了一封邮件，小心翼翼地建议：我的天，你们需要读这些网站，它们比教授好太多了。那晚，这些网站出现在计算机实验室的索引卡上。我们中有多少人在挣扎？

b3cb4bab4f94.source: I was studying, staring at a blue book when an aha! moment struck. I could see the Matrix: equations were a description of twists, turns and rotations. Their meaning became “obvious” in the way a circle must be round. What else could it be?
b3cb4bab4f94.target: 我正在学习，盯着蓝色本子，突然灵光一闪。我看到了矩阵：方程是对扭转、转弯和旋转的描述。它们的含义变得“显而易见”，就像圆必须是圆的一样。还能是别的吗？

f95bb596387f.source: I was elated and furious: “Why didn’t they explain it like that the first time?!”
f95bb596387f.target: 我既兴奋又愤怒：“为什么他们第一次不这样解释？！”

244467938dcf.source: Paranoid I’d forget, I put my notes online and they evolved into this site: insights that
244467938dcf.target: 我担心自己会忘记，于是把我的笔记放到网上，它们演变成了这个网站：那些对我有用的见解。关于

338bc6dbab87.source: became popular — I think we all crave deep understanding. Bad teaching was a burst of gamma rays: I’m normally mild mannered, but enter Hulk Mode when recalling how my passion nearly died.
338bc6dbab87.target: 的文章变得流行起来——我认为我们都渴望深刻的理解。糟糕的教学像一阵伽马射线爆发：我平时性情温和，但想起我的热情差点被扼杀时，就会进入浩克模式。

cca175a02226.source: A bad experience can undo years of good ones. Students need resources to sidestep bad teaching.
cca175a02226.target: 一次糟糕的经历可以抵消多年的美好经历。学生需要资源来避开糟糕的教学。

7acd76379a2d.source: Hard-won insights, sometimes found after years of teaching, need to be shared
7acd76379a2d.target: 来之不易的见解，有时在多年教学后才被发现，需要被分享。

1e92c788b1a5.source: Learning “success” means having basic skills and the passion to learn more. A year, 5 years from now, do people seek out math? Or at least not hate it? (Compare #ihatemath to #ihategeography)
1e92c788b1a5.target: 学习的“成功”意味着拥有基本技能和进一步学习的热情。一年、五年后，人们会主动寻找数学吗？或者至少不讨厌它？（对比#我讨厌数学和#我讨厌地理）

f30851423c0c.source: (Oh, I had great teachers too, like
f30851423c0c.target: （哦，我也有过很棒的老师，比如

3d21f4638dc2.source: . The bad one just unlocked the Hulk.)
3d21f4638dc2.target: 。只是那位糟糕的老师解锁了浩克。）

cff983a03961.source: I recently heard a quote about constructive dialog: “Don’t argue the exact point a person made. Consider their position and respond to the best point they
cff983a03961.target: 我最近听到一句关于建设性对话的名言：“不要争论一个人提出的确切观点。考虑他们的立场，并回应他们最好的观点。”

f7f54fcfcc0a.source: Yes, other resources and tutorials exist, but there’s power in a giant, organized collection. We visit Wikipedia because we know what to expect, and it’s consistent.
f7f54fcfcc0a.target: 是的，其他资源和教程也存在，但一个庞大且组织有序的集合有着强大的力量。我们访问维基百科是因为我们知道会得到什么，而且它是一致的。

2bfb7aa6caea.source: Khan Academy provides consistent, non-judgmental tutorials. There are exercises and discussions for every topic. You don’t need to scour YouTube, digest hour-long calculus lectures, or open up PDF worksheets for practice.
2bfb7aa6caea.target: 可汗学院提供一致、不带评判的教程。每个主题都有练习和讨论。你不需要在YouTube上搜索、消化一小时的微积分讲座，或者打开PDF工作表来练习。

5e47e7f8a100.source: So, let’s use the magic of friendly, exploratory, bite-sized learning of topics.
5e47e7f8a100.target: 那么，让我们利用友好、探索性、小片段学习的魔力。

e69e5e4e3fa1.source: Teachers and online tools don’t “compete” any more than Mr. Rogers and Sesame Street did. They’re both ways to help.
e69e5e4e3fa1.target: 老师和在线工具并不“竞争”，就像罗杰斯先生和芝麻街一样。它们都是帮助的方式。

79c9acf0a3ae.source: I do think the name “Khan Academy” presents a challenge to community building. Would you rather write for Wikipedia or the Jimmy-Wales-o-pedia?
79c9acf0a3ae.target: 我确实认为“可汗学院”这个名字给社区建设带来了挑战。你更愿意为维基百科还是吉米·威尔士百科写作？

2a223130509f.source: Wikipedia really feels like a community effort, and though there are alternatives, in general it’s a well-loved resource.
2a223130509f.target: 维基百科真的感觉像是社区的努力，尽管有替代品，但总的来说它是一个深受喜爱的资源。

ada8ff2c49fa.source: I think teachers may hesitate to use Khan Academy, not out of jealousy, but concern that a single pedagogical approach could overpower all others. Let’s build an online resource that can take input from the math community.
ada8ff2c49fa.target: 我认为老师们可能对使用可汗学院犹豫不决，不是出于嫉妒，而是担心单一的教学方法可能会压倒所有其他方法。让我们建立一个可以从数学社区获取输入的在线资源。

2d9b4233ecd8.source: It’s easy to misunderstand Khan Academy’s goal. I’ve seen many of their blog posts and videos, and believe Khan Academy wants to work
2d9b4233ecd8.target: 很容易误解可汗学院的目标。我看过他们的许多博客文章和视频，相信可汗学院希望与

3504206a9e03.target: 教师合作，以促进深入理解。

24d432cb0ee8.source: But, some news coverage shows students working silently in front of computers
24d432cb0ee8.target: 但是，一些新闻报道显示学生在电脑前默默学习

f98dd545d4bf.source: , not watching at home to free up class time for personal discussions.
f98dd545d4bf.target: ，而不是在家观看以腾出课堂时间进行个人讨论。

2806b3af2c15.source: The teacher doesn’t appear to be involved or interacting, and that misuse of a learning tool is a nightmare for teachers who want a personal connection. Let’s have an online resource that directly contributes to offline interactions also.
2806b3af2c15.target: 老师似乎没有参与或互动，这种对学习工具的误用对于希望建立个人联系的老师来说是一场噩梦。让我们拥有一个可以直接促进线下互动的在线资源。

a586f96bde44.source: I’ve seen that insights emerge hours (or years) after learning a subject. For example, we’ve “known” since 4th grade what a million and billion are: 1,000,000 and 1,000,000,000.
a586f96bde44.target: 我注意到洞察力是在学习一个主题数小时（或数年）后出现的。例如，我们从四年级起就“知道”百万和十亿是什么：1,000,000 和 1,000,000,000。

484254f6b32d.source: But do we feel it? How long is a million seconds, roughly? C’mon, guess. Ready? It’s 12 days.
484254f6b32d.target: 但我们能感觉到吗？一百万秒大约有多长？来吧，猜一下。准备好了吗？是12天。

93972c32d107.source: Ok, now how long is a billion seconds? It’s… wait for it… 31 years. 31 years!
93972c32d107.target: 好了，现在十亿秒有多长？它是……等等……31年。31年！

6f71c94885d5.source: That’s the difference between knowing and feeling an idea. Passion comes from feeling.
6f71c94885d5.target: 这就是知道和感受到一个想法之间的区别。激情来自感受。

9328f3a25400.source: Teachers draw on years of experience to get ideas to click — let’s feed this back into the online lessons.
9328f3a25400.target: 老师凭借多年的经验让想法被理解——让我们把这些反馈到在线课程中。

7de37e66ebd6.source: We teach for the same reason: to help students. Here’s a few specific situations to consider.
7de37e66ebd6.target: 我们教学出于同样的原因：帮助学生。这里有一些具体的情况需要考虑。

be79ee948927.source: For many, Khan Academy is their only positive math experience: not teachers, or peers, or parents, but a video. Sure, it’s not the same as an in-person teacher, but it’s miles beyond an absent or hostile one. If an education experience gets someone excited to learn, and coming back to math, we should celebrate.
be79ee948927.target: 对许多人来说，可汗学院是他们唯一积极的数学体验：不是老师、同学或家长，而是一个视频。当然，它比不上真人老师，但比缺席或充满敌意的老师好得多。如果一个教育体验能让人对学习产生兴趣，并重新回到数学中，我们应该为之庆祝。

5ff6ab228049.source: Remember, despite years of positive experiences and acing tests, a sufficiently bad class nearly drove me away from math. Resources like Khan Academy offer a lifeline: “Even with a bad teacher, I can still learn”.
5ff6ab228049.target: 记住，尽管有多年积极经历和考试高分，一堂极其糟糕的课几乎让我远离数学。像可汗学院这样的资源提供了救命稻草：“即使老师不好，我仍然可以学习。”

959f0557c254.source: When someone is interested, we need to feed their curiosity. I get a lot of traffic from Khan Academy comments — how can we help students dive deeper, without making them trudge randomly through the internet?
959f0557c254.target: 当有人感兴趣时，我们需要满足他们的好奇心。我从可汗学院的评论中获得了很多流量——我们如何帮助学生深入探索，而不用让他们在互联网上盲目乱逛？

320c305a412f.source: Lastly, we all learn differently. I generally prefer text to videos (faster to read, and I can “pause” with my eyes and think). Some like the homemade feel of Khan’s videos. Others might like the polished overviews in
320c305a412f.target: 最后，我们学习的方式各不相同。我通常更喜欢文本而不是视频（阅读更快，而且可以用眼睛“暂停”并思考）。有些人喜欢可汗视频的自制感。其他人可能喜欢其中精美的概述

bee17d9f567b.source: Let’s offer several types of resources for students to enjoy.
bee17d9f567b.target: 让我们提供多种类型的资源供学生享受。

0b6a613780a4.source: Still here? Fantastic. To all teachers, online and non:
0b6a613780a4.target: 还在看吗？太好了。致所有老师，在线和线下：

5fe53a762c57.source: One idea: Make a curated, collaborative, easy-to-explore teaching resource.
5fe53a762c57.target: 一个想法：制作一个精心策划、协作、易于探索的教学资源。

1709aef217b9.source: Khan Academy is well-organized: each topic has a video and sample problems. How about sections for complementary teaching styles, projects, and misconceptions?
1709aef217b9.target: 可汗学院组织得很好：每个主题都有视频和示例问题。那么补充教学风格、项目和误解的章节呢？

b072029c66bb.source: Imagine a student could select their “Math hero” as Khan Academy or PatrickJMT or James Tanton and see lessons in the style they prefer (like Wikipedia, curate the list to “notable” resources).
b072029c66bb.target: 想象一下，学生可以选择他们的“数学英雄”为可汗学院或PatrickJMT或James Tanton，并以他们喜欢的风格看到课程（像维基百科一样，将列表策划为“著名”资源）。

320a686e82e7.source: Imagine teachers could explore the best in-class activities (“What projects work well for negative numbers?”).
320a686e82e7.target: 想象一下，教师可以探索最好的课堂活动（“哪些项目对负数效果好？”）。

77ecc0eba366.source: Whatever the style, make it easy for other educators to contribute. Want project-based videos? Sure. Need step-by-step tutorials? Great. Prefer a conceptual overview? No problem.
77ecc0eba366.target: 无论风格如何，让其他教育工作者易于贡献。想要基于项目的视频？当然。需要逐步教程？很好。偏爱概念性概述？没问题。

1a17e28fe86d.source: Each teacher keeps their house style. Let Hulk smash, and Captain America handle the hostage negotiations. Use the hero that suits you.
1a17e28fe86d.target: 每位老师保持自己的风格。让浩克去砸东西，美国队长处理人质谈判。使用适合你的英雄。

b3cd88fb284c.source: Perfect? Nope. But it’s a starting point to think about how we can work together.
b3cd88fb284c.target: 完美吗？不。但这是一个思考我们如何合作的起点。

fb5c330d671f.source: Let’s focus on the overlap and align our efforts: different heroes, different tactics, and on the same side.
fb5c330d671f.target: 让我们专注于共同点，调整我们的努力：不同的英雄，不同的策略，但站在同一阵营。

4d9b1d831586.source: positive flux leaves a closed surface

43b405c550e8.source: negative flux enters a closed surface

f07f9b74cfce.source: Flux is the amount of “something” (electric field, bananas, whatever you want) passing through a surface.
f07f9b74cfce.target: 通量(Flux)是通过某个表面的“某物”（电场、香蕉等任何东西）的量。

0dc8a80e855b.source: depends on strength of the field, the size of the surface it passes through, and their orientation.
0dc8a80e855b.target: 取决于场的强度、它所通过的表面大小以及它们的朝向。

4384d6ddded2.source: The surface you are considering (shape, size and orientation)
4384d6ddded2.target: 你所考虑的表面（形状、大小和朝向）

2e0ad6734c54.source: The source of the flux (strength of the field, and which way it is spitting out
2e0ad6734c54.target: 通量的来源（场的强度以及它喷出的方向）

94edadaf3ab9.source: Find the flux at each piece

c1fb43ca0fbc.source: Add up the small units of flux to get total flux (integrate).
c1fb43ca0fbc.target: 将所有小的通量单位加起来得到总通量（积分）。

5f4e69f84291.source: Once you understand flux intuitively, you don’t need to memorize equations. The formulas become “obvious” dare I say. However, it took a lot of effort to truly understand that:
5f4e69f84291.target: 一旦你直观地理解了通量，就不需要死记硬背方程了。公式会变得“显而易见”，我敢说。但是，要真正理解这一点需要付出很多努力：

4d3f0bcaa704.source: Your vector calculus math life will be so much better once you understand flux. And who doesn’t want that?
4d3f0bcaa704.target: 一旦你理解了通量，你的向量微积分学习生涯会好得多。谁不想这样呢？

589b310a04bf.source: Think of flux as the amount of

2c3191fa5040.source: crossing a surface. This “something” can be water, wind, electric field, bananas, pretty much anything you can imagine. Math books will use abstract concepts like electric fields, which is pretty hard to visualize. I find bananas more memorable, so we’ll be using those.
2c3191fa5040.target: 穿过一个表面的量。这个“某种东西”可以是水、风、电场、香蕉，几乎任何你能想象的东西。数学书会使用像电场这样的抽象概念，这很难可视化。我觉得香蕉更容易记住，所以我们将使用香蕉。

36370156c098.source: To measure the flux (i.e. bananas) passing through a surface, we need to know
36370156c098.target: 为了测量穿过一个表面的通量（即香蕉），我们需要知道

37a38a526ca1.source: The strength of the field is important – would you rather have a handful of \$5 or \$20 bills “flux” into your bank account? Would you rather have a big or little banana come your way? No need to answer that one.
37a38a526ca1.target: 场的强度很重要——你希望一把 \$5 还是 \$20 的钞票“通量”进入你的银行账户？你希望一个大香蕉还是小香蕉朝你而来？不需要回答这个问题。

151144649b1d.source: Keep a few ideas in mind when considering flux:
151144649b1d.target: 在考虑通量时，记住几个要点：

dccca54c4cdf.source: Vector Field: This is the source of the flux: the thing shooting out bananas, or exerting some force (like gravity or electromagnetism). Flux doesn’t have to be a physical object — you can measure the “pulling force” exerted by a field.
dccca54c4cdf.target: 向量场：这是通量的来源：即射出香蕉或施加某种力（如重力或电磁力）的东西。通量不一定是物理对象——你可以测量一个场施加的“拉力”。

e21bb2466dc1.source: Surface: This is the boundary the flux is crossing through or acting on. The boundary could be a sphere, a plane, even the top of a bucket. Notice that the boundary may not exist — the top of a bucket traces out a circle, but the hole isn’t actually there. We’re considering the flux passing through the region the circle defines.
e21bb2466dc1.target: 表面：这是通量穿过或作用的边界。边界可以是球体、平面，甚至桶的顶部。注意边界可能不存在——桶的顶部画出一个圆，但孔实际上并不存在。我们考虑穿过该圆所定义区域的通量。

fd97f2bc439c.source: Timing: We measure flux at a single point in time. Freeze time and ask “Right now, at this moment, how much stuff is passing through my surface?”. If your field doesn’t change over time, then all is well. If your field
fd97f2bc439c.target: 时间：我们在一个时间点测量通量。冻结时间并问“现在，此刻，有多少物质穿过我的表面？”如果你的场不随时间变化，那么一切正常。如果你的场

cc64fc6e448c.source: change, then you need to pick a point in time to measure the flux.
cc64fc6e448c.target: 变化，那么你需要选择一个时间点来测量通量(Flux)。

b2097856e50c.source: Measurement: Flux is a total, and is not “per unit area” or “per unit volume”. Flux is the total force you feel, the total number of bananas you see flying by your surface. Think of flux like weight. (There is a separate idea of "flux density" (flux/volume) called
b2097856e50c.target: 测量：通量是一个总量，不是“每单位面积”或“每单位体积”。通量是你感受到的总力，是你看到飞过表面的香蕉总数。把通量想象成重量。（有一个单独的概念叫“通量密度(Flux Density)”（通量/体积），称为

6a6ee7efe838.source: The source of flux has a huge impact on the total flux. Doubling the source (doubling the “banana-ness” of each banana), will double the flux passing through a surface.
6a6ee7efe838.target: 通量的源对总通量有巨大影响。将源加倍（将每根香蕉的“香蕉性”加倍），会使通过表面的通量加倍。

7ed5c53c004b.source: Total flux also depends on the orientation of the field and the surface. When our surface completely faces the field it captures maximum flux, like a sail facing directly into the wind. As the surface tilts away from the field, the flux decreases as less and less flux crosses the surface.
7ed5c53c004b.target: 总通量还取决于场和表面的方向。当我们的表面完全朝向场时，它捕获最大通量，就像帆直接面对风。随着表面偏离场的方向，通量减少，因为越来越少的通量穿过表面。

af1118bcced0.source: Eventually, we get zero flux when the source and boundary are parallel — the flux is passing over the boundary, but not crossing through it. It would be like holding a bucket
af1118bcced0.target: 最终，当源和边界平行时，我们得到零通量——通量经过边界，但没有穿过它。就像在水瀑布下拿着一个桶

0fae26ba3161.source: under a waterfall. You wouldn’t capture much water (ignoring splashing) and may get a few funny looks.
0fae26ba3161.target: ，你几乎接不到水（忽略飞溅），还可能招来奇怪的目光。

c53d0c37e766.source: Total flux also depends on the size of our surface. In the same field, a bigger bucket will capture more flux than a smaller one. When we figure out our total flux, we need to see how much field is passing through our entire surface.
c53d0c37e766.target: 总通量还取决于我们表面的大小。在同一场中，大桶比小桶捕获更多通量。当我们计算总通量时，需要看看有多少场穿过我们的整个表面。

e8243398ce54.source: This is simple stuff so far, right? If you forget, just think about capturing water from a waterfall. What matters? The strength of the waterfall, the size of the bucket and the orientation of the bucket.
e8243398ce54.target: 到目前为止，这些都是简单的内容，对吗？如果你忘记了，就想想从瀑布接水。什么因素重要？瀑布的强度、桶的大小和桶的方向。

ef75e979b2e4.source: One last detail – we need to decide on a positive and negative direction for flux. This decision is arbitrary, but by convention (aka your math teacher will penalize you if you don’t agree),
ef75e979b2e4.target: 最后一个细节——我们需要为通量确定正方向和负方向。这个决定是任意的，但按照惯例（即你的数学老师会因为你不同意而扣分），

0e28a280e627.source: Think of flux as a hose spraying water. Positive flux means flux is leaving the hose; the hose is a source of flux. Negative flux is like water entering a sink; it is a sink of flux. So positive flux = leaving, negative = entering. Got it? (By the way, the terms “source” and “sink” are sometimes used to describe fields).
0e28a280e627.target: 把通量想象成喷水的水管。正通量意味着通量离开水管；水管是通量的源。负通量就像水进入水槽；它是通量的汇。所以正通量 = 离开，负通量 = 进入。明白了吗？（顺便说一下，“源”和“汇”这两个术语有时用来描述场。）

2e429eb330cb.source: A fire hose shooting at a tiny bucket (small surface, large magnitude) could have the same flux as a garden hose aimed at a large bucket (large surface, small magnitude). And in case you forgot, flux reminds us to hold the bucket so it is facing the source. This should be obvious – but don’t you want ideas (especially in math!) to be obvious?
2e429eb330cb.target: 一个消防水带喷向小桶（小表面，大幅度）可能和一个花园水管对准大桶（大表面，小幅度）具有相同的通量。如果你忘了，通量提醒我们要让桶朝向源。这应该是显而易见的——但你不想让想法（尤其是在数学中！）变得显而易见吗？

4d1d27c58618.source: Now that we have a physical intuition, let’s try to derive the math. In most cases, the source of flux will be described as a vector field: Given a point (x,y,z), there's a formula giving the flux vector at that point.
4d1d27c58618.target: 现在有了物理直觉，让我们尝试推导数学公式。大多数情况下，通量的源被描述为向量场(Vector Field)：给定一个点 (x,y,z)，有一个公式给出该点的通量向量。

14841c14b675.source: We want to know how much of that vector field is acting/passing through our surface, taking the magnitude, orientation, and size into account. From our intuition, it should look something like this:
14841c14b675.target: 我们想知道那个向量场有多少作用/穿过我们的表面，考虑幅度、方向和大小。根据直觉，它应该看起来像这样：

d96ba1271386.source: Total flux = Field Strength * Surface Size * Surface Orientation
d96ba1271386.target: 总通量 = 场强 * 表面积 * 表面方向

f41d84c70b7e.source: However, this formula only works if the vector field is the same at every point. Usually, it’s not, so we’ll take the standard calculus approach to solving problems:
f41d84c70b7e.target: 然而，这个公式只有在向量场在每个点都相同时才有效。通常并非如此，所以我们将采用标准的微积分方法来解决问题：

7aab55e9c0ad.source: Let’s go out on a limb and call the tiny piece of the surface dS. Total flux is:
7aab55e9c0ad.target: 让我们假设表面的微小部分为 dS。总通量为：

cf7bfe67f1ab.source: Total flux = (Field Strength * dS * Orientation) for every dS.
cf7bfe67f1ab.target: 总通量 = 对于每个 dS（场强 * dS * 方向）

0ac4ee84a154.source: Total flux = Integral (Field Strength * Orientation * dS)
0ac4ee84a154.target: 总通量 = 积分（场强 * 方向 * dS）

cb9c169a3473.source: Make sense so far? Now, we need to figure out how much orientation actually matters. Like we said before, if the field and the surface are parallel, then there is zero flux. If they are perpendicular, there is full flux.
cb9c169a3473.target: 到目前为止还明白吗？现在，我们需要弄清楚方向到底有多重要。如前所述，如果场和表面平行，则通量为零；如果垂直，则通量最大。

d8a6b40862d1.source: (In this diagram, the flux is parallel with the top surface, and nothing enters from that direction. Mathematically, we represent surfaces by their
d8a6b40862d1.target: （在此图中，通量与顶面平行，因此没有从该方向进入。在数学上，我们用表面的

f078f81ea5a5.source: vector, which sticks out of the surface. Don’t let this bookkeeping detail disrupt your visualization.)
f078f81ea5a5.target: 向量表示表面，该向量垂直于表面伸出。不要让这个记账细节干扰你的可视化。）

99f978af17ea.source: If there is an angle, then it is some factor in-between:
99f978af17ea.target: 如果存在角度，则通量介于两者之间：

c2e05ab33bd9.source: How much, exactly? Well, this is a job for the

b45d7799d776.target: 的任务，即场在表面上的

4889c624931c.source: of the field onto the surface. The dot product gives us a number (from 0 to 1) that tells us what percent of the field is passing through the surface. So, the equation becomes:
4889c624931c.target: 。点积给出一个0到1之间的数，告诉我们场穿过表面的百分比。因此，方程变为：

3f25e0948d50.source: Total flux = Integral( Vector Field Strength dot dS )
3f25e0948d50.target: 总通量 = 积分( 向量场强度点乘 dS )

1bba346986b6.source: And finally, we convert to the stuffy equation you’ll see in your textbook, where
1bba346986b6.target: 最后，我们将其转换为你教科书中的沉闷方程，其中

8c93cb169fe5.source: is a unit of area and

648c6450639d.source: is the normal vector of the surface:

880c6d03bbdb.source: Time for one last detail — how do we find the normal vector for our surface?
880c6d03bbdb.target: 还有一个细节——我们如何找到表面的法向量？

f91c8a642e5b.source: Good question. For a surface like a plane, the normal vector is the same in every direction. For a sphere, the normal vector is in the same direction as $\vec{r}$, your position on the sphere: the top of a sphere has a normal vector that goes out the top; the bottom has one going out the bottom, etc.
f91c8a642e5b.target: 好问题。对于像平面这样的表面，法向量在各个方向都相同。对于球体，法向量与你的位置向量 $\vec{r}$ 方向相同：球体顶部有指向顶部的法向量；底部有指向底部的法向量，等等。

116a0febf5ab.source: More complicated shapes may have a normal vector that varies quite a bit. In this case, try to break the shape into smaller regions (like spheres, cylinders and planes) and find the flux in each part. Then, add up the flux in each region to get the total flux (keeping in mind positive and negative flux).
116a0febf5ab.target: 更复杂的形状可能有变化较大的法向量。在这种情况下，尝试将形状分解为更小的区域（如球体、圆柱体和平面），并找出每个部分的通量。然后，将每个区域的通量相加（考虑正负通量）得到总通量。

7ecf902e1339.source: If the shape is more complicated than that, you may need a computer model or more advanced theorems; but at least you know what is happening behind the scenes.
7ecf902e1339.target: 如果形状比这更复杂，你可能需要计算机模型或更高级的定理；但至少你知道了背后发生了什么。

970facc6e9e3.source: Let’s do a few thought experiments to understand flux. Imagine a tube, that lets water pass right through it. We hold the tube under a waterfall, wait a few seconds, then ask what the flux is. I want a numeric answer – what is the flux?
970facc6e9e3.target: 让我们做几个思想实验来理解通量。想象一根管子，可以让水直接通过。我们把管子放在瀑布下，等几秒钟，然后问通量是多少。我想要一个数值答案——通量是多少？

6ddfb5ce9317.source: You might think we need to know the speed of the waterfall, the size of the tube, the orientation, etc. But that isn’t the case.
6ddfb5ce9317.target: 你可能认为我们需要知道瀑布的速度、管子的大小、方向等。但事实并非如此。

c8899aa19d63.source: Remember our convention for flux orientation: positive means flux is leaving, negative means flux is entering. In this example, water is falling downward, or entering the tube. This means the top surface has negative flux (it appears to be siphoning up water).
c8899aa19d63.target: 记住我们关于通量方向的约定：正表示离开，负表示进入。在这个例子中，水向下落，即进入管子。这意味着顶部表面具有负通量（它看起来在吸取水）。

c964af16315d.source: However, what’s happening at the bottom of the box? The water passed through the top and is now leaving the bottom, which is positive flux:
c964af16315d.target: 然而，盒子底部发生了什么？水通过顶部，现在正从底部离开，即正通量：

1df3f780829f.source: Ah, this beautiful diagram shows what is going on. The top of the box / tube says that water is entering, and the bottom says water is leaving. Assuming the same amount of water is leaving and entering (the rate of water falling is a constant), the net flux would be zero. Think of it as  X + (-X) = 0.
1df3f780829f.target: 啊，这个漂亮的图表说明了情况。盒子/管道的顶部写着水正在进入，底部写着水正在离开。假设进出水量相同（水下落速率恒定），净通量(Net Flux)将为零。可以将其视为 X + (-X) = 0。

d197fe158e6c.source: What if we had increased the rate of water? Decreased? What would happen?
d197fe158e6c.target: 如果我们增加水的速率呢？减少呢？会发生什么？

cfc94c206974.source: My (possibly incorrect) answer: If we increased the rate, it means more water would enter than leaves, for a brief moment. We’d have a momentary spike in negative flux (the tube would look like a sink), until the rates equalized. Vice versa if we decreased the rate of water – we’d have a brief spike of positive flux (more water was leaving than entering), until the rate equalized.
cfc94c206974.target: 我的（可能不正确的）答案：如果我们增加速率，意味着瞬间进入的水比离开的多。我们会有一个短暂的负通量脉冲（管道看起来像一个水槽），直到速率相等。反之，如果我们减少水的速率——我们会有一个短暂的正通量脉冲（离开的水比进入的多），直到速率相等。

98de3423608b.source: Even though net flux is zero, this is different from having zero flux pass through each surface. If you are in an empty field, no shape will generate any flux. But if you are in a field where flux is canceling, changing your shape or orientation could create a non-zero flux. Recognize the difference between having zero flux because the field is zero, vs. having all the flux cancel.
98de3423608b.target: 即使净通量为零，这也不同于没有通量穿过每个表面。如果你在一个空场中，任何形状都不会产生通量。但如果你在一个通量相互抵消的场中，改变你的形状或方向可能会产生非零通量。要认识到场为零导致通量为零与所有通量相互抵消之间的区别。

5987c02138fb.source: One more point – the “tube” we are considering is a region we define, not a physical tube. Measuring flux is about drawing imaginary boundaries, not having a physical shape. So, when we define the region of a “bucket”, it would not “fill up” with flux. Flux is what is passing through the sides of a bucket at a moment in time. Clearly, if we put in a physical bucket it would fill up, but that’s not what we’re measuring. We’re seeing how much flux would be entering a region we define, from any and all sides (not just the opening). Got it?
5987c02138fb.target: 还有一点——我们考虑的“管道”是我们定义的一个区域，而不是物理管道。测量通量是关于划定想象的边界，而不是有一个物理形状。因此，当我们定义“桶”的区域时，它不会“充满”通量。通量是某一时刻穿过桶壁的东西。显然，如果我们放一个真实的桶，它会装满，但那不是我们在测量的。我们在看有多少通量会从任何和所有侧面（不仅仅是开口）进入我们定义的区域。明白了吗？

906e94fac69c.source: And one more point. We haven’t really talked about the units of flux. What is it measured in? As far as I understand, the units can be anything – it depends on the unit of your vector field. So, your vector field might represent bananas, in which case you get total bananas crossing a surface. Or, your field could represent bananas-per-second, in which case you’d get the bananas-per-second crossing your surface. The units of flux depend on the units of your vector field.
906e94fac69c.target: 还有一点。我们还没有真正讨论通量的单位。它用什么来度量？据我所知，单位可以是任何东西——取决于你的向量场(Vector Field)的单位。所以，你的向量场可能代表香蕉，那么你得到的是穿过表面的香蕉总数。或者，你的场可能代表每秒香蕉数，那么你得到的是穿过表面的每秒香蕉数。通量的单位取决于向量场的单位。

a5eaee5e3b5e.source: Flux is relatively simple to understand, and is really helpful in vector calculus and physics. Trying to understand flux by looking at a mess of integrals is not the way to go. First get an intuitive understanding, and the details will make more sense.
a5eaee5e3b5e.target: 通量相对容易理解，并且在向量微积分和物理学中非常有用。试图通过一堆积分来理解通量是不可取的。先获得直观理解，细节会更有意义。

09cd8dfc4248.source: Here’s a few insights that hit me after learning about flux:
09cd8dfc4248.target: 以下是我学习通量后得到的一些见解：

c0c94a07ae5a.source: You can take the time derivative of flux. If the vector field (F) changes with time (t), you can use dF/dt to see how the total flux changes over time. Even though flux is taken at a unit in time, you can measure flux at two consecutive moments to see how fast it is changing.
c0c94a07ae5a.target: 你可以对通量求时间导数。如果向量场 F 随时间 t 变化，你可以使用 dF/dt 来查看总通量如何随时间变化。尽管通量是在某个时间点测量的，但你可以测量两个连续时刻的通量，看看它变化得有多快。

987759308b34.source: You can integrate flux, which means finding how much flux has crossed over a certain time. If the field F is constant over time, you can multiply the flux at one instant by your duration. But if F changes with time, then you need to measure at each moment and integrate. Each flux calculation is done at an instant of time, then they are summed together. Again, this is the standard calculus technique.
987759308b34.target: 你可以对通量进行积分，这意味着找出在一段时间内穿过了多少通量。如果场 F 随时间恒定，你可以将某一瞬间的通量乘以持续时间。但如果 F 随时间变化，那么你需要在每个时刻测量并积分。每次通量计算都在一个时间瞬间完成，然后将它们加在一起。再次强调，这是标准的微积分技巧。

384a51c024e7.source: In our waterfall example, we looked at a single point in time where water had been flowing for a while. If we chose an early point in time, we would have negative flux: water had entered the top, but not yet left the bottom. If we turned off the water, there’d be an instant in time with positive flux: water had stopped entering, but was continuing to leave.
384a51c024e7.target: 在我们的瀑布例子中，我们考察了一个水流已经流动了一段时间的时间点。如果我们选择一个较早的时间点，我们会得到负的通量：水已经进入顶部，但尚未从底部离开。如果我们关掉水，会有一个瞬间的正通量：水已停止进入，但仍在继续离开。

680373383ba7.source: Flux is important for math, electricity and magnetism, and your science life will be better for knowing it. Your social life – not so much.
680373383ba7.target: 通量对于数学、电磁学很重要，了解它会让你的科学生活更美好。至于社交生活——那就不一定了。

dc07120480a8.target: 这是一篇长文章。休息一下。洗个澡。出去走走。看看你的家人。或者，继续阅读关于

608ce3504609.source: Have a timer running every minute (keep track of the minute as “n”)

1a68c574312e.source: 3x / hour means once every 60/3 = 20 minutes. So task A runs whenever “n % 20 == 0”

ee406e9aff4d.source: 3a + 5b = 8… let’s “mod 3 it”: 0 + 2b ≡ 2 mod 3, or b ≡ 1 mod 3

001324d0d088.source: The iTouch and iPad are identical, modulo size ;)

641adff604de.source: Range reducer: take an input, mod N, and you have a number from 0 to N-1.
641adff604de.target: 范围缩减器：输入一个数，mod N，得到一个0到N-1之间的数。

d87ed977fcbe.source: Group assigner: take an input, mod N, and you have it tagged as a group from 0 to N-1. This group can be agreed upon by any number of parties — for example, different servers that know N = 20 can agree what group ID=57 belongs to.
d87ed977fcbe.target: 分组分配器：输入一个数，mod N，将其标记为0到N-1的组。任意多方可以约定这个组——例如，知道N=20的不同服务器可以约定ID=57属于哪个组。

f1b6066d23e0.source: Property deducer: treat numbers according to properties (even, threeven, and so on) and work out principles derived at the property level
f1b6066d23e0.target: 属性推导器：根据属性（偶数、三的倍数等）处理数字，并推导出基于属性的原理

4ae2225cfb2c.source: A reader recently suggested I write about modular arithmetic (aka “taking the remainder”). I hadn’t given it much thought, but realized the modulo is extremely powerful: it should be in our mental toolbox next to addition and multiplication.
4ae2225cfb2c.target: 一位读者最近建议我写写模运算（也就是“取余数”）。我之前没怎么考虑过，但意识到模运算非常强大：它应该像加法和乘法一样，成为我们思维工具箱中的一员。

b24f34b9c49d.source: Instead of hitting you in the face with formulas, let’s explore an idea we’ve been subtly exposed to for years. There’s a
b24f34b9c49d.target: 不要直接用公式砸你脸，让我们先探索一个多年来我们已微妙接触过的想法。有一篇

fcd6eb1fcf1a.target: 激发了这篇文章的灵感。

87956b796e35.source: Shortly after discovering whole numbers (1, 2, 3, 4, 5…) we realized they fall into two groups:
87956b796e35.target: 在发现整数（1, 2, 3, 4, 5……）后不久，我们意识到它们分为两组：

a3d53aec9226.source: Why’s this distinction important? It’s the beginning of abstraction — we’re noticing the
a3d53aec9226.target: 为什么这个区别很重要？这是抽象的开始——我们注意到一个数字的

472e7fab443e.source: of a number (like being even or odd) and not just the number itself (“37”).
472e7fab443e.target: 特性（比如是偶数还是奇数），而不仅仅是数字本身（“37”）。

0284ca774fab.source: This is huge — it lets us explore math at a deeper level and find relationships between
0284ca774fab.target: 这非常重要——它让我们能在更深层次探索数学，并找到数字

afb9bd4ccfe9.source: of numbers, not specific ones. For example, we can make rules like this:
afb9bd4ccfe9.target: 之间的关系，而不仅仅是具体数字。例如，我们可以制定这样的规则：

6d72f9f562e5.source: These rules are general — they work at the property level. (Intuitively, I have a
6d72f9f562e5.target: 这些规则是通用的——它们在性质层面起作用。（直觉上，我有个

2ec377b8cb9c.source: that “evenness” is a molecule some numbers have, and cannot be removed by multiplication.)
2ec377b8cb9c.target: 观点：“偶数性”是一些数字拥有的分子，且不能被乘法移除。）

7806db585110.source: But even/odd is a very specific property: division by 2. What about the number 3? How about this:
7806db585110.target: 但是奇偶性是一个非常特定的性质：除以2。那么数字3呢？试试这个：

060cad642a75.source: Weird, but workable. You’ll notice a few things: there’s two types of throdd. A number like “4” is 1 away from being threeven (remainder 1), while the number 5 is two away (remainder 2).
060cad642a75.target: 奇怪但可行。你会注意到两点：有三余数（throdd）的两种类型。像“4”这样的数离三整（threeven）差1（余数为1），而数5差2（余数为2）。

60ebc43644c1.source: Being “threeven” is just another property of a number. Perhaps not as immediately useful as even/odd, but it’s there: we can make rules like “threeven x threeven = threeven” and so on.
60ebc43644c1.target: “三整”只是数字的另一个性质。也许不像奇偶性那样立即可用，但它确实存在：我们可以制定像“三整乘三整等于三整”这样的规则，等等。

669b72b31700.source: But it’s getting crazy. We can’t make new words all the time.
669b72b31700.target: 但这变得疯狂了。我们不能一直创造新词。

eb1c78bd6a54.source: The modulo operation (abbreviated “mod”, or “%” in many programming languages) is the remainder when dividing. For example, “5 mod 3 = 2” which means 2 is the remainder when you divide 5 by 3.
eb1c78bd6a54.target: 模运算（缩写为“mod”，在许多编程语言中为“%”）是除法后的余数。例如，“5 mod 3 = 2”意味着2是5除以3的余数。

752621673876.source: Converting everyday terms to math, an “even number” is one where it’s “0 mod 2” — that is, it has a remainder of 0 when divided by 2. An odd number is “1 mod 2” (has remainder 1).
752621673876.target: 将日常用语转换为数学，“偶数”就是“0 mod 2”——即除以2余数为0。奇数是“1 mod 2”（余数为1）。

cfce38f324d8.source: Why’s this cool? Well, our “odd/even” rules become this:
cfce38f324d8.target: 为什么这很酷？嗯，我们的“奇/偶”规则变成了这样：

9c254c3304a2.source: Cool, huh? Pretty easy to work out — we converted “properties” into actual equations and found some new facts.
9c254c3304a2.target: 很酷吧？很容易理解——我们把“性质”转换成了实际方程，并发现了一些新事实。

ec7a9c0c764c.source: What’s even x even x odd x odd? Well, it’s 0 x 0 x 1 x 1 = 0. In fact, you can see if there’s an even being multiplied
ec7a9c0c764c.target: 偶数乘偶数乘奇数乘奇数是多少？嗯，就是0 × 0 × 1 × 1 = 0。实际上，你可以看到只要有一个偶数相乘，

0c02b3a02e4a.source: the entire result is going to be zero… I mean even :).
0c02b3a02e4a.target: 整个结果就是零……我是说偶数:)。

f7b6c6f705dc.source: The sneaky thing about modular math is we’ve already been using it for keeping time — sometimes called “clock arithmetic”.
f7b6c6f705dc.target: 模运算的狡猾之处在于我们已经用它来计时——有时称为“时钟算术”。

6edb6237ea87.source: For example: it’s 7:00 (am/pm doesn’t matter). Where will the hour hand be in 7 hours?
6edb6237ea87.target: 例如：现在是7:00（上午/下午不重要）。7小时后时针会在哪里？

ec2889b2f108.source: Hrm. 7 + 7 = 14, but we can’t show “14:00” on a clock. So it must be 2. We do this reasoning intuitively, and in math terms:
ec2889b2f108.target: 嗯。7 + 7 = 14，但我们不能在时钟上显示“14:00”。所以一定是2。我们凭直觉推理，用数学术语来说：

beb25b5b2b95.source: The equation “14 mod 12 = 2 mod 12” means, “14 o’clock” and “2 o’clock” look the same on a 12-hour clock. They are
beb25b5b2b95.target: 方程“14 mod 12 = 2 mod 12”意味着，“14点”和“2点”在12小时制时钟上看起来一样。它们是等价的，

d81ace89fa41.source: , indicated by a triple-equals sign: 14 ≡ 2 mod 12.

b0e9eb213eeb.source: Another example: it’s 8:00. Where will the big hand be in 25 hours?
b0e9eb213eeb.target: 另一个例子：现在是8:00。25小时后时针会在哪里？

7d7d5fbf838f.source: Instead of adding 25 to 8, you might realize that 25 hours is just “1 day + 1 hour”. So, the clock will end up 1 hour ahead, at 9:00.
7d7d5fbf838f.target: 不用把25加到8上，你可能意识到25小时就是“1天+1小时”。所以，时钟会向前走1小时，到9:00。

505da1377dcb.source: You intuitively converted 25 to 1, and added that to 8.
505da1377dcb.target: 你凭直觉把25转换为1，然后加到8上。

b6a5eb256430.source: Using clocks as an analogy, we can figure out whether the rules of modular arithmetic “just work” (they do).
b6a5eb256430.target: 以时钟为类比，我们可以判断模运算的规则是否“成立”（它们确实成立）。

9b8c89d9988e.source: Let’s say two times look the same on our clock (“2:00” and “14:00”). If we add the same “x” hours to both, what happens?
9b8c89d9988e.target: 假设两个时间在时钟上看起来相同（“2:00”和“14:00”）。如果我们给两者都加上相同的“x”小时，会发生什么？

4d7219c125bc.source: Well, they change to the same amount on the clock! 2:00 + 5 hours ≡ 14:00 + 5 hours — both will show 7:00.
4d7219c125bc.target: 嗯，它们在时钟上变化相同！2:00 + 5小时 ≡ 14:00 + 5小时 — 两者都会显示7:00。

025938c0682e.source: Why? Well, we never cared about the excess “12:00” that the 14 was carrying around. We can just add 5 to the 2 remainder that both have, and they advance the same. For all congruent numbers (2 and 14), adding and subtracting has the same result.
025938c0682e.target: 为什么？嗯，我们从不关心14携带的多余的“12:00”。我们可以直接将5加到两者都有的余数2上，它们前进相同。对于所有同余数（2和14），加法和减法的结果相同。

a111a702c6d0.source: It’s harder to see whether multiplication stays the same. If 14 ≡ 2 (mod 12), can we multiply both sides and get the same result?
a111a702c6d0.target: 乘法是否保持不变更难判断。如果14 ≡ 2 (mod 12)，我们可以两边乘以同一个数得到相同的结果吗？

8956bd9f334c.source: Let’s see — what happens when we multiply by 3?
8956bd9f334c.target: 让我们看看——乘以3会发生什么？

f82dceaa7c62.source: The first part (12 * 3) can be ignored! The “12 hour overflow” that 14 is carrying around just gets repeated a few times. But who cares? We ignore the overflow anyway.
f82dceaa7c62.target: 第一部分（12 * 3）可以忽略！14携带的“12小时溢出”只是重复了几次。但谁在乎呢？我们反正忽略溢出。

d56e296af689.source: When multiplying, it’s only the remainder that matters, which is the same 2 hours for 14:00 and 2:00. Intuitively, this is how I see that multiplication doesn’t change relationships with modular math (you can multiply both sides of a modular relationship and get the same result). See the
d56e296af689.target: 相乘时，只有余数才重要，对于14:00和2:00，余数都是相同的2小时。直观上，这就是我认为乘法不会改变模运算关系的原因（你可以对模关系两边乘以同一个数，得到相同的结果）。参见

22a2b0756100.source: for more rigorous proofs — these are my
22a2b0756100.target: 更严谨的证明——这些是我

90adfde3b70c.source: Now the fun part — why is modular arithmetic useful?
90adfde3b70c.target: 现在是有趣的部分——为什么模算术有用？

74deba18b850.source: We do this intuitively, but it’s nice to give it a name. You have a flight arriving at 3pm. It’s getting delayed 14 hours. What time will it land?
74deba18b850.target: 我们直观地这样做，但给它起个名字很好。你有一架下午3点到达的航班，延误了14小时。它将在什么时间降落？

60b22ea8fece.source: Well, 14 ≡ 2 mod 12. So I think of it as “2 hours and an am/pm switch”, so I know it will be “3 + 2 = 5am”.
60b22ea8fece.target: 嗯，14 ≡ 2 mod 12。所以我把它看作“2小时加上上午/下午切换”，因此我知道它将是“3 + 2 = 凌晨5点”。

54108bc0dc24.source: This is a bit more involved than a plain modulo operator, but the principle is the same.
54108bc0dc24.target: 这比简单的模运算符更复杂一些，但原理相同。

ef9e426524f9.source: Suppose you have people who bought movie tickets, with a confirmation number. You want to divide them into 2 groups.
ef9e426524f9.target: 假设有一些买了电影票的人，带有确认号。你想把他们分成两组。

f123446206f8.source: What do you do? “Odds over here, evens over there”. You don’t need to know how many tickets were issued (first half, second half), everyone can figure out their group instantly (without contacting a central authority), and the scheme works as more people buy tickets.
f123446206f8.target: 你怎么做？“奇数这边，偶数那边”。你不需要知道发出了多少张票（前半部分，后半部分），每个人都能立即算出自己的组（无需联系中央权威），而且随着更多人购票，这个方案仍然有效。

e44d9019fd74.source: Need 3 groups? Divide by 3 and take the remainder (aka mod 3). You’ll have groups “0”, “1” and “2”.
e44d9019fd74.target: 需要3组？除以3取余数（即 mod 3）。你将得到组“0”、“1”和“2”。

3cfe7390fc6a.source: In programming, taking the modulo is how you can fit items into a hash table: if your table has N entries, convert the item key to a number, do mod N, and put the item in that bucket (perhaps keeping a linked list there). As your hash table grows in size, you can recompute the modulo for the keys.
3cfe7390fc6a.target: 在编程中，取模是将项目放入哈希表的方式：如果你的表有N个条目，将项目键转换为数字，进行 mod N，然后将项目放入那个桶（可能在那里维护一个链表）。随着哈希表大小增长，你可以重新计算键的模。

84c37db8c7bc.source: I use the modulo in real life. Really. We have 4 people playing a game and need to pick someone to go first.
84c37db8c7bc.target: 我在现实生活中使用模运算。真的。我们有4个人玩游戏，需要选一个人先开始。

7581082b2464.source: Now everyone goes “one, two, three, shoot!” and puts out a random number of fingers. Add them up and divide by 4 — whoever gets the remainder exactly goes first. (For example: if the sum of fingers is 11, whoever had “3” gets to go first, since 11 mod 4 = 3).
7581082b2464.target: 现在每个人都喊“一、二、三、出！”并伸出随机数量的手指。把它们加起来除以4——谁得到正好那个余数谁就先走。（例如：如果手指总和是11，那么编号为“3”的人先走，因为 11 mod 4 = 3）。

a54c93ee061b.source: Suppose tasks need to happen on a certain schedule:
a54c93ee061b.target: 假设任务需要按特定时间表进行：

f69358f32220.source: How do you store this information and make a schedule? One way:
f69358f32220.target: 如何存储这些信息并制定时间表？一种方法是：

fd1ea76291aa.source: Oh, you need task C1 which runs 1x per hour, but not the same time as task C? Sure, have it run when “n mod 60 == 1” (still once per hour, but not the same as C1).
fd1ea76291aa.target: 哦，你需要任务C1，它每小时运行1次，但不要与任务C同时运行？没问题，让它当“n mod 60 == 1”时运行（仍然是每小时一次，但和C1不同时）。

83d9a00eeebc.source: Mentally I see a cycle I want to “hit” at various intervals, so I insert a mod. The neat thing is that the hits can overlap independently. It’s a bit like XOR in that regard (each XOR can be layered — but that’s another article!).
83d9a00eeebc.target: 在脑海中，我看到一个想要以不同间隔“命中”的循环，于是我插入了一个模运算。巧妙之处在于，这些命中可以独立重叠。在这方面有点像XOR（每个XOR都可以分层——但那是另一篇文章了！）。

b33e411669b9.source: Similarly, when programming you can print every 100th log item by doing: if (n % 100 == 0){ print… }.
b33e411669b9.target: 类似地，编程时你可以通过 if (n % 100 == 0){ print… } 来每隔100条日志打印一次。

77cc163ffc54.source: It’s a very flexible, simple way to have items run on a schedule. In fact, it’s the way to answer the
77cc163ffc54.target: 这是一种非常灵活、简单的方式让任务按时间表运行。事实上，这就是回答

4df115b4fea3.source: . If you don’t have the modulo operation in your batbelt the question becomes much more tricky.
4df115b4fea3.target: 的方式。如果你的工具箱里没有模运算，这个问题就会棘手得多。

2ffa6f7c85e6.source: What can you deduce quickly? Well, “a” must be even, since it’s equal to something which involves multiplication by 2.
2ffa6f7c85e6.target: 你能快速推断出什么？嗯，“a”必须是偶数，因为它等于一个乘以2的式子。

8f793386f1ff.source: You’d balk. Not because you “know” the two products are different, but because one is clearly even, and the other is odd. There’s a problem: a can’t be the same number in both since the
8f793386f1ff.target: 你会犹豫。不是因为你“知道”这两个乘积不同，而是因为一个明显是偶数，另一个是奇数。有问题：a不能同时出现在两个方程中，因为

c3d566e6d86d.source: Things like “even”, “threeven” and “mod n” are properties that are more general than individual numbers, and which we can check for consistency. So we can use modulo to figure out whether numbers are consistent, without knowing what they are!
c3d566e6d86d.target: 像“偶数”、“三倍数”和“模n”这样的属性，比单个数字更通用，我们可以用来检查一致性。所以我们可以在不知道数字具体是什么的情况下，利用模运算来判断它们是否一致！

08e48a7e07e1.source: Can these equations be solved with the integers? Let’s see:
08e48a7e07e1.target: 这些方程能否用整数求解？让我们看看：

f47ab32fbb43.source: A contradication, good fellows! B can’t be both “1 mod 3” and “2 mod 3” — it’s as absurd as being even and odd at the same time!
f47ab32fbb43.target: 矛盾了，好朋友们！B不能同时是“1 mod 3”和“2 mod 3”——这就像同时是偶数和奇数一样荒谬！

4083b70ec2ce.source: But there’s one gotcha: numbers like “1.5” are neither even nor odd — they aren’t integers! The modular properties apply to integers, so what we can say is that b cannot be an
4083b70ec2ce.target: 但有一个陷阱：像“1.5”这样的数既不是偶数也不是奇数——它们不是整数！模运算性质适用于整数，所以我们能说的是b不可能是一个

a9c6f89aa314.source: Because, in fact, we can solve that equation:
a9c6f89aa314.target: 因为，实际上，我们可以解这个方程：

bcee3ed39cbd.source: Don’t get seduced by the power of modulo! Know its limits: it applies to integers.
bcee3ed39cbd.target: 不要被模运算的力量迷惑！要知道它的局限性：它只适用于整数。

d85206221d3f.source: Playing with numbers has very important uses in cryptography. It’s too much to cover here, but modulo is used in
d85206221d3f.target: 玩转数字在密码学中有非常重要的应用。这里无法详述，但模运算被用于

de485273535f.source: — used in setting up SSL connections to encrypt web traffic.
de485273535f.target: — 用于建立SSL连接以加密网络流量。

4fa5979429b8.source: Geeks love to use technical words in regular contexts. You might hear “X is the same as Y modulo Z” which means roughly “Ignoring Z, X and Y are the same.”
4fa5979429b8.target: 极客们喜欢在日常语境中使用技术词汇。你可能会听到“X is the same as Y modulo Z”，大致意思是“忽略Z，X和Y是相同的。”

a1274c3d82a4.source: It’s strange thinking about the “utility” of the modulo operator — it’s like someone asking why exponents are useful. In everyday life, not very, but it’s a tool to understand patterns in the world, and create your own.
a1274c3d82a4.target: 思考取模运算符的“实用性”很奇怪——就像有人问指数为什么有用。在日常生活中，它们不太常用，但它是理解世界模式并创造自己模式的工具。
