e4afd597c96f.source: Rote details are arrows, intuition is the bow.
e4afd597c96f.target: 死记硬背的细节是箭，直觉是弓。

9a8f1b52ad24.source: Details (arrows) do the actual work, but are cheap & plentiful
9a8f1b52ad24.target: 细节（箭）完成实际工作，但廉价且丰富

630be92b7dad.source: Intuition (the bow) is the framework that makes the details effective (in theory, it’s optional; in practice, it’s not)
630be92b7dad.target: 直觉（弓）是使细节有效的框架（理论上它是可选的；实际上并非如此）

17dea9ec0d1e.source: Hunting (effective problem solving) is the ultimate goal: how can the entire system help us?
17dea9ec0d1e.target: 狩猎（有效的问题解决）是最终目标：整个系统如何帮助我们？

37ff0b829e97.source: Psst… if students truly enjoy math in middle school, they’ll probably enjoy it in high-school, and end up graduating with calculus anyway. But if you start to expect that, you’re back to square one!
37ff0b829e97.target: 喂……如果学生在中学时真正喜欢数学，他们很可能在高中也会喜欢，并最终以微积分毕业。但如果你一开始就期望这样，你又回到了原点！

e929d79d1f6f.source: Since you’re curious: 4d numbers are called “quaternions” and used to model spinny things in video games. But beyond that, shouldn’t we be using a list or something? What’s that? You want to learn about
e929d79d1f6f.target: 既然你好奇：四维数被称为“四元数”，用于在视频游戏中模拟旋转物体。但除此之外，我们难道不应该使用列表之类的吗？那是什么？你想了解

b7a2d93c9d91.source: My favorite analogies explain a thought and help you explore deeper truths. Here’s a metaphor that captures my stance on learning:
b7a2d93c9d91.target: 我最喜欢的类比能解释一个想法并帮助你探索更深层的真理。这里有一个比喻抓住了我对学习的立场：

5924e2c078b7.source: Our goal is to hunt down problems. You can use arrows alone, sure, but intuition is the framework that makes details astoundingly useful. Here’s a few insights I explored over a
5924e2c078b7.target: 我们的目标是追捕问题。当然，你可以单独使用箭，但直觉是让细节变得极其有用的框架。以下是我在

b8088dfa8860.source: and in email (thanks Jay, Stan, Luke and David!).

558139f2df39.source: I hate the false choice between rigor and intuition. We can have both! Each has a role to play:
558139f2df39.target: 我讨厌在严谨与直觉之间做出虚假选择。我们可以两者兼得！各有各的作用：

7b27df4ec2d0.source: Yes, you can have details without intuition: it’s chasing a buffalo, waving an arrow overhead. Victories are possible, though exhausting, and the process hardly encourages you to learn more. It’s like memorizing a math proof you don’t understand (honestly, I’d prefer the buffalo).
7b27df4ec2d0.target: 是的，你可以有细节而没有直觉：就像追一头野牛，在头顶挥舞着箭。胜利是可能的，尽管累人，而且这个过程几乎不会鼓励你学更多。这就像背诵你不理解的数学证明（老实说，我宁愿要那头野牛）。

b8187284476a.source: Intuition without rigor is bad, too: having a tool but never firing it in the real world. We need both: bows AND arrows, not bows OR arrows. (But between the two, give me a single arrow and let me practice my bowmanship).
b8187284476a.target: 没有严谨的直觉也不好：拥有工具却从不在现实中发射。我们需要两者：弓和箭，而不是弓或箭。（但在这两者之间，给我一支箭，让我练习我的箭术。）

58d5ef316acc.source: Imagine a cavemen who sees me launch an arrow from a cave. The bow is hidden: because he’s only seen spears, he thinks I threw the arrow by hand. Probably with the help of shaman magic.
58d5ef316acc.target: 想象一个穴居人看到我从洞穴中射出一支箭。弓是隐藏的：因为他只见过长矛，他以为我是用手扔出箭的。可能借助了萨满魔法。

bac341516624.source: Our mental bows are similarly hidden, and the way some people use math seems like dark magic. We don’t see their intuition, just the problem being obliterated.
bac341516624.target: 我们脑海中的弓同样隐藏着，有些人使用数学的方式就像黑魔法。我们看不到他们的直觉，只看到问题被消灭。

b505044f636e.source: Let’s unlock those insights. My goal is to share the “bows in our heads”, not to show a flying arrow and having you invent the bow that launched it.
b505044f636e.target: 让我们解锁那些见解。我的目标是分享“我们脑海中的弓”，而不是展示一支飞行的箭，然后让你发明射出它的弓。

b0643e683ad3.source: When teaching, it’s easy to focus on how many arrows you can name, classify, and stick into motionless targets. We pretend it’s preparation for the real world and “learning how to learn”.
b0643e683ad3.target: 教学时，很容易聚焦于你能命名、分类并射入静止目标的箭的数量。我们假装这是为现实世界做准备，是“学会如何学习”。

189ba8dfac3d.source: I disagree. “Learning how to learn” means mastering a single arrow in a single bow. Truly mastering it. New techniques follow.
189ba8dfac3d.target: 我不同意。“学会如何学习”意味着掌握一把弓中的一支箭。真正掌握它。新技巧随之而来。

bcf2955f5eb1.source: I’d rather train an Aboriginal hunter to use a modern rifle than a random person on the street. We laymen (myself included!) have no idea about aiming, tracking, breathing, timing, precision and the intangibles you discover when mastering a tool. I’ve heard about guns my entire life and played dozens of video games: that hunter could still outshoot me within 15 minutes of seeing his first rifle. It’s not the arrows, it’s the bow. That is “learning how to learn”.
bcf2955f5eb1.target: 我宁愿训练一个原住民猎人使用现代步枪，也不愿训练街上的普通人。我们外行（包括我自己！）对瞄准、跟踪、呼吸、时机、精度以及掌握工具时发现的无形之物一无所知。我一生都听说过枪支，玩过几十个电子游戏：那个猎人仍然可以在看到第一支步枪的15分钟内射得比我准。不是箭的问题，是弓的问题。这就是“学会如何学习”。

907f32b689c3.source: In modern times: what’s the point of force-feeding students until they hate learning? Until they never read another classic book? Until they have a life-long aversion to math?
907f32b689c3.target: 在现代：强迫学生直到他们讨厌学习有什么意义？直到他们再也不读经典书籍？直到他们对数学产生终身厌恶？

acce5d9a757f.source: I’d prefer to have “basic” high schoolers that love middle-school algebra vs. “advanced” ones that hate calculus. Because in 5 years the ones who loved algebra will love calculus too.
acce5d9a757f.target: 我宁愿要喜欢中学代数的“基础”高中生，也不要讨厌微积分的“高级”高中生。因为五年后，那些喜欢代数的也会喜欢微积分。

275cd8b500df.source: A thought: what about a dynamite arrow, the super-useful detail that levels the playing field? Couldn’t that equate the amateur and expert?
275cd8b500df.target: 一个想法：如果有一种“炸药箭”，一个能拉平比赛场地的超级实用细节呢？难道不能因此让业余者和专家平起平坐吗？

54853f154419.source: Perhaps. But even so, you need a decent shot to make the system effective. It’s no good shooting the dynamite arrow 10 feet, or in a backwards direction. And again: if an amateur was able to get that dynamite arrow, how quickly can the expert get it?
54853f154419.target: 也许吧。但即便如此，你需要一个像样的射手才能让系统有效。把炸药箭射10英尺远，或者射向反方向，是没用的。再说一次：如果一个业余者能拿到炸药箭，专家又能多快拿到呢？

8f681f146794.source: I’m not saying “never get more arrows”. I’m saying it’s pointless to collect more arrows until you can shoot the ones you have.
8f681f146794.target: 我不是说“永远不要获取更多的箭”。我是说，在你能够射好现有的箭之前，收集更多箭是没有意义的。

8f1d60cc1209.source: Quick: You have 2 minutes to explain archery to caveman. Go!
8f1d60cc1209.target: 快：你有2分钟时间向穴居人解释射箭。开始！

6983fecd0de7.source: Do you focus on arrow-building technique, chopping tress, finding rocks, etc.? Or do you say “Arrows are made of wood and have feathers to stabilize their flight. That’s enough there. Here’s how to make an awesome bow (longbow, crossbow, compound bow…)”.
6983fecd0de7.target: 你会专注于制箭技术，比如砍树、找石头等吗？还是会说“箭是木头做的，有羽毛来稳定飞行。这就够了。下面是做一个超棒的弓的方法（长弓、十字弓、复合弓……）”。

f4163c68d59e.source: Most systems have a “core operating principle”. The rest are details about arrows. For example: Cellular phones talk to a collection of “cells” which hand off the signal between towers as you move. Done. Cells = cellular telephone. GSM, CDMA, & friends are the language each tower understands, and not important to core understanding.
f4163c68d59e.target: 大多数系统都有一个“核心操作原理”。其余的都是关于箭的细节。例如：手机与一组“蜂窝”通信，当你移动时，这些蜂窝会在塔之间切换信号。完事。蜂窝 = 蜂窝电话。GSM、CDMA 等是每个塔理解的语言，对核心理解不重要。

9275ac836938.source: When explaining, look for the bows.

5aaa2d162e7e.source: How do you know someone truly enjoyed learning? They start asking archery questions, not arrow questions.
5aaa2d162e7e.target: 你怎么知道某人真的享受学习？他们开始问关于射箭的问题，而不是关于箭的问题。

ed22966a6314.source: are numbers in another dimension, it’s about 10 minutes until you have genuine interest in “Hey… could there be 3d or 4d numbers too?”.
ed22966a6314.target: 是另一个维度的数字，大约10分钟内你就会真正感兴趣“嘿……还能有3维或4维的数字吗？”。

e8b8edb0699f.source: Good luck getting that after teaching “The square root of -1 is i. No, I won’t tell you why, nobody told me why — just memorize it for the test!”.
e8b8edb0699f.target: 祝你在教完“-1的平方根是i。不，我不会告诉你为什么，也没人告诉过我为什么——只是为了考试记住它！”之后能获得这种兴趣。

d34121c6847f.source: Understanding is measured by the questions we ask, not the tests we answer.
d34121c6847f.target: 理解是通过我们提出的问题来衡量的，而不是通过我们回答的考试。

2ecb38571ce2.source: I’ve always been bothered by “educational games” which are thin veneers on rote memorization. Memorizing the name of every US President doesn’t tell you much about history. Being a spelling bee champion doesn’t make you a good writer. Knowing pi to 1000 places doesn’t mean you understand the concept.
2ecb38571ce2.target: 我一直对“教育游戏”感到困扰，它们只是死记硬背的薄薄一层外衣。记住每一位美国总统的名字并不能让你了解多少历史。成为拼写冠军并不能让你成为好作家。记住圆周率的前1000位并不意味着你理解这个概念。

fd092bcaa161.source: The cheap way to make math “fun” is to make a game of picking up arrows. The real way to make math fun is experience the joy of shooting an arrow on your own.
fd092bcaa161.target: 让数学变得“有趣”的廉价方法是做一个捡箭的游戏。让数学变得有趣的真正方法是体验自己射出一支箭的乐趣。

90c0688f4553.source: That said, skill-building games like Math Blaster can be awesome. It’s a matter of picking up the right arrows and not stopping there. A mathematician doesn’t enjoy crunching numbers any more than a writer enjoys conjugating verbs –it’s a necessary step to enjoy the art.
90c0688f4553.target: 话虽如此，像《数学爆破手》这样的技能培养游戏可以很棒。关键在于捡起正确的箭，并且不止于此。数学家并不比作家更喜欢动词变位——这是一个享受艺术的必要步骤。

ac29d8375125.source: We start with the same details, but have different ways of using them. Similar to how rifling (grooves in a barrel to spin a bullet) increases range, what tricks do we have?
ac29d8375125.target: 我们从相同的细节开始，但有不同的使用方法。类似于膛线（枪管内的沟槽，使子弹旋转）可以增加射程，我们有哪些技巧？

65dced9b69d3.source: I’ve gained immense value from upgrading the bow that holds the Pythagorean Theorem. That “arrow” ($a^2 + b^2 = c^2$) can be launched in so many ways — each year I find a new personal discovery (it’s
65dced9b69d3.target: 我从升级支撑勾股定理的弓中获得了巨大的价值。那个“箭”（$a^2 + b^2 = c^2$）可以被发射出很多种方式——每年我都有新的个人发现（它是

9105ddb03531.source: How can you upgrade your understanding to a longbow, a crossbow, a compound bow, an automatic machine-gun bow?
9105ddb03531.target: 你如何将自己的理解升级为长弓、十字弓、复合弓、自动机关枪弓？

4619bbecef25.source: The “bow and arrow” metaphor keeps giving: I’ve found over a half-dozen interpretations I love, and I’m sure there’s more (share ’em if you’ve got ’em).
4619bbecef25.target: “弓与箭”这个比喻不断带来启发：我已经找到了超过半打我喜欢的解读，而且我相信还有更多（如果你有，请分享出来）。

af189998f3ac.source: In a sentence: The joy and value of learning is in archery, not arrow-finding.
af189998f3ac.target: 一句话总结：学习的乐趣和价值在于射箭本身，而不是寻找箭矢。

00c30c2990a7.target: 首先：当前微积分的教学方式有什么问题？（哈！）

9090d355988a.source: Just look at the results. The vast majority of survivors, the STEM folks who used calculus in several classes, have
9090d355988a.target: 看看结果就知道了。绝大多数幸存者，即那些在几门课程中使用过微积分的STEM人士，

2036c62f0fd2.source: . We memorized procedures, applied them to pre-packaged problems (“Say, fellow, what is the derivative of $x^2$?”), and internalized nothing.
2036c62f0fd2.target: 。我们记住了步骤，将它们应用于预先打包好的问题（“喂，老兄，$x^2$的导数是多少？”），而没有内化任何东西。

ba7c32e04076.source: Want proof? No problem. Take a string and wrap it tight around a quarter. Take another string and wrap it tight around the Earth.
ba7c32e04076.target: 想要证据？没问题。拿一根绳子，紧紧绕在一枚25美分硬币上。再拿另一根绳子，紧紧绕在地球上。

3529cabce0ce.source: Ok. Now, lengthen both strings, adding more to the ends, so there’s a 1-inch gap all the way around around the quarter, and a 1-inch gap all the way around the Earth (sort of like having a ring floating around Saturn). Got it?
3529cabce0ce.target: 好了。现在，将两根绳子都加长，在末端增加更多长度，使得硬币周围有1英寸的间隙，地球周围也有1英寸的间隙（有点像土星周围的环）。明白了吗？

8b7f1f21bfe5.source: Quiz time: Which scenario uses more extra string? Does it take more additional string to put a 1-inch gap around the quarter, or to put a 1-inch gap around the Earth?
8b7f1f21bfe5.target: 小测验：哪种情况需要更多的额外绳子？是在硬币周围增加1英寸间隙需要更多绳子，还是在地球周围增加1英寸间隙需要更多绳子？

73e021a519f9.source: Think about it. Ponder it over. Ready? It’s the… same.
73e021a519f9.target: 想一想。仔细琢磨。准备好了吗？结果……一样。

bfbdf2617972.source: Adding a 1-inch gap around the Earth, and a quarter, uses the same
bfbdf2617972.target: 在地球和硬币周围增加1英寸间隙所需的额外绳子是相同的

c148aa407b54.source: And to be blunt: if you “learned” calculus but didn’t have the answer within 3 seconds, you don’t truly know it. At least not deep down.
c148aa407b54.target: 坦率地说：如果你“学过”微积分，但在3秒内没有得出答案，你就没有真正理解它。至少没有深刻理解。

0488f141ede7.source: Now don’t feel bad, I didn’t know it either. Only one engineer in the dozens I’ve asked came up with the answer instantly, without second-guesses (my karate teacher, Mr. Rose).
0488f141ede7.target: 现在，别难过，我以前也不知道。在我问过的几十个工程师中，只有一位工程师毫不犹豫地给出了答案（我的空手道老师，罗斯先生）。

c3a0d733af20.source: This question has a few levels of understanding:
c3a0d733af20.target: 这个问题有几个理解层次：

b5028293f5bc.source: . They are the same. Calculation complete.
b5028293f5bc.target: 它们是一样的。计算完成。

2c3f6fdde08e.source: , a constant, which means the current radius has no impact on a changing circumference.
2c3f6fdde08e.target: ，一个常数，这意味着当前半径对周长变化没有影响。

0c916bb525eb.source: : I see the true nature of things. We’re changing a 1-dimensional radius and watching a 1-dimensional perimeter. A dimension in, a dimension out, it’s like making a fence 1-foot longer: the initial size doesn’t change the work needed. The gap could be made around a circle, square, rectangle, or Richard Nixon mask, and it’s the same effort for similar shapes. (
0c916bb525eb.target: ：我看到了事物的本质。我们正在改变一维半径，并观察一维周长。一维输入，一维输出，就像把篱笆加长1英尺：初始尺寸不会改变所需的工作量。这个间隙可以绕圆、正方形、矩形或理查德·尼克松面具的边缘增加，对于相似形状，所需的工作量相同。（

639eebd2277c.source: We can be calculus warrior-monks, cutting through problems with our intuition. Notice how the most advanced approach didn’t need specific equations — it was just thinking about the problem! Equations are nice tools, but are they your only source of understanding?
639eebd2277c.target: 我们可以成为微积分战士僧侣，凭借直觉攻克问题。注意，最先进的方法并不需要特定方程——它只是思考问题本身！方程是很好的工具，但它们是唯一理解的来源吗？

691bebb94c50.source: See, according to standardized tests and final exams, I “knew” calculus — but clearly only to the beginning level. I didn’t immediately recognize how calculus could help with a question about making a string longer. If you asked someone for the amount of cash in a wallet with six \$20 bills, and they didn’t think to use multiplication, would you say they’ve internalized arithmetic? (“Oh geez, you didn’t tell me this would be a
691bebb94c50.target: 看，根据标准化考试和期末考试，我“知道”微积分——但显然只是初级的。我并没有立即认识到微积分如何帮助解决关于让线变长的问题。如果你问某人一个钱包里有六张20美元钞票的现金总额，而他们没想到用乘法，你会认为他们已经内化了算术吗？（“哦天啊，你没告诉我这是个

408667ee0806.source: question! Could you set up the problem for me?”)
408667ee0806.target: 问题！你能帮我设置这个问题吗？”）

276c506e6962.source: intuition, shared by an excited friend, and built with the test of “If you haven’t internalized the idea, the material must change.”
276c506e6962.target: 直觉，由一个兴奋的朋友分享，并建立在这样的测试之上：“如果你没有内化这个想法，那么材料必须改变。”

716a8ca245de.source: With Progressive Refinement. You may have seen these two methods to download and display an image:
716a8ca245de.target: 通过渐进式细化。你可能见过这两种下载和显示图像的方法：

31c1dd10e75d.target: 教授一门学科是类似的：

965451474747.source: The “start-to-finish” approach seems official. Orderly. Rigorous. And it doesn’t work.
965451474747.target: “从头到尾”的方法看起来很正式。有条理。严谨。但它不起作用。

71cd367858f3.source: What, exactly, do you know when you’ve seen the first 20% of a portrait in full resolution? A forehead? Do you even know the gender? The age? The teacher has forgotten that
71cd367858f3.target: 当你以全分辨率看到肖像的前20%时，你确切知道什么？一个额头？你甚至知道性别吗？年龄？老师已经忘记

c2e6391f2951.source: and likely can’t appreciate that you’re even seeing a forehead!
c2e6391f2951.target: 并且可能无法理解你甚至看到了一个额头！

c1025981c4bf.source: Progressive rendering (blurry-to-sharp) gives a full overview, a
c1025981c4bf.target: 渐进式渲染（从模糊到清晰）提供了完整的概览，一个

8aefbbcec58a.source: of what the expert sees, and gets you curious about more. After the overview, we start filling in the details. And because you have an idea of where you’re going, you’re excited to learn. What’s better: “Let’s download the next 10% of the forehead”, or “Let’s sharpen the picture”?
8aefbbcec58a.target: 专家所见的部分，并让你对更多内容感到好奇。在概览之后，我们开始填充细节。而且因为你知道方向，你学习起来很兴奋。哪个更好：“让我们下载额头的下10%”，还是“让我们锐化图像”？

4366255e2fe1.source: Let’s admit it: we forget the details of most classes. If we’ll have a hazy memory anyway, shouldn’t it be of the entire picture? That has the best shot of enticing us to sharpen the details later on.
4366255e2fe1.target: 让我们承认：我们会忘记大部分课程的细节。如果我们无论如何都会有一个模糊的记忆，难道不应该是整个画面的记忆吗？这样最有希望吸引我们日后去锐化细节。

fa1897df6841.source: With the Pizza Box Test. Imagine you pass a dumpster while walking home. You see a message scrawled on a discarded pizza box. Is the note so insightful and compelling that you’d take the pizza box home to finish reading it?
fa1897df6841.target: 通过披萨盒测试。想象你步行回家时经过一个垃圾箱。你看到废弃披萨盒上潦草的信息。这个便条是否如此有见地和令人信服，以至于你会把披萨盒带回家读完？

7728fa6b88f7.source: Ignore the sparkle of a lesson being digital, mobile-friendly, gamified, interactive, or a gesture-based hologram.
7728fa6b88f7.target: 忽略一堂课是数字化的、移动友好的、游戏化的、交互式的或基于手势的全息影像的闪光点。

e11166b55296.source: If yes, great! Clean it up and add in the glitz. But if the
e11166b55296.target: 如果是，太好了！把它清理干净并添加华丽装饰。但如果

8bcf867a5120.source: is not compelling without the trimmings, it must be redone.
8bcf867a5120.target: 没有点缀就不吸引人，那它必须重做。

de440d1969a8.source: Everyone’s “pizza box” standard varies; just have one. Here’s a few things I wish were written on the boxes outside my high school:
de440d1969a8.target: 每个人的“披萨盒”标准各不相同；只要有一个就行。以下是一些我希望高中教室外盒子上写着的内容：

42d6f3ed36ee.source: as a universal component in all growth rates, just like pi is a universal factor in all circles…
42d6f3ed36ee.target: 视为所有增长率中的通用组件，就像π是所有圆中的通用因子一样……

e01f94209456.source: Hey buddy! Degrees are from the observer’s perspective.
e01f94209456.target: 嘿，伙计！角度是从观察者的视角来看的，

da82383ccff9.source: are from the mover’s. That’s why radians are more natural. Let me show you…
da82383ccff9.target: 是从运动者的视角来看的。这就是为什么弧度更自然。让我告诉你……

9dc19ea87aec.source: are another dimension, and multiplication by i is a 90-degree rotation into that dimension! Two rotations and you’re facing backwards, aka -1.
9dc19ea87aec.target: 是另一个维度，乘以i就是向那个维度旋转90度！旋转两次你就面朝后方，即-1。

bd28bbaff504.source: By focusing on what future-you would teach current-you.
bd28bbaff504.target: 通过专注于未来的你会教给现在的你什么。

875b0ee7d7b2.source: Teachers, like all of us, face external incentives which may interfere with their goals (publish or perish, mandated curriculum, need to impress others with jargon, etc.). The test of “What would future-me teach present-me?” helps me focus on the essentials:
875b0ee7d7b2.target: 老师们，和我们所有人一样，面临外部激励，这些激励可能干扰他们的目标（发表或灭亡、规定课程、需要用术语给他人留下深刻印象等）。“未来的我会教现在的我什么？”这个测试帮助我专注于要点：

105104e6690c.source: . There’s no word count to meet. The same insight in fewer words is preferred.
105104e6690c.target: 。没有字数限制。用更少的词语表达相同的见解更受欢迎。

e24a617acf3f.source: . It’s future-me talking to current-me. There’s nobody to impress here.
e24a617acf3f.target: 。这是未来的我和现在的我对话。这里没有需要给谁留下深刻印象。

67f9adea0f41.source: . I’m not embarrassed by “childish” analogies. If a metaphor excites me, and helps, I’m going to use it. Nyah.
67f9adea0f41.target: 。我不会因为“幼稚”的类比而尴尬。如果一个比喻让我兴奋并且有帮助，我就会用它。哼。

28378628404e.source: . I want a buddy, a guide who happened to experience the material before I did, not a pompous schoolmarm I can’t question.
28378628404e.target: 。我想要一个伙伴，一个碰巧比我更早经历过这些材料的向导，而不是一个我不敢质疑的自命不凡的女教师。

dac3a604f457.source: . Most calculus classes cover “limits, derivatives, integrals” in that order because… why? Limits are the most nuanced concept, invented in the mid-1800s. Were mathematicians like Newton, Leibniz, Euler, Gauss, Taylor, Fourier and Bernoulli inadequate because they didn’t use them? (Conversely: are you better than them because you do?). Most courses are too timid (or oblivious) to question the strategy of covering the most elusive, low-level topic first.
dac3a604f457.target: 。大多数微积分课程按照“极限、导数、积分”的顺序讲授……为什么？极限是最微妙的概念，发明于19世纪中期。牛顿、莱布尼茨、欧拉、高斯、泰勒、傅里叶和伯努利等数学家是否因为没有使用极限而有所不足？（反过来：你因为使用了极限就比他们更好吗？）大多数课程太胆怯（或无知）而不去质疑先介绍最难以捉摸、最低级主题的策略。

effbe5fd9726.source: . The elephant in the room is that most math courses are a stepping-stone to some credential. Future-me doesn’t play that game: he only benefits when current-me
effbe5fd9726.target: 。房间里的大象是，大多数数学课程是获得某种证书的垫脚石。未来的我不玩那个游戏：他只会在现在的我

ed4804fe0dc5.target: 真正理解某件事情时受益。

97e7b46d39f8.source: Let’s learn calculus intuition-first. The goal is a lasting upgrade to your intuition and storehouse of analogies. If that doesn’t happen, the course isn’t working, and it will be enhanced until it does.
97e7b46d39f8.target: 让我们先凭直觉学习微积分。目标是让你的直觉和类比库得到持久升级。如果没有实现，课程就不奏效，并且会不断改进直到达成目标。

d48a5383b812.source: and I’ll let you know when the course preview is ready, in November.
d48a5383b812.target: 我会在课程预览准备好时通知您，时间是在十一月。

e723aa7c91b7.source: When we graph a function $f(x)$, there's a few ways we can modify it:
e723aa7c91b7.target: 当我们绘制函数 $f(x)$ 的图像时，有几种方法可以修改它：

e078f01b0a39.source: Using $f(ax + b) + c$ instead of $f(x)$ has a few effects:
e078f01b0a39.target: 使用 $f(ax + b) + c$ 代替 $f(x)$ 有几个效果：

9e18b73dfbe8.source: What's going on? Well, we're describing the visual result on the graph, but aren't describing that underlying process that made the change. Let's take a look at the root cause.
9e18b73dfbe8.target: 这是怎么回事？嗯，我们是在描述图像上的视觉结果，但并没有描述引起变化的基础过程。让我们来看看根本原因。

7c1d71dc2aaf.source: The "a" in f(ax) is a fast-forward factor. Normally, we experience time as "1 minute per minute" -- for every minute we wait, the world advances one minute into the future.
7c1d71dc2aaf.target: f(ax) 中的 "a" 是一个快进因子。通常，我们体验时间的方式是“每分钟一分钟”——每等一分钟，世界就向前推进一分钟。

d4e71b28c612.source: What if we saw life on fast forward?
d4e71b28c612.target: 如果我们看到快进的生活呢？

00802ba9be92.source: Here, 1 minute passing to us (in our "x" timeline) means 2 minutes passed in the real world. Or 10 minutes, an hour, or a year.
00802ba9be92.target: 在这里，我们（在我们的“x”时间线上）经过1分钟意味着现实世界中过去了2分钟。或者是10分钟、一小时或一年。

8141e39c6c63.source: On our timeline (x), time passes as normal. But our function, which determines the results we see, is being fed a modified timeline. While we leisurely stroll from x=1 to x=2, f has to jump from  f(10) to f(11) to f(12) ... up to f(20). Here, f needs to graph 10 minutes of events while we casually waited one minute. Cramming more data points into the same time period is a squashed, sped-up graph.
8141e39c6c63.target: 在我们的时间线（x）上，时间正常流逝。但决定我们所见结果的函数被输入了一个修改过的时间线。当我们悠闲地从 x=1 漫步到 x=2 时，f 必须从 f(10) 跳到 f(11)、f(12)……一直到 f(20)。在这里，f 需要在我们随意等待一分钟的时间里绘制 10 分钟的事件。将更多数据点塞进相同的时间段会导致一个被压缩、加速的图像。

fc80eececab5.source: Intuitively, "Squashing the graph" really means "running time faster".
fc80eececab5.target: 直观地说，“压缩图像”实际上意味着“让时间运行得更快”。

906e451d0a14.source: A simpler version of time travel is a basic shift. If things happen
906e451d0a14.target: 一种更简单的时间旅行是基本的平移。如果事情发生

352fd0434600.source: of schedule, what does that mean?
352fd0434600.target: 偏离计划，那是什么意思？

0589ffbb11c5.source: Imagine a German/Japanese city where the trains run an hour ahead of schedule.
0589ffbb11c5.target: 想象一个德国/日本城市，那里的火车提前一小时运行。

c7e45dddca7a.source: The 4pm train arrives at 3pm. The 11am flight takes off at 10am. In other words, a dystopian hellscape.
c7e45dddca7a.target: 下午4点的火车在下午3点到达。上午11点的航班在上午10点起飞。换句话说，一个反乌托邦的地狱景象。

a8e7312666a6.source: If I managed not to burst into flames upon arriving, I'd describe the situation like this:
a8e7312666a6.target: 如果我在到达时没有着火，我会这样描述情况：

6cca695507d7.source: In other words, f(x + 1) means things run ahead of schedule. We think it's 3pm (x = 3), but the 4pm events are happening [f(4) is happening].
6cca695507d7.target: 换句话说，f(x + 1) 意味着事情提前了。我们认为现在是下午3点 (x=3)，但下午4点的事件正在发生 [f(4) 正在发生]。

6034bc43c41b.source: Again, this can be tricky: doesn't it seem like we remove time to make things happen earlier? This is our visual intuition fooling us: if it's 3pm but f is running the 4pm events, it's going ahead of schedule. Note that we
6034bc43c41b.target: 同样，这可能有点棘手：我们移除时间让事情更早发生，难道不觉得奇怪吗？这是我们的视觉直觉在欺骗我们：如果现在是下午3点，但 f 正在运行下午4点的事件，那么它是在提前进行。注意，我们

f9b1ed16609b.source: time to our watch in order to arrive early.)
f9b1ed16609b.target: 给我们的手表增加时间才能早到。)

521d083ff042.source: See "slide to the left" as "ahead of schedule".
521d083ff042.target: 将“向左滑动”理解为“提前”。

49d191c410c5.source: Adding a value to a function moves it vertically.
49d191c410c5.target: 给函数加上一个值会使其垂直移动。

c19283037e5f.source: What's happening? Unbeknownst to f(x), we take take the final result and make it larger.
c19283037e5f.target: 发生了什么？在 f(x) 不知情的情况下，我们取最终结果并使其变大。

d570be43a197.source: Intuitively, we have a "bias". When f(x) = 0, it's telling us "don't change, stay at 0". Except our default value isn't zero, it's
d570be43a197.target: 直观上，我们有一个“偏置”。当 f(x) = 0 时，它告诉我们“不要改变，保持在0”。但是我们的默认值不是零，而是

3210109d3ba1.source: . When f(x) says don't change, that means "use your default value, c".
3210109d3ba1.target: 。当 f(x) 说不改变时，意思是“使用你的默认值 c”。

89749c06577d.source: See "sliding up the function" as "changing the default value".
89749c06577d.target: 将“向上滑动函数”理解为“改变默认值”。

af95923b8093.source: (In neural networks, you might have a default value if there's zero input. A "default bias" is a nice way to describe this, vs. "vertically sliding the function".)
af95923b8093.target: (在神经网络中，如果没有输入，你可能有一个默认值。“默认偏置”是一个很好的描述方式，相比“垂直滑动函数”。)

aa1062ba017c.source: In Calculus, the chain rule lets us compose functions. (Fancy phrase for cramming one function inside another.)
aa1062ba017c.target: 在微积分中，链式法则让我们可以组合函数。(把一个函数塞进另一个函数的花哨说法。)

936f44a91f3e.source: , and take the derivative, we get:

7f34969b910c.target: 链式法则告诉我们先求外层函数的导数 (sin(2x) => cos(2x))，再乘以内层函数的导数 (2x => 2)。

a4c0a9a76d05.source: Now, what happens if we run sin(x) at twice the speed? Eat, eat, eat!
a4c0a9a76d05.target: 现在，如果我们以两倍速度运行 sin(x) 会发生什么？吃，吃，吃！

eeef65c85016.source: Well...  the derivative (slope we see) should double! Compared to the original, sin(2x) runs through changes twice as fast as we do. 1 minute in our world means sin(2x) has chomped through 2 minutes of changes.
eeef65c85016.target: 嗯……导数（我们看到的斜率）应该加倍！与原始函数相比，sin(2x)经历变化的速度是我们速度的两倍。我们世界中的1分钟意味着sin(2x)已经吞噬了2分钟的变化。

b6cb76371691.source: in the cycle, we should expect double the slope. Let's make sure.
b6cb76371691.target: 处，我们预期斜率会加倍。让我们确认一下。

8137fb69a78d.target: 不再询问x=1，我们知道sin(2x)时间线上的同一点现在是x=0.5。所以让我们求该点的导数：

1693679ff49b.source: We can mechanically describe the chain rule as a way to compose functions. But intuitively, we've strapped a fast-forward device inside our function, speeding up the changes we experience.
1693679ff49b.target: 我们可以从机械上描述链式法则为函数组合的一种方式。但直观上，我们在函数内部绑定了一个快进装置，加速了我们所经历的变化。

ca6077f2b089.source: Regular sin(x) keeps its derivative between -1.0 and +1.0, the limits imposed by cos(x). But now we have a fast-forward trick to make sin(x) move as fast as we want.
ca6077f2b089.target: 普通的sin(x)将其导数保持在-1.0到+1.0之间，这是cos(x)施加的限制。但现在我们有了一个快进技巧，可以使sin(x)以我们想要的任何速度移动。

0a9db71192f2.source: Extra: Imagine we weren't fast-forwarding at a constant rate of 2; what if sped up more, the further along we went? That function could be sin(x * x), where one x is our regular location in the cycle, and the other x is our fast-forward rate. For fun (yes!), you can take the derivative of sin(x * x) (
0a9db71192f2.target: 额外思考：假设我们不是以固定的速度2快进，而是随着进程的推进加速越来越快？这样的函数可能是sin(x * x)，其中一个x是我们周期中的常规位置，另一个x是我们的快进速率。为了好玩（是的！），你可以求sin(x * x)的导数（

70f8da02ae55.source: We can run the exponential function ($e^x$) ahead of schedule with $e^{x + b}$.
70f8da02ae55.target: 我们可以通过$e^{x + b}$让指数函数($e^x$)提前运行。

e3a501b828b3.source: But we can rewrite this to:
e3a501b828b3.target: 但我们可以将其重写为：

21c54ef69cbc.source: "ahead of schedule" can be seen as the regular exponential function with a bigger starting point.
21c54ef69cbc.target: "提前"视为具有更大起始点的常规指数函数。

feb6930e9fbc.source: Normal exponentials start at 1.0 and begin compounding continuously. Instead, we can see it as starting with a bigger starting value from 1.0. (For example, $e^{x + 2}$ starts compounding from $e^2 = 7.389$.)
feb6930e9fbc.target: 普通的指数函数从1.0开始并连续复利。相反，我们可以将其视为从比1.0更大的起始值开始。（例如，$e^{x + 2}$从$e^2 = 7.389$开始复利。）

9ebcff2012fd.source: Depending on the function, interpretations other than "ahead of schedule" might make more sense.
9ebcff2012fd.target: 根据函数的不同，"提前"之外的解释可能更有意义。

7303cc9915f3.target: 加/减/乘/除一个常数，或者：

14f6cb8f007a.target: 多项式进行加法/乘法运算后仍为多项式

ec0d88d27e3b.source: Divide a polynomial by its roots, $(x - r)$, and get a polynomial (like dividing a compound number by one of its factors)
ec0d88d27e3b.target: 用多项式的根 $(x - r)$ 去除多项式，得到另一个多项式（类似于用复合数的因子去除它）

303a8f80c6be.target: 将一个多项式代入另一个多项式 $f(g(x))$

6f7de546bcd7.source: The derivative / integral of a polynomial is a polynomial, making them easy to optimize
6f7de546bcd7.target: 多项式的导数/积分仍是多项式，便于优化

f6e5befa8905.target: 多项式将简单算术整合在一起

f422868b27ab.source: Polynomials are simpler to study than arbitrary functions
f422868b27ab.target: 多项式比任意函数更容易研究

6359c08daa97.source: Polynomials are the DNA inside a function

a11c299bacde.target: 多项式将加法转化为乘法

35232a71bbdb.source: Polynomials are those long equations you were forced to learn about in school:
35232a71bbdb.target: 多项式就是你在学校里被迫学习的那些长方程：

a1d18e06b528.source: We have a bunch of coefficients ($c_0, c_1...$ ) multiplied by various powers of $x$.
a1d18e06b528.target: 我们有一堆系数（$c_0, c_1...$）乘以$x$的各个幂次。

938616f6575c.source: Why are we forced to learn about them? Here's the insights I wish I had.
938616f6575c.target: 为什么我们被迫学习它们？以下是我希望当时能有的见解。

227bfcb72310.source: Why it's one hundred twenty three, of course. Oh, you'd probably have preferred the number in its polynomial form:
227bfcb72310.target: 当然是123。哦，你可能更喜欢这个数字的多项式形式：

2045e3996de3.source: The digits "123" are simply the coefficients for various powers of 10, written largest to smallest. The "simple" idea of counting by adding powers of 10 is pretty game-changing, right?
2045e3996de3.target: 数字"123"只是10的各个幂次的系数，从大到小排列。这种通过相加10的幂次来计数的"简单"想法相当具有变革性，对吧？

63f702b5bc16.source: We can even switch bases. The general interpretation of "123", for any base, is:
63f702b5bc16.target: 我们甚至可以切换进制。对于任意进制，"123"的通用解释是：

5ffb889f3ea5.source: We're already familiar with decimals, so turn the coefficients "123" into a quantity we can understand. For hexadecimal numbers, we plug in $x = 16$ and work out $(1)16^2 +(2)16 + (3)16^0 = 291$.
5ffb889f3ea5.target: 我们已经熟悉十进制，所以将系数"123"转换为我们能理解的数量。对于十六进制数，我们代入$x=16$并计算出$(1)16^2+(2)16+(3)16^0=291$。

012bf4ad20cb.source: Strictly speaking, 123.4 involves negative powers, since $0.1 = 10^{-1}$. Still, the original notion of polynomials gave us a good start, better than counting with lines in the sand.
012bf4ad20cb.target: 严格来说，123.4 涉及负指数，因为 $0.1 = 10^{-1}$。不过，多项式(polynomials)的原始概念为我们提供了一个良好的起点，比在沙地上划线计数要好。

d49732cf7c41.source: Imagine you're taking notes for someone who's too lazy to use a calculator. They might say:
d49732cf7c41.target: 想象你正在为一个懒得用计算器的人做笔记。他们可能会说：

21fbeb62f486.source: "Take the number 15. Actually, add 3. Multiply by 7. Now multiply by the original number again."
21fbeb62f486.target: "取数字15。实际上，加3。乘以7。现在再乘以原来的数字。"

2e6aa5190ea8.target: 作为算术，你可能会写出：

4edf052f4b45.source: This is an exact expression, with specific numbers. But maybe your buddy is indecisive.
4edf052f4b45.target: 这是一个精确的表达式，带有具体的数字。但也许你的朋友犹豫不决。

078f0cf4a58a.source: Making this more general -- with any starting number -- we'd write:
078f0cf4a58a.target: 使其更通用——以任意起始数字——我们会写出：

0afabf4a6c2b.source: Notice that all the intermediate steps have been reduced to 2 terms. A polynomial!
0afabf4a6c2b.target: 注意，所有中间步骤都被简化为两项。一个多项式(polynomial)！

cc23120b9c91.source: Instead of thinking "When will I see this equation", think "This equation is the simplified version of a bunch of arithmetic". The steps don't look similar (where's the "add 3" portion?) but the polynomial boils everything to the essentials.
cc23120b9c91.target: 不要想“我什么时候会看到这个方程”，而是想“这个方程是一系列算术的简化版本”。这些步骤看起来并不相似（“加3”的部分在哪？）但多项式将所有内容精炼到本质。

fb2ba8d23f21.source: So, another big insight: we don't always need to
fb2ba8d23f21.target: 因此，另一个重要见解：我们并不总是需要

11817e718703.source: a polynomial, we can use it to simplify many stages of arithmetic.
11817e718703.target: 一个多项式，我们可以用它来简化多个阶段的算术。

dd12588e4ead.source: But... now that it's in its equation form, we
dd12588e4ead.target: 但是……既然它已经是方程形式，我们

75622df69dd0.source: solve it. If we wanted to. Because it seems like a mathy thing to do.
75622df69dd0.target: 解它。如果我们想的话。因为这看起来像是一件数学的事。

828f92752d53.source: Let's say we want to know when this process hits 100. What input number would that be?
828f92752d53.target: 假设我们想知道这个过程何时达到100。那会是什么输入数字？

817c181175df.source: gives $x = -5.56$ and $x=2.56$. (Yes, in the real world we can use tools to solve equations; grinding through the quadratic formula is an intuition for another day.)
817c181175df.target: 得到 $x = -5.56$ 和 $x=2.56$。（是的，在现实世界中我们可以使用工具来解方程；钻研二次公式是另一天的直觉。）

633062288452.source: The big aha is that polynomials are the boiled down version of arithmetic, assuming you can only:
633062288452.target: 最大的惊喜是多项式(polynomials)是算术的精简版本，假设你只能：

7e0d260e726c.source: Strictly speaking, polynomials don't allow for square roots ($\sqrt{x}$) or variable exponents ($2^x$), just the simple operations above.
7e0d260e726c.target: 严格来说，多项式不允许平方根($\sqrt{x}$)或可变指数($2^x$)，只允许上述简单运算。

511eccc4ef35.source: Why limit ourselves this way? Well, we can see how far we can get.
511eccc4ef35.target: 为什么这样限制自己？嗯，我们可以看看能走多远。

743c1b599b1d.source: is limited to linear functions, but even then we can model complex behavior if we chain enough of them together.
743c1b599b1d.target: 限于线性函数(linear functions)，但即使如此，如果我们把足够多的它们串联起来，也能模拟复杂行为。

139e53dcd506.source: Similarly, polynomials are simple, but with
139e53dcd506.target: 类似地，多项式很简单，但通过

c4dc2eb6818b.source: we can model pretty complex behavior. The simple structure gives us several nice properties:
c4dc2eb6818b.target: 我们可以模拟相当复杂的行为。简单的结构给了我们几个不错的性质：

5ee358a0485f.source: Polynomials are like the integers -- simpler than real numbers, and still useful.
5ee358a0485f.target: 多项式就像整数——比实数简单，但仍然有用。

a00b1a04cc7b.source: What does $x$ mean? Well, it's a number, a quantity, something we want to represent.
a00b1a04cc7b.target: $x$ 是什么意思？嗯，它是一个数字，一个量，我们想要表示的东西。

c4ca279e3b8c.source: What does $x \cdot x = x^2$ mean? It's an
c4ca279e3b8c.target: $x \cdot x = x^2$ 是什么意思？它表示一个

08586645fd26.source: : a quantity interacting with itself. In the case of length interacting with length, the result is area: length * length = square units. Or, we could have speed interacting with time to make distance: 30 mph for 30 hours is 900 miles.
08586645fd26.target: ：一个量与自身的相互作用。在长度与长度相互作用的情况下，结果是面积：长度 * 长度 = 平方单位。或者，我们可以有速度与时间相互作用得到距离：30 英里/小时持续 30 小时得到 900 英里。

aa21bae47148.source: A big aha: math doesn't care about units. A polynomial does the bookkeeping to show
aa21bae47148.target: 一个大发现：数学不关心单位。多项式负责记账，以展示

4d482184752e.source: an interaction like $x \cdot x$ happened, along with plain old $x$ and $x^{17}$.
4d482184752e.target: 像 $x \cdot x$ 这样的相互作用发生了，还有普通的 $x$ 和 $x^{17}$。

62d7b0361067.source: In physics, you can't sensibly write $3cm + 5cm^2$ because the powers of the units don't match. You can't write $3 cm + 5kg$ because the types of units don't match.
62d7b0361067.target: 在物理学中，你不能合理地写出 $3cm + 5cm^2$，因为单位的幂次不匹配。你不能写出 $3 cm + 5kg$，因为单位的类型不匹配。

b2a6993539b5.source: But math doesn't care. Numbers are numbers, and "1 + 2*2 = 5" works, even though "2 times 2" represents an interaction.
b2a6993539b5.target: 但数学不关心。数字就是数字，"1 + 2*2 = 5" 是成立的，尽管 "2 乘以 2" 表示一个相互作用。

3822e4f8e034.source: A polynomial is a general-purpose accounting system where we can throw
3822e4f8e034.target: 多项式是一个通用记账系统，我们可以把

fd4b4f2496dc.source: in the pot of soup. Will it taste good? Not the polynomial's problem. You'll get a formula and decide what, if anything, to do with it.
fd4b4f2496dc.target: 扔进汤锅里。它会好吃吗？那不是多项式的问题。你会得到一个公式，然后决定用它做什么——如果有的话。

a13b3a924a98.source: Given an input $x$, polynomials show what happens when we perform an ungodly amount of arithmetic to that input.
a13b3a924a98.target: 给定一个输入 $x$，多项式展示了当我们对该输入进行大量算术运算时会发生什么。

400eef04f748.source: It seems, if we twiddle enough levers, we can get that polynomial to behave like almost any pattern we choose.
400eef04f748.target: 似乎，如果我们拨动足够多的杠杆，就能让该多项式表现得像我们选择的几乎任何模式。

00ccce95a5e4.source: With enough terms, our humble polynomial -- composed of
00ccce95a5e4.target: 有了足够多的项，我们谦逊的多项式——由

fad6e2febdf3.source: -- can model undulating sine waves and other things that give students nightmares.
fad6e2febdf3.target: 组成——可以模拟起伏的正弦波和其他让学生做噩梦的东西。

4362e552dd48.source: Each term in the polynomial gets us a better approximation of the original function:
4362e552dd48.target: 多项式中的每一项都让我们更接近原函数的近似：

ecc803cb94df.source: We can analyze the coefficients in the polynomial and extract the "DNA" inside a function:
ecc803cb94df.target: 我们可以分析多项式中的系数，提取函数内部的 "DNA"：

b34cb327ee7d.source: If the pattern in the coefficients are similar, the functions are likely related.
b34cb327ee7d.target: 如果系数中的模式相似，那么这些函数很可能相关。

46155de4f140.source: Back to the decimal analogy: 12 and 120 are similar, but that's only obvious when I write them as "decimal polynomials". If I spilled 12 and 120 skittles on the floor, you wouldn't notice any obvious connection between the two piles. Well, one might look slightly more appetizing.
46155de4f140.target: 回到十进制类比：12 和 120 是相似的，但只有当我将它们写成 "十进制多项式" 时才明显。如果我把 12 和 120 颗彩虹糖撒在地上，你不会注意到两堆之间有任何明显的联系。嗯，其中一堆可能看起来稍微更有食欲。

5453e8e3662b.source: One of the big insights in math -- called the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra -- is that a polynomial can be rewritten as a sequence of
5453e8e3662b.target: 数学中的一个重要见解——称为代数基本定理——是多项式可以重写为一系列

e022d89c94d4.source: That is, adding powers of $x$ can
e022d89c94d4.target: 也就是说，将 $x$ 的幂相加可以

7171762ea2fb.source: be seen as multiplying $x$ with various offsets:
7171762ea2fb.target: 看作是 $x$ 与各种偏移量的乘法：

7831e42b663e.source: There's as many roots ($a_1, a_2, a_3$) as the highest power of $x$ (roots can be used multiple times).
7831e42b663e.target: 根的个数（$a_1, a_2, a_3$）与 $x$ 的最高幂次相同（根可以重复使用）。

13c55b194bfa.source: Why is this important? Well, besides being incredibly surprising (
13c55b194bfa.target: 为什么这很重要呢？嗯，除了令人难以置信的惊讶之外（

515dafc5ea62.source: ), it makes solving equations much easier (see:
515dafc5ea62.target: ），它还使得解方程变得容易得多（参见：

5447e41be449.source: In short, it's not instantly obvious how to satisfy this:
5447e41be449.target: 简言之，如何满足这个方程并不是显而易见的：

a77dacd8b32f.source: Want to guess answers? Plug in $x$ and it shows up in places terms which need to be balanced. But what about this:
a77dacd8b32f.target: 想猜答案吗？代入 $x$，它出现在需要平衡的各个项中。但下面这个方程呢：

8077397faca3.source: It's the same scenario, factored into multiplications. If
8077397faca3.target: 这是相同的场景，被分解为乘法形式。如果

1b7610b74fc1.source: term goes to zero, the entire product becomes zero and we're done. Just pick $(x + 6)$ or $(x - 2)$, make it zero ($x = -6$ or $x = 2$), and we've solved the puzzle.
1b7610b74fc1.target: 项为零，整个乘积就为零，问题就解决了。只需选出 $(x + 6)$ 或 $(x - 2)$，使其为零（$x = -6$ 或 $x = 2$），我们就解开了谜题。

f6d820b5af09.source: Remember how "123" was secretly a polynomial? For fun, using the accursed quadratic formula, we can
f6d820b5af09.target: 还记得 "123" 实际上是一个多项式吗？有趣的是，使用那该死的二次方程求根公式，我们可以

6538f5b01140.source: What does this mean? We can get the digits "123" to equal zero if we use a complex base! Not that you'd
6538f5b01140.target: 这意味着什么？如果我们使用一个复数基，就可以让数字 "123" 等于零！虽然你不会真的去

45052414b34e.source: A polynomial is simple: just a bunch of powers of x. That means it's easy to use the
45052414b34e.target: 多项式很简单：只是 $x$ 的一些幂次。这意味着在微积分中很容易使用

651fcaf80d28.source: in Calculus to find the derivative, and find the min/max.
651fcaf80d28.target: 来求导数，并找到最小值/最大值。

52b7fdc847a0.source: The reverse works as well: we can take a bunch of data points, fit a polynomial to them, and estimate the min/max of the sequence. The more terms, the more accurate (but beware overfitting).
52b7fdc847a0.target: 反过来也成立：我们可以取一组数据点，拟合一个多项式，并估计序列的最小值/最大值。项数越多，精度越高（但要注意过拟合）。

08dedd460447.source: A simple structure has its advantages.

e3765fdd4fce.source: Polynomials usually evoke memories of the quadratic equation, or equations force-fit into word problems. Ugh, let's skip past that.
e3765fdd4fce.target: 多项式通常让人想起二次方程，或者那些硬套进应用题中的方程。呃，我们跳过这些吧。

e95741d155d2.source: Polynomials are a simple, powerful model used from arithmetic to algebra to calculus. They're as applicable in the "real world" as a 2-digit number.
e95741d155d2.target: 多项式是从算术到代数再到微积分的一种简单而强大的模型。它们就像两位数数字一样适用于“现实世界”。

4c23268d5ce8.source: Q: Find the equation for a line between (3, 4) and (7, 12)
4c23268d5ce8.target: Q: 求经过(3,4)和(7,12)两点的直线方程

864f4d0e8465.source: x is the change we intend to make

91a2f14d7dad.source: m is the percentage (rate) of the change we actually see
91a2f14d7dad.target: m是我们实际看到的变化的百分比（速率）

fd72b7636d2c.source: Figure out the rate of change between two points (m)
fd72b7636d2c.target: 求出两点之间的变化率(m)

a04e5aee201b.source: Using that rate of change, work backwards to find our starting point (b)
a04e5aee201b.target: 利用该变化率，反向推导出我们的起始点(b)

11234d7d4b51.source: Example: Find the slope-intercept form between two points
11234d7d4b51.target: 示例：求两点之间的斜率-截距形式

cad8c1df2cbe.source: Ok, y is usually the final value in an equation. It's based on two parts... mx and b. If x goes up by 1 let's say, we only change y by... m.
cad8c1df2cbe.target: 好的，y 通常是方程中的最终值。它由两部分组成... mx 和 b。如果 x 增加 1，我们只改变 y 乘以 m。

e5c8c067dd7b.source: Hrm, let's try an example. If m=.3, and x goes from 0 to 10, we go up by 10 * .3 = 3. Ok. Got that part.
e5c8c067dd7b.target: 嗯，让我们举个例子。如果 m=0.3，x 从 0 增加到 10，我们增加了 10 * 0.3 = 3。好的，这部分理解了。

2e8adfc619e1.source: Now what does b do? It adds something on top of whatever we just figured out. If x = 0, I guess we just have y = b.
2e8adfc619e1.target: 现在 b 是做什么的？它在我们已经算出的结果之上加上一些东西。如果 x = 0，我想我们只有 y = b。

26c790025567.source: Here's the typical presentation for slope-intercept form for a line:
26c790025567.target: 这是直线斜截式的典型表示：

b9dde6af79fe.source: What goes through our head the first time we see this?
b9dde6af79fe.target: 第一次看到这个时，我们脑子里想的是什么？

e329ffce1d2e.source: There's a lot of Math <> English translation. In math jargon, we say the slope (m) determines the direct rate of change between x and y, and the y-intercept (b) adds a starting value.
e329ffce1d2e.target: 有很多数学与英语之间的转换。在数学术语中，我们说斜率(m)决定了 x 和 y 之间的直接变化率，而 y 截距(b)增加了一个起始值。

8f08c71bfc66.source: Ugh. It's abstract and doesn't resonate. Here's a more natural wording:
8f08c71bfc66.target: 呃。这很抽象，没有共鸣。这里有一个更自然的表述：

c18bc3a3adbe.source: Makes sense, right? Start somewhere, make a change, get to the new location.
c18bc3a3adbe.target: 有道理，对吧？从某处开始，做出改变，到达新位置。

c38f52327ae3.source: Zooming in, we realize a change has two parts: how fast are we changing, and for how long?
c38f52327ae3.target: 放大来看，我们意识到一个变化包含两部分：我们变化的速率是多少，以及持续了多长时间？

7315d4daa7b8.source: Ah. I can see the role of each of the pieces. Turning this into succint math notation:
7315d4daa7b8.target: 啊。我能看到每个部分的作用。将其转化为简洁的数学符号：

d7e30afda80f.source: "Start at b, add the rate of change (m) times the duration (x), and you end up at y."
d7e30afda80f.target: "从 b 开始，加上变化率(m)乘以持续时间(x)，最终得到 y。"

15f4b0efddea.source: We aren't forced to graph the equation and describe b as the "y intercept". Who thinks (or talks) like that?
15f4b0efddea.target: 我们不必画图并将 b 描述为 'y 截距'。谁会这么想（或这么说）？

28b1a086dffb.source: to graph our line (fun fun), then b is the height on the y-axis when x=0. But thinking in terms of our starting value (the
28b1a086dffb.target: 要绘制我们的直线（乐趣无穷），那么 b 是当 x=0 时 y 轴上的高度。但用起始值（偏

5d5ac5565d00.source: The graphed line is a list of all possible outcomes under some set of circumstances. We might not wait at all (x =0), or wait for a bit (x=1), wait for a long time (x = 100), or look back in time (x = -3). The line shows what would have happened in each of those scenarios.
5d5ac5565d00.target: 绘制的直线是在一组特定条件下所有可能结果的列表。我们可能根本不等待（x=0），或者等待一会儿（x=1），等待很长时间（x=100），或者回顾过去（x=-3）。直线展示了在每种情况下会发生什么。

340c657a4cfc.source: People claim they don't think in terms of math. Sure, you might not describe things as slope/intercept form, but I'd guess "original + change = new" is pretty familiar to you. Even the simplest equations can use an intuitive wording.
340c657a4cfc.target: 人们声称他们不以数学的方式思考。当然，你可能不会用斜率/截距形式来描述事物，但我猜测'原始值 + 变化 = 新值'对你来说相当熟悉。即使是最简单的方程也可以用直观的措辞。

2dd8a0b29dda.source: Let's think about this intuitively. We know ultimately we want an equation of the form:
2dd8a0b29dda.target: 让我们直观地考虑一下。我们知道最终我们想要一个形式如下的方程：

e3f63eb8e0d1.source: For the two points we have, let's look at the change:
e3f63eb8e0d1.target: 对于这两个点，让我们看看变化：

8509921b636f.source: Between those points, x changed by 4 and y changed by 8. The rate was 8/4 = 2, so the equation should look like:
8509921b636f.target: 在这些点之间，x变化了4，y变化了8。变化率为8/4=2，因此方程应该像这样：

4a0453306d61.source: We don't know where we started, but whenever x changes, y changes by 2 times that amount.
4a0453306d61.target: 我们不知道起点在哪里，但只要x变化，y就会变化2倍于x的变化量。

2d21eff1dd73.source: Ok. Now what was the starting position, when x = 0? Let's see the change between our default value of (0, ?) to (3, 4).
2d21eff1dd73.target: 好的。现在我们看看当x=0时的起始位置。让我们看看从默认值(0, ?)到(3,4)的变化。

6d275b72075d.source: Well, if x changed by 3, and the rate of change is 2, then y changed by 3*2 = 6. The original value was therefore 4 - 6 = -2.
6d275b72075d.target: 如果x变化了3，变化率为2，那么y变化了3*2=6。因此初始值是4-6=-2。

933e4350039e.source: and written in more standard y = mx + b notation,
933e4350039e.target: 用更标准的y = mx + b形式书写，

f9b9bb83cc49.source: Let's double check. We start at (0, -2) and go 3 units on the x coordinate. Our final position is
f9b9bb83cc49.target: 让我们复核一下。我们从(0,-2)开始，在x坐标上移动3个单位。最终位置是

1f084f78a9a8.source: This approach is slower than a plug-and-chug shortcut, but the idea is to figure out exactly what you're doing:
1f084f78a9a8.target: 这种方法比直接套用公式要慢，但目的是让你确切地理解自己在做什么：

69a7b1ffce75.source: True learning goes beyond memorized facts. While I can forget the equation of a circle, I can't forget that it's round. And knowing it's perfectly round quickly leads me back to the equation.
69a7b1ffce75.target: 真正的学习超越了记忆的事实。虽然我可能会忘记圆的方程，但我不会忘记它是圆的。而知道它是完美的圆形很快就能让我回想起方程。

804541779577.source: True understanding implies joy. And practically, you'll only continue studying what you like.
804541779577.target: 真正的理解意味着快乐。实际上，你只会继续学习你喜欢的东西。

379ee9265c77.source: We aren't robots, and we should embrace the subjective aspects of learning. A teacher's goal goes beyond knowledge-transfer to enjoyment-transfer.
379ee9265c77.target: 我们不是机器人，应该拥抱学习的主观方面。教师的目标超越了知识传递，延伸到快乐传递。

814116d55521.source: There are deeper, richer levels of understanding than what's traditionally used. Explore a higher standard.
814116d55521.target: 存在比传统方式更深入、更丰富的理解层次。探索更高的标准。

6e70473465c8.source: Your own standards greatly influence your understanding. External tests won't check if facts are comfortably connected.
6e70473465c8.target: 你自己的标准极大地影响着你的理解。外部测试不会检验事实是否被舒适地关联起来。

f2082fa09c6d.source: Do I have a visceral, ingrained analogy? Can it help solve problems?
f2082fa09c6d.target: 我是否有一个直观、根深蒂固的类比？它能帮助解决问题吗？

b8a00eedc4f1.source: Can I explain the concept to others? Do they want to explain it to their friends afterwards?
b8a00eedc4f1.target: 我能否向他人解释这个概念？他们之后是否想向朋友解释？

1b9cb9c65334.source: Will I remember the essential idea after a few months or years?
1b9cb9c65334.target: 几个月或几年后，我还能记住核心思想吗？

4870d5a7b426.source: Can I find something to enjoy in the topic? Will I return after I inevitably forget 95% of it?
4870d5a7b426.target: 我能否在这个主题中找到一些乐趣？当我不可避免地忘记95%的内容后，我还会回来吗？

3e3ab2ee8661.target: 步骤1：向著名学习者学习

b6258c756a19.source: Step 2: Ask Questions That Check Your Standards
b6258c756a19.target: 步骤2：提出能检验你标准的问题

ffe7a040f721.source: My learning progress skyrocketed after adopting a new standard:
ffe7a040f721.target: 在采纳了一个新标准后，我的学习进度飞速提升：

d549d2cb4189.source: Imagine a chef who follows a new recipe to the letter. No matter how it looks, no matter the reviews the recipe has, if the dish
d549d2cb4189.target: 想象一位厨师严格按照新食谱操作。无论菜品外观如何，无论食谱评价多高，如果这道菜

66c8608a9d1e.source: we know something is wrong. A sense of taste is the ultimate cooking tool.
66c8608a9d1e.target: 我们就知道出了问题。味觉是最终的烹饪工具。

18c17e457565.source: When learning, we defer to external indicators (tests, teachers) to inform us we've learned something. External standards are made to be objective and easily-verified (Did you pick the correct answer?), but the important, subjective question is how well a concept sits in your mind. Did you actually experience it?
18c17e457565.target: 在学习时，我们依赖外部指标（测试、老师）来告知我们是否学会了。外部标准具有客观性和易验证性（你选对答案了吗？），但重要的、主观的问题是概念在你的脑海中安放得如何。你真的体验到了吗？

e2952427ad39.source: My checklist of truly learning a topic means it is:
e2952427ad39.target: 我真正学会一个主题的清单要求它：

33f2fd94bc14.source: Did I have an aha! moment? Can I explain the concept in simple language? Does it connect to other topics I know?
33f2fd94bc14.target: 我是否有顿悟时刻？能否用简单的语言解释这个概念？它是否与我已知的其他主题相关联？

0e0d60fc4fd0.source: : Do I have an analogy, diagram, or example that will stick with me for months or years?
0e0d60fc4fd0.target: ：我是否有一个能在未来几个月或几年内牢记的类比、图表或例子？

766038baebcf.source: : Do I want to revisit or use this knowledge? Don't study literature in a way that makes you hate reading.
766038baebcf.target: ：我是否愿意重新审视或使用这些知识？不要用让你厌恶阅读的方式学习文学。

f6464d11dd08.source: That's my current definition of "intuitive understanding", and for subjects I care about, I keep digging until I have all three aspects.
f6464d11dd08.target: 这是我对“直观理解”目前的定义，对于我在意的学科，我会一直挖掘直到拥有全部三个方面。

aaeda4dfbe39.source: It's ok to take your time (

d156a1c1227b.source: took years to become enjoyable) and it's ok to not care about everything equally (biology isn't particularly compelling for me). I firmly believe any subject can become intuitive if I put in the effort to find analogies, diagrams, examples, plain-english descriptions, and technical details (the
d156a1c1227b.target: 经过多年才变得有趣），而且不必对每件事情都同样上心（生物学对我而言并没有特别吸引人）。我坚信只要付出努力去找到类比、图表、实例、通俗解释和技术细节，任何学科都可以变得直观（

939fb89621ea.source: So, how do you set your own learning standard?
939fb89621ea.target: 那么，你如何设定自己的学习标准？

7148c6e0634c.source: Let's not recreate the wheel: famous learners have already described their thinking process, which we can adopt. It's not about memorizing Einstein's Theory of Relativity, it's about internalizing the mindset that could lead to that idea.
7148c6e0634c.target: 我们不必重新发明轮子：著名的学习者已经描述过他们的思考过程，我们可以加以借鉴。这不是要记住爱因斯坦的相对论，而是要内化能产生那种想法的思维方式。

9954ec809f88.source: Here's a few viewpoints that resonated for me:
9954ec809f88.target: 以下是一些让我产生共鸣的观点：

280abd0ad1ff.source: "Education is what remains after one has forgotten what one has learned in school." —Albert Einstein
280abd0ad1ff.target: “教育就是当一个人把在学校所学全部忘光之后剩下的东西。” ——阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦

ae77b3578278.source: “The only real valuable thing is intuition.” —Albert Einstein
ae77b3578278.target: “唯一真正有价值的东西是直觉。” ——阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦

3d30ac61fe9f.source: "The noblest pleasure is the joy of understanding." —Da Vinci
3d30ac61fe9f.target: “最高贵的快乐是理解的喜悦。” ——达·芬奇

8d14a2cc10a2.source: "To teach effectively a teacher must develop a feeling for his subject; he cannot make his students sense its vitality if he does not sense it himself. He cannot share his enthusiasm when he has no enthusiasm to share. How he makes his point may be as important as the point he makes; he must personally feel it to be important.” —George Póyla
8d14a2cc10a2.target: “要有效地教学，教师必须对自己的学科有感受；如果他自己都感受不到学科的活力，就无法让学生感受到。如果他没有热情可以分享，就无法分享他的热情。他表达观点的方式可能和观点本身一样重要；他必须亲自感受到它的重要性。” ——乔治·波利亚

adc64aabb769.source: “Education is the kindling of a flame, not the filling of a vessel.” —Socrates
adc64aabb769.target: “教育不是灌输，而是点燃火焰。” ——苏格拉底

6b2523dbc2b5.source: "I think most people can learn a lot more than they think they can. They sell themselves short without trying. One bit of advice: it is important to view knowledge as sort of a semantic tree — make sure you understand the fundamental principles, ie the trunk and big branches, before you get into the leaves/details or there is nothing for them to hang on to." —Elon Musk
6b2523dbc2b5.target: “我认为大多数人能学到的比他们想象的多得多。他们不尝试就低估了自己。一个建议：把知识看作语义树——确保你理解了基本原理，也就是树干和大树枝，然后再去研究树叶/细节，否则它们无处可依附。” ——埃隆·马斯克

bdcd9c2db4f2.source: of quotes that help align my thinking.
bdcd9c2db4f2.target: 有助于调整思维的名言集。

08b2a32cdab2.source: After rummaging through quotes that resonate, build a set of questions that capture your standard. For me, it became:
08b2a32cdab2.target: 在整理那些引起共鸣的名言之后，建立一组能代表你标准的问题。对我来说，变成了：

85f40c7c3b6a.source: Questions seem to prompt more interest than a statement: "Do I have an analogy?" vs. "I must have an analogy".
85f40c7c3b6a.target: 问题似乎比陈述更能激发兴趣：“我有类比吗？” vs “我必须有一个类比”。

81c8617df401.source: With this approach, strange corners of math I didn't previously enjoy (like

8ab5a74ac9ca.source: When people say "I hate math" I doubt they actually hate numbers (arithmetic), patterns & relationships (algebra), or shapes (geometry). They hate lessons that don't contain insight, enjoyment, and basic human empathy. It's fine to be disinterested in Ancient Egyptian Civilization, but

8e43a835a3e5.target: 结果角度将大于90度。3 + 4i大于45度（因为3 + 3i是45度），所以两倍该角度将超过90度。

ba3184a5d73d.source: Angle: Since -7 is negative and 24i is positive, we know we are going “backwards and up”, which means we’ve crossed 90 degrees (“straight up”). Getting geeky, we compute atan(24/-7) = 106.2 degrees (keeping in mind we’re in quadrant 2). This guess checks out too.
ba3184a5d73d.target: 角度：由于-7为负，24i为正，可知我们在“向后向上”移动，即已越过90度（“垂直向上”）。从专业角度，计算atan(24/-7) = 106.2度（注意我们在第二象限）。这个猜测也成立。

d3b3cf41831c.source: I rotated a line by doing several multiplications: (3 + 4i), (1 + i), and (2 + 10i). What rotation will cancel these out? To find the opposite: multiply the complex numbers together, and take the conjugate of the result.

84c12c0eedfa.source: : they “rotate” numbers, just like negatives make a “mirror image” of a number. This insight makes arithmetic with complex numbers easier to understand, and is a great way to double-check your results. Here’s our cheatsheet:
84c12c0eedfa.target: ：它们“旋转”数字，就像负数产生数字的“镜像”一样。这种见解让复数算术更容易理解，也是检查结果的好方法。这里是我们速查表：

00f0fbe14391.source: This post will walk through the intuitive meanings.
00f0fbe14391.target: 本文会逐步讲解直观含义。

9f12b20e8fb4.source: In regular algebra, we often say “x = 3″ and all is dandy — there’s some number “x”, whose value is 3. With complex numbers, there’s a gotcha: there’s two dimensions to talk about. When writing
9f12b20e8fb4.target: 在常规代数中，我们常说“x = 3”就万事大吉了——有一个数“x”，其值为3。对于复数，有个坑：有两个维度需要讨论。当写

8549366b7284.source: we’re saying there’s a number “z” with two parts: 3 (the real part) and 4i (imaginary part). It is a bit strange how “one” number can have two parts, but we’ve been doing this for a while. We often write:
8549366b7284.target: 时，我们是在说有一个数“z”有两个部分：3（实部）和4i（虚部）。一个“单一”数字有两个部分有点奇怪，但我们已经这样做很久了。我们经常写：

baa2cd55fed0.source: and it doesn’t bother us that a single number “y” has both an integer part (3) and a fractional part (.4 or 4/10). Y is a combination of the two. Complex numbers are similar: they have their real and imaginary parts “contained” in a single variable (shorthand is often Re and Im).
baa2cd55fed0.target: 而这并不会困扰我们，因为一个数字“y”同时有整数部分(3)和小数部分(.4或4/10)。Y是两者的组合。复数类似：它们将实部和虚部“包含”在一个变量中（常简写为Re和Im）。

9229943f21c2.source: Unfortunately, we don’t have nice notation like (3.4) to “merge” the parts into a single number. I had an idea to write the imaginary part vertically, in fading ink, but it wasn’t very popular. So we’ll stick to the “a + bi” format.
9229943f21c2.target: 不幸的是，我们没有像(3.4)这样好的符号来将各部分“合并”成一个数字。我曾有想法将虚部垂直写在渐淡的墨水中，但并不流行。所以我们还是用“a + bi”的格式。

c9206542cda0.source: Because complex numbers use two independent axes, we find size (magnitude) using the Pythagorean Theorem:
c9206542cda0.target: 因为复数使用两个独立的轴，我们通过勾股定理求大小（模）：

3a66665ee611.source: So, a number z = 3 + 4i would have a magnitude of 5. The shorthand for “magnitude of z” is this: |z|
3a66665ee611.target: 所以，数z = 3 + 4i的模为5。“z的模”的简写是：|z|

e08918a2d17d.source: See how it looks like the absolute value sign? Well, in a way, it is. Magnitude measures a complex number’s “distance from zero”, just like absolute value measures a negative number’s “distance from zero”.
e08918a2d17d.target: 注意到它看起来像绝对值符号吗？实际上，在某种程度上是的。模衡量复数“到零的距离”，就像绝对值衡量负数“到零的距离”一样。

5a6cc562a32f.source: can be thought of as “sliding” by a number. Addition with complex numbers is similar, but we can slide in two dimensions (real or imaginary). For example:
5a6cc562a32f.target: 可以看作是在数轴上“滑动”。复数的加法类似，但我们可以沿两个维度（实部或虚部）滑动。例如：

8bd66258312d.source: Again, this is a visual interpretation of how “independent components” are combined: we track the real and imaginary parts separately.
8bd66258312d.target: 同样，这是对“独立分量”如何组合的一种可视化解释：我们分别跟踪实部和虚部。

fb3a4d1cf60c.source: Subtraction is the reverse of addition — it’s sliding in the
fb3a4d1cf60c.target: 减法是加法的逆运算——它是在

6cfc806f386c.source: direction. Subtracting (1 + i) is the same as adding -1 * (1 + i), or adding (-1 – i).
6cfc806f386c.target: 方向上滑动。减去 (1 + i) 等同于加上 -1 * (1 + i)，也就是加上 (-1 - i)。

614a3d1b5b37.source: Here’s where the math gets interesting. When we multiply two complex numbers (x and y) to get z:
614a3d1b5b37.target: 这里就是数学变得有趣的地方。当我们用两个复数 (x 和 y) 相乘得到 z 时：

84b999b335db.source: That is, the angle of z is the sum of the angles of x and y, and the magnitude of z is the product of the magnitudes. Believe it or not, the magic of complex numbers makes the math work out!
84b999b335db.target: 也就是说，z 的角度是 x 和 y 的角度之和，z 的模是 x 和 y 的模之积。信不信由你，复数的魔力确保了这一数学结果成立！

0e28dea98c20.source: Multiplying by the magnitude (size) makes sense — we’re used to that happening in regular multiplication (3 × 4 means you multiply 3 by 4′s size). The reason the angle addition works is more detailed, and we’ll save it for another time. (Curious? Find the sine and cosine
0e28dea98c20.target: 乘以模（大小）是合理的——我们在普通乘法中已经习惯了这一点（3 × 4 表示用 3 乘以 4 的大小）。角度加法的原理更为复杂，我们改天再讨论。（好奇吗？找出正弦和余弦

e0e2325745b4.source: and compare them to how (a + bi) * (c + di) get multiplied out).
e0e2325745b4.target: 并将它们与 (a + bi) * (c + di) 的乘法展开方式进行比较。）

ddaf76aec79c.target: 来看一个例子：让我们将 z = 3 + 4i 与自身相乘。在进行全部数学运算之前，我们知道以下几点：

27cb48b88a65.source: Nice. While we can always do the math out, the intuition about rotations and scaling helps us check the result. If the resulting angle was less than 90 (“forward and up”, for example), or the resulting magnitude not 25, we’d know there was a mistake in our math.
27cb48b88a65.target: 很好。虽然我们总是可以计算出来，但关于旋转和缩放（scaling）的直觉有助于我们检验结果。如果得到的角度小于 90°（例如“向前向上”），或者模不是 25，我们就知道数学计算中出了错。

7fa1ae7787aa.source: Division is the opposite of multiplication, just like subtraction is the opposite of addition. When dividing complex numbers (x divided by y), we:
7fa1ae7787aa.target: 除法是乘法的逆运算，就像减法是加法的逆运算一样。当复数相除时（x 除以 y），我们：

f54cf2b0a12d.source: Sounds good. Now let’s try to do it:
f54cf2b0a12d.target: 听起来不错。现在让我们试试：

1060f629ca95.source: Hrm. Where to start? How do we actually
1060f629ca95.target: 嗯……从何入手？我们到底如何

10583b0028a2.source: the division? Dividing regular algebraic numbers gives me the creeps, let alone weirdness of i (
10583b0028a2.target: 进行除法？普通代数式的除法就让我头疼，更不用说 i 的古怪了（

dfc653f604b8.target: 哎呀。）幸运的是，有一个捷径。

dc29739b3fd7.source: Our first goal of division is to subtract angles. How do we do this? Multiply by the opposite angle! This will “add” a negative angle, doing an angle subtraction.
dc29739b3fd7.target: 除法的主要目标是减去角度。如何做到？乘以相反角度！这将“加上”一个负角度，从而实现角度相减。

95eb1af22e05.source: Instead of z = a + bi, think about a number z* = a – bi, called the “complex conjugate”. It has the same real part, but is the “mirror image” in the imaginary dimension. The conjugate or “imaginary reflection” has the same magnitude, but the opposite angle!
95eb1af22e05.target: 先不要考虑 z = a + bi，而是考虑一个数 z* = a – bi，称为“复共轭(complex conjugate)”。它具有相同的实部，但在虚数维度上是“镜像”。共轭或“虚数反射”具有相同的大小，但角度相反！

056f22ebef5c.source: So, multiplying by a – bi is the same as
056f22ebef5c.target: 所以，乘以 a – bi 相当于

199f7a073fee.source: Complex conjugates are indicated by a star (z*) or bar above the number — mathematicians love to argue about these notational conventions. Either way, the conjugate is the complex number with the imaginary part flipped:
199f7a073fee.target: 复共轭用星号(z*)或数字上方的横线表示——数学家们喜欢争论这些符号惯例。无论哪种方式，共轭就是虚部取反的复数：

1fa7df3e8b93.source: Note that b doesn’t have to be “negative”. If z = 3 – 4i, then z* = 3 + 4i.
1fa7df3e8b93.target: 注意 b 不一定是“负数”。如果 z = 3 – 4i，那么 z* = 3 + 4i。

b3d23af6f140.source: What happens if you multiply by the conjugate? What is z times z*? Without thinking, think about this:
b3d23af6f140.target: 如果乘以共轭会发生什么？z 乘以 z* 等于多少？不用计算，思考一下：

0d3b4bd42dd1.source: So we take 1 (a real number), add angle(z), and add angle (z*). But this last angle is negative — it’s a subtraction! So our final result should be a real number, since we’ve canceled the angles. The number should be |z|^2 since we scaled by the size twice.
0d3b4bd42dd1.target: 所以我们取 1（一个实数），加上 angle(z)，再加上 angle(z*)。但最后这个角度是负的——是减法！所以最终结果应该是一个实数，因为我们抵消了角度。这个数应该是 |z|^2，因为我们两次乘以了大小。

075c1c4e00b6.source: We got a real number, like we expected! The math fans can try the algebra also:
075c1c4e00b6.target: 我们得到了一个实数，正如预期！数学爱好者也可以尝试代数运算：

8c2d093740c6.source: Tada! The result has no imaginary parts, and is the magnitude squared. Understanding complex conjugates as a “negative rotation” lets us predict these results in a different way.
8c2d093740c6.target: 瞧！结果没有虚部，而且是大小的平方。将复共轭理解为“负旋转”让我们可以用不同的方式预测这些结果。

804c90747be0.source: When multiplying by a conjugate z*, we scale by the magnitude |z*|. To reverse this effect we can divide by |z|, and to actually
804c90747be0.target: 当乘以共轭 z* 时，我们按大小 |z*| 缩放。要逆转这个效果，我们可以除以 |z|，而要实际

5f3f4c588e1a.source: by |z| we have to divide again. All in all, we have to divide by |z| * |z| to the original number after multiplying by the conjugate.
5f3f4c588e1a.target: 除以 |z| 我们必须再除一次。总之，乘以共轭后，要恢复原数，我们必须除以 |z| * |z|。

d56fce9f0e76.source: I’ve been sidestepping the division, and here’s the magic. If we want to do
d56fce9f0e76.target: 我一直回避除法，但这里就是魔法所在。如果我们想做

bfde7b3e48e9.source: The answer, using this approach, is:

c53533d1a012.source: The more traditional “plug and chug” method is to multiply top and bottom by the complex conjugate:
c53533d1a012.target: 更传统的“代入计算”方法是分子分母同时乘以复共轭：

bb72f0f69166.source: We’re traditionally taught to “just multiply both sides by the complex conjugate” without questioning what complex division really means. But not today.
bb72f0f69166.target: 我们传统上被教导“只需两边乘以复共轭”，而不去质疑复数除法的真正含义。但今天不一样。

db504def2c03.source: We know what’s happening: division is subtracting an angle and shrinking the magnitude. By multiplying top and bottom by the conjugate, we subtract by the angle of (1-i), which happens to make the denominator a real number (it’s no coincidence, since it’s the exact opposite angle). We scaled both the top and bottom by the same amount, so the effects cancel. The result is to turn division into a multiplication in the numerator.
db504def2c03.target: 我们知道发生了什么：除法是减去一个角度并缩小大小。通过分子分母同时乘以共轭，我们减去 (1-i) 的角度，这恰好使分母成为一个实数（这并非巧合，因为这是完全相反的角度）。我们以相同的量缩放分子和分母，效果抵消。结果是将除法转化为分子上的乘法。

41cba6e3f397.source: Both approaches work (you’re usually taught the second), but it’s nice to have one to double-check the other.
41cba6e3f397.target: 两种方法都有效（通常你学的是第二种），但有一种方法可以相互验证是很好的。

36a3e408d6bd.source: Now that we understand the conjugate, there’s a few properties to consider:
36a3e408d6bd.target: 既然我们已经理解了共轭，还有几个性质需要考虑：

5909578f8c07.source: The first should make sense. Adding two numbers and “reflecting” (conjugating) the result, is the same as adding the reflections. Another way to think about it: sliding two numbers then taking the opposite, is the same as sliding both times in the opposite direction.
5909578f8c07.target: 第一条应该容易理解。将两个数相加然后对结果进行“反射”（共轭），等同于对反射分别相加。另一种思考方式：先滑动两个数再取反，等同于每次都向相反方向滑动。

10cab1371aca.source: The second property is trickier. Sure, the
10cab1371aca.target: 第二条性质更棘手。当然，这个

90e7eb2f6009.target: 而 x* 乘以 y* 表示：

38ae1c4438ba.source: Aha! We get the same angle and magnitude in each case, and we didn’t have to jump into the traditional algebra explanation. Algebra is fine, but it isn’t always the most satisfying explanation.
38ae1c4438ba.target: 啊哈！我们在每种情况下都得到相同的角度和幅度，而且无需陷入传统的代数解释。代数没问题，但并非总是最令人满意的解释。

cc7dbd961ec4.source: The conjugate is a way to “undo” a rotation. Think about it this way:
cc7dbd961ec4.target: 共轭(Conjugate)是一种“撤销”旋转的方式。可以这样理解：

8f36f5d114c4.source: See the conjugate z* as a way to “cancel” the rotation effects of z, just like a negative number “cancels” the effects of addition. One caveat: with conjugates, you need to divide by |z| * |z| to remove the scaling effects as well.
8f36f5d114c4.target: 将共轭 z* 视为“抵消”z 旋转效应的一种方法，就像负数“抵消”加法效应一样。注意：使用共轭时，还需要除以 |z| * |z| 以去除缩放效应。

e0ddef031aed.source: The math here isn’t new, but I never realized
e0ddef031aed.target: 这里的数学并不新鲜，但我从未意识到

82e444f3ca67.source: complex conjugates worked as they did. Why a – bi and not -a + bi? Well, complex conjugates are not a random choice, but a mirror image from the imaginary perspective, with the exact opposite angle.
82e444f3ca67.target: 共轭复数(Complex Conjugate)的工作原理如此。为什么是 a – bi 而不是 -a + bi？嗯，共轭复数不是随机选择的，而是从虚数角度的镜像，具有完全相反的角度。

6ef056d71216.source: Seeing imaginary numbers as rotations gives us a
6ef056d71216.target: 将虚数视为旋转为我们提供了

00b657888de3.source: to approach problems; the “plug and chug” formulas can make intuitive sense, even for a strange topic like complex numbers. Happy math.
00b657888de3.target: 解决问题的方法；即使是像复数这样的奇怪话题，“代入计算”公式也能变得直观易懂。数学愉快。

54867647fdc1.source: On Monday, 1 patient comes in. It's her first day, so she gets 3 units.
54867647fdc1.target: 周一，1名患者进来。这是她的第一天，所以她获得3单位。

5ab6b1351762.source: On Tuesday, the Monday gal gets 2 units (her second day), but two new patients arrive, who get 3 each (2 * 3 = 6). The total is 2 + (2 * 3) = 8 units.
5ab6b1351762.target: 周二，周一的女孩获得2单位（她的第二天），但两名新患者到达，每人获得3单位（2 * 3 = 6）。总计为2 + (2 * 3) = 8单位。

555ea37d51e7.source: On Wednesday, it's trickier: The Monday gal finishes (1 unit, her last day), the Tuesday people get 2 units (2 * 2), and there are 3 new Wednesday people... argh.
555ea37d51e7.target: 周三，更复杂了：周一的女孩结束（1单位，她的最后一天），周二的人获得2单位（2 * 2），还有3名新的周三患者……唉。

5b522cbe4e31.source: In the official definition, you'll see $g(t - \tau)$ instead of $g(- \tau+ t)$. The second version shows the flip ($-\tau$) and slide ($+t$). Writing $g(t - \tau)$ makes it seem like we're interested in the difference between the variables, which confused me.
5b522cbe4e31.target: 在官方定义中，你会看到 $g(t - \tau)$ 而不是 $g(- \tau+ t)$。第二种形式显示了翻转（$-\tau$）和滑动（$+t$）。写成 $g(t - \tau)$ 会让人觉得我们关心变量之间的差异，这曾让我困惑。

ab0592f63105.target: 问题：你每天使用多少药物？嗯，那只是一个简单的乘法：

8e8358b63dc0.source: ) means using the plan with a single day of patients:
8e8358b63dc0.target: ）意味着只使用一天病人的计划：

90d124b2dbf1.source: Let's say the disease mutates and requires a multi-day treatment. You create a new plan:
90d124b2dbf1.target: 假设疾病发生变异，需要多日治疗。你制定了一个新计划：

045cfc748f8c.source: That means 3 units of the cure on the first day, 2 on the second, and 1 on the third. Ok. Given the same patient schedule
045cfc748f8c.target: 这意味着第一天用3个单位的药物，第二天2个，第三天1个。好的。在相同的病人计划下

6bdb8a927e29.source: , what's our medicine usage each day?

bb81bd8e848b.source: Uh... shoot. It's not a quick multiplication:
bb81bd8e848b.target: 呃……糟糕。这不是一个简单的乘法：

a0d98cfe2754.source: The patients are overlapping and it's hard to track. How can we organize this calculation?
a0d98cfe2754.target: 病人相互重叠，很难追踪。我们如何组织这个计算？

93aaef7e198a.source: the patient list, so the first patient is on the right:
93aaef7e198a.target: 病人列表，以便第一个病人在右边：

4f17b123af41.source: Next, imagine we have 3 separate rooms where we apply the proper dose:
4f17b123af41.target: 接下来，想象我们有3个独立的房间，每个房间施加适当的剂量：

b6edad3840b9.source: On your first day, you walk into the first room and get 3 units of medicine. The next day, you walk into room #2 and get 2 units. On the last day, you walk into room #3 and get 1 unit. There's no rooms afterwards, and your treatment is done.
b6edad3840b9.target: 第一天，你走进第一个房间，得到3个单位的药物。第二天，你走进2号房间，得到2个单位。最后一天，你走进3号房间，得到1个单位。之后没有房间，你的治疗结束。

e2da06ad3596.source: To calculate the total medicine usage, line up the patients and walk them through the rooms:
e2da06ad3596.target: 要计算总药物用量，让患者排成一队，依次走过这些房间：

60032e9c90c8.source: On Monday (our first day), we have a single patient in the first room. She gets 3 units, for a total usage of 3. Makes sense, right?
60032e9c90c8.target: 周一（我们的第一天），第一个房间里只有一位患者。她得到3个单位，总用量为3。有道理，对吧？

7f2ca6c45295.source: On Tuesday, everyone takes a step forward:
7f2ca6c45295.target: 周二，每个人都往前一步：

f8073c5b67c4.source: The first patient is now in the second room, and there's 2 new patients in the first room. We multiply each room's dose by the patient count, then combine.
f8073c5b67c4.target: 第一位患者现在在第二个房间，而第一个房间里有2位新患者。我们将每个房间的剂量乘以患者人数，然后相加。

937b0cec2b5e.source: Every day we just walk the list forward:
937b0cec2b5e.target: 每天我们都让列表向前滑动：

d91af2314ea2.source: Whoa! It's intricate, but we figured it out, right? We can find the usage for any day by reversing the list, sliding it to the desired day, and combining the doses.
d91af2314ea2.target: 哇！这很复杂，但我们搞清楚了，对吧？我们可以通过反转列表、将其滑动到所需日期并组合剂量来找到任何一天的用量。

79c94a648203.source: Let's use convolution to estimate ventilator usage for incoming patients.
79c94a648203.target: 让我们用卷积(Convolution)来估计新入院患者的呼吸机使用量。

3ac10c797329.source: With these numbers, we expect a max ventilator use of 2.2k in 2 weeks:
3ac10c797329.target: 根据这些数字，我们预计在2周内最大呼吸机使用量为2.2千：

b2310249df45.source: The convolution drops to 0 after 9 weeks because the patient list has run out. In this example, we're interested in the peak value the convolution hits, not the long-term total.
b2310249df45.target: 卷积在9周后降至0，因为患者名单已用完。在这个例子中，我们关心的是卷积达到的峰值，而不是长期总量。

e506b31df4ce.source: Other plans to convolve may be drug doses, vaccine appointments (one today, another a month from now), reinfections, and other complex interactions.
e506b31df4ce.target: 其他需要进行卷积(Convolve)的场景可能包括药物剂量、疫苗接种预约（今天一剂，一个月后另一剂）、再感染以及其他复杂的相互作用。

4c56ddf82f64.source: The hospital analogy is the mental model I wish I had when learning. Now that we've tried it with actual numbers, let's add the Math Juice and convert the analogy to calculus.
4c56ddf82f64.target: 医院类比是我在学习时所希望拥有的心智模型。既然我们已经用实际数字试过了，现在让我们加入数学要素，将这个类比转化为微积分。

5258491fd167.source: So, what happened in our example? We had a list of patients and a plan. If the plan were simple (single day
5258491fd167.target: 那么，在我们的例子中发生了什么？我们有一个患者列表和一个计划。如果计划很简单（单日

3eb8dc04b000.source: ), regular multiplication would have worked. Because the plan was complex, we had to "convolve" it.
3eb8dc04b000.target: ），那么普通的乘法就可以了。但因为计划复杂，我们不得不对其进行“卷积”(Convolve)。

7e20c9eec91d.target: 现在来一些趣味事实(Fun Facts™)：

c3558d1fa1d6.source: Convolution is written $f * g$, with an asterisk. Yes, an asterisk usually indicates multiplciation, but in advanced calculus class, it indicates a convolution. Regular multiplication is just implied ($fg$).
c3558d1fa1d6.target: 卷积写作 $f * g$，使用星号。是的，星号通常表示乘法，但在高等微积分课程中，它表示卷积。普通乘法只是隐含的（$fg$）。

3e9e650bd017.source: The result of a convolution is a new
3e9e650bd017.target: 卷积的结果是一个新的

5438512cae19.source: that gives the total usage for any day ("What was the total usage on day $t=3$?"). We can graph the convolution over time to see the day-by-day totals.
5438512cae19.target: 它给出了任意一天的总使用量（“第 $t=3$ 天的总使用量是多少？”）。我们可以将卷积随时间绘制成图表，以查看逐日总量。

99c15a8bebe3.target: 患者列表（输入）是 $g(x)$。然而，我们需要

fc2ac6a09ee5.source: this list as we slide it, so the earliest patient (Monday) enters the hospital first (first in, first out). This means we need to use $g(-x)$, the horizontal reflection of $g(x)$.
fc2ac6a09ee5.target: 在滑动列表时反转它，这样最早的患者（周一）先入院（先进先出）。这意味着我们需要使用 $g(-x)$，即 $g(x)$ 的水平镜像。

3835a5667b74.source: Now that we have the reversed list, pick a day to compute ($t = 0, 1, 2...$). To slide our patient list by this much, we use: $g(-x + t)$. That is, we reverse the list ($-x$) and jump to the correct day ($+t$).
3835a5667b74.target: 现在我们有了反转后的列表，选择一个日期进行计算（$t = 0, 1, 2...$）。要将患者列表滑动这么多天，我们使用：$g(-x + t)$。也就是说，我们反转列表（$-x$）并跳到正确的那一天（$+t$）。

4852eefd8ece.target: 我们得到了我们的场景：

aed30432adc0.source: To get the total usage on day $t$, we multiply each patient with the plan, and sum the results (an integral). To account for any possible length, we go from -infinity to +infinity.
aed30432adc0.target: 要得到第 $t$ 天的总使用量，我们将每个患者与计划相乘，并将结果相加（一个积分）。为了考虑任何可能的长度，我们从负无穷到正无穷进行。

96750f6f908d.source: What is a number? A list of digits:

c587a6a0bf2e.source: And what is regular, grade-school multiplication? A digit-by-digit convolution! We sweep one list of digits by the other, multiplying and adding as we go:
c587a6a0bf2e.target: 那么常规的小学乘法是什么？逐位的卷积！我们用一个数字列表扫描另一个，一边乘一边加：

2a26985cdd26.target: 我们可以通过卷积数字列表来执行计算（

1caa27b8b7ea.target: 注意我们预先翻转了其中一个列表（稍后在卷积中会被交换），中间计算有点不同。但是，组合小计得到预期结果。

5a080e646be7.source: Why convolve instead of doing a regular digit-by-digit multiplication? Well, the convolution theorem lets us substitute convolution with Fourier Transforms:
5a080e646be7.target: 为什么要卷积而不是常规的逐位乘法？嗯，卷积定理让我们可以用傅里叶变换替换卷积：

b3c60b497d75.source: The convolution ($f * g$) has complexity $O(n^2)$. We have $n$ positions to process, with $n$ intermediate multiplications at each position.
b3c60b497d75.target: 卷积（$f * g$）的复杂度为 $O(n^2)$。我们有 $n$ 个位置需要处理，每个位置有 $n$ 个中间乘法。

0add00b76a46.source: And the total complexity is: $O(n\log(n)) + O(n\log(n)) + O(n) + O(n\log(n)) = O(n\log(n))$
0add00b76a46.target: 总复杂度为：$O(n\log(n)) + O(n\log(n)) + O(n) + O(n\log(n)) = O(n\log(n))$

9bd524eb54c6.source: , without a simple reason why (discussions involving Taylor series are not simple). Today we’ll uncover what radians really are, and the intuitive reason they make math easier.
9bd524eb54c6.target: ，却没有一个简单的理由（涉及泰勒级数的讨论并不简单）。今天我们将揭示弧度的真正含义，以及它们让数学更简单的直观原因。

437a07c6baef.source: A degree is the amount I, an observer, need to tilt my head to see you, the mover. It’s a tad self-centered, don’t you think?

3963c2a8774a.source: Suppose you saw a friend go running on a large track:
3963c2a8774a.target: 假设你看到一个朋友在一个大跑道上跑步：

71435becce93.source: “Hey Bill, how far did you go?”

7f0d6cd418af.source: “Well, I had a really good pace, I think I went 6 or 7 mile–”
7f0d6cd418af.target: “嗯，我配速很好，大概跑了6到7英里——”

f3c1267ac058.source: “Shuddup. How far did I turn my head to see you move?”
f3c1267ac058.target: “闭嘴。我转动头部看你移动了多少？”

bee25bc1f690.source: “I’ll use small words for you. Me in middle of track. You ran around. How…much…did…I…turn…my…head?”
bee25bc1f690.target: “我用简单的话跟你说。我在跑道中间。你绕着跑。我……转……了……多少……头？”

350eb8c80cff.source: We write equations in terms of “Hey, how far did I turn my head see that planet/pendulum/wheel move?”. I bet you’ve never bothered to think about the pendulum’s feelings, hopes and dreams.
350eb8c80cff.target: 我们写方程时会想：“嘿，我转头多远才能看到那个行星/钟摆/轮子移动？” 我敢打赌你从未费心思考过钟摆的感受、希望和梦想。

2426c9d96180.source: Do you think the equations of physics should be made simple for the mover or observer?
2426c9d96180.target: 你认为物理方程应该为运动者简化还是为观察者简化？

9194e2e70a35.source: Much of physics (and life!) involves leaving your reference frame and seeing things from another’s viewpoint. Instead of wondering how far we tilted our heads,
9194e2e70a35.target: 很多物理学（和生活！）都涉及离开自己的参考系，从他人的视角看问题。与其思考我们倾斜了多远，
