35b8cc275bef.target: 角度以我们倾斜头部的距离来度量。弧度则以

58a61fa5dc5e.source: But absolute distance isn’t that useful, since going 10 miles is a different number of laps depending on the track. So we divide by radius to get a normalized angle:
58a61fa5dc5e.target: 但绝对距离并不那么有用，因为跑10英里在跑道上对应的圈数不同。所以除以半径得到归一化角度：

b6e454f9f7c9.source: or angle in radians (theta) is arc length (s) divided by radius (r).
b6e454f9f7c9.target: 或者弧度角(theta)等于弧长(s)除以半径(r)。

957ca7c36a2c.source: A circle has 360 degrees or 2pi radians — going all the way around is 2 * pi * r / r. So a radian is about 360 /(2 * pi) or 57.3 degrees.
957ca7c36a2c.target: 一个圆有360度或2π弧度——绕一圈的距离是2 * π * r / r。因此1弧度约为360/(2*π)或57.3度。

a3d6026dbbf3.source: Now don’t be like me, memorizing this thinking “Great, another unit. 57.3 degrees is so weird.” Because it is weird when you’re still thinking about you!
a3d6026dbbf3.target: 现在别像我一样，记住这个并认为“太好了，又一个单位。57.3度真奇怪。” 因为当你还在考虑自己时，它确实很奇怪！

3496c00d4c03.source: Moving 1 radian (unit) is a perfectly normal distance to travel. Put another way, our idea of a “clean, 90 degree angle” means the mover goes a very
3496c00d4c03.target: 移动1弧度（单位）是完全正常的距离。换句话说，我们所谓的“干净的90度角”意味着运动者走了非常

0d49103a8478.source: Radians are the empathetic way to do math — a shift from away from head tilting and towards the mover’s perspective.
0d49103a8478.target: 弧度是一种感同身受的数学方式——从倾斜头部转向运动者的视角。

5977c008ec5f.source: Radians are a count of distance in terms of “radius units”, and I think of “radian” as shorthand for that concept.
5977c008ec5f.target: 弧度是以“半径单位”计数的距离，我把“弧度”看作那个概念的简写。

179425e8548b.source: Strictly speaking, radians are just a number like 1.5 or 73, and don’t have any units (in the calculation “radians = distance traveled / radius”, we see length is divided by length, so any units would cancel).
179425e8548b.target: 严格来说，弧度只是一个数字，如1.5或73，没有任何单位（在计算“弧度 = 行进距离 / 半径”中，长度除以长度，任何单位都会约去）。

1d90a28b8356.source: But practically speaking, we’re not math robots, and it helps to think of radians as “distance” traveled on a unit circle.
1d90a28b8356.target: 但实际上，我们不是数学机器人，将弧度看作单位圆上行进的距离是有帮助的。

ee315374fbf0.source: I’m still getting used to thinking in radians. But we encounter the concept of “mover’s distance” quite a bit:
ee315374fbf0.target: 我仍在习惯用弧度思考。但我们经常遇到“运动者距离”的概念：

8579446ae3d6.source: We use “rotations per minute” not “degrees per second” when measuring certain rotational speeds. This is a shift towards the mover’s reference point (“How many laps has it gone?”) and away from an arbitrary degree measure.
8579446ae3d6.target: 在测量某些旋转速度时，我们使用“转每分钟”而非“度每秒”。这是向运动者参考点（“它转了多少圈？”）的转变，脱离了任意度数的度量。

96c2bb508f9b.source: When a satellite orbits the Earth, we understand its speed in “miles per hour”, not “degrees per hour”. Now divide by the distance to the satellite and you get the orbital speed in radians per hour.
96c2bb508f9b.target: 当卫星绕地球运行时，我们理解其速度为“英里每小时”，而非“度每小时”。现在除以到卫星的距离，你就得到了以弧度每小时为单位的轨道速度。

20f8bce30f68.source: Sine, that wonderful function, is defined in terms of
20f8bce30f68.target: 正弦函数，这个奇妙的函数，是根据

53898bdf104f.source: This formula only works when x is in radians! Why? Well, sine is fundamentally related to
53898bdf104f.target: 这个公式只在 x 以弧度为单位时成立！为什么？嗯，正弦从根本上与

202c9daed6e1.source: , not head-tilting. But we’ll save that discussion for another day.
202c9daed6e1.target: 有关，而不是头倾斜。但我们把讨论留到另一天。

48bcf81ba70a.source: Let’s try a real example: you have a bus with wheels of radius 2 meters (it’s a monster truck bus). I’ll say how fast the wheels are turning and you say how fast the bus is moving. Ready?
48bcf81ba70a.target: 让我们尝试一个真实的例子：你有一辆轮子半径为 2 米的公交车（这是一辆怪物卡车公交车）。我说轮子转得有多快，你说公交车移动得有多快。准备好了吗？

8a6be79179d1.source: “The wheels are turning 2000 degrees per second”. You’d think:

8391ff913b63.source: Ok. Now imagine a car with wheels of radius 2 meters (also a monster). “The car wheels are turning 6 radians per second”. You’d think:
8391ff913b63.target: 好了。现在想象一辆轮子半径为 2 米的汽车（也是怪物）。“汽车轮子每秒转 6 弧度”。你会想：

64e172308196.source: Wow -- the car was easier to figure out than the bus! No crazy formulas, no
64e172308196.target: 哇——汽车比公交车更容易计算！没有复杂的公式，没有

25c4819137e2.source: to convert rotational speed to linear speed. All because radians speak in terms of the mover.
25c4819137e2.target: 将旋转速度转换为线速度。全因弧度用移动者的术语说话。

91cae8d73b98.source: The reverse is easy too. Suppose you’re cruising 90 feet per second on the highway (60 miles per hour) on your 24″ inch rims (radius 1 foot). How fast are the wheels turning?
91cae8d73b98.target: 反过来也很容易。假设你在高速公路上以每秒 90 英尺（每小时 60 英里）行驶，你的轮辋是 24 英寸（半径 1 英尺）。轮子转得有多快？

ad787d4ccde4.source: Well, 90 feet per second / 1 foot radius = 90 radians per second.
ad787d4ccde4.target: 好吧，每秒 90 英尺 / 1 英尺半径 = 每秒 90 弧度。

7fba5e321013.source: That was easy. I suspect rappers sing about 24″ rims for this very reason.
7fba5e321013.target: 那很简单。我怀疑说唱歌手唱 24 英寸轮辋正是因为这个原因。

73b79cf5c46b.source: Time for a beefier example. Calculus is
73b79cf5c46b.target: 是时候看一个更强大的例子了。微积分是关于

b163b60bebf6.source: , and one is what happens when numbers get really big or really small.
b163b60bebf6.target: ，其中之一是当数字变得非常大或非常小时会发生什么。

794cf864a8de.source: Choose a number of degrees (x), and put sin(x) into your calculator:
794cf864a8de.target: 选择一个度数 (x)，然后将 sin(x) 输入计算器：

1b36a597fcad.source: When you make x small, like .01, sin(x) gets small as well. And the ratio of sin(x)/x seems to be about .017 — what does that mean? Even stranger, what does it mean to multiply or divide by a degree? Can you have square or cubic degrees?
1b36a597fcad.target: 当你让 x 很小，比如 .01，sin(x) 也会变小。而 sin(x)/x 的比值似乎是 .017 — 这意味着什么？更奇怪的是，乘以或除以一个度数意味着什么？你能有平方度或立方度吗？

725be069bb4f.source: Radians to the rescue! Knowing they refer to distance traveled (they’re not just a ratio!), we can interpret the equation this way:
725be069bb4f.target: 弧度来拯救！知道它们指的是行进距离（它们不只是一个比值！），我们可以这样解释这个方程：

359e6ee21902.source: So sin(x)/x is the ratio of how high you are to how far you’ve gone: the amount of energy that went in an “upward” direction. If you move vertically, that ratio is 100%. If you move horizontally, that ratio is 0%.
359e6ee21902.target: 所以 sin(x)/x 是你有多高与你走了多远的比值：能量进入“向上”方向的部分。如果你垂直移动，这个比值是 100%。如果你水平移动，这个比值是 0%。

7bc58db93d92.source: When something moves a tiny amount, such as 0 to 1 degree from our perspective, it’s basically going straight up. If you go an even smaller amount, from 0 to .00001 degrees, it’s
7bc58db93d92.target: 当某物移动很小，比如从我们的角度看 0 到 1 度，它基本上是直线向上。如果你移动更小的量，从 0 到 .00001 度，它

0d89bf22ede9.source: going straight up. The distance traveled (x) is very close to the height (sin(x)).
0d89bf22ede9.target: 是直线向上。行进距离 (x) 非常接近高度 (sin(x))。

8d86aec0d6e3.source: As x shrinks, the ratio gets closer to 100% — more motion is straight up. Radians help us see, intuitively, why sin(x)/x approaches 1 as x gets tiny. We’re just nudging along a tiny amount in a vertical direction. By the way, this also explains why sin(x) ~ x for small numbers.
8d86aec0d6e3.target: 随着x缩小，比率越来越接近100%——运动更接近垂直向上。弧度帮助我们直观理解，为什么当x很小时sin(x)/x趋于1。我们只是在垂直方向上微微移动一点。顺便说一句，这也解释了为什么对于小的数，sin(x)~x。

a7c736176184.target: 当然，你可以用微积分严格

db7e9989fc12.source: this using calculus, but the radian intuition helps you
db7e9989fc12.target: 证明这一点，但弧度直觉有助于你

f351f27d477a.source: Remember, these relationships only work when measuring angles with radians. With degrees, you’re comparing your height on a circle (sin(x)) with how far some observer tilted their head (x degrees), and it gets ugly fast.
f351f27d477a.target: 记住，这些关系只在用弧度测量角度时成立。用度数时，你是在比较你在圆上的高度(sin(x))与某个观察者转头多远(x度)，结果很快就会变得难看了。

8137cf667a5d.source: Degrees have their place: in our own lives, we’re the focal point and want to see how things affect us. How much do I tilt my telescope, spin my snowboard, or turn my steering wheel?
8137cf667a5d.target: 度数有它们的用途：在我们自己的生活中，我们是焦点，想看看事物如何影响我们。我倾斜望远镜、旋转滑雪板或转动方向盘多少？

46dcf84c6c95.source: With natural laws, we’re an observer describing the motion of others. Radians are about them, not us. It took me many years to realize that:
46dcf84c6c95.target: 对于自然法则，我们是描述他人运动的观察者。弧度是关于它们，而不是我们。我花了很多年才意识到：

24cbb7f86ef4.source: Even angles can be seen from more than one viewpoint, and understanding radians makes math and physics equations more intuitive. Happy math.
24cbb7f86ef4.target: 甚至角度也可以从多个视角来看待，理解弧度让数学和物理方程更直观。祝数学愉快。

1820e461986c.source: Example: Find the sine of angle x.
1820e461986c.target: 示例：求角 x 的正弦值。

bfc3a9342da7.source: Example: Can you make it to shore?

0bf64e5986b1.source: Visualize a dome, a wall, and a ceiling
0bf64e5986b1.target: 想象一个穹顶、一面墙和一个天花板

3df5d7a45c47.target: 对应三种形状(to the three shapes)

2484d39ef034.source: The armspan (fingertip to fingertip) is approximately the height
2484d39ef034.target: 臂展（指尖到指尖）大约等于身高

206e92784c86.target: 一个头宽约等于五个眼宽

4a09ae4613cb.target: 成年人身高约等于八个头高

8eac4080a0ce.source: sine(x) = sin(x) = height of the screen, hanging like a sign
8eac4080a0ce.target: sine(x) = sin(x) = 屏幕高度，像标志一样悬挂

960f616d79a7.source: cosine(x) = cos(x) = distance to the screen along the ground [“cos” ~ how “close”]
960f616d79a7.target: cosine(x) = cos(x) = 沿地面到屏幕的距离 [“cos” ~ 有多“近”]

16fe9236c338.source: the hypotenuse, the distance to the top of the screen, is always the same
16fe9236c338.target: 斜边，即到屏幕顶端的距离，始终保持不变

867bc1561feb.source: tangent(x) = tan(x) = height of screen on the wall
867bc1561feb.target: tangent(x) = tan(x) = 墙上屏幕的高度

1e03e4ce2e5c.source: distance to screen: 1 (the screen is always the same distance along the ground, right?)
1e03e4ce2e5c.target: 到屏幕的距离：1（屏幕沿地面总是相同的距离，对吗？）

e3b138b6f349.source: secant(x) = sec(x) = the “ladder distance” to the screen
e3b138b6f349.target: secant(x) = sec(x) = 到屏幕的“梯子距离”

653c8cb0b3f8.source: Secant starts at 1 (ladder on the floor to the wall) and grows from there
653c8cb0b3f8.target: 正割(Secant)从1开始（梯子在地板上靠墙），然后逐渐增大

4fb9d7b48d22.source: Secant is always longer than tangent. The leaning ladder used to put up the screen must be longer than the screen itself, right? (At enormous sizes, when the ladder is nearly vertical, they’re close. But secant is always a smidge longer.)
4fb9d7b48d22.target: 正割(Secant)总是比正切(Tangent)长。用来挂屏幕的倾斜梯子必须比屏幕本身长，对吗？（在极大尺寸时，当梯子几乎垂直时，它们很接近。但正割总是稍长一点。）

d7961c6a532f.source: cotangent(x) = cot(x) = how far the ceiling extends before we connect
d7961c6a532f.target: cotangent(x) = cot(x) = 天花板在我们连接之前延伸了多远

875ce8147743.source: cosecant(x) = csc(x) = how long we walk on the ramp
875ce8147743.target: cosecant(x) = csc(x) = 我们在斜坡上走了多远

94a9da329fb4.source: the vertical distance traversed is always 1
94a9da329fb4.target: 垂直穿过的距离始终为1

c21be4442b2e.source: If you pick an angle of 0, your ramp is flat (infinite) and never reachers the ceiling. Bummer.
c21be4442b2e.target: 如果你选择0度角，你的斜坡是平坦的（无穷大），永远碰不到天花板。真遗憾。

3919ac2072cd.source: The shortest “ramp” is when you point 90-degrees straight up. The cotangent is 0 (we didn’t move along the ceiling) and the cosecant is 1 (the “ramp length” is at the minimum).
3919ac2072cd.target: 最短的“斜坡”是当你垂直向上指向90度时。余切为0（我们没有沿着天花板移动），余割为1（“斜坡长度”达到最小值）。

922dfeb60a7d.source: Trig explains the anatomy of “math-made” objects, such as circles and repeating cycles
922dfeb60a7d.target: 三角函数解释了“数学制造”的物体（如圆和重复周期）的解剖结构。

081394e48dc1.source: The dome/wall/ceiling analogy shows the connections between the trig functions
081394e48dc1.target: 穹顶/墙/天花板类比展示了三角函数之间的联系。

19a954bfb46c.source: Trig functions return percentages, that we apply to our specific scenario
19a954bfb46c.target: 三角函数返回百分比，我们将其应用于特定场景。

99491039f01e.target: 动机：三角函数是解剖学

eba5446ec7f5.target: 提示：三角函数值是百分比

b398927c99d6.target: 总结：我们应该记住什么？

2627ec8911a8.source: Appendix: The Original Definition Of Tangent

280ba1eddc9d.source: What’s with this guy? Oh, the naked-man-on-my-wall incident…
280ba1eddc9d.target: 这家伙怎么了？哦，是我墙上那个裸男事件……

034c7084048c.target: 休伯特的反余弦大全，第三版

ebdcf10e5592.source: TOA explains the tangent about as well as $x^2 + y^2 = r^2$ describes a circle. Sure, if you’re a math robot, an equation is enough. The rest of us, with organic brains half-dedicated to vision processing, seem to enjoy imagery. And “TOA” evokes the stunning beauty of an abstract ratio.
ebdcf10e5592.target: TOA解释正切（tangent）就像$x^2 + y^2 = r^2$描述圆一样。当然，如果你是个数学机器人，一个方程就足够了。我们其余的人，拥有半专用于视觉处理的有机大脑，似乎喜欢图像。而“TOA”唤起了抽象比率的惊人美感。

7b02821d7878.source: I think you deserve better, and here’s what made trig click for me.
7b02821d7878.target: 我认为你值得更好的，以下就是让我理解三角学的原因。

d703cb3ee35f.source: Imagine Bob The Alien visits Earth to study our species.
d703cb3ee35f.target: 想象一下外星人鲍勃（Bob The Alien）访问地球研究我们的物种。

b9c3e05b847f.source: Without new words, humans are hard to describe: “There’s a sphere at the top, which gets scratched occasionally” or “Two elongated cylinders appear to provide locomotion”.
b9c3e05b847f.target: 没有新的词汇，人类很难描述：“顶部有一个球体，偶尔被刮擦”，或者“两个拉长的圆柱体似乎提供运动”。

5702c77332d9.source: After creating specific terms for anatomy, Bob might jot down typical
5702c77332d9.target: 在创建了解剖学的具体术语后，鲍勃可能会记下典型的

b72535bcb040.source: Well, when Bob finds a jacket, he can pick it up, stretch out the arms, and estimate the owner’s height. And head size. And eye width. One fact is linked to a variety of conclusions.
b72535bcb040.target: 嗯，当鲍勃找到一件夹克时，他可以拿起来，伸展袖子，估计主人的身高。还有头部大小。还有眼间距。一个事实联系到多种结论。

542f6ee26411.source: Even better, human biology explains human thinking. Tables have legs, organizations have heads, crime bosses have muscle. Our biology offers ready-made analogies that appear in man-made creations.
542f6ee26411.target: 更好的是，人类生物学解释了人类的思维。桌子有腿，组织有头，犯罪头目有肌肉。我们的生物学提供了现成的类比，这些类比出现在人造物中。

35fd8d52a3b8.source: are Bob the alien, studying creatures in math-land!
35fd8d52a3b8.target: 你是外星人鲍勃，在研究数学之地的生物！

12fa9e3fca03.source: Generic words like “triangle” aren’t overly useful. But labeling sine, cosine, and hypotenuse helps us notice deeper connections. And scholars might study haversine, exsecant and
12fa9e3fca03.target: 像“三角形”这样的通用词汇不太有用。但给正弦（sine）、余弦（cosine）和斜边（hypotenuse）命名有助于我们注意到更深层的联系。学者们可能会研究半正矢（haversine）、外正割（exsecant）和

b0078d3436ad.source: , like biologists who find a link between your tibia and clavicle.
b0078d3436ad.target: 就像生物学家发现你胫骨和锁骨之间的关联一样。

cbb6ad20290d.source: And because triangles show up in circles…
cbb6ad20290d.target: 而且因为三角形出现在圆中……

c7566c197ea5.source: …and circles appear in cycles, our triangle terminology helps describe repeating patterns!
c7566c197ea5.target: ……而圆出现在周期中，我们的三角形术语有助于描述重复的模式！

fb6735194959.source: Trig is the anatomy book for “math-made” objects. If we can find a metaphorical triangle, we’ll get an armada of conclusions for free.
fb6735194959.target: 三角学是“数学制造”物体的解剖学书籍。如果我们能找到一个隐喻的三角形，我们将免费获得一大群结论。

7c461b050273.source: Instead of staring at triangles by themselves, like a caveman frozen in ice, imagine them in a scenario, hunting that mammoth.
7c461b050273.target: 与其像冰封的穴居人一样独自盯着三角形，不如想象它们在一个场景中，猎杀那头猛犸象。

f187c6f87538.source: Pretend you’re in the middle of your dome, about to hang up a movie screen. You point to some angle “x”, and that’s where the screen will hang.
f187c6f87538.target: 假装你站在你的穹顶中央，正准备挂上一块电影屏幕。你指向某个角度“x”，屏幕就会挂在那里。

8dd6d6b6fe54.source: The angle you point at determines:

073e44bcb5c7.source: Want the biggest screen possible? Point straight up. It’s at the center, on top of your head, but it’s
073e44bcb5c7.target: 想要尽可能大的屏幕？直接指向上方。它就在你头顶正中心，但是——

1eb08dbfc098.source: Want the screen the furthest away? Sure. Point straight across, 0 degrees. The screen has “0 height” at this position, and it’s far away, like you asked.
1eb08dbfc098.target: 想要屏幕最远？没问题。水平指向，0度。此时屏幕高度为“0”，并且如你所愿，它很远。

2563b59dfe6c.source: The height and distance move in opposite directions: bring the screen closer, and it gets taller.
2563b59dfe6c.target: 高度和距离朝相反方向变化：屏幕越近，高度越大。

e807a1cc8985.source: Nobody ever told me in my years of schooling:
e807a1cc8985.target: 上学这么多年，从来没人告诉我：

0a5e0f1b97e6.source: . They vary from +100% to 0 to -100%, or max positive to nothing to max negative.
0a5e0f1b97e6.target: 它们从+100%到0再到-100%，即从最大正值到零再到最大负值。

705b6958566e.source: Let’s say I paid \$14 in tax. You have no idea if that’s expensive. But if I say I paid 95% in tax, you know I’m getting ripped off.
705b6958566e.target: 假设我交了14美元的税。你无法判断这贵不贵。但如果我说交了95%的税，你就知道我被宰了。

bac28f895583.source: An absolute height isn’t helpful, but if your sine value is .95, I know you’re almost at the top of your dome. Pretty soon you’ll hit the max, then start coming down again.
bac28f895583.target: 绝对高度没有意义，但如果你的正弦值为0.95，我就知道你几乎在穹顶顶部。很快你就会达到最大值，然后开始下降。

f662a9859ff8.source: How do we compute the percentage? Simple: divide the current value by the maximum possible (the radius of the dome, aka the hypotenuse).
f662a9859ff8.target: 如何计算百分比？很简单：用当前值除以最大可能值（穹顶的半径，也就是斜边）。

6093446d06a6.source: why we’re told “Sine = Opposite / Hypotenuse”. It’s to get a percentage! A better wording is “Sine is your height, as a percentage of the hypotenuse”. (Sine becomes negative if your angle points “underground”. Cosine becomes negative when your angle points backwards.)
6093446d06a6.target: 这就是为什么我们被告知“正弦 = 对边 / 斜边”。这是为了得到一个百分比！更好的表述是“正弦是你的高度，相对于斜边的百分比”。（如果你指向“地下”，正弦会变为负值。当你指向后方时，余弦变为负值。）

4f785436546c.source: Let’s simplify the calculation by assuming we’re on the unit circle (radius 1). Now we can skip the division by 1 and just say sine = height.
4f785436546c.target: 我们假设在单位圆（半径为1）上计算来简化。现在我们可以省去除法，直接说正弦等于高度。

f02ae9851e0d.source: Every circle is really the unit circle, scaled up or down to a different size. So work out the connections on the unit circle and apply the results to your particular scenario.
f02ae9851e0d.target: 每个圆实际上都是单位圆按比例缩放的结果。因此，先理清单位圆上的关系，再将结果应用到你的具体场景中。

800a393afb7c.source: Try it out: plug in an angle and see what percent of the height and width it reaches:
800a393afb7c.target: 试试看：输入一个角度，看看它达到的高度和宽度的百分比：

98a518084c03.source: The growth pattern of sine isn’t an even line. The first 45 degrees cover 70% of the height, and the final 10 degrees (from 80 to 90) only cover 2%.
98a518084c03.target: 正弦函数的增长模式并非一条直线。前45度覆盖了70%的高度，而最后10度（从80到90）只覆盖了2%。

b1b317b644e3.source: This should make sense: at 0 degrees, you’re moving nearly vertical, but as you get to the top of the dome, your height changes level off.
b1b317b644e3.target: 这应该可以理解：在0度时，你几乎垂直向上移动，但当到达穹顶顶部时，高度变化趋于平缓。

49d78edb2421.source: One day your neighbor puts up a wall
49d78edb2421.target: 有一天，你的邻居在你的穹顶旁竖起了一堵墙

ecfe75d059bd.source: to your dome. Ack, your view! Your resale value!
ecfe75d059bd.target: 。天啊，你的视野！你的房产价值！

3bbb59bfc54b.source: But can we make the best of a bad situation?
3bbb59bfc54b.target: 但我们能否在糟糕的情况下做到最好呢？

b1196dadbba7.source: Sure. What if we hang our movie screen on the wall? You point at an angle (x) and figure out:
b1196dadbba7.target: 当然。如果我们把电影屏幕挂在墙上呢？你指向一个角度 (x) 并计算出：

a8d51f73eb2f.source: We have some fancy new vocab terms. Imagine seeing the Vitruvian “TAN GENTleman” projected on the wall. You climb the ladder, making sure you can “SEE, CAN’T you?”. (Yeah, he’s naked… won’t forget the analogy now, will you?)
a8d51f73eb2f.target: 我们有一些花哨的新词汇。想象一下，你看到维特鲁威的“正切绅士”投影在墙上。你爬上梯子，确保你能“看到，难道你看不见吗？”（是的，他是裸体的……现在你不会忘记这个类比了，对吧？）

79a5679dbb1f.source: Let’s notice a few things about tangent, the height of the screen.
79a5679dbb1f.target: 让我们注意一下切线（即屏幕高度）的几个特点。

70c179af0441.source: It starts at 0, and goes infinitely high. You can keep pointing higher and higher on the wall, to get an infinitely large screen! (That’ll cost ya.)
70c179af0441.target: 它从 0 开始，并趋向无穷大。你可以一直指向墙上更高的位置，得到一个无限大的屏幕！（那可得花不少钱。）

9051201c8c65.source: Tangent is just a bigger version of sine! It’s never smaller, and while sine “tops off” as the dome curves in, tangent keeps growing.
9051201c8c65.target: 切线只是正弦的放大版！它永远不会更小，而且当正弦随着穹顶弯曲而“封顶”时，切线却在继续增长。

bf916eabdb77.source: How about secant, the ladder distance?

b0e4ac23857c.source: . If you’re pointing at a 50-degree angle, tan(50) = 1.19. Your screen is 19% larger than the distance to the wall (the radius of the dome).
b0e4ac23857c.target: 。如果你指向一个 50 度的角度，tan(50) = 1.19。你的屏幕比到墙的距离（穹顶半径）大 19%。

11bd2cd25d82.source: (Plug in x=0 and check your intuition that tan(0) = 0, and sec(0) = 1.)
11bd2cd25d82.target: （代入 x=0 并验证你的直觉：tan(0)=0，sec(0)=1。）

127dc5cbda86.source: Amazingly enough, your neighbor now decides to build a ceiling on top of your dome, far into the horizon. (
127dc5cbda86.target: 令人惊讶的是，你的邻居现在决定在你的穹顶上方建造一个天花板，一直延伸到地平线。（

69627f62bc9a.source: Well, time to build a ramp to the ceiling, and have a little chit chat. You pick an angle to build and work out:
69627f62bc9a.target: 好吧，是时候建一个坡道到天花板，然后聊聊天了。你选择一个角度来建造，并计算出：

7e18c92fc8e3.source: Tangent/secant describe the wall, and COtangent and COsecant describe the ceiling.
7e18c92fc8e3.target: 正切/正割描述墙，而余切和余割描述天花板。

0614be527642.source: A short time ago I had
0614be527642.target: 不久前，我对余割有一些

c139b9a8daf6.source: “intuitive conclusions” about the cosecant. But with the dome/wall/ceiling metaphor, here’s what we see:
c139b9a8daf6.target: “直观结论”。但借助穹顶/墙/天花板的比喻，我们看到的是：

e66b24023b64.source: Whoa, it’s the same triangle, just scaled to reach the wall and ceiling. We have vertical parts (sine, tangent), horizontal parts (cosine, cotangent), and “hypotenuses” (secant, cosecant). (Note: the labels show where each item “goes up to”. Cosecant is the full distance from you to the ceiling.)
e66b24023b64.target: 哇，这是同一个三角形，只是按比例放大到墙和天花板。我们有垂直部分（正弦、正切）、水平部分（余弦、余切）和“斜边”（正割、余割）。（注意：标签显示每个部分“延伸到”哪里。余割是从你到天花板的完整距离。）

a7145004bc7c.source: Now the magic. The triangles have similar facts:
a7145004bc7c.target: 现在神奇之处来了。这些三角形有相似的事实：

a13176487af0.source: ($a^2 + b^2 = c^2$) we see how the sides of each triangle are linked.
a13176487af0.target: （$a^2 + b^2 = c^2$）我们可以看出每个三角形的边是如何关联的。

4a2b1a84156a.source: , ratios like “height to width” must be the same for these triangles. (Intuition: step away from a big triangle. Now it looks smaller in your field of view, but the internal ratios couldn’t have changed.)
4a2b1a84156a.target: ，像“高与宽”这样的比率对于这些三角形必须相同。（直觉：远离一个大三角形。现在它在你的视野中看起来更小，但内部比率不可能改变。）

02a013d310a1.source: This is how we find out “sine/cosine = tangent/1”.
02a013d310a1.target: 这就是我们发现“正弦/余弦 = 正切/1”的方式。

177567b6752c.source: I’d always tried to memorize these facts, when they just jump out at us when visualized. SOH-CAH-TOA is a nice shortcut, but get a real understanding first!
177567b6752c.target: 我一直试图记住这些事实，然而当可视化时它们就跳出来了。SOH-CAH-TOA 是一个很好的捷径，但首先要真正理解！

933429db5412.source: … don’t over-focus on a single diagram, thinking tangent is always smaller than 1. If we increase the angle, we reach the ceiling before the wall:
933429db5412.target: …不要过度关注单个图表，认为正切总是小于1。如果增大角度，我们在碰到墙壁之前先碰到天花板：

37198b56b926.source: The Pythagorean/similarity connections are always true, but the relative sizes can vary.
37198b56b926.target: 毕达哥拉斯/相似性联系总是成立的，但相对大小可以变化。

c044b5c582b1.source: (But, you might notice that sine and cosine are always smallest, or tied, since they’re trapped inside the dome. Nice!)
c044b5c582b1.target: （但你可能注意到正弦和余弦总是最小的，或者并列，因为它们被困在穹顶内。很好！）

0e1815554977.source: For most of us, I’d say this is enough:
0e1815554977.target: 对我们大多数人来说，我认为这就足够了：

eea62e0c570b.source: You don’t need to memorize $1^2 + \cot^2 = \csc^2$, except for silly tests that mistake trivia for understanding. In that case, take a minute to draw the dome/wall/ceiling diagram, fill in the labels (a tan gentleman you can see, can’t you?), and create a
eea62e0c570b.target: 你不需要记住 $1^2 + \cot^2 = \csc^2$，除了那些把琐事误认为理解的愚蠢考试。在这种情况下，花一分钟画出穹顶/墙壁/天花板示意图，填入标签（你可以看到一个正切绅士，不是吗？），然后创建一个

19c64a71db55.source: In a follow-up, we’ll learn about graphing, complements, and using
19c64a71db55.target: 在后续内容中，我们将学习绘图、余角以及使用

0fef6be0b570.source: You may see tangent defined as the length of the tangent line from the circle to the x-axis (geometry buffs can work this out).
0fef6be0b570.target: 你可能看到正切被定义为从圆到x轴的切线长度（几何爱好者可以推导出来）。

43ec01cff10b.source: As expected, at the top of the circle (x=90) the tangent line can never reach the x-axis and is infinitely long.
43ec01cff10b.target: 正如所料，在圆的顶部（x=90），切线永远无法到达x轴，并且是无限长的。

861de5298a7b.source: I like this intuition because it helps us remember the name “tangent”, and here’s a nice
861de5298a7b.target: 我喜欢这种直觉，因为它帮助我们记住“正切”这个名字，这里有一个很好的

5656d707a40d.source: Still, it’s critical to put the tangent vertical and recognize it’s just sine projected on the back wall (along with the other triangle connections).
5656d707a40d.target: 不过，关键是要将正切竖直放置，并认识到它只是正弦投影到后墙上（连同其他三角形联系）。

61f90563021d.source: Trig functions take an angle and return a percentage. $\sin(30) = .5$ means a 30-degree angle is 50% of the max height.
61f90563021d.target: 三角函数接受一个角度并返回一个百分比。$\sin(30) = .5$ 意味着30度的角度是最大高度的50%。

a586ba191f0b.source: The inverse trig functions let us work backwards, and are written $\sin^{-1}$ or $\arcsin$ (“arcsine”), and often written
a586ba191f0b.target: 反三角函数让我们逆向工作，写为 $\sin^{-1}$ 或 $\arcsin$（反正弦），并且经常写为

8c852737ad62.source: If our height is 25% of the dome, what’s our angle?
8c852737ad62.target: 如果我们的高度是穹顶的25%，我们的角度是多少？

517254ee887b.source: Plugging asin(.25) into a calculator gives an angle of 14.5 degrees.
517254ee887b.target: 在计算器中输入反正弦(0.25)得到14.5度角。

971134cfa2f5.source: Now what about something exotic, like inverse secant? Often times it’s not available as a calculator function (even the one I built, sigh).
971134cfa2f5.target: 那像反余割这样冷门的函数呢？通常计算器上没有这个功能（就连我写的那个也没有，唉）。

fbdde65e6a08.source: Looking at our trig cheatsheet, we find an easy ratio where we can compare secant to 1. For example, secant to 1 (hypotenuse to horizontal) is the same as 1 to cosine:
fbdde65e6a08.target: 查看我们的三角速查表，我们可以找到一个简单的比例，将正割与1进行比较。例如，正割与1之比（斜边比邻边）等于1比余弦：

bd50729250a1.source: Suppose our secant is 3.5, i.e. 350% of the radius of the unit circle. What’s the angle to the wall?
bd50729250a1.target: 假设我们的正割是3.5，即单位圆半径的350%。与墙壁的夹角是多少？

3df7767f92c9.source: Ack, what a boring question. Instead of “find the sine” think, “What’s the height as a percentage of the max (the hypotenuse)?”.
3df7767f92c9.target: 哎呀，好无聊的问题。不要总想着‘求正弦’，而是思考‘高度相对于最大值（斜边）的百分比是多少？’

69f9c13c144d.source: First, notice the triangle is “backwards”. That’s ok. It still has a height, in green.
69f9c13c144d.target: 首先，注意这个三角形是‘反向的’。没关系。它仍然有一个高度，用绿色表示。

64dd9a8ebb77.source: What’s the max height? By the Pythagorean theorem, we know
64dd9a8ebb77.target: 最大高度是多少？根据勾股定理，我们知道

1fa9cdcd8af1.source: Ok! The sine is the height as a percentage of the max, which is 3/5 or .60.
1fa9cdcd8af1.target: 好的！正弦是高度相对于最大值的百分比，即3/5或0.60。

ae98fb66bbed.source: Of course. We have a few ways. Now that we know sine = .60, we can just do:
ae98fb66bbed.target: 当然。我们有几种方法。既然我们知道正弦=0.60，我们可以直接这样做：

5b785a2eaa3b.source: Here’s another approach. Instead of using sine, notice the triangle is “up against the wall”, so tangent is an option. The height is 3, the distance to the wall is 4, so the tangent height is 3/4 or 75%. We can use arctangent to turn the percentage back into an angle:
5b785a2eaa3b.target: 另一种方法。不用正弦，注意三角形是‘靠着墙的’，所以正切也是一种选择。高度为3，到墙的距离为4，所以正切高度是3/4或75%。我们可以用反正切将百分比转换回角度：

6d6c582ba7a9.source: You’re on a boat with enough fuel to sail 2 miles. You’re currently .25 miles from shore. What’s the largest angle you could use and still reach land? Also, the only reference available is
6d6c582ba7a9.target: 你在一艘船上，燃料足够航行2英里。你目前离海岸0.25英里。你能使用的最大角度是多少才能仍然到达陆地？此外，唯一可用的参考资料是

ae8a81663593.source: Ok. Here, we can visualize the beach as the “wall” and the “ladder distance” to the wall is the secant.
ae8a81663593.target: 好的。在这里，我们可以将海滩视为‘墙’，到墙的‘梯子距离’就是正割。

e6e905a6701e.source: First, we need to normalize everything in terms of percentages. We have 2 / .25 = 8 “hypotenuse units” worth of fuel. So, the largest secant we could allow is 8 times the distance to the wall.
e6e905a6701e.target: 首先，我们需要将所有东西归一化为百分比。我们拥有2 / 0.25 = 8个‘斜边单位’的燃料。因此，我们能允许的最大正割是到墙距离的8倍。

ecfc3fedd927.source: to ask “What angle has a secant of 8?”. But we can’t, since we only have a book of arccosines.
ecfc3fedd927.target: 问‘什么角度的正割是8？’。但我们做不到，因为我们只有一本反余弦表。

a3ff01cedaf4.source: We use our cheatsheet diagram to relate secant to cosine: Ah, I see that “sec/1 = 1/cos”, so
a3ff01cedaf4.target: 我们用速查表图将正割与余弦联系起来：啊，我看到‘sec/1 = 1/cos’，所以

8cc1d03977f5.source: A secant of 8 implies a cosine of 1/8. The angle with a cosine of 1/8 is arccos(1/8) = 82.8 degrees, the largest we can afford.
8cc1d03977f5.target: 正割为8意味着余弦为1/8。余弦为1/8的角度是arccos(1/8) = 82.8度，这是我们能承受的最大角度。

14187c2586e4.source: Not too bad, right? Before the dome/wall/ceiling analogy, I’d be drowning in a mess of computations. Visualizing the scenario makes it simple, even fun, to see which trig buddy can help us out.
14187c2586e4.target: 还不错，对吧？在穹顶/墙/天花板类比之前，我会淹没在一堆计算中。将场景可视化使得找出哪个三角朋友能帮助我们变得简单，甚至有趣。

cf5408112e2c.source: In your problem, think: am I interested in the dome (sin/cos), the wall (tan/sec), or the ceiling (cot/csc)?
cf5408112e2c.target: 在你遇到的问题中，思考：我感兴趣的是穹顶（sin/cos）、墙（tan/sec）还是天花板（cot/csc）？

34746229b249.source: put together interactive diagrams for the analogies (drag the slider on the left to change the angle):
34746229b249.target: 为类比制作了交互式图表（拖动左侧滑块更改角度）：

39939914636a.source: Euler's formula describes two equivalent ways to move in a circle.
39939914636a.target: 欧拉公式描述了在圆周上移动的两种等价方式。

b050cebd0a46.source: Why use $e^x$, aren't we rotating the number 1?
b050cebd0a46.target: 为什么使用 $e^x$，难道我们不是在旋转数字 1 吗？

0cf92ad174b2.source: But what does i as an exponent do?
0cf92ad174b2.target: 但作为指数的 i 有什么作用？

f52140ae695e.source: Starting at the number 1, see multiplication as a
f52140ae695e.target: 从数字1开始，将乘法视为一种

a253056e716f.source: 1 by some rate for some time period;
a253056e716f.target: 以某个速率在某个时间段内增长1；

e7d612a3b8ac.source: Growing for "pi" units of time means going pi
e7d612a3b8ac.target: 增长“π”个单位时间意味着旋转π

8e0c217f4d5c.source: Therefore, $e^{i \pi}$ means starting at 1 and rotating pi (halfway around a circle) to get to -1
8e0c217f4d5c.target: 因此，$e^{i \pi}$ 表示从1开始，旋转π（半圆）到达-1

c085cfc6d4a3.target: cos(x) 是 x 坐标（水平距离）

d218a29f7b7c.target: sin(x) 是 y 坐标（垂直距离）

53ff45578a7b.source: of growing instantly (using e) at a rate of ln(3). In other words: $3 = e^{\ln(3)}$
53ff45578a7b.target: 以速率 ln(3) 瞬间增长（使用 e）的结果。换句话说：$3 = e^{\ln(3)}$

6d5b1e8cc1f5.source: $3^4$ is the same as growing to 3, but then growing for 4x as long. So $3^4 = e^{\ln(3) \cdot 4} = 81$
6d5b1e8cc1f5.target: $3^4$ 等同于先增长到3，然后继续增长4倍的时间。所以 $3^4 = e^{\ln(3) \cdot 4} = 81$

1310de94e393.source: What is the implicit growth rate? We're growing from 1 to 3 (the base of the exponent).
1310de94e393.target: 隐含的增长速率是多少？我们从1增长到3（指数的底数）。

3eefdce41bb8.source: that growth rate? We scale it by 4x (the power of the exponent).
3eefdce41bb8.target: 获得那个增长率？我们将其乘以4倍（指数的幂）。

12071e826d16.source: Radius: How big of a circle do we need? Well, the magnitude is $\sqrt{6^2 + 8^2} = \sqrt{100} = 10$. Which means we need to grow for ln(10) = 2.3 seconds to reach that amount.
12071e826d16.target: 半径：我们需要一个多大的圆？嗯，数值是 $\sqrt{6^2 + 8^2} = \sqrt{100} = 10$。这意味着我们需要增长 ln(10) = 2.3 秒才能达到那个数量。

2593299cc48b.source: Amount to rotate: What's the angle of that point? We can use arctan to figure it out: atan(8/6) = 53 degrees = .93 radian.
2593299cc48b.target: 旋转角度：那个点的角度是多少？我们可以用反正切来算：atan(8/6) = 53 度 = .93 弧度。

a6f710adf0aa.source: Grid system: Go 3 units east and 4 units north
a6f710adf0aa.target: 网格系统：向东3个单位，向北4个单位

699b3ba80847.source: Polar coordinates: Go 5 units at an angle of 53.13 degrees
699b3ba80847.target: 极坐标：以53.13度角移动5个单位

f75e1db4dfa5.target: Brian Slesinsky 有一个简洁的

b1a25702b555.source: Visual Complex Analysis has a great discussion on Euler's formula -- see p. 10 in the
b1a25702b555.target: Visual Complex Analysis 中对欧拉公式有很好的讨论——参见第10页

bb4b8c9ee08d.source: which explains Euler's Identity more visually:
bb4b8c9ee08d.target: 的讲座，更直观地解释了欧拉恒等式：

7fedf3c8f8a8.source: But Shouldn't We Spin Faster and Faster?
7fedf3c8f8a8.target: 难道我们不应该越转越快吗？

a5ba1b2b6c26.target: 欧拉恒等式看似令人困惑：

95c377caf730.source: It emerges from a more general formula:
95c377caf730.target: 它源于一个更一般的公式：

e5ba09070d96.source: to sine and cosine! And somehow plugging in pi gives -1? Could this ever be intuitive?
e5ba09070d96.target: 与正弦和余弦！而不知何故将π代入会得到-1？这难道能直观理解吗？

66ea84e892c8.source: Not according to 1800s mathematician Benjamin Peirce:
66ea84e892c8.target: 根据19世纪数学家本杰明·皮尔斯的说法，不能：

786f23913363.source: It is absolutely paradoxical; we cannot understand it, and we don't know what it means, but we have proved it, and therefore we know it must be the truth.
786f23913363.target: 它绝对矛盾；我们无法理解它，也不知道它意味着什么，但我们证明了它，因此我们知道它必定是真理。

7a4399a56bfb.source: Argh, this attitude makes my blood boil! Formulas are not magical spells to be memorized: we must, must,
7a4399a56bfb.target: 啊，这种态度让我热血沸腾！公式不是需要背诵的魔法咒语：我们必须，必须，

cbee0a4fd7e6.target: 找到一个洞察。这是我的：

21c0700b799a.source: That's it? This stunning equation is about spinning around? Yes -- and we can understand it by building on a few analogies:
21c0700b799a.target: 就这些？这个惊人的方程是关于旋转的？是的——我们可以通过建立几个类比来理解它：

8de89fc732b0.source: That's the high-level view, let's dive into the details. By the way, if someone tries to impress you with $e^{i \pi} = -1$, ask them about
8de89fc732b0.target: 这是高层次的观点，让我们深入细节。顺便一提，如果有人想用 $e^{i \pi} = -1$ 给你留下深刻印象，问问他们

4f9cb5c201a4.source: -th power. If they can't think it through, Euler's formula is still a magic spell to them.
4f9cb5c201a4.target: 次幂。如果他们想不明白，欧拉公式对他们来说仍然是一个魔法咒语。

f9fd2e022c9a.source: While writing, I thought a video might help explain the ideas more clearly:
f9fd2e022c9a.target: 在写作过程中，我想一段视频可能有助于更清晰地解释这些想法：

fb6dda1eee7d.source: The equals sign is overloaded. Sometimes we mean "set one thing to another" (like x = 3) and others we mean "these two things describe the same concept" (like $\sqrt{-1} = i$).
fb6dda1eee7d.target: 等号被重载了。有时我们的意思是“将一个东西设置为另一个”（比如 x = 3），其他时候我们的意思是“这两个东西描述同一个概念”（比如 $\sqrt{-1} = i$）。

3447e9c03016.source: Euler's formula is the latter: it gives two formulas which explain how to move in a circle. If we examine circular motion using trig, and travel x radians:
3447e9c03016.target: 欧拉公式属于后者：它给出了两个解释如何在圆上运动的公式。如果我们用三角学考察圆周运动，并移动 x 弧度：

f72d520b4542.source: is a clever way to smush the x and y coordinates into a single number. The analogy "complex numbers are 2-dimensional" helps us interpret a single complex number as a position on a circle.
f72d520b4542.target: 是将 x 和 y 坐标压缩成一个数字的巧妙方法。类比“复数是二维的”帮助我们理解单个复数作为圆上的一个位置。

a62bb65e5457.source: When we set x to $\pi$, we're traveling $\pi$ units along the outside of the unit circle. Because the total circumference is $2\pi$, plain old $\pi$ is halfway around, putting us at -1.
a62bb65e5457.target: 当我们设置 x 为 $\pi$ 时，我们沿着单位圆的外侧移动了 $\pi$ 个单位。因为总周长是 $2\pi$，普通的 $\pi$ 正好是一半，把我们带到 -1。

0cfd6822e978.source: Neato: The right side of Euler's formula ($\cos(x) + i \sin(x)$) describes circular motion with imaginary numbers. Now let's figure out how the
0cfd6822e978.target: 不错：欧拉公式的右侧（$\cos(x) + i \sin(x)$）用虚数描述了圆周运动。现在让我们弄清楚等式的

4be1295bf11a.source: side of the equation accomplishes it.
4be1295bf11a.target: 一侧是如何实现这一点的。

b59c1ccfc61a.source: Combining x- and y- coordinates into a complex number is tricky, but manageable. But what does an imaginary
b59c1ccfc61a.target: 将 x 和 y 坐标组合成一个复数很棘手，但可以处理。但虚部的

1555659f0116.source: Let's step back a bit. When I see $3^4$, I think of it like this:
1555659f0116.target: 让我们退后一步。当我看到 $3^4$ 时，我是这样想的：

9cfd0201e185.source: Instead of seeing numbers on their own, you can think of them as something e had to "grow to". Real numbers, like 3, give an interest rate of ln(3) = 1.1, and that's what e "collects" as it's going along, growing continuously.
9cfd0201e185.target: 与其单独看待数字，不如将它们视为 e 必须“增长到”的东西。实数，如 3，给出了一个利率 ln(3) = 1.1，这就是 e 在连续增长时“收集”到的。

7502eb186452.source: Regular growth is simple: it keeps "pushing" a number in the same, real direction it was going. 3 × 3 pushes in the original direction, making it 3 times larger (9).
7502eb186452.target: 常规增长很简单：它不断地将数字推向它原本的、相同的实数方向。3 × 3 推向原始方向，使其变大 3 倍（9）。

751710504cb3.source: Imaginary growth is different: the "interest" we earn is in a different direction! It's like a jet engine that was strapped on sideways -- instead of going forward, we start pushing at 90 degrees.
751710504cb3.target: 虚数增长不同：我们赚取的“利息”是在不同的方向上！就像一个侧绑的喷气发动机——不是向前，而是开始以 90 度方向推进。

cafef964c3d1.source: The neat thing about a constant orthogonal (perpendicular) push is that it doesn't speed you up or slow you down -- it rotates you! Taking any number and multiplying by
cafef964c3d1.target: 恒定正交（垂直）推力的巧妙之处在于它不会加速或减速——它旋转你！取任意一个数乘以

7e4606e4ca5c.source: will not change its magnitude, just the direction it points.
7e4606e4ca5c.target: 不会改变其大小，只会改变其指向的方向。

ba7cd2f18385.source: : "When I grow, don't push me forward or back in the direction I'm already going. Rotate me instead."
ba7cd2f18385.target: ：“当我增长时，不要在我已有的方向上把我向前或向后推。而是旋转我。”

d1aa24b812d8.source: I wondered that too. Regular growth compounds in our original direction, so we go 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, multiplying 2x each time and staying in the real numbers. We can consider this $e^{\ln(2)x}$, which means grow instantly at a rate of ln(2) for "x" seconds.
d1aa24b812d8.target: 我也有这个疑问。常规增长是在我们原有的方向上复利，所以我们得到1, 2, 4, 8, 16，每次乘以2，且保持在实数范围内。我们可以将其视为$e^{\ln(2)x}$，这意味着以ln(2)的速率瞬时增长“x”秒。

ed31ccab59ca.source: And hey -- if our growth rate was twice as fast, 2ln(2) vs ln(2), it would look the same as growing for twice as long (2x vs x). The magic of e lets us swap rate and time; 2 seconds at ln(2) is the same growth as 1 second at 2ln(2).
ed31ccab59ca.target: 而且——如果我们的增长率快一倍，即2ln(2)对比ln(2)，那么看起来就和增长时间加倍（2x对比x）一样。e的神奇之处在于它允许我们在速率和时间之间互换；以ln(2)的速率增长2秒等同于以2ln(2)的速率增长1秒。

800e303b3c2a.source: Now, imagine we have some purely imaginary growth rate (Ri) that rotates us until we reach i, or 90 degrees upward. What happens if we double that rate to 2Ri, will we spin off the circle?
800e303b3c2a.target: 现在，假设我们有一个纯虚数增长率(Ri)，它将我们旋转直到到达i，即向上90度。如果我们把速率加倍到2Ri，会发生什么？我们会脱离圆吗？

dc982b3e8498.source: Nope! Having a rate of 2Ri means we just spin twice as fast, or alternatively, spin at a rate of R for twice as long, but we're staying on the circle. Rotating twice as long means we're now facing 180 degrees.
dc982b3e8498.target: 不会！速率2Ri意味着我们只是旋转速度加倍，或者等价地，以速率R旋转两倍的时间，但我们仍然在圆上。旋转两倍的时间意味着我们现在面向180度。

16eac5ca9aa2.source: Once we realize that some exponential growth rate can take us from 1 to i, increasing that rate just spins us more. We'll never escape the circle.
16eac5ca9aa2.target: 一旦我们意识到某个指数增长率可以将我们从1带到i，那么增加这个速率只会让我们旋转更多。我们永远无法脱离这个圆。

1e43d25bacf3.source: However, if our growth rate is complex (a+bi vs Ri) then the real part (a) will grow us like normal, while the imaginary part (bi) rotates us. But let's not get fancy: Euler's formula, $e^{ix}$, is about the
1e43d25bacf3.target: 然而，如果我们的增长率是复数(a+bi对比Ri)，那么实部(a)会像通常一样增长我们，而虚部(bi)则旋转我们。不过我们还是先别搞得太复杂：欧拉公式$e^{ix}$是关于

2d1eb0c15f34.source: growth that keeps us on the circle (more later).
2d1eb0c15f34.target: 让我们保持在圆上的增长（更多内容稍后）。

722377f313b7.source: While writing, I had to clarify a few questions for myself:
722377f313b7.target: 在写作过程中，我不得不为自己澄清几个问题：

3259b0662cca.source: represents the process of starting at 1 and growing continuously at 100% interest for 1 unit of time.
3259b0662cca.target: 表示从1开始，以100%的利率连续增长1单位时间的过程。

00c7f71b058f.source: we're capturing that entire process in a single number -- e represents all the whole rigmarole of continuous growth. So really, $e^x$ is saying "start at 1 and grow continuously at 100% for x seconds", and starts from 1 like we want.
00c7f71b058f.target: 我们是在用一个数字捕捉整个过程——e代表了连续增长这整个繁琐的过程。所以实际上，$e^x$的意思是“从1开始，以100%的速率连续增长x秒”，并且如我们所愿从1开始。

1f52d3f05cd6.source: For a regular exponent like $3^4$ we ask:
1f52d3f05cd6.target: 对于像$3^4$这样的常规指数，我们会问：

e622c283e346.source: We can convert our growth into "e" format: our instantaneous rate is ln(3), and we increase it to ln(3) * 4. Again, the power of the exponent (4) just scaled our growth rate.
e622c283e346.target: 我们可以将增长转换为“e”格式：我们的瞬时速率是ln(3)，然后将其增加到ln(3)*4。同样，指数部分(4)只是缩放了我们增长率。

fd588b373d1c.source: When the top exponent is i (as in $3^i$), we just multiply our implicit growth rate by i. So instead of growing at plain old ln(3), we're growing at ln(3) * i.
fd588b373d1c.target: 当顶部指数是i时（如$3^i$），我们只需将隐式增长率乘以i。因此，我们不再是按普通的ln(3)增长，而是按ln(3)*i增长。

20c658fe8bb5.source: The top part of the exponent

de73a9d39fed.source: the implicit growth rate of the bottom part.
de73a9d39fed.target: 底部部分的隐式增长率。

4390ace22017.source: Let's take a closer look. Remember this definition of
4390ace22017.target: 让我们仔细看看。记住这个定义

c9520358ac0a.source: That $\frac{100\%}{n}$ represents the portion interest we earned in each microscopic period. We assumed the interest rate was 100% in the real dimension -- but what if it were 100% in the imaginary direction?
c9520358ac0a.target: 这个 $\frac{100\%}{n}$ 代表我们在每个微观周期内赚取的利息部分。我们假设真实维度中的利率是 100%——但如果虚数方向也是 100% 呢？

6f981611b0ae.source: Now, our newly formed interest adds to us in the 90-degree direction. Surprisingly, this does not change our length -- this is a tricky concept, because it appears to make a triangle where the hypotenuse must be larger. We're dealing with a limit, and the extra distance is within the error margin we specify. This is something I want to tackle another day, but take my word: continuous perpendicular growth will rotate you. This is the heart of sine and cosine, where your change is perpendicular to your current position, and you move in a circle.
6f981611b0ae.target: 现在，我们新形成的利息以 90 度方向添加到我们身上。令人惊讶的是，这并没有改变我们的长度——这是一个棘手的概念，因为它似乎形成了一个斜边必须更大的三角形。我们处理的是一个极限，额外的距离在我们指定的误差范围内。这是我想改天再谈的事情，但请相信我的话：连续的垂直增长会让你旋转。这是正弦和余弦的核心，你的变化垂直于你当前位置，你沿着圆运动。

803e94fec22b.source: units of growth in infinitely small increments, each pushing us at a 90-degree angle. There is no "faster and faster" rotation - instead, we crawl along the perimeter a distance of |i| = 1 (magnitude of i).
803e94fec22b.target: 以无穷小的增量进行增长，每次推动我们以 90 度角方向移动。没有“越来越快”的旋转——相反，我们沿着周长爬行距离 |i| = 1（i 的模长）。

6c2ae0517d89.source: And hey -- the distance crawled around a circle is an angle in radians! We've found another way to describe circular motion!
6c2ae0517d89.target: 嘿——绕着圆爬行的距离就是弧度角！我们找到了另一种描述圆周运动的方式！

d7ffaff32f8b.source: Change continuously by rotating at 90-degree angle (aka imaginary growth rate).
d7ffaff32f8b.target: 以 90 度角旋转进行连续变化（即虚增长率）。

57a2aca43c96.source: So, Euler's formula is saying "exponential, imaginary growth traces out a circle". And this path is the same as moving in a circle using sine and cosine in the imaginary plane.
57a2aca43c96.target: 所以，欧拉公式说的是“指数虚增长描绘出一个圆”。这个路径与在虚平面上使用正弦和余弦绕圆运动相同。

46b98ed7b3ce.source: In this case, the word "exponential" is confusing because we travel around the circle at a constant rate. In most discussions, exponential growth is assumed to have a cumulative, compounding effect.
46b98ed7b3ce.target: 在这种情况下，“指数”这个词令人困惑，因为我们以恒定速率绕圆运动。在大多数讨论中，指数增长被认为具有累积的复利效应。

7913110ab4d5.source: You don't really believe me, do you? Here's a few examples, and how to think about them intuitively.
7913110ab4d5.target: 你并不真的相信我，对吧？这里有几个例子，以及如何直观地理解它们。

6eb8a489be03.source: Where's the x? Ah, it's just 1. Intuitively, without breaking out a calculator, we know that this means "travel 1 radian along the unit circle". In my head, I see "e"
6eb8a489be03.target: x 在哪里？啊，它只是 1。直观地说，不用计算器，我们就知道这意味着“沿单位圆运动 1 弧度”。在我脑海里，我看到“e”

102a3b9c8cb0.source: to grow 1 at 100% all in the same direction, but i keeps moving the ball and forces "1" to grow along the edge of a circle:
102a3b9c8cb0.target: 以 100% 的增长率沿着同一方向增长 1，但 i 不断移动着球并迫使“1”沿着圆的边缘增长：

cb256adf1587.source: Not the prettiest number, but there it is. Remember to put your calculator in radian mode when punching this in.
cb256adf1587.target: 不是最漂亮的数字，但就是这样。输入时请记得将计算器切换到弧度模式。

28b45c536258.source: This is tricky -- it's not in our standard format. But remember,
28b45c536258.target: 这很棘手——它不在我们的标准格式中。但请记住，

6f74129a3975.source: We want an initial growth of 3x at the end of the period, or an instantaneous rate of ln(3). But, the
6f74129a3975.target: 我们希望在周期结束时获得 3 倍的初始增长，即瞬时增长率为 ln(3)。但是，

cd572dde13f1.source: comes along and changes that rate of ln(3) to "i * ln(3)":
cd572dde13f1.target: 出现了，并将 ln(3) 的增长率变成了“i * ln(3)”：

7de19c1a4bd1.source: we were going to transform at a regular rate of ln(3), a little faster than 100% continuous growth since e is about 2.718. But oh no,
7de19c1a4bd1.target: 我们本来要以常规增长率 ln(3) 变换，比 100% 的连续增长稍快一些，因为 e 大约是 2.718。但哦不，

bfd050a5e989.source: spun us around: now we're transforming at an imaginary rate which means we're just rotating about. If
bfd050a5e989.target: 让我们旋转了：现在我们以虚增长率变换，这意味着我们只是在旋转。如果

d65fb6738efe.source: was a regular number like 4, it would have made us grow 4x faster. Now we're growing at a speed of ln(3), but sideways.
d65fb6738efe.target: 是一个像 4 这样的常规数字，它会让我们增长 4 倍。现在我们以 ln(3) 的速度增长，但是侧向的。

b89f1d871e15.source: We should expect a complex number on the unit circle -- there's nothing in the growth rate to increase our size. Solving the equation:
b89f1d871e15.target: 我们应该期望单位圆上的一个复数——增长率中没有任何东西会增加我们的大小。解这个方程：

7b3d14e56e4e.source: So, rather than ending up "1" unit around the circle (like $e^i$) we end up ln(3) units around.
7b3d14e56e4e.target: 所以，我们不是绕着圆结束在“1”个单位的位置（如 $e^i$ 那样），而是结束在 ln(3) 个单位的位置。

ea02139cf621.source: A few months ago, this would have had me tears. Not today! Let's break down the transformations:
ea02139cf621.target: 几个月前，这还会让我泪流满面。但今天不会了！让我们分解这些变换：

d98027141b30.source: We start with 1 and want to change it. Like solving $3^i$, what's the instantaneous growth rate represented by
d98027141b30.target: 我们从1开始，想要改变它。就像求解$3^i$，其瞬时增长速率是多少？

34e734a78b60.source: Hrm. Normally we'd do ln(x) to get the growth rate needed to reach x at the end of 1 unit of time. But for an imaginary rate? We need to noodle this over.
34e734a78b60.target: 嗯。通常我们取ln(x)得到在1单位时间内达到x所需的增长率。但对于虚数速率呢？我们需要仔细琢磨。

310ffeeb36c5.source: In order to start with 1 and grow to

d3e0ef1fa7d7.source: we need to start rotating at the outset. How fast? Well, we need to get 90 degrees (pi/2 radians) in 1 unit of time. So our rate is $i \frac{\pi}{2}$. Remember our rate must be imaginary since we're rotating, not growing! Plain old $\frac{\pi}{2}$ is about 1.57 and results in regular growth.
d3e0ef1fa7d7.target: 我们需要从一开始就旋转。多快？嗯，我们需要在1单位时间内旋转90度（π/2弧度）。所以我们的速率是$i \frac{\pi}{2}$。记住我们的速率必须是虚数，因为我们在旋转，而不是增长！普通的$\frac{\pi}{2}$大约是1.57，会导致常规增长。

0435cfe854de.source: This should make sense: to turn 1.0 to
0435cfe854de.target: 这应该合理：要在1单位结束时将1.0变成

4ebdbca76f70.source: at the end of 1 unit, we should rotate $\frac{\pi}{2}$ radians (90 degrees) in that amount of time. So, to get "i" we can use $e^{i \frac{\pi}{2}}$.
4ebdbca76f70.target: ，我们应该在那段时间内旋转$\frac{\pi}{2}$弧度（90度）。所以，要得到"i"，我们可以用$e^{i \frac{\pi}{2}}$。

b8fd72c23db4.source: Phew. That describes i as the base. How about the exponent?
b8fd72c23db4.target: 呼。这描述了以i为底数。那指数呢？

776fa1d37615.source: i tells us to change our rate -- yes, that rate we spent so long figuring out! So rather than rotating at a speed of $i \frac{\pi}{2}$, which is what a base of
776fa1d37615.target: i告诉我们改变我们的速率——没错，就是那个我们花了很长时间才搞清楚的速率！所以，不是以$i \frac{\pi}{2}$的速度旋转（这是以

636b03a072b2.source: means, we transform the rate to:
636b03a072b2.target: 为底数的含义），我们将速率变换为：

8fd23524921a.source: The i's cancel and make the growth rate real again! We rotated our rate and pushed ourselves into the negative numbers. And a negative growth rate means we're shrinking -- we should expect $i^i$ to make things smaller. And it does:
8fd23524921a.target: i相互抵消，使增长率再次变为实数！我们旋转了速率，把自己推入了负数。负增长率意味着我们在缩小——我们应该预料到$i^i$会使东西变小。事实确实如此：

423c7dcecc56.source: Tada! (Search "i^i" on Google to use its calculator)
423c7dcecc56.target: 瞧！（在Google上搜索"i^i"来使用其计算器）

ad24e669f428.source: Take a breather: You can intuitively figure out how imaginary bases and imaginary exponents should behave. Whoa.
ad24e669f428.target: 喘口气：你可以直观地理解虚数底数和虚数指数应该如何运作。哇。

c084d1cb78ea.source: And as a bonus, you figured out ln(i) -- to make $e^x$ become i, make e rotate $\frac{\pi}{2}$ radians.
c084d1cb78ea.target: 额外收获是，你算出了ln(i)——要使$e^x$变成i，让e旋转$\frac{\pi}{2}$弧度。

4bfa653e1549.source: A double imaginary exponent? If you insist. First off, we know what our growth rate will be inside the parenthesis:
4bfa653e1549.target: 双重虚数指数？如果你坚持的话。首先，我们知道括号内的增长率是：

0f1c3373defa.source: We get a negative (shrinking) growth rate of -pi/2. And now we modify that rate
0f1c3373defa.target: 我们得到一个负的（缩小的）增长率 -π/2。现在我们要修改那个速率

2df1b14303de.source: And now we have a negative rotation! We're going around the circle a rate of $-\frac{\pi}{2}$ per unit time. How long do we go for? Well, there's an implicit "1" unit of time at the very top of this exponent chain; the implied default is to go for 1 time unit (just like $e = e^1$). 1 time unit gives us a rotation of $-\frac{\pi}{2}$ radians (-90 degrees) or -i!
2df1b14303de.target: 现在我们有了一个负的旋转！我们以每单位时间$-\frac{\pi}{2}$的速率绕圆旋转。我们持续多久？嗯，在这个指数链的最顶端有一个隐式的"1"单位时间；默认是持续1单位时间（就像$e = e^1$）。1单位时间给我们一个$-\frac{\pi}{2}$弧度（-90度）的旋转，也就是-i！

773d06ef08b2.source: And, just for kicks, if we squared that crazy result:
773d06ef08b2.target: 并且，只是为了好玩，如果我们把这个疯狂的结果平方：

73f01a65cb61.source: It's "just" twice the rotation: 2 is a regular number so doubles our rotation rate to a full -180 degrees in a unit of time. Or, you can look at it as applying -90 degree rotation twice in a row.
73f01a65cb61.target: 这“仅仅”是旋转的两倍：2 是一个常规数字，所以它将我们的旋转速率加倍，在一个单位时间内完成完整的 -180 度。或者，你可以将其视为连续两次应用 -90 度旋转。

95c388c234e0.source: At first blush, these are really strange exponents. But with our analogies we can take them in stride.
95c388c234e0.target: 乍一看，这些指数真的很奇怪。但借助我们的类比，我们可以从容应对。

e5fb14879a34.source: We can have real and imaginary growth at the same time: the real portion scales us up, and the imaginary part rotates us around:
e5fb14879a34.target: 我们可以同时拥有实增长和虚增长：实部将我们按比例缩放，虚部使我们旋转：

5455bef0c0fb.source: A complex growth rate like (a + bi) is a mix of real and imaginary growth. The real part a, means "grow at 100% for
5455bef0c0fb.target: 像 (a + bi) 这样的复增长速率是实增长和虚增长的混合。实部 a 表示“以 100% 的速度增长

1dca2bbdc3b8.source: seconds" and the imaginary part b means "rotate for
1dca2bbdc3b8.target: 秒”，虚部 b 表示“旋转

b89089bf24d7.source: seconds". Remember, rotations don't get the benefit of compounding since you keep 'pushing' in a different direction -- rotation adds up linearly.
b89089bf24d7.target: 秒”。记住，旋转不会获得复利效应，因为你持续向不同方向“推”——旋转是线性叠加的。

35bc954fe52e.source: With this in mind, we can represent any point on any sized circle using (a+bi)! The radius is $e^a$ and the angle is determined by $e^{bi}$. It's like putting the number in the expand-o-tron for two cycles: once to grow it to the right size (a seconds), another time to rotate it to the right angle (b seconds). Or, you could rotate it first and then grow!
35bc954fe52e.target: 考虑到这一点，我们可以使用 (a+bi) 表示任何大小圆上的任意点！半径是 $e^a$，角度由 $e^{bi}$ 决定。这就像将数字放入 expand-o-tron 两个周期：一次将其增长到合适的大小（a 秒），另一次将其旋转到正确的角度（b 秒）。或者，你也可以先旋转再增长！

a66311ecdf5c.source: Let's say we want to know the growth amount to get to 6 + 8i. This is really asking for the natural log of an imaginary number: how do we grow e to get (6 + 8i)?
a66311ecdf5c.target: 假设我们想得到达到 6 + 8i 的增长量。这实际上是在问一个虚数的自然对数：我们如何增长 e 才能得到 (6 + 8i)？

f048fd08cae0.source: That is, we can reach the random point (6 + 8i) if we use $e^{2.3 + .93i}$.
f048fd08cae0.target: 也就是说，如果我们使用 $e^{2.3 + .93i}$，就能到达随机点 (6 + 8i)。

cbd49fe26961.source: Euler's formula gives us another way to describe motion in a circle. But we could already do that with sine and cosine -- what's so special?
cbd49fe26961.target: 欧拉公式为我们提供了另一种描述圆周运动的方式。但我们已经用正弦和余弦做到了这一点，有什么特别的呢？

619d8f44c96c.source: It's all about perspective. Sine and cosine describe motion in terms of a
619d8f44c96c.target: 这完全取决于视角。正弦和余弦用

1f29d2fe698d.source: , plotting out horizontal and vertical coordinates.
1f29d2fe698d.target: 来描述运动，绘制出水平和垂直坐标。

dd9000e96ed0.source: Euler's formula uses polar coordinates -- what's your angle and distance? Again, it's two ways to describe motion:
dd9000e96ed0.target: 欧拉公式使用极坐标——你的角度和距离是多少？同样，这是描述运动的两种方式：

c8623e3f15b6.source: Depending on the problem, polar or rectangular coordinates are more useful. Euler's formula lets us convert between the two to use the best tool for the job. Also, because $e^{ix}$ can be converted to sine and cosine, we can rewrite formulas in trig as variations on e, which comes in very handy (no need to memorize sin(a+b), you can derive it -- more another day). And it's beautiful that every number, real or complex, is a variation of e.
c8623e3f15b6.target: 根据问题，极坐标或直角坐标更有用。欧拉公式让我们在两者之间转换，以便使用最适合工作的工具。此外，由于 $e^{ix}$ 可以转换为正弦和余弦，我们可以将三角公式重写为 e 的变体，这非常方便（无需记忆 sin(a+b)，你可以推导它——改天再详谈）。而且，每个数，无论是实数还是复数，都是 e 的变体，这很美。

4451a1670054.source: But utility, schmutility: the most important result is the realization that baffling equations can become intuitive with the right analogies. Don't let beautiful equations like Euler's formula remain a magic spell --
4451a1670054.target: 但实用与否，最重要的结果是认识到，令人困惑的方程可以通过恰当的类比变得直观。不要让像欧拉公式这样美丽的方程仍然是一个魔法咒语——

a55acba84646.source: you know to see the insights inside the equation.
a55acba84646.target: 你知道要洞察方程内部的含义。

829eed44522e.source: The screencast was fun, and feedback is definitely welcome. I think it helps the ideas pop, and walking through the article helped me find gaps in my intuition.
829eed44522e.target: 录屏很有趣，也欢迎反馈。我认为它有助于让想法更突出，并且浏览文章帮助我发现了自己直觉中的缺口。

3cab87642e27.source: Sine is a natural sway, the epitome of smoothness: it makes circles "circular" in the same way lines make squares "square".
3cab87642e27.target: 正弦是一种自然的摇摆，平滑的缩影：它使圆变得“圆”，就像直线使正方形变得“方”一样。

135525f54a15.source: What do the values of sine mean?

91ccf6419fa4.source: What's is the input 'x' in sin(x)?

93f04a0ba633.source: how far along we are in the cycle

c2000aa7f129.source: So x is the 'amount of your cycle'. What's the cycle?
c2000aa7f129.target: 所以x是'你的周期的量'。周期是什么？

dd25d3778d54.source: Let's define pi as the time sine takes from 0 to 1 and back to 0.
dd25d3778d54.target: 让我们定义π为正弦(sine)从0到1再回到0所需的时间。

c08cacf881a5.source: pi is about sine returning to center

f5aedfd59e26.source: Question: If pi is half of a natural cycle, why isn't it a clean, simple number?
f5aedfd59e26.target: 问题：如果π是自然周期的一半，为什么它不是一个简洁、简单的数字？

e1a38f152585.source: how in blazes do we actually calculate sine!?
e1a38f152585.target: 我们到底如何实际计算正弦(sine)！？

495b2d151a60.source: Sine is acceleration opposite to your current position
495b2d151a60.target: 正弦(Sine)是与当前位置相反的加速度

49f0c9870127.source: . Go, Hubert go! Notice that smooth back and forth motion? That's Hubert, but more importantly (sorry Hubert), that's sine! It's natural, the way springs bounce, pendulums swing, strings vibrate... and many things move.
49f0c9870127.target: . 加油，Hubert加油！注意到那个平滑的来回运动了吗？那是Hubert，但更重要的是（抱歉Hubert），那是正弦(sine)！它是自然的，就像弹簧弹跳、钟摆摆动、琴弦振动……以及许多物体的运动。

4c1ee14ab2cd.source: . Big difference -- see how the motion gets constant and robotic, like a game of pong?
4c1ee14ab2cd.target: . 巨大差异——看运动如何变得恒定且机械，就像乒乓球游戏一样？

713e50147eb1.source: You're traveling on a square. Each side takes 10 seconds.
713e50147eb1.target: 你在一个正方形上移动。每条边需要10秒钟。

314cbcbdac6a.source: After 1 second, you are 10% complete on that side

e1187b180347.source: After 10 seconds, you finished the side

8b4eff4dc675.source: We're traveling on a sine wave, from 0 (neutral) to 1.0 (max). This portion takes 10 seconds.
8b4eff4dc675.target: 我们在一个正弦波上移动，从0（中性）到1.0（最大）。这部分需要10秒钟。

c0faa1a07e67.source: After 5 seconds we are... 70% complete! Sine rockets out of the gate and slows down. Most of the gains are in the first 5 seconds
c0faa1a07e67.target: 5秒后我们完成了……70%！正弦波从起点快速上升然后减慢。大部分的增长都在前5秒内。

95b71b663d45.source: It takes 5 more seconds to get from 70% to 100%. And going from 98% to 100% takes almost a full second!
95b71b663d45.target: 从70%到100%还需要5秒钟。而从98%到100%几乎需要一整秒！

4f9db24ac6e1.source: Basic trig: 'x' is degrees, and a full cycle is 360 degrees
4f9db24ac6e1.target: 基础三角学：'x'是度数，一个完整周期是360度

c5364b01fee7.source: ), and a full cycle is going around the unit circle (2*pi radians)
c5364b01fee7.target: ），一个完整周期是绕单位圆一周（2*pi弧度）

0dc66923658e.source: Sine is a gentle back and forth rocking
0dc66923658e.target: 正弦波是一种轻柔的前后摇摆

2e5b3997f92b.source: Pi is the time from neutral to max and back to neutral
2e5b3997f92b.target: Pi是从中性到最大值再回到中性的时间

0aa472559950.source: n * Pi (0 * Pi, 1 * pi, 2 * pi, and so on) are the times you are at neutral
0aa472559950.target: n * Pi（0 * Pi, 1 * pi, 2 * pi，等等）是你处于中性的时刻

70e15347865f.source: 2 * Pi, 4 * pi, 6 * pi, etc. are full cycles
70e15347865f.target: 2 * Pi, 4 * pi, 6 * pi 等是完整周期

c12635c0712b.source: , off-the-shelf sine wave, that indeed takes pi units of time from 0 to max to 0 (or 2*pi for a complete cycle)
c12635c0712b.target: 标准的正弦波，确实从0到最大值再到0需要pi个单位时间（或者一个完整周期需要2*pi）

83341b9feecc.source: sin(2x) is a wave that moves twice as fast
83341b9feecc.target: sin(2x)是一个移动速度快两倍的波

b88ac216b812.source: sin(0.5x) is a wave that moves twice as slow
b88ac216b812.target: sin(0.5x)是一个移动速度慢两倍的波

53f5965351e8.target: : 余弦 = 邻边 / 斜边

4c5bf854f1f3.target: : 正切 = 对边 / 邻边

6e0c7aad769b.source: Lay down a 10-foot pole and raise it 45 degrees. It is 10 * sin(45) = 7.07 feet off the ground
6e0c7aad769b.target: 放下一根10英尺的杆子，并将其抬高45度。它离地面10 * sin(45) = 7.07英尺

2b142bd29755.source: An 8-foot pole would be 8 * sin(45) = 5.65 feet
2b142bd29755.target: 一根8英尺的杆子会离地8 * sin(45) = 5.65英尺

1b0c401ee8cc.source: Start at 0 and grow at unit speed
1b0c401ee8cc.target: 从0开始，以单位速度增长

e2e530dbc1f0.source: At every instant, get pulled back by negative acceleration
e2e530dbc1f0.target: 在每一瞬间，被负加速度拉回

8b01ff6da8cf.source: Our initial kick increases distance linearly: y (distance from center) = x (time taken)
8b01ff6da8cf.target: 我们的初始冲击使距离线性增加：y（距中心的距离）= x（经过的时间）

9604cafda2b1.source: At any moment, we feel a restoring force of $-x$. We integrate twice to turn negative acceleration into distance:
9604cafda2b1.target: 在任何时刻，我们感受到一个恢复力$-x$。我们积分两次将负加速度转化为距离：

86398128b40a.source: $y = x$ is our initial motion, which creates a restoring force of impact...
86398128b40a.target: $y = x$ 是我们的初始运动，它产生了一个影响恢复力...

8729c4de59f8.source: $y = \frac{-x^3}{3!}$ which creates a restoring force of impact...
8729c4de59f8.target: $y = \frac{-x^3}{3!}$ 产生了一个影响恢复力...

3cc4107eb710.source: $y = \frac{x^5}{5!}$ which creates a restoring force of impact...
3cc4107eb710.target: $y = \frac{x^5}{5!}$ 产生了一个影响恢复力...

194b056a896b.source: $y = \frac{-x^7}{7!}$ which creates a restoring force of impact...
194b056a896b.target: $y = \frac{-x^7}{7!}$ 产生了一个影响恢复力...

a735808d9486.source: Consider the "restoring force" like "positive or negative interest". This makes the sine/e connection in
a735808d9486.target: 把“恢复力”想象成“正利息或负利息”。这使得正弦和e之间的联系

00bf64a22050.source: easier to understand. Sine is like e, except sometimes it earns negative interest. There's more to learn here :).
00bf64a22050.target: 更容易理解。正弦就像e，只不过有时会获得负利息。这里还有更多要学的:)。

9d3860fccf7a.source: For very small angles, "y = x" is a good guess for sine. We just take the initial impulse and ignore any restoring forces.
9d3860fccf7a.target: 对于非常小的角度，“y = x”是对正弦的良好猜测。我们只考虑初始冲击，忽略任何恢复力。

23fe2643b490.source: Sine: Start at 0, initial impulse of y = x (100%)
23fe2643b490.target: 正弦：从0开始，初始冲击y = x (100%)

1fa447660c55.source: Cosine: Start at 1, no initial impulse
1fa447660c55.target: 余弦：从1开始，无初始冲击

7df816eb62bc.target: 我们当前的位置是 y

82aa11ad7c0a.source: Our acceleration (2nd derivative, or y'') is the opposite of our current position (-y)
82aa11ad7c0a.target: 我们的加速度(二阶导数, 或 y'') 与我们当前位置相反 (-y)

60ec4e758094.source: Sine is a smooth, swaying motion between min (-1) and max (1). Mathematically, you're accelerating opposite your position. This "negative interest" keeps sine rocking forever.
60ec4e758094.target: 正弦是一种在最小值(-1)和最大值(1)之间平滑摇摆的运动。从数学上讲，你在加速反向你的位置。这种“负利息”使正弦永远摇摆。

1f69594fc3fd.source: in circles and triangles (and springs, pendulums, vibrations, sound...).
1f69594fc3fd.target: 在圆和三角形中(以及弹簧、钟摆、振动、声音...)。

878e8503f8df.source: Pi is the time from neutral to neutral in sin(x). Similarly, pi doesn't "belong" to circles, it just happens to show up there.
878e8503f8df.target: 圆周率是 sin(x) 中从平衡到平衡的时间。同样，圆周率并不“属于”圆，它只是恰好出现在那里。

38005a066ad7.source: Part 2: Understanding the definitions of sine
38005a066ad7.target: 第二部分：理解正弦的定义

091c8c23be39.source: Definition 1: The height of a triangle / circle!

24b93b506f7b.source: combination of an initial impulse and restoring forces
24b93b506f7b.target: 初始冲量与恢复力的组合

577e15149dc1.source: Hrm, I need a formula to make smooth changes...
577e15149dc1.target: 嗯，我需要一个公式来实现平滑的变化……

19ad45a095da.source: Sine waves confused me. Yes, I can mumble "SOH CAH TOA" and draw lines within triangles.
19ad45a095da.target: 正弦波曾让我困惑。是的，我能咕哝“SOH CAH TOA”并在三角形内画线。

26cee48923ab.source: I was stuck thinking sine had to be extracted from other shapes. A quick analogy:
26cee48923ab.target: 我曾被困在认为正弦必须从其他形状中提取出来。一个简单的类比：

159317a8187e.source: You: Geometry is about shapes, lines, and so on.
159317a8187e.target: 你：几何学是关于形状、线条等。

623010ce63cd.source: Alien: Oh? Can you show me a line?
623010ce63cd.target: 外星人：哦？你能给我看一条线吗？

d3406f9e2c2d.source: You (looking around): Uh... see that brick, there? A line is one edge of that brick.
d3406f9e2c2d.target: 你（环顾四周）：呃……看到那块砖了吗？线是那块砖的一条边。

9df7e0599b59.source: Alien: So lines are part of a shape?
9df7e0599b59.target: 外星人：所以线条是形状的一部分？

e13917584f25.source: You: Sort of. Yes, most shapes have lines in them. But a line is a basic concept on its own: a beam of light, a route on a map, or even--
e13917584f25.target: 你：算是吧。是的，大多数形状都有线条。但线条本身是一个基本概念：一束光、地图上的一条路线，甚至——

f9627a0a38cf.source: Alien: Bricks have lines. Lines come from bricks. Bricks bricks bricks.
f9627a0a38cf.target: 外星人：砖块有线条。线条来自砖块。砖块砖块砖块。

3c8c907e1fe9.source: Most math classes are exactly this. "Circles have sine. Sine comes from circles. Circles circles circles."
3c8c907e1fe9.target: 大多数数学课正是如此。“圆有正弦。正弦来自圆。圆圆圆。”

414c881df1f5.target: 正弦的一个例子。用一句话来说：

2f23afad994a.source: Let's build our intuition by seeing sine as its own shape, and
2f23afad994a.target: 让我们通过将正弦视为其自身的形状来建立直觉，并

45bc3fae7b92.source: understand how it fits into circles and the like. Onward!
45bc3fae7b92.target: 理解它如何融入圆等概念。继续！

62dedb97ba72.source: of lines. Sine clicked when it became its own idea, not "part of a circle."
62dedb97ba72.target: 线段的。正弦在其成为独立概念（而非“圆的一部分”）时才真正被理解。

2aafbcb42c12.source: Let's observe sine in a simulator:
2aafbcb42c12.target: 让我们在模拟器中观察正弦：

4a81be60206a.source: Let's explore the differences with video:
4a81be60206a.target: 让我们通过视频探索差异：

a9fb16f7dc8e.source: is constant: we go a set speed and turn around instantly. It's the unnatural motion in the robot dance (notice the linear bounce with no slowdown vs. the strobing effect).
a9fb16f7dc8e.target: 是恒定的：我们以固定速度运动并瞬间转向。这是机器人舞蹈中不自然的运动（注意线性弹跳没有减速，与频闪效果对比）。

b95a22c8a090.source: changes its speed: it starts fast, slows down, stops, and speeds up again. It's the enchanting smoothness in liquid dancing (human sine wave and natural bounce).
b95a22c8a090.target: 改变速度：它开始快速，然后减速、停止，再次加速。这是液体舞蹈中迷人的流畅感（人类正弦波与自然弹跳）。

bb55c760cba9.source: Unfortunately, textbooks don't show sine with animations or dancing. No, they prefer to introduce sine with a timeline (try setting "horizontal" to "timeline"):
bb55c760cba9.target: 不幸的是，教科书不会用动画或舞蹈来展示正弦。不，他们更喜欢用时序图来介绍正弦（尝试将“水平”设置为“时间线”）：

8648d9d655d3.source: Egads. This is the schematic diagram we've always been shown. Does it give you the feeling of sine? Not any more than a skeleton portrays the agility of a cat. Let's watch sine move and
8648d9d655d3.target: 天哪。这就是我们一直看到的示意图。它能给你正弦的感觉吗？不比一具骨架能展现猫的敏捷性好多少。让我们观察正弦的运动并

e4ce7904aac6.source: Circles have sine. Yes. But seeing the sine inside a circle is like getting the eggs back out of the omelette. It's all mixed together!
e4ce7904aac6.target: 圆中有正弦。没错。但在圆中看到正弦就像从煎蛋中把鸡蛋分离出来。它们完全混合在一起！

e18b4390bf17.source: Let's take it slow. In the simulation, set Hubert to vertical:none and horizontal: sine*. See him wiggle sideways? That's the motion of sine. There's a small tweak: normally sine starts the cycle at the neutral midpoint and races to the max. This time, we start at the max and fall towards the midpoint. Sine that "starts at the max" is called cosine, and it's just a version of sine (like a horizontal line is a version of a vertical line).
e18b4390bf17.target: 我们慢慢来。在模拟器中，将Hubert设置为垂直：无，水平：sine*。看到他在水平方向摆动了？这就是正弦的运动。有一点小调整：通常正弦从中性中点开始循环并冲向最大值。这次我们从最大值开始并向中点下降。这种“从最大值开始”的正弦称为余弦，它只是正弦的一种形式（就像水平线是垂直线的一种形式）。

777cda86f9dc.source: Ok. Time for both sine waves: put vertical as "sine" and horizontal as "sine*". And... we have a circle!
777cda86f9dc.target: 好的。现在来看两个正弦波：将垂直设置为“sine”，水平设置为“sine*”。然后……我们就得到了一个圆！

2fedcb04a0cb.source: A horizontal and vertical "spring" combine to give circular motion. Most textbooks draw the circle and try to extract the sine, but I prefer to build up: start with pure horizontal or vertical motion and add in the other.
2fedcb04a0cb.target: 水平和垂直的“弹簧”组合产生圆周运动。大多数教科书画出圆并试图提取正弦，但我更喜欢构建：从纯水平或垂直运动开始，再加入另一个。

1135978d042c.source: A few insights I missed when first learning sine:
1135978d042c.target: 初学正弦时我错过的一些见解：

ca55fa80ef29.source: Sine wiggles in one dimension. Really. We often graph sine over time (so we don't write over ourselves) and sometimes the "thing" doing sine is also moving, but this is optional! A spring in one dimension is a perfectly happy sine wave.
ca55fa80ef29.target: 正弦在一维中摆动。真的。我们经常随时间绘制正弦（以免重叠），有时执行正弦的“东西”也在运动，但这不是必需的！一维中的弹簧就是一个完美的正弦波。

f18d1b47f94e.source: Circles and squares are a combination of basic components (sines and lines). The circle is made from two connected 1-d waves, each moving the horizontal and vertical direction.
f18d1b47f94e.target: 圆和正方形是基本组成部分（正弦波和直线）的组合。圆由两个相连的一维波构成，每个波分别在水平和垂直方向运动。

5817d191e6b2.target: 但请记住，圆并非正弦波的

8421df541b97.source: of sines any more than squares are the origin of lines. They're examples of two sine waves working together, not their source.
8421df541b97.target: 起源，正如正方形并非直线的起源。它们是两个正弦波协同工作的例子，而非其源头。

a1d5594dbc15.source: Sine cycles between -1 and 1. It starts at 0, grows to 1.0 (max), dives to -1.0 (min) and returns to neutral. I also see sine like a percentage, from 100% (full steam ahead) to -100% (full retreat).
a1d5594dbc15.target: 正弦在-1到1之间循环。它从0开始，增长到1.0（最大值），下降到-1.0（最小值），然后回到中性。我也将正弦视为百分比，从100%（全速前进）到-100%（全速后退）。

c383fc367f50.source: Tricky question. Sine is a cycle and x, the input, is
c383fc367f50.target: 棘手的问题。正弦是一个周期，而输入x是

6fc340e34e92.source: Linear motion has few surprises. Now for sine (focusing on the "0 to max" cycle):
6fc340e34e92.target: 直线运动没什么意外。现在来看正弦（关注"0到最大值"周期）：

b08ca8c42c1d.source: Despite our initial speed, sine slows so we gently kiss the max value before turning around. This smoothness makes sine, sine.
b08ca8c42c1d.target: 尽管有初始速度，正弦会减速，使得我们在掉头前轻轻触碰最大值。这种平滑性造就了正弦。

24c149326fd3.source: For the geeks: Press "show stats" in the simulation. You'll see the percent complete of the total cycle, mini-cycle (0 to 1.0), and the value attained so far. Stop, step through, and switch between linear and sine motion to see the values.
24c149326fd3.target: 给极客们：在模拟中点击"显示统计"。你会看到整个周期的完成百分比、小周期（0到1.0）以及到目前为止达到的值。暂停、逐步进行，并在直线运动和正弦运动之间切换以观察数值。

885582f424cc.source: Quick quiz: What's further along, 10% of a linear cycle, or 10% of a sine cycle? Sine. Remember, it barrels out of the gate at max speed. By the time sine hits 50% of the cycle, it's moving at the average speed of linear cycle, and beyond that, it goes slower (until it reaches the max and turns around).
885582f424cc.target: 快速测验：哪个更靠前，直线周期的10%还是正弦周期的10%？正弦。记住，它一开始就以最大速度冲出大门。当正弦达到周期的50%时，它以直线周期的平均速度运动，之后它会变慢（直到达到最大值并掉头）。

fc6e506bd847.source: Play with values of x here:

6702cd885323.source: But again, cycles depend on circles! Can we escape their tyranny?
6702cd885323.target: 但再次强调，周期依赖于圆！我们能逃脱它们的暴政吗？

e7ea9055d73d.source: Imagine a sightless alien who only notices shades of light and dark. Could you describe pi to it? It's hard to flicker the idea of a circle's circumference, right?
e7ea9055d73d.target: 想象一个盲眼的外星人，它只能感知明暗变化。你能向它描述π吗？很难用闪烁来传达圆周长的概念，对吧？

da1f6fdc7495.source: Let's step back a bit. Sine is a repeating pattern, which means it must... repeat! It goes from 0, to 1, to 0, to -1, to 0, and so on.
da1f6fdc7495.target: 我们退一步。正弦是一种重复的模式，这意味着它必须……重复！它从0到1，到0，到-1，再到0，如此循环。

3e5bcf26b7c8.source: Whoa! Now we're using pi without a circle too! Pi is a concept that
3e5bcf26b7c8.target: 哇！现在我们也在没有圆的情况下使用π！π是一个概念，它

793ed64a80ec.source: Aha! That is why pi appears in so many formulas! Pi doesn't "belong" to circles any more than 0 and 1 do --
793ed64a80ec.target: 啊哈！这就是为什么π出现在这么多公式中！π并不比0和1更"属于"圆——

e934d3e9c029.source: of a shape that repeats and returns to center every 2*pi units. But springs, vibrations, etc. return to center after pi too!
e934d3e9c029.target: 每2π单位重复并返回中心的形状的例子。但弹簧、振动等也在π后返回中心！

c93017e87d73.source: Let's answer a question with a question. Why does a 1x1 square have a diagonal of length $\sqrt{2} = 1.414...$ (an irrational number)?
c93017e87d73.target: 让我们用一个问题来回答一个问题。为什么一个1x1的正方形对角线长度为$\sqrt{2} = 1.414...$（一个无理数）？

fdc64cba99a9.source: when nature doesn't line up with our number system. I don't have a good intuition. My hunch is simple rules (1x1 square + Pythagorean Theorem) can still lead to complex outcomes.
fdc64cba99a9.target: 当自然界与我们的数字系统不一致时。我没有很好的直觉。我的直觉是，简单的规则（1x1正方形 + 勾股定理）仍然可能导致复杂的结果。

f18780c14c7a.source: I've been tricky. Previously, I said "imagine it takes sine 10 seconds from 0 to max". And now it's pi seconds from 0 to max back to 0? What gives?
f18780c14c7a.target: 我一直在耍花招。之前我说“想象正弦从0到最大值需要10秒”。而现在是从0到最大值再回到0需要π秒？怎么回事？

814f3a29d69c.source: So, we use sin(n*x) to get a sine wave cycling as fast as we need. Often, the phrase "sine wave" is referencing the general shape and not a specific speed.
814f3a29d69c.target: 所以，我们使用sin(n*x)来得到按所需速度循环的正弦波。通常，“正弦波”这个短语指的是大致形状，而不是特定速度。

2f4084c6c5e2.source: That's a brainful -- take a break if you need it. Hopefully, sine is emerging as its own pattern. Now let's
2f4084c6c5e2.target: 这信息量很大——如果需要就休息一下。希望正弦正逐渐显现出它自己的模式。现在让我们

e3d638c83b50.source: by seeing how common definitions of sine connect.
e3d638c83b50.target: 通过看看正弦的常见定义是如何联系在一起的。

0c4fb168ff98.source: Sine was first found in triangles. You may remember "SOH
0c4fb168ff98.target: 正弦最初出现在三角形中。你可能记得“SOH

5f90915cb343.source: For a right triangle with angle x, sin(x) is the length of the opposite side divided by the hypotenuse. If we make the hypotenuse 1, we can simplify to:
5f90915cb343.target: 对于角度为x的直角三角形，sin(x)是对边长度除以斜边。如果我们让斜边为1，就可以简化成：

fa67a2dafd40.source: And with more cleverness, we can draw our triangles with hypotenuse 1 in a circle with radius 1:
fa67a2dafd40.target: 再巧妙一点，我们可以将斜边为1的三角形画在半径为1的圆中：

52be1c26d2af.source: Voila! A circle containing all possible right triangles (since they can be scaled up using similarity). For example:
52be1c26d2af.target: 瞧！一个包含所有可能直角三角形的圆（因为它们可以通过相似性缩放）。例如：

5cbc02c85f22.source: These direct manipulations are great for construction (the
5cbc02c85f22.target: 这些直接的操作对于构造很有用（

e03e58e8a86f.source: won't calculate themselves). Unfortunately, after thousands of years we start thinking the
e03e58e8a86f.target: 不会自己计算）。不幸的是，几千年后我们开始认为

530c952c12f6.source: of sine is the height of a triangle. No no, it's a shape that
530c952c12f6.target: 正弦就是三角形的高度。不，不，它是一个在圆（和三角形）中

6d9cdf669d78.source: Realistically, for many problems we go into "geometry mode" and start thinking "sine = height" to speed through things. That's fine -- just don't get stuck there.
6d9cdf669d78.target: 实际上，对于许多问题，我们会进入“几何模式”并开始思考“正弦 = 高度”来快速处理。这没问题——只是不要卡在那里。

c06b46f8c501.source: I've avoided the elephant in the room:
c06b46f8c501.target: 我回避了房间里的大象：

87364f4c6918.source: Is my calculator drawing a circle and measuring it?
87364f4c6918.target: 我的计算器是在画一个圆并测量它吗？

8b89fbf32cbc.source: Glad to rile you up. Here's the circle-less secret of sine:
8b89fbf32cbc.target: 很高兴激怒你。这是正弦无圆的秘密：

8c727b1cec61.source: Using our bank account metaphor: Imagine a perverse boss who gives you a raise the exact
8c727b1cec61.target: 用我们的银行账户比喻：想象一个变态的老板，他给你的加薪幅度正好等于

865b4f7e169f.source: of your current bank account! If you have \$50 in the bank, then your raise next week is  \$50. Of course, your income might be \$75/week, so you'll still be earning some money  \$75 - \$50 for that week), but eventually your balance will decrease as the "raises" overpower your income.
865b4f7e169f.target: 你当前的银行存款！如果你的银行存款是 \$50，那么下周你的加薪就是 \$50。当然，你的收入可能是 \$75/周，所以你还是能赚一些钱（该周 \$75 - \$50），但最终当“加薪”超过你的收入时，你的余额会减少。

b851d6a15fe4.source: But never fear! Once your account hits negative (say you're at  \$50), then your boss gives a legit \$50/week raise. Again, your income might be negative, but eventually the raises will overpower it.
b851d6a15fe4.target: 但别担心！一旦你的账户变成负数（比如 -\$50），你的老板就会给你合法地加薪 \$50/周。同样，你的收入可能是负数，但最终加薪会超过它。

d5339e11e312.source: This constant pull towards the center keeps the cycle going: when you rise up, the "pull" conspires to pull you in again. It also explains why neutral is the max speed for sine: If you are at the max, you begin falling and accumulating more and more "negative raises" as you plummet. As you pass through then neutral point you are feeling all the negative raises possible (once you cross, you'll start getting positive raises and slowing down).
d5339e11e312.target: 这种向中心的持续拉力维持着循环：当你上升时，“拉力”会合谋把你拉回来。这也解释了为什么中性点是正弦的最大速度：如果你处于最大值，你开始下降，并在下降过程中积累越来越多的“负加薪”。当你经过中性点时，你感受到了所有可能的负加薪（一旦你越过，你将开始获得正加薪并减速）。

f90b81f3a8d8.source: By the way: since sine is acceleration opposite to your current position, and a circle is made up of a horizontal and vertical sine... you got it! Circular motion can be described as "a constant pull opposite your current position, towards your horizontal and vertical center".
f90b81f3a8d8.target: 顺便说一句：因为正弦是与你当前位置相反的加速度，而圆由水平和垂直的正弦组成……你说对了！圆周运动可以描述为“一个与你当前位置相反的、朝向你的水平和垂直中心的恒定拉力”。

1249e60d97b4.source: Let's describe sine with calculus. Like e, we can break sine into smaller effects:
1249e60d97b4.target: 让我们用微积分来描述正弦。像 e 一样，我们可以将正弦分解为更小的效应：

31b54d8adb6e.source: How should we think about this? See how each effect above changes our distance from center:
31b54d8adb6e.target: 我们该如何思考这个问题？看看上述每个效应如何改变我们与中心的距离：

3b1baffa495f.source: Seeing how acceleration impacts distance is like seeing how a raise hits your bank account. The "raise" must change your income, and your income changes your bank account (two integrals "up the chain").
3b1baffa495f.target: 理解加速度如何影响距离，就像看加薪如何影响你的银行账户。“加薪”必须改变你的收入，而你的收入又改变你的银行账户（沿着链条向上积分两次）。

48fabf18eece.source: So, after "x" seconds we might guess that sine is "x" (initial impulse) minus $\frac{x^3}{3!}$ (effect of the acceleration):
48fabf18eece.target: 所以，经过“x”秒后，我们可能会猜测正弦是“x”（初始冲量）减去 $\frac{x^3}{3!}$（加速度的效应）：

1e8960b1067e.source: Something's wrong -- sine doesn't nosedive! With e, we saw that "interest earns interest" and sine is similar. The "restoring force" changes our distance by $\frac{-x^3}{3!}$, which creates
1e8960b1067e.target: 有点不对劲——正弦并没有俯冲！对于 e，我们看到“利息生利息”，正弦也类似。“恢复力”将我们的距离改变为 $\frac{-x^3}{3!}$，这又产生了

66ee543ed207.source: restoring force to consider. Consider a spring: the pull that yanks you down goes too far, which shoots you downward and creates
66ee543ed207.target: 需要考虑的恢复力。考虑一个弹簧：把你往下拉的力太大，导致你向下冲并产生

6f4ed4b0145a.source: pull to bring you up (which again goes too far). Springs are crazy!
6f4ed4b0145a.target: 把你向上拉的力（同样也太大）。弹簧真是疯狂！

a974611bd54e.source: We need to consider every restoring force:
a974611bd54e.target: 我们需要考虑每一个恢复力：

e979516a9c05.source: Just like e, sine can be described with an infinite series:
e979516a9c05.target: 就像 e 一样，正弦可以用无穷级数来描述：

a860c9d88a37.source: I saw this formula a lot, but it only clicked when I saw sine as a
a860c9d88a37.target: 我经常看到这个公式，但只有当我将正弦视为

8598473fcb53.source: . The initial push (y = x, going positive) is eventually overcome by a restoring force (which pulls us negative), which is overpowered by its own restoring force (which pulls us positive), and so on.
8598473fcb53.target: 时才真正理解。初始推动（y = x，正向）最终被恢复力（把我们拉向负方向）所压倒，而这个恢复力又被它自己的恢复力（把我们拉回正方向）所压倒，如此循环。

3d3a29822fd6.source: Cosine is just a shifted sine, and is fun (yes!) now that we understand sine:
3d3a29822fd6.target: 余弦只是一个平移了的正弦，既然我们理解了正弦，它也很好玩（是的！）：

427ec5511e78.source: So cosine just starts off... sitting there at 1. We let the restoring force do the work:
427ec5511e78.target: 所以余弦一开始就……坐在那里等于 1。我们让恢复力来做功：

5823ba5ae667.source: Again, we integrate -1 twice to get $\frac{-x^2}{2!}$. But this kicks off another restoring force, which kicks off another, and before you know it:
5823ba5ae667.target: 同样，我们对 -1 积分两次得到 $\frac{-x^2}{2!}$。但这又引发了另一个恢复力，再引发另一个，不知不觉中：

fb110cbfc96c.source: We've described sine's behavior with specific equations. A more succinct way (
fb110cbfc96c.target: 我们用特定的方程描述了正弦函数的行为。一种更简洁的方式（

90db09f70582.target: 这个优美的表达式表明：

424ee9ff7db8.source: Both sine and cosine make this true. I first hated this definition; it's so divorced from a visualization. I didn't realize it described the essence of sine, "acceleration opposite your position".
424ee9ff7db8.target: 正弦和余弦都满足这一性质。我最初讨厌这一定义；它太脱离直观形象了。我没有意识到它描述了正弦的本质，即“加速度与位置相反”。

fa9138fb6126.source: And remember how sine and e are connected? Well, $e^x$ can be be described by (
fa9138fb6126.target: 还记得正弦和e是如何联系的吗？嗯，$e^x$可以用（

40cef4bd2639.source: The same equation with a positive sign ("acceleration equal to your position")! When sine is "the height of a circle" it's really hard to make the connection to e.
40cef4bd2639.target: 同样的方程，只是符号为正（“加速度等于你的位置”）！当正弦被视为“圆的高度”时，很难将其与e联系起来。

9c86c85aca75.source: One of my great mathematical regrets is not learning differential equations. But I want to, and I suspect having an intuition for sine and e will be crucial.
9c86c85aca75.target: 我最大的数学遗憾之一是没有学习微分方程。但我希望学习，而且我怀疑对正弦和e的直觉会至关重要。

aefc0bbd6bde.source: The goal is to move sine from some mathematical trivia ("part of a circle") to its own shape:
aefc0bbd6bde.target: 目标是让正弦从某种数学琐事（“圆的一部分”）转变为它自身的形态：

4f78fdf61d29.target: , 正弦...），它们可以被混搭成一道美味的数学沙拉。享受吧！

c748f47e7ccb.target: 使用这种方法，Alistair MacDonald制作了一个

008081d58bd4.source: use Outlook Express as a middleman

4dd8481fb088.source: keep a directory for all the various installers

5bb1d232c780.target: 并下载到Outlook Express或Thunderbird中。你仍然可以将邮件保留在服务器上，并从Gmail中阅读。

e229db9f088a.source: in order to import into Thunderbird. Crazy, I know.

34dae21814fb.source: Automatic: Pretty much. Mail is downloaded will “automatically” be downloaded every time you open Thunderbird or Outlook Express. I’ve started using Thunderbird more and more — I still enjoy the desktop mail experience.
