4df0997aba32.source: I have a directory (c:tools) with a bunch of portable applications. You’d be surprised how many apps just work without a proper install. I have a subset of these on a USB drive.
4df0997aba32.target: 我有一个目录（c:tools），里面有一堆便携应用程序。你会惊讶于有多少应用程序无需正确安装就能运行。我把其中一部分放在U盘上。

48456dd23f4c.source: of my start menu to remind myself what I have installed. For most apps, if you move to a machine and can’t remember an app, you probably didn’t use it that much. Here’s how to make a list of your start menu and program files:
48456dd23f4c.target: 在我的开始菜单中，提醒自己安装了哪些程序。对于大多数应用程序，如果你换台机器却想不起某个程序，那说明你很可能并不经常使用它。以下是如何列出开始菜单和程序文件的方法：

3b93225283ff.source: (c:download). I periodically back this up to CD (last “backup” was sometime last year) so I can re-install a new machine quickly. Realistically, I don’t mind losing the installers since I want the latest version anyway.
3b93225283ff.target: （c:download）。我会定期将其备份到CD上（上次“备份”是去年某个时候），以便快速重新安装新机器。实际上，我并不介意丢失安装程序，因为我总是想要最新版本。

84673f37f5ac.source: to get older (aka faster, simpler, less annoying) versions of software like Acrobat Reader or AOL Instant Messenger.
84673f37f5ac.target: 获取较旧（即更快、更简单、更不烦人）版本的软件，如Acrobat Reader或AOL Instant Messenger。

35d3182b1525.source: Backing Up Code and Websites You’ve Made
35d3182b1525.target: 备份您制作的代码和网站

e24ff6fd3017.source: Backing up data is like flossing – we know we should, we just don’t get around to it as much as we’d like. Nobody will admit it, though ;).
e24ff6fd3017.target: 备份数据就像用牙线洁牙——我们知道应该做，但就是没能像我们希望的那样经常去做。不过，没有人会承认这一点 ;)。

bedf95b350ec.source: Here are the rules of backup club:
bedf95b350ec.target: 以下是备份俱乐部规则：

d76fc2912c2c.source: You might hear that the full-blown solution is to keep multiple versions of files around so if one got corrupted by a virus 3 months ago, you could restore to a version 3 months + 1 day old. Also, you need to make your backups in triplicate, sending each to geographically dispere locations along with one set of tapes constantly in the mail (re-mail it to yourself once you receive them).
d76fc2912c2c.target: 你可能会听说，完整的解决方案是保留文件的多个版本，这样如果3个月前某个文件被病毒感染，你可以恢复到3个月零1天前的版本。此外，你需要进行三份备份，分别发送到地理上分散的位置，同时始终有一组磁带在邮寄中（收到后重新邮寄给自己）。

b4bdcd6a417f.source: Yeah, right. Just getting a redundant copy would be great for most people — don’t ask for the moon. Realistically, the best backup system is the one you actually use. So here’s the system I actually use.
b4bdcd6a417f.target: 嗯，没错。对大多数人来说，只要有一个冗余副本就很好了——别要求太多。实际上，最好的备份系统就是你真正在用的那个。所以，以下是我实际使用的系统。

b8dd417837a6.source: Why would you backup email? Or Web-based email?
b8dd417837a6.target: 为什么要备份电子邮件？或者基于网页的电子邮件？

2160641b69a4.source: . To backup gmail, do the following:
2160641b69a4.target: 要备份Gmail，请执行以下操作：

a4af66269cb9.source: Other providers: Hopefully they provide POP or IMAP access so you can download messages as above. Otherwise, consider forwarding your important messages. It’s a pain, I know, so be thankful if your email provider supports POP or IMAP.
a4af66269cb9.target: 其他提供商：希望它们提供POP或IMAP访问，以便你可以像上面那样下载邮件。否则，考虑转发你的重要邮件。我知道这很麻烦，所以如果你的电子邮件提供商支持POP或IMAP，那就谢天谢地了。

ca690a0febda.source: is an easy way to keep directories in sync. You can even access files online if one of your computers is up.
ca690a0febda.target: 是一种保持目录同步的简单方法。如果你的某台电脑在线，你甚至可以在线访问文件。

5b8abce51ffd.source: I have my photos & personal notes sync’d between a laptop and desktop. If either is online, they start syncing.
5b8abce51ffd.target: 我的照片和个人笔记在笔记本电脑和台式机之间同步。如果其中一台在线，它们就会开始同步。

f03077608ca2.source: Have a friend? (I know you do, somewhere). Agree to have a common foldershare folder with a subdirectory for each of you. Each person drops their non-confidential files (or encrypted confidential files) in the backup folder. Everyone backs each other’s stuff up. Haven’t tried this yet, due to the aforementioned friend requirement.
f03077608ca2.target: 有朋友吗？（我知道你有的，在某个地方）。同意设置一个共同的文件夹共享，每个人都有一个子目录。每个人将他们的非机密文件（或加密的机密文件）放入备份文件夹。每个人都备份其他人的东西。由于前面提到的朋友要求，我还没试过这个。

34cb9a0bdce7.source: is a simple tool that can copy one folder to another, on a schedule. I use this to copy my personal notes to my USB drive (always plugged in) on a schedule. Foldershare wouldn’t work because the USB drive is not actually a computer.
34cb9a0bdce7.target: 是一个简单的工具，可以按计划将一个文件夹复制到另一个文件夹。我用它来按计划将我的个人笔记复制到U盘（总是插着）。Foldershare不行，因为U盘实际上不是一台电脑。

df876c26e5dc.source: These are both automatic (foldershare more so – it transfers in the background), and fairly easy to setup. Again, this is only a one-time setup.
df876c26e5dc.target: 这两种都是自动的（Foldershare更是如此——它在后台传输），而且设置相当简单。同样，这只需要一次性设置。

104ed1650464.source: I haven’t found a great way to backup programs and settings. Luckily they are the most replacable of the bunch. Here’s what I use:
104ed1650464.target: 我还没有找到一个好的方法来备份程序和设置。幸运的是，它们是所有东西中最容易替换的。以下是我使用的方法：

1dfba0e0a15a.source: These techniques aren’t automatic but are fairly easy.
1dfba0e0a15a.target: 这些技术不是自动的，但相当简单。

8f689e711805.source: Now these suckers aren’t replaceable, and if they change often (they do), you really want to use some type of version control.
8f689e711805.target: 现在这些家伙是不可替换的，而且如果它们经常变化（确实如此），你真的很想使用某种版本控制。

556531f5e2ed.source: I use subversion to backup and update the code, text and images on
556531f5e2ed.target: 我使用Subversion来备份和更新代码、文本和图片，网址为

d07d924e70f2.source: . I have a few other works-in-progress with their own repos.
d07d924e70f2.target: 。我还有几个正在进行中的项目，各有自己的仓库。

d344bfad05f6.source: However, I don’t backup the backup repository. Is it a theoretically problem? Yes. Does it bother me? Not too much – it’s on my list of upgrades.
d344bfad05f6.target: 不过，我没有备份备份仓库。这理论上是个问题吗？是的。它困扰我吗？不太多——这在我的升级清单上。

9f115d1960b8.source: Using source control is the most difficult backup strategy, but gives you the most power. Trust me, for the amount of time you spent on your code, it’s worth it.
9f115d1960b8.target: 使用版本控制是最难的备份策略，但它能给你最大的能力。相信我，基于你在代码上花费的时间，这是值得的。

5b49d8f460b2.source: Ah, blogging – I’m pretty new to this. WordPress has a database backup plugin which, for some reason, isn’t working for me. So I’m going to dump the raw sql periodically:
5b49d8f460b2.target: 啊，博客——我对这个还比较陌生。WordPress有一个数据库备份插件，但出于某种原因，我无法让它正常工作。所以我打算定期导出原始SQL：

77c126e328f7.source: You can also email yourself a file periodically using PHP. I’ve put both tasks into a script that runs as a cron job, so I get a database backup every day.
77c126e328f7.target: 你也可以使用PHP定期通过邮件发送文件给自己。我已经将这两个任务放在一个脚本中，作为cron作业运行，这样我每天都能得到数据库备份。

55a84166e420.source: between -p and the password. You can turn this into a shell script and have a cron job to email it to yourself every night.
55a84166e420.target: -p和密码之间没有空格。你可以将其转化为shell脚本，并通过cron作业每晚将其通过邮件发送给自己。

2c14a9deafcd.source: You might also want to consider version-controlling your theme in case it changes. I haven’t done this yet, so this again falls in the theoretical-fixes category. Again, not too hard to remedy, but you need to watch out for setting files and caches that shouldn’t be saved.
2c14a9deafcd.target: 你可能还想考虑对主题进行版本控制，以防它发生变化。我还没有这样做，所以这再次属于理论修复类别。同样，修复起来不难，但需要注意设置文件和缓存不应该被保存。

53c3b145c89e.source: Depending on the plugins you have, this can be easy and automatic. For a blog containing your memories, again, it’s worth it.
53c3b145c89e.target: 根据你拥有的插件，这可能是简单且自动的。对于一个包含你回忆的博客，这再次是值得的。

25074ce1c234.source: It’s tough to start a backup program before you really need it (it’s a bit like exercise that way). But try, try, try, and use the easy techniques above. It brings you peace of mind. Take even a small step each week, like backing up your web-based email.
25074ce1c234.target: 在真正需要备份程序之前开始实施它很难（有点像锻炼）。但请尝试、尝试、再尝试，并使用上面的简单技巧。它能让你安心。即使每周迈出一小步，比如备份你的网络邮件。

7ebfd8ba7e42.source: The Law of Interactions: The whole is based on the parts and the interaction between them.
7ebfd8ba7e42.target: 交互定律：整体基于各部分及其之间的相互作用。

5ceaa11ea896.target: 完全对齐或完全不对齐

15b0c83053ad.source: Add to find the total contribution

84d84dafb743.target: 转换为等效的“单个部分”

8dc9840c0149.source: Perfect alignment means they help 100% (5 and 3)
8dc9840c0149.target: 完美对齐意味着它们100%助力（5和3）

1d1a0834ceff.source: Perfect mis-alignment means they hurt 100% (5 and -3)
1d1a0834ceff.target: 完美错位意味着它们100%损害（5和-3）

f79313fe85dd.source: Partial alignment or mis-alignment means they help or hurt by a percentage
f79313fe85dd.target: 部分对齐或错位意味着它们按百分比助力或损害

7aa35e882307.source: No alignment means they ignore each other
7aa35e882307.target: 无对齐意味着它们互不影响

bdae2ae89630.source: $2ab = 2 \cdot 10 \cdot 20 = 400$, but we need to adjust by the interaction factor. That is $\cos(45) = .707$, so the real interaction factor is $400 \cdot .707 = 282.8$
bdae2ae89630.target: $2ab = 2 \cdot 10 \cdot 20 = 400$, 但我们需要根据交互因子进行调整。即 $\cos(45) = .707$，所以实际交互因子为 $400 \cdot .707 = 282.8$

2f9ac1c3ec8c.source: Fully mis-aligned: 10 – 20 = -10 (pointing in direction of B).

dabda227ba92.source: Full mis-alignment: $a^2 + b^2 – 2ab\cos(\theta) =  100 + 400 + 400\cos(180) = 100$ which means $c = \sqrt{100} = 10$ (pointing backwards).

a9cc27d7e765.source: Imagine an assistant chef whose interactions may (or may not) be helpful.
a9cc27d7e765.target: 想象一个助理厨师，他的互动可能有帮助也可能没有。

a4101311cbf1.source: Suppose $a = 10$ and $b = 20$ in a triangle. If they are aligned 45-degrees, their interaction is $a^2 + b^2 + 2ab\cos(45) = 782.8$ and the remaining side is $\sqrt{782.8} = 27.97$ units long.
a4101311cbf1.target: 假设三角形中 $a = 10$ 且 $b = 20$。如果它们以45度对齐，它们的交互是 $a^2 + b^2 + 2ab\cos(45) = 782.8$，剩余边长为 $\sqrt{782.8} = 27.97$ 单位。

839bdf399693.source: Triangle with internal angle C: $c^2 = a^2 + b^2 – 2ab\cos(C) $
839bdf399693.target: 内角为C的三角形：$c^2 = a^2 + b^2 – 2ab\cos(C) $

fa57e00b8fcf.source: Where’d you put the whisk again?
fa57e00b8fcf.target: 你把打蛋器又放哪儿了？

8c670185b565.source: I’m peeling carrots anyway, use some of mine.
8c670185b565.target: 反正我在削胡萝卜，用我的一些吧。

fe0205948e71.source: The Law of Cosines is presented as a geometric result that relates the parts of a triangle:
fe0205948e71.target: 余弦定理被表述为一个几何结果，它关联三角形的各个部分：

91122081a0bb.source: While true, there’s a deeper principle at work.
91122081a0bb.target: 虽然如此，但有一个更深的原理在起作用。

3e950cbe4166.source: The wording “Law of Cosines” gets you thinking about the mechanics of the formula, not what it means. Part of my
3e950cbe4166.target: “余弦定理”这个措辞让你思考公式的机制，而不是它的含义。我的部分

b62ad509c4b1.source: is rewording ideas into ones that make sense.
b62ad509c4b1.target: 是将想法重新表述为有意义的表述。

62465e78c3c6.source: The Law of Cosines, after cranking through geometric steps we’re prone to forget, looks like $c^2 = a^2 + b^2 – 2ab\cos(C)$.
62465e78c3c6.target: 余弦定理，经过我们容易忘记的几何推导步骤后，看起来是这样的：$c^2 = a^2 + b^2 – 2ab\cos(C)$。

a732835fa7f8.source: This is suspiciously like the expansion that if $c = (a + b)$, then $c^2 = a^2 + b^2 + 2ab$
a732835fa7f8.target: 这看起来就像展开式：如果 $c = (a + b)$，那么 $c^2 = a^2 + b^2 + 2ab$。

2b3aa2bb6c00.source: The difference is that $2ab$ has an extra factor, $\cos(C)$, which measures the “actual overlap percentage” ($2ab$ assumes we fully overlap, i.e. where $\cos(C) = 1$).
2b3aa2bb6c00.target: 不同之处在于 $2ab$ 多了一个因子 $\cos(C)$，它衡量“实际重叠百分比”（$2ab$ 假设完全重叠，即 $\cos(C) = 1$）。

a4444d24d35d.source: So, the Law of Cosines is really a generalization of how $c^2 = (a + b)^2$ expands when components aren’t fully lined up. We’re treating geometric lines as terms in an algebraic expansion.
a4444d24d35d.target: 所以，余弦定理实际上是 $c^2 = (a + b)^2$ 在分量未完全对齐时展开方式的推广。我们是在将几何线段视为代数展开中的项。

2ffbeff7f0f7.source: Imagine a restaurant with a single chef, Alice. She’s overworked, so Bob is hired as her assistant (sous chef).
2ffbeff7f0f7.target: 想象一家餐厅只有一位厨师Alice。她工作过度，所以Bob被聘为她的助手（副厨师）。

65462741d4f1.source: Based on Alice’s current performance, and Bob’s performance in his interview, what happens when they work together?
65462741d4f1.target: 基于Alice目前的表现和Bob在面试中的表现，他们合作时会发生什么？

113749be5f42.source: Surely the new result must be their combined effort:
113749be5f42.target: 新结果肯定是他们共同努力的成果：

4c2464ce90dc.source: Hah! Office workers everywhere are rolling their eyes. You can’t just assume people contribute identically when they’re put together: there are interactions to account for.
4c2464ce90dc.target: 哈！世界各地的上班族都会翻白眼。你不能想当然地认为人们组合在一起时贡献是相同的：还要考虑相互作用。

e4fad73d17a1.source: Beyond their individual contributions, the two might slow each other down (
e4fad73d17a1.target: 除了各自的贡献，两人还可能互相拖累（

f78eb8e52383.source: ), or find ways to work together (

b504857dc3d4.source: In a system with several parts, start with the individual contributions and then ask if their interaction will:
b504857dc3d4.target: 在一个由多个部分组成的系统中，首先考虑各自的贡献，然后问他们的相互作用是否：

c5b246680114.source: The original idea that “Total = Alice + Bob” is more generally expressed as:
c5b246680114.target: “总计 = Alice + Bob”这个原始想法更一般地表示为：

e67380c9f8b5.source: of participants (Alice, Bob) from the result of their interaction.
e67380c9f8b5.target: 参与者（Alice, Bob）与他们的相互作用结果分开。

503a5d5d4ee1.source: Take the numbers 5 and 3. We can write them like so:
503a5d5d4ee1.target: 以数字5和3为例。我们可以这样写：

1d7f164badc0.source: and we’re pretty sure they combine to make 8. But is there another way to get that conclusion?
1d7f164badc0.target: 并且我们确信它们加起来等于8。但有没有其他方法得出这个结论呢？

f2c203c839b0.source: Yes: we multiply. Beyond repeated counting, multiplication shows what happens when the parts of a system interact:
f2c203c839b0.target: 有：乘法。除了重复计数，乘法展示了系统各部分相互作用时的结果：

6cfb00362429.source: We’ve gone from “parts view”, $(5, 3)$, to “interaction view”, $(5 + 3)^2$. The result of interaction mode says the system would result in 64 if it
6cfb00362429.target: 我们从“部分视图”$(5, 3)$ 转换到“相互作用视图”$(5 + 3)^2$。相互作用模式的结果表明，如果系统

8ec43876af54.target: 与自身相互作用，结果将是64。

851c85294080.source: One caveat: when going to interaction view, we wrote down $(5 + 3)(5 + 3)$, but we can’t simplify $(5 + 3) = 8$ on the outset. We’re using addition for bookkeeping until multiplication can combine the parts.
851c85294080.target: 一个注意事项：当进入相互作用视角时，我们写下了$(5 + 3)(5 + 3)$，但不能一开始就简化$(5 + 3) = 8$。我们使用加法来记账，直到乘法能将各部分合并。

199b52b9c365.source: Oh, another caveat: why can we just add the interactions, but not the parts? Great question. The individual parts might be pointing in different dimensions, and don’t line up nicely on the same scale. The interacting parts turn into
199b52b9c365.target: 哦，另一个注意事项：为什么我们只能相加相互作用，而不能相加各部分？好问题。各个部分可能指向不同的维度，不能很好地在同一尺度上对齐。相互作用的各部分变成了

ced898be1e40.source: , which can be combined to the same result no matter the orientation.
ced898be1e40.target: ，无论方向如何，都能组合成相同的结果。

fb9f812aae9d.source: (I’ll investigate this concept more in a follow-up. It’s a neat idea that area is a generic, easily combinable quantity but individual paths are not.)
fb9f812aae9d.target: （我将在后续文章中进一步探讨这个概念。面积是一种通用的、易于合并的量，而单个路径则不然，这是一个巧妙的想法。）

1b38240efdc7.source: Simple setups like (5, 3) are easy to think through, like eyeballing $2x + 3 = 7$ and guessing $x = 2$. But a more complex scenario like $x^2 + 3x = 15$ requires a systematic approach.
1b38240efdc7.target: 像(5, 3)这样的简单设置容易思考，就像目测$2x + 3 = 7$并猜测$x = 2$。但更复杂的场景，比如$x^2 + 3x = 15$，则需要系统的方法。

d3111b678060.source: The Law of Cosines is a systematic approach to working through the parts:
d3111b678060.target: 余弦定理(Law of Cosines)是一种处理各部分的系统方法：

f30d306123a9.source: The last step is often implied. Once we’ve merged the jumble of interactions, we want the
f30d306123a9.target: 最后一步通常是隐含的。一旦合并了杂乱无章的相互作用，我们想要得到

13b6deccfa4b.source: part that could represent the entire system. Is there a single person (Charlie) whose efforts are identical to that of Alice and Bob working together?
13b6deccfa4b.target: 能够代表整个系统的部分。是否存在一个人（查理），其努力等于爱丽丝和鲍勃合作的总和？

27e681b9c88b.source: The Law of Cosines gives us a way to find Charlie.
27e681b9c88b.target: 余弦定理给了我们找到查理的方法。

90c8e1cf5ba0.source: When two parts interact, they can help, hurt, or ignore each other:
90c8e1cf5ba0.target: 当两部分相互作用时，它们可以互相帮助、损害或忽略：

9040847f5624.source: How do we measure alignment? With cosine.
9040847f5624.target: 我们如何衡量对齐程度？用余弦(cosine)。

ed7292abd0ce.source: an angle moves along the ground.

f021f047f004.source: A 0-degree angle follows the ground perfectly (100%), and moving vertically doesn’t follow it at all (0%). Other angles are a fraction in-between.
f021f047f004.target: 0度角完全沿地面（100%），垂直移动则完全不沿地面（0%）。其他角度则是介于两者之间的分数。

17512cb959ac.source: If the parts in our system can be written as paths, and we know the angle between them is theta ($\theta$), then we can measure the overlap with cosine. One path acts as the ground, and the other is the path we’re following:
17512cb959ac.target: 如果系统中的各部分可以写成路径，并且我们知道它们之间的夹角为θ($\theta$)，那么我们可以用余弦来测量重叠。一条路径作为地面，另一条是我们跟随的路径：

a166e62876af.source: When paths are perfectly aligned, their full strength is used ($ab$ and $ba$). The interaction factor $\cos(\theta)$ modifies that strength to show much they
a166e62876af.target: 当路径完全对齐时，它们会发挥全部力量（$ab$和$ba$）。相互作用因子$\cos(\theta)$修正了该力量，以显示它们

bb181668ff06.source: So, our jumble of interactions becomes:
bb181668ff06.target: 因此，我们杂乱无章的相互作用变为：

a6b60f5ec5e3.source: Phew! And that’s the Law of Cosines: collect every interaction, account for the alignment, and simplify it to a single part. (The formula is usually written without the square root, but usually you want $c$, not $c^2$.)
a6b60f5ec5e3.target: 呼！这就是余弦定理：收集每一次相互作用，考虑对齐程度，并简化为一个单一的部分。（公式通常写时不带平方根，但你通常想要的是$c$，而不是$c^2$。）

0cd2cfc81df1.source: Now, why is the Law of Cosines often written with a negative sign? Well, the assumption is that in a typical triangle, a small
0cd2cfc81df1.target: 现在，为什么余弦定理通常用负号来写？嗯，假设在一个典型的三角形中，一个小的

96676272bcb4.source: angle $C$ means the sides are negatively aligned, while theta ($\theta$) is an
96676272bcb4.target: 角 $C$ 意味着边是负对齐的，而 theta（$\theta$）是一个

5019a13c9b93.source: Similarly, a large internal angle means the sides are positively aligned, and will help each other. Typically, a small angle means you’re moving in the same direction, but this internal/external difference means we reverse the sign.
5019a13c9b93.target: 类似地，大的内角意味着边是正对齐的，并且会互相帮助。通常，小角意味着你在同一方向移动，但这种内/外角的差异导致我们反转符号。

bddb9d3fd53b.source: Personally, I don’t memorize whether there’s a positive or negative sign: I think about whether the parts will help or hurt each other in the scenario, and make the interaction positive or negative. Don’t be a slave to the formula.
bddb9d3fd53b.target: 就我个人而言，我不会记住是正号还是负号：我会思考在场景中各个部分会互相帮助还是互相伤害，从而将相互作用定为正或负。不要做公式的奴隶。

e4fcf07d5df9.source: and the equivalent single side (c) is:

78773108fb36.target: 再次，我们需要余弦定理。我们可以看到角相互对抗，因此相互作用将为负：

fb8a176e5cda.source: Our intuition says this arrangement should be

59b297a517e4.source: When our “triangle” has an angle of 0 degrees (or 180), all the parts are lying flat. Here, the parts are in the same dimension, and can be treated as regular numbers:

1e2a7f245a88.source: Again, we shouldn’t robotically follow the formula: have a rough idea what the result should be, and think through the calculations. (“The overall interaction is this, so the individual side would that…”).
1e2a7f245a88.target: 再次，我们不应该机械地套用公式：对结果应该有一个大致的想法，并仔细思考计算。（“总的相互作用是这样，所以单边应该是那样……”）。

d7948974e0f7.source: Thinking of interactions is one interpretation: next time, we’ll see it as the Law of Projections.
d7948974e0f7.target: 将相互作用视为一种解释：下次，我们会把它看作投影定理。

4ee45fad147d.target: 如果我们知道各部分不会相互作用，就可以忽略相互作用效应。然而，

391deb55ae99.source: are still there and must be combined: $a^2$ and $b^2$ are fine, but the crossover terms $ab$ and $ba$ disappear.

8687ecf50901.source: Here’s another version of the Pythagorean Theorem. We can’t combine $a$ and $b$ directly, so combine their interactions and reduce them to a single part:
8687ecf50901.target: 这是毕达哥拉斯定理的另一个版本。我们不能直接合并 $a$ 和 $b$，所以合并它们的相互作用并将它们简化为一个部分：

cf792ffd4d41.source: You might be hankering for a geometric proof. Here’s one from
cf792ffd4d41.target: 你可能渴望一个几何证明。这里有一个来自

7cb4789794ad.source: , based on a paper by Knuth:

e0b911280dd5.source: The insight is that we take our original $a-b-c$ triangle and scale it by $a$ (giving the $a^2-ab-ac$ triangle) and $b$ (giving the $ab-b^2-bc$ triangle). These two triangles build a larger, similar triangle $ac-bc-c^2$, and with some trig, the bottom portion can be shown to equal $a^2 + b^2 – 2ab\cos(\theta)$.
e0b911280dd5.target: 关键在于我们取原始的 $a-b-c$ 三角形，将其缩放 $a$ 倍（得到 $a^2-ab-ac$ 三角形）和 $b$ 倍（得到 $ab-b^2-bc$ 三角形）。这两个三角形构建了一个更大的相似三角形 $ac-bc-c^2$，通过一些三角学，底部部分可以证明等于 $a^2 + b^2 – 2ab\cos(\theta)$。

76c08157d9a0.source: While interesting, I don’t like these types of proofs up front. The Law of Cosines is about interactions, not re-arranging triangles. Does this explanation get you thinking about what cosine represents? About when it should be positive, negative, or zero?
76c08157d9a0.target: 虽然有趣，但我不喜欢这种前置的证明。余弦定理是关于相互作用的，而不是重新排列三角形。这个解释是否让你思考余弦代表什么？思考它何时应为正、负或零？

ea36fa5e6b36.source: Imagine sides A and B are pointing in the same direction along the horizontal number line. This means $c = a + b$ and the Law of Cosines reduces to:
ea36fa5e6b36.target: 想象边 A 和 B 沿着水平数轴指向同一方向。这意味着 $c = a + b$ 且余弦定理简化为：

2da1bbd7ab83.source: So, for a 180-degree interior angle, we get a regular algebraic statement. This helps me remember, on the fly, when to add vs. subtract. We add $2ab\cos(\theta)$ when the interior angle is large.
2da1bbd7ab83.target: 所以，对于 180 度的内角，我们得到一个常规的代数陈述。这帮助我在现场记住何时加何时减。当内角大时，我们加上 $2ab\cos(\theta)$。

603e8cd0223a.source: percentage height compared to the max possible

73e6b576c42b.source: Each angle & side can independently find the circle that wraps up the whole triangle.

8aeabce380fc.source: We can lock down angle $B$ and side $b$, and trace out a circle of possibilities
8aeabce380fc.target: 我们可以锁定角 $B$ 和边 $b$，并描绘出一个可能的圆。

d5f400c1f964.source: We can lock down angle $C$ and side $c$, and trace out a circle of possibilities
d5f400c1f964.target: 我们可以锁定角 $C$ 和边 $c$，并描绘出一个可能的圆。

bd86c30b7b01.source: All parts of the triangle have a perspective on the whole
bd86c30b7b01.target: 三角形的所有部分都对整体有全局视角。

ff22a26c0b24.source: Sine is the “exaggeration factor” that scales up an individual side to the full diameter. (Sine is the percentage of the max possible, and we divide by it.)
ff22a26c0b24.target: 正弦是“放大因子”，它将单个边长放大到整个直径。（正弦是相对于最大可能值的百分比，我们用它来除。）

5e4870d63299.source: Imagine kids describing the same monster with varying degrees of exaggeration.
5e4870d63299.target: 想象一下，孩子们用不同程度的夸张来描述同一个怪物。

6b4c7f65d3de.source: Suppose $A$=30 and $a$=1 inch. Since $\sin(A) = 0.5$, the Monster Circle is 1 / 0.5 = 2 inches wide. Given another angle, I can figure out the length of its side. If $B$ = 45 degrees, then side $b$ takes up $\sin(45) = .707$ of the diameter, and is 1.414 inches.
6b4c7f65d3de.target: 假设 $A$=30°，$a$=1 英寸。由于 $\sin(A)=0.5$，怪物圆的宽度为 1/0.5 = 2 英寸。给定另一个角，我可以算出其对边的长度。如果 $B$=45°，那么边 $b$ 占直径的 $\sin(45)=0.707$，长度为 1.414 英寸。

6b34e7eb09d5.source: Any angle + side can deduce the size of the wrapping circle.
6b34e7eb09d5.target: 任意一个角加一条边都可以推导出外接圆的大小。

fd16791e09e1.source: The Law Of Sines is something I memorized in a class once, but didn’t internalize:

965dbcaa3929.source: Rather than the Law of Sines, think of the Law of Equal Perspectives:
965dbcaa3929.target: 与其说是正弦定理，不如把它看作是等视角定律：

b390e9e9df1d.source: This connection lets us start with one angle and work out facts about the others.

e14b5fdefb73.source: I occasionally frighten the neighborhood children by unchaining the mutant gorilla in my front yard.
e14b5fdefb73.target: 我偶尔会解开前院变异大猩猩的锁链，吓唬邻居家的孩子们。

743c0a04e9b2.source: The kids run screaming, telling different stories of what they’ve seen:
743c0a04e9b2.target: 孩子们尖叫着跑开，讲述着他们看到的不同故事：

44bef53f402c.source: “Alice claims the monster was 20 feet tall, but we all know she exaggerates by doubling. And Billy’s a bit of a crybaby, and said it was 30 feet tall. Charlie’s fairly no-nonsense and said the beast was exactly 10 feet high.”
44bef53f402c.target: “爱丽丝声称怪物有20英尺高，但我们都知道她喜欢夸大事实，会说两倍高。比利有点爱哭，他说有30英尺高。查理相当务实，说那只野兽正好有10英尺高。”

2bd401cd0196.source: If we know a kid’s “exaggeration factor” and the size they claim, we can deduce the true size of the monster. (Furious George has a name, you know.)
2bd401cd0196.target: 如果我们知道一个孩子的‘夸张因子’和他们声称的尺寸，就可以推断出怪物的真实大小。（你知道的，愤怒的乔治是有名字的。）

c6bcacbe5d27.source: Even better, we can predict what
c6bcacbe5d27.target: 更好的是，我们可以预测

fc4fdf8a6d93.target: 其他孩子可能会说什么：如果爱丽丝声称有40英尺，查理会怎么说？

13c4e128aebe.source: In a way, $\sin(A)$ is the “exaggeration factor” that converts the size the angle measured ($a$) to the full diameter ($d$). Each angle is a different kid, and some really misjudge the size of the full circle based on what they see. (90-degrees is right on target.)
13c4e128aebe.target: 从某种意义上说，$\sin(A)$ 是“夸张因子”，它将测量的角度尺寸（$a$）转换为整个直径（$d$）。每个角度都是一个不同的孩子，有些真的根据他们所见错误判断了整个圆的大小。（90度正好命中目标。）

846ad0315c74.source: In our example above, $A$ is 30 degrees and $a$ is 1 inch.
846ad0315c74.target: 在我们上面的例子中，$A$ 是30度，$a$ 是1英寸。

91a408223f5f.source: We can calculate the diameter pretty fast. First, we get the sine:
91a408223f5f.target: 我们可以很快算出直径。首先，我们得到正弦值：

19ff09c748d7.target: 这意味着我们的长度 $a$ 是最大高度的50%，所以整个直径必须是2英寸。

ba99c4b793a6.source: This isn’t enough to figure out the triangle by itself. Let’s say angle $B$ comes along and says it is 45 degrees. How long is $b$?
ba99c4b793a6.target: 仅凭这一点还不足以确定三角形。假设角 $B$ 出现并说它是45度。那么 $b$ 有多长？

0503c72bc12e.source: which means that $b$ is .707 of the max diameter. Therefore,
0503c72bc12e.target: 这意味着 $b$ 是最大直径的0.707。因此，

4cdd0bb840d7.source: Previously, I would plug numbers into the Law of Sines formula and chug away algebraically. Now I can think in terms of the Monster Circle: “Ok, I have the max diameter. I take the sine, and get the fraction of the max diameter for that side.”
4cdd0bb840d7.target: 以前，我会将数字代入正弦定理（Law of Sines）公式并代数求解。现在我可以从怪物圆的角度思考：“好的，我有最大直径。我取正弦，得到该边相对于最大直径的比例。”

c9374ce69f75.source: Most books write the formula with sin(A) in the numerator. It might read better “Sine A over A” but it distorts the conclusion that $\frac{a}{\sin(A)}$ is the size of the circle.
c9374ce69f75.target: 大多数书将公式写为 sin(A) 在分子中。读作“A分之正弦A”可能更好，但它扭曲了结论：$\frac{a}{\sin(A)}$ 是圆的大小。

1d7785d72a2b.source: Put the concept in your own words. The “Law of Sines” is a generic description of what’s in the formula, but the “Law of Equal Perspectives” explains what it means:
1d7785d72a2b.target: 用自己的话理解概念。“正弦定理”是公式内容的通用描述，而“相等视角定理”（Law of Equal Perspectives）则解释了其含义：

82d7186e077a.source: Technically, because $B$ is over 90 degrees, we can’t ever spin it and have either $A$ or $C$ be a right angle (if we could, the triangle would have over 180 degrees).
82d7186e077a.target: 严格来说，由于 $B$ 大于90度，我们无法旋转它使 $A$ 或 $C$ 成为直角（如果可以，三角形内角和将超过180度）。

72c25806749c.source: What to do? Realize the 180-degree complement of $B$ (call it $B\star$) acts like a stand-in on the other side:
72c25806749c.target: 怎么办？认识到 $B$ 的180度补角（称为 $B\star$）在另一边充当替代：

804959444529.source: $B \star$ has the same sine as $B$, which should make sense: they both point upwards along the same trajectory. To help us sleep better at night, we start with $B \star$ in the right-angle setup:
804959444529.target: $B \star$ 与 $B$ 具有相同的正弦值，这应该可以理解：它们都沿着同一轨迹向上。为了让我们晚上睡得更好，我们从直角设置中的 $B \star$ 开始：

9005189932ec.source: and get to the same conclusion as before. Phew.
9005189932ec.target: 并得到与之前相同的结论。呼。

87b098e78d1a.source: However, the fact that $B$ and $B\star$ can be swapped can lead to problems.

26050dd2dbbd.source: Also, the triangle may not be possible given a hypothetical scenario. If I say $b$ is 3 inches, you know something’s amiss. The max diameter was already calculated to be 2. Even a 90-degree angle, the best possible, could only have a side of 2 inches.

0c5e3aa7c802.source: Railroads forced the creation of a national time-table. Previously, cities kept their own time. New York and Philadelphia could be 5 minutes off, for example, which would disrupt train schedules.
0c5e3aa7c802.target: 铁路迫使国家统一时刻表的诞生。此前，城市各自保留自己的时间。例如，纽约和费城可能相差5分钟，这会打乱火车时刻表。

17196d71fa6b.source: The car gave rise to the current popularity of radio. If not for the car, when else would you be stuck in an enclosed area with nothing to do? At home, the TV clearly dominates.
17196d71fa6b.target: 汽车带来了无线电的普及。如果不是因为汽车，你还会在什么地方被困在封闭空间里无所事事呢？在家里，电视显然占据主导地位。

ea996460aad7.source: , a project I’m developing, was developed for an English-speaking audience. However, the calculator interface is inherently multi-lingual (numbers are numbers wherever you go) and much to my surprise, a huge portion of my traffic comes from non-English speaking countries.
ea996460aad7.target: ，我正在开发的一个项目，最初是为英语受众设计的。然而，计算器界面本质上是多语言的（数字无论在哪里都是数字），令我惊讶的是，我的流量中有很大一部分来自非英语国家。

c05c924f6ffc.source: In Mega Man, you look forward to fighting more bosses.
c05c924f6ffc.target: 在《洛克人》中，你期待与更多Boss战斗。

7ef408a62ad8.source: Mega Man was one of my favorite video games. You're a little cyborg, running through levels and fighting end bosses:
7ef408a62ad8.target: 《洛克人》是我最喜欢的电子游戏之一。你是一个小机器人，跑过关卡，与最终Boss战斗：

23b201434ac1.source: Here's the trick: every boss has a weakness. After you beat Fire-man with your regular gun, you earn a fire weapon. This makes your upcoming fight with Ice-man easier, which helps defeat the next boss, and so on.
23b201434ac1.target: 窍门在于：每个Boss都有弱点。当你用普通枪击败火人后，会获得火武器。这让你接下来与冰人的战斗更容易，从而帮助击败下一个Boss，以此类推。

0b35440315d8.target: 那么这有什么特别之处呢？

4b7181cff70d.source: Every level is a chance to permanently upgrade your abilities, not a grind you're trying to survive.
4b7181cff70d.target: 每一关都是永久提升能力的机会，而不是你试图生存的苦差事。

99f524f197a4.source: Think about Tetris: would you look forward to a variety of new shapes appearing?
99f524f197a4.target: 想想俄罗斯方块：你会期待各种新形状出现吗？

e2ab377de3c1.source: Heck no. Tetris can be fun in a "survive hordes of incoming zombies" sort of way, but in terms of learning, it's a frustrating, Sisyphean task. Every new piece is something to move beyond, not a learning opportunity. It's a test to find your breaking point.
e2ab377de3c1.target: 当然不。俄罗斯方块可能以“抵御僵尸大军”的方式有趣，但就学习而言，它是一个令人沮丧的西西弗斯式任务。每一块新方块都是要克服的东西，而不是学习机会。这是一个找到你极限的测试。

a998f20ed68e.source: In Mega Man, the game gets easier the more bosses you have. It's specifically designed for you to improve over time. Guess which game has 10+ sequels?
a998f20ed68e.target: 在《洛克人》中，拥有的Boss越多，游戏就越容易。它是专门设计让你逐步提升的。猜猜哪个游戏有10多个续作？

d91be804bccf.target: 数学学习可以遵循《洛克人》的模式。如果我们想击败“欧拉博士”，我们需要击败他的手下并掌握他们的武器：
