1162bbad2952.target: 通讯赞助及其他推广：\$700-1500/月

b4b308fdf9a5.source: HowStuffWorks - One of my favorite sites when young. But it got bought, and is now filled with ads, "relevant links" (celebrity gossip), and articles split over 6 pages to maximize pageviews. There's still some good content but you have to hold your nose and dive in.
b4b308fdf9a5.target: HowStuffWorks - 我年轻时最喜欢的网站之一。但它被收购了，现在充满了广告、“相关链接”（名人八卦），以及为了最大化页面浏览量而分成6页的文章。虽然仍有一些好内容，但你必须捏着鼻子跳进去。

86715bcbb799.source: Google Knol - an attempt to monetize crowdsourced information (Wikipedia with ads). The incentives for quality weren't there and it wasn't built to last. A few years later all its articles were gone.
86715bcbb799.target: Google Knol - 试图通过众包信息盈利（带广告的维基百科）。质量激励不足，且并非为持久而建。几年后，其所有文章都消失了。

fa7a3aabd272.source: About.com, eHow, etc. - designed to pump out uninspired copy/pasted material for pageviews. Again, incentives drive behavior.
fa7a3aabd272.target: About.com、eHow等 - 旨在大量输出缺乏灵感的复制粘贴内容以获取页面浏览量。再次说明，激励驱动行为。

ba39e6a8acf2.source: . (This resolved well, it's often just a misunderstanding.)
ba39e6a8acf2.target: 。（这个问题解决了，通常只是误解。）

cdf4562f155b.source: Thanks to Marquis Butler (intern / Kalid Wrangler), I've written more in 2016 than I have in several years.
cdf4562f155b.target: 感谢Marquis Butler（实习生 / Kalid Wrangler），我在2016年写的文章比过去几年都多。

e1c24951bc1a.source: The site appeared organically on reddit, hacker news, and total readership is at an all-time high.
e1c24951bc1a.target: 该网站在红迪、黑客新闻上自然出现，总读者量达到历史最高。

ffa4e1e22469.source: . Math books and courses are pretty passive, and they continued to hum along.
ffa4e1e22469.target: 。数学书籍和课程相当被动，但它们继续稳步发展。

8393343a71a2.source: I've finally figured out what worked emotionally (not taking things personally, see above), physically (low sugar / low carb) and I'm the healthiest I've been in years. In 2016 we moved into our first house, and a dedicated office for work (and rooftop for parties) made me feel energized.
8393343a71a2.target: 我终于弄明白了在情感上（不把事情个人化，见上文）、身体上（低糖/低碳水）有效的方法，这是我多年来最健康的状态。2016年我们搬进了第一所房子，有一个专用的办公室（和用于派对的屋顶）让我感到充满活力。

8812ce3c91c6.source: I wanted to release new editions of my Calculus and Math courses but didn't.
8812ce3c91c6.target: 我想发布我的微积分和数学课程的新版本，但没有。

8bc39f69ee6e.source: I wanted to add more community features to the site. This is a long-standing vision, with several attempts, but I find myself stymied by the (imagined) overhead of maintaining a community.
8bc39f69ee6e.target: 我想为网站添加更多社区功能。这是一个长期的愿景，有过几次尝试，但我发现自己被（想象中的）维护社区的开销所阻碍。

c50dded66aec.source: I wanted to add to my YouTube channel / start a podcast, but didn't.
c50dded66aec.target: 我想在我的YouTube频道上增加内容/开始播客，但没有。

eee211854b7b.source: I'd like to do more "topic" posts (on specific math concepts) vs. "strategy" posts (how to learn). I'm wary of getting too meta and writing about learning without learning things myself.
eee211854b7b.target: 我想做更多关于“主题”的文章（关于具体数学概念）而不是“策略”文章（如何学习）。我担心变得过于元化，在学习的同时不学习自己。

ae911f7c74cc.source: I'm usually behind in my personal communication.
ae911f7c74cc.target: 我通常在个人交流中落后。

8c5a1c28664f.source: I still hesitate quite a bit before publishing. I'd like to reframe writing as a skill to constantly practice, vs. a performance.
8c5a1c28664f.target: 我在发布之前仍然会犹豫很久。我想将写作重新定义为一项需要不断练习的技能，而不是一种表演。

54a3a284f0db.source: which I haven't promoted (I fear being externally obligated, which needs to be reframed)
54a3a284f0db.target: 我没有推广（我害怕被外部义务束缚，这需要重新定义）

fcdfbd433bce.source: I feel held back by concerns about losing my passion, a contributor to many of the above.
fcdfbd433bce.target: 我感到被担心失去热情所拖累，这是导致上述许多问题的原因之一。

7d2c9035a64c.source: Ideas can be understood intuitively and truly enjoyed.
7d2c9035a64c.target: 概念可以被直观地理解并真正享受。

8017532da6ee.source: Did you read and apply issue 1
8017532da6ee.target: 你阅读并应用了第1期吗

a91718d39e1d.target: 但说真的，你为什么不呢？

edbb82ec0036.source: imaginary numbers are like rotations in 2d
edbb82ec0036.target: 虚数就像二维中的旋转

3c2fb79e17c3.source: If your Calculus class completely ignores Archimedes, I'm going to... suggest you
3c2fb79e17c3.target: 如果你的微积分课程完全忽略了阿基米德，我将会...建议你

3e46c4f87ebd.source: I'm not good enough because of XYZ

3066b2936bc3.source: Inner critic: You're not sharing real knowledge, just generalities. Without rigorous oversight, your readers will build a nuclear reactor based on a flawed understanding.
3066b2936bc3.target: 内心批评者：你没有分享真正的知识，只是泛泛而谈。没有严格的审查，你的读者会基于有缺陷的理解建造核反应堆。

6fa02d4f9d90.source: Inner critic: You started in 2006? It should be a giant-mega-business by now.
6fa02d4f9d90.target: 内心批评者：你从2006年开始？现在应该是一个巨无霸企业了。

c83bd6ea4aa3.source: Inner critic: You should be writing more. You have dozens of half-finished drafts lying around.
c83bd6ea4aa3.target: 内心批评者：你应该写更多。你周围有几十篇半成品的草稿。

0dfcb73ab3a2.source: In 2006, a new entry popped into my idea journal: take my
0dfcb73ab3a2.target: 在2006年，我的想法日记中出现了一个新条目：把我的

024e6bf3004f.source: and put them on a learning site.

a18f2a1e82a9.source: What should I call it: My name? Stuff explained simply? Better Explained? (Hey, not looking for the best... just... better!)
a18f2a1e82a9.target: 该怎么称呼它呢？我的名字？简单解释？BetterExplained？（嘿，我不是在追求最好……只是……更好！）

4eedc74df2bf.source: 10 years, 168 posts and 220k words later, here we are.
4eedc74df2bf.target: 10年、168篇文章和22万字之后，我们来到了这里。

b64062fc81ea.source: Below is a collection of thoughts on life and business after pursuing an idea for a decade.
b64062fc81ea.target: 以下是在坚持一个想法十年后，对生活和商业的一些思考。

c93fcdb03dc6.source: Advice gets skewed by our personal experience, cognitive biases, and the presence of someone else (smile for the camera!). My strategy is to write for a single person -- a younger me -- as simply as I can.
c93fcdb03dc6.target: 建议常受个人经验、认知偏差和其他人的存在（对着镜头微笑！）的影响而偏离。我的策略是为一个人——年轻时的自己——写作，尽可能简单。

55b193cd9318.source: It's ok. Be gentle with yourself. Not "gentle now to work harder later" but genuine compassion. Delay, confusion or disappointment may have a role, or be a warning sign, and you don't know ahead of time how things will work out. Better Explained exists because you didn't go to grad school as you'd hoped -- would you trade the two now?
55b193cd9318.target: 没关系。对自己温柔点。不是那种“现在温柔以便以后更努力”，而是真正的同情。延迟、困惑或失望可能有其作用，或是警告信号，而你无法提前预知事情的结果。BetterExplained的存在是因为你没能如愿读研究生——你现在愿意用两者交换吗？

885cf96c7f0d.source: There's an inner tug that knows what matters to you:
885cf96c7f0d.target: 内心有一种牵引力，知道什么对你重要：

70dcd8a4f7a9.source: What lit a fire in your belly in the past? Better Explained began after overcoming immense frustration with a poorly-taught traditional math class. Concepts finally clicked after weeks, months or years -- argh, why couldn't they explain it simply in the first place?
70dcd8a4f7a9.target: 过去是什么点燃了你内心的火焰？BetterExplained始于克服对一门教得很差的传统数学课的极度沮丧。概念在几周、几个月甚至几年后终于理解——唉，为什么他们一开始就不能简单解释呢？

4248d8fae4f6.source: Do you remember the exhilaration of being up all night and finally having an idea make sense? The anger of seeing friends discouraged from careers by poorly-taught, hoop-jumping math requirements?
4248d8fae4f6.target: 你是否记得熬夜后终于弄明白一个想法的兴奋？看到朋友因教得差、充满跳栏式数学要求而泄气放弃职业时的愤怒？

45ca5b5e538f.source: : if you have a great day, when you feel truly alive, write it down. A few scattered entries:
45ca5b5e538f.target: : 如果你过得很棒，当你感到真正有活力时，把它写下来。一些零散的记录：

f5e02ff93886.source: 12/3/16 - WOW! I am cranking like insanity on instacalc. I have that fire in my belly where I just want to make things. Create what I can and see where it goes.
f5e02ff93886.target: 12/3/16 - 哇！我像疯子一样在instacalc上疯狂工作。内心有那种只想创造的火焰。尽我所能创造，看看会怎样。

e1efe69b2eae.source: 10/26/16 - Feeling great about myself for finishing the Coursera class! (machine learning). It’s motivating me to do other classes (Complex analysis.)
e1efe69b2eae.target: 10/26/16 - 完成Coursera课程（机器学习）后对自己感觉非常好。这激励我去上其他课程（复分析）。

3f621864e8b7.source: 5/20/16 - from Chris XXXX. Loves getting the Better Explained emails.
3f621864e8b7.target: 5/20/16 - 来自Chris XXXX。他喜欢收到BetterExplained的邮件。

2109b0701cda.source: Review the list for patterns. Even better, put your notes into a
2109b0701cda.target: 回顾列表寻找模式。更好的是，把你的笔记放入一个

14cadeff6649.source: and see what themes pop out:

f1cc0aa60c66.source: My core values, things that make me feel alive, are:
f1cc0aa60c66.target: 我的核心价值观，那些让我感到有活力的事情是：

054f869092a0.source: Find an outlet that engages your values.
054f869092a0.target: 找到一个能激发你价值的出口。

8b07d11da0bb.source: Based on your values, find things that make you come alive. What grinds your gears? What do you desperately wish someone told you when starting?
8b07d11da0bb.target: 基于你的价值观，找到那些让你活跃起来的事情。什么让你焦虑？你多么希望有人在你刚开始时告诉你什么？

300e09ec0177.source: : Imagine 5 years have passed since publishing your article. Is it still useful or interesting? If not, add strategy/insights that go beyond current events. (E.g., a post on
300e09ec0177.target: ：想象一下，你的文章已经发布5年了。它现在还有用或有趣吗？如果没有，请补充超越当前事件的策略/见解。（例如，一篇关于

db009b785602.source: might use an old video as an example, that's ok.). Fortunately, math is about as evergreen as it gets, since we study 2000-year old theorems. Even there, make connections and present general strategies.
db009b785602.target: 可能会用一段旧视频作为例子，这没问题。幸运的是，数学几乎是最常青的领域，因为我们研究的是2000年前的定理。即便如此，也要建立联系并呈现通用策略。

b4f91ad6ce23.source: : Most technical content doesn't have enough analogies, diagrams, or examples (interactive is even better). For evergreen content, this effort pays back since the post is read for years. A 10-year old post on
b4f91ad6ce23.target: ：大多数技术内容缺乏足够的类比、图表或示例（交互式更好）。对于常青内容，这种努力是值得的，因为文章会被阅读多年。一篇10年前的关于

5ffbda013632.source: , ancient in the tech world, shows up in many presentations because of its images.
5ffbda013632.target: 的文章，在技术世界里堪称古老，却因其图表而出现在许多演示中。

33dc77737f74.source: : Evergreen text with evolutionary updates is a sweet spot. It can be updated years later with a fresh diagram, example, or embedded video. With video as a
33dc77737f74.target: ：带有渐进式更新的常青文本是一个最佳点。它可以在多年后用新的图表、示例或嵌入视频进行更新。而视频作为

ea4039429329.source: source, you have to redo the whole thing. (Where's the video version of Wikipedia?)
ea4039429329.target: 来源，你必须重做整个内容。（维基百科的视频版本在哪里？）

2235a6a37f43.source: : Matter-of-fact textbooks sweep decades of math debate under the rug (imaginary numbers, Fourier Transforms). Share your personal struggle and how you overcame it. Highlighting your confusions makes the content both approachable and helpful. What would you share with a younger version of yourself?
2235a6a37f43.target: ：就事论事的教科书掩盖了数十年的数学争论（虚数、傅里叶变换）。分享你个人的挣扎以及如何克服它。突出你的困惑使内容既亲切又有帮助。你会对年轻时的自己分享什么？

4c8ccc2ad0b7.source: Aim to genuinely help your readers in the long run. (You become your own reader when you revisit your post years later.)
4c8ccc2ad0b7.target: 目标是长期真正帮助你的读者。（当你多年后重读自己的文章时，你就成了自己的读者。）

0c7aab89fd06.source: My goodness, what I would have paid for
0c7aab89fd06.target: 天哪，我当年多么愿意花钱买

f16b31481ec6.target: 令人满意的直觉来理解

b80be52aae9f.source: in college. Read the article once and you're done. Boom, you have an insight that sticks in your mind and makes every math class so much easier.
b80be52aae9f.target: 在大学里。读一遍文章就够了。砰，你获得了一个深入脑海的洞见，让每节数学课都轻松许多。

4f0b5193c483.source: Today we see parades of clickbait "15 easy ways to learn imaginary numbers" articles. Maybe this week's lesson will finally work!
4f0b5193c483.target: 如今我们看到一连串的标题党文章"15 easy ways to learn imaginary numbers"。也许这周的课最终会有效！

d64b3e39e356.source: I call it the Men's Health problem. In theory, you should read one magazine and become superhuman. Yet somehow there's hundreds of issues, over years, detailing the same goal. Either the tips don't work, or the problem is with
d64b3e39e356.target: 我称之为《男士健身》问题。理论上，你读一本杂志就能变成超人。但不知何故，多年来有数百期杂志都在阐述同一个目标。要么这些技巧不起作用，要么问题在于

dd5ecd3fb61e.source: the tips, not getting more of them. (Issue 1:
dd5ecd3fb61e.target: 技巧本身，而不是获得更多技巧。（第1期：

b33a0029351a.source: Help your readers graduate beyond you. Google search is useful because it sends you
b33a0029351a.target: 帮助你的读者超越你。谷歌搜索之所以有用，是因为它把你送到

d5a0e2cf9dc2.source: as fast as possible. And that's why you come back with new questions.
d5a0e2cf9dc2.target: 尽可能快的地方。这就是为什么你会带着新问题回来。

05af2ee3b664.source: With evergreen content, you're building nets to throw into the ocean. More nets = more fish.
05af2ee3b664.target: 有了常青内容，你就在编织网抛向大海。网越多 = 鱼越多。

5c14f61e4e55.source: With read-once content (like a newspaper), you're spear-fishing. More effort = more fish, but yesterday's effort doesn't help you today. It's exhausting.
5c14f61e4e55.target: 而对于一次性阅读的内容（如报纸），你是在叉鱼。更多努力 = 更多鱼，但昨天的努力对今天没有帮助。这很累人。

86f260decdfb.source: Just keep writing content you'd read yourself, and over time you'll see a buildup:
86f260decdfb.target: 只需坚持写你自己愿意读的内容，随着时间的推移，你会看到累积效应：

e935da9a18d7.source: Old posts are just as good as new ones for generating today's traffic. Let that back catalog work for you - 45% of my traffic comes from 2007-era posts. (At that time, I had a lot of back content to import. I also had more time to spend after leaving my first job.)
e935da9a18d7.target: 旧文章在产生今日流量方面与新文章一样好。让那些旧目录为你工作——我45%的流量来自2007年左右的文章。（那时，我有很多旧内容要导入。离开第一份工作后，我也有更多时间可以支配。）

a7feb18b4fe4.source: Each evergreen post has a chance to become a hit years later. A 2015 post on cross products was published quietly, and later became a top 5 article:
a7feb18b4fe4.target: 每篇常青文章都有机会在几年后成为热门。一篇2015年关于叉积的文章悄悄发布，后来成为前五名文章：

80d7aa67d0cc.source: Maybe one of the 2016 posts will pick up steam next year. (In 2016 I tended to write more "strategy" posts, which are fun but don't get as much traffic as tactical "How to learn X" articles.)
80d7aa67d0cc.target: 也许2016年的某篇文章明年会火起来。（2016年我倾向于写更多“策略”类文章，这些文章很有趣，但流量不如战术性的“如何学习X”类文章。）

a0a0caedd8cf.source: Evergreen content doesn't have to be long. The average wordcount was 1310 (median 1273, max 3604).
a0a0caedd8cf.target: 常青内容不一定很长。平均字数为1310（中位数1273，最大3604）。

2ef8c4377939.source: Let's see how wordcount impacts traffic:
2ef8c4377939.target: 让我们看看字数如何影响流量：

84ce3a756266.source: There is a modest positive correlation (.26) between wordcount and traffic. After an article reaches 1000 words, shorter articles (Combinations vs. Permutations, 1000 words) and longer articles (Fourier Transform, 3600 words) have similar popularity.
84ce3a756266.target: 字数与流量之间呈现适度的正相关性（0.26）。当文章达到1000字后，较短的（如“组合与排列”，1000字）和较长的（如“傅里叶变换”，3600字）文章受欢迎程度相似。

c68e39eece1e.source: I don't have a wordcount goal, just a "helpfulness" goal: include an analogy, diagram, example, plain-english description, and technical description (
c68e39eece1e.target: 我没有字数目标，只有一个“有用性”目标：包含类比、图表、例子、通俗英语描述和技术描述（

db6e4feb7930.source: (For what it's worth, this article is now the longest at about 4000 words.)
db6e4feb7930.target: （不管怎样，这篇文章现在是当前最长的，大约4000字。）

d591f88818f9.source: Be willing to write for years. But if your content aligns with your values, and is interesting to you, it's a win-win. Traffic grows, you'll feel fulfilled, and you'll improve your writing and subject matter expertise.
d591f88818f9.target: 愿意坚持写作多年。但如果你的内容与你的价值观相符，并且你自己觉得有趣，那就是双赢：流量增长，你会感到满足，还能提高你的写作能力和专业知识。

8cd41c5c75c9.source: It's not the fast path to Mega Blogging Success. But whether it takes 2 years or 10 doesn't matter, you're building something that fulfills you, and the time will pass anyway.
8cd41c5c75c9.target: 这不是通向超级博客成功的捷径。但无论需要2年还是10年都不重要，你正在建设能让你满足的东西，时间总会过去。

d9348deb3025.source: I wrote 220k words, averaging 60 words a day over the past 10 years. At 95 words per minute, that should only take 38 seconds a day (right?).
d9348deb3025.target: 我写了22万字，过去10年平均每天60个字。按每分钟95字的速度，每天只需38秒（对吧？）。

f8e43ffea2fe.source: A desire for consistency, while helpful, isn't necessary and certainly shouldn't invoke a feeling of dread ("I shouldn't start if I can't be consistent").
f8e43ffea2fe.target: 对一致性的渴望虽然有益，但并非必要，当然也不应引发恐惧感（“如果我不能保持一致，我就不该开始”）。

2df8c01bb01d.source: In 2015, I wrote 4 posts (3 months per post) but hopped back on the wagon in 2016 (16 posts, 3 weeks per post). Being gentle with myself meant I looked forward to writing more without guilt.
2df8c01bb01d.target: 2015年我写了4篇（每篇3个月），但2016年又重拾干劲（16篇，每篇3周）。善待自己意味着我期待更多地写作，而不再感到愧疚。

e36046e5a132.source: There's a saying that long after you forget what someone did, you remember how they made you feel. Long after you forget what you wrote about, you remember how coming back to your project made you feel.
e36046e5a132.target: 有句话说，在你忘记某人做过什么很久之后，你仍会记得他们让你感受到什么。在你忘记自己写过什么很久之后，你仍会记得重返项目时感受到什么。

0661a6364d12.source: Have an account on a social service, use it normally, and learn the norms. If your content shows up, reply to questions and take feedback. It's ok if everyone doesn't love your cooking. (
0661a6364d12.target: 在社交服务上注册一个账号，正常使用并学习规则。如果你的内容出现，回复问题并接受反馈。不是每个人都喜欢你的“菜”也没关系。（

8e3b5dcd4467.source: In 2006, I posted articles to smaller communities (like dzone.com), while today that'd be a small subreddit or forum. Participate, make things you'd want to read yourself, and stick with your values.
8e3b5dcd4467.target: 2006年，我把文章发布到较小的社区（如dzone.com），而现在那可能是一个小的子版块或论坛。参与其中，做出你自己想读的东西，并坚持你的价值观。

3b98bbbd3907.source: You don't need to merge the two. If you want to make money, it's
3b98bbbd3907.target: 你不需要将两者合并。如果你想赚钱，这更

377ac11b4355.source: easier to just get a day job, live simply, and invest your savings. However, financial independence is alluring and a side business (or other project) can speed that up.
377ac11b4355.target: 更容易：找一份日常工作，简单生活，并进行储蓄投资。然而，财务独立很诱人，而副业（或其他项目）可以加速这一进程。

37f1de846dbc.source: For Better Explained, my goals are:

6071de6aece5.source: Goal #1: Do not lose my passion for learning (evergreen motivation)
6071de6aece5.target: 目标 #1：保持对学习的热情（永远的动力）

d9fc7fd47e04.source: Goals 2-1000: Help improve education, make a living, make friends/connections, have a portfolio, improve my writing, explore new technologies, get better at design, marketing, copywriting, and business.
d9fc7fd47e04.target: 目标 2-1000：帮助改善教育，谋生，结交朋友/建立联系，拥有作品集，提高写作水平，探索新技术，提升设计、营销、文案和商业能力。

0b267796cbad.source: This project is a lifelong mission and I'm ok if it accelerates slowly while enthusiasm is preserved.
0b267796cbad.target: 这个项目是一项终身使命，只要热情还在，即使进展缓慢我也能接受。

905d342b52c1.source: I fear burnout because math enthusiasm is what I peddle.
905d342b52c1.target: 我害怕倦怠，因为我贩卖的是对数学的热情。

40ab66da4841.source: There's plenty of standard details out there, I don't need to be the 105th site that introduces Calculus using the slope of the tangent line. (
40ab66da4841.target: 外面已经有大量标准细节，我不需要成为第105个用切线斜率介绍微积分的网站。（

9a9dd827f3ea.source: I'm slowly coming to grips with the risks of diving deeper. If I lose interest, for whatever reason, it'll be ok. I'll forgive myself for a mistake made in the pursuit of something I care about. One door closing may open another.
9a9dd827f3ea.target: 我渐渐意识到深入探索的风险。如果因为某种原因失去兴趣，那也没关系。我会原谅自己在追求所爱之事时犯下的错误。一扇门关闭，另一扇可能会打开。

345fb9a33717.source: But I really like the room opposite the door that's open now.
345fb9a33717.target: 但我真的很喜欢现在这扇门对面的房间。

8b89629097ce.source: inspired me about what was possible with my own products, and I share stats in that spirit. Given the traffic so far, the site more than covers my expenses, coming from:
8b89629097ce.target: 激励了我，让我看到了自己的产品可能达到的高度，我以这种精神分享数据。考虑到目前的流量，网站的收入足以覆盖支出，来源包括：

087d8bc27e8a.source: I think there's a lot of growth potential. But I don't want a growth
087d8bc27e8a.target: 我认为有很大的增长潜力。但我不想要增长

8d961064d4cb.source: , or the mission warped by the grow-or-die monster.
8d961064d4cb.target: ，或者让使命被“要么增长要么死亡”的怪物扭曲。

e9d4a1a8b89f.source: A few scenarios I'd like to avoid:

e8ebf896bf34.source: I'd prefer a slower, harder-to-kill path to sustainability while always being proud to send my Mom to the site.
e8ebf896bf34.target: 我倾向于一条更慢、更难被扼杀的可持续发展道路，同时始终能以让我妈妈自豪地访问网站为荣。

22dee5b7682e.source: The most important business advice: start an email list. Other services (RSS readers, twitter, facebook, etc.) are ephemeral and not really in your control. In 10 years, who knows what we'll use. But email will probably remain as a direct conversation to your audience. At a minimum, you can use email to publicize whatever platform comes next.
22dee5b7682e.target: 最重要的商业建议：开始建立邮件列表。其他服务（RSS阅读器、Twitter、Facebook等）转瞬即逝，且不受你控制。10年后谁知道我们会用什么。但邮件很可能会保留为与受众直接对话的方式。至少，你可以用邮件来宣传接下来出现的任何平台。

1724aa4fdb95.source: Currently there's 38k email subscribers (growing about 1k/month) and I wish I started in 2006 not 2013. The best time to plant a tree is 20 years ago, the second best time is now.
1724aa4fdb95.target: 目前有3.8万邮件订阅者（每月增长约1000人），我真希望是从2006年开始而不是2013年。种树最好的时间是20年前，其次是现在。

2218be1c5f00.source: The next business-y thing: practice making products. An ebook, guide, book, video tutorial, in-person lesson, anything. Overcome the trepidation of creating something, selling it, getting feedback, and iterating -- there's only so much you can read about swimming.
2218be1c5f00.target: 下一件商业相关的事：练习制作产品。电子书、指南、书籍、视频教程、线下课程，任何形式都可以。克服创作、销售、获取反馈和迭代的恐惧——关于游泳，你能读的只有那么多。

bec519a63d73.source: After reading and replying to thousands of comments, you realize that any external judgment (whether positive or negative) is not necessarily true, and you can't tie your worth to it.
bec519a63d73.target: 在阅读并回复了数千条评论后，你会发现任何外部评价（无论是正面还是负面）都不一定是真实的，你不能将自我价值与之挂钩。

fc0ffc031933.source: It's wonderful when an article helps someone. Maybe it was the right time, the right place, they were in the right mindset and they saw the right analogy. I'm happy for them. Maybe I can learn to do more articles like it.
fc0ffc031933.target: 当一篇文章帮助到某人时，那感觉太棒了。也许是因为天时地利人和，他们处在正确的心态中，看到了恰当的类比。我为他们感到高兴。也许我可以学习写出更多这样的文章。

5067155e9013.source: Sometimes someone hates an article. They found it boring, or confusing, or they didn't have the right background, or were expecting something else. That's too bad, and maybe a different approach will help them.
5067155e9013.target: 有时有人讨厌一篇文章。他们觉得无聊、困惑，或者没有合适的背景知识，或者期望看到别的内容。这很遗憾，也许换个方法能帮到他们。

1e5ece5e89c8.source: But "Another person loved it" and "Another person hated it" aren't in your control. Put in an honest effort and separate your sense worth from the outcome. (Like playing poker: the right play can still lose, the wrong play can still win.)
1e5ece5e89c8.target: 但“别人喜欢”和“别人讨厌”并不在你的掌控之中。付出真诚的努力，并将你的自我价值与结果分开。（就像玩扑克：正确的打法仍可能输，错误的打法仍可能赢。）

07f09cc2af37.source: You'd think the "no blame, no praise" mindset removes joy, but it doesn't -- my feelgood list is ever growing! I'm vicariously happy when someone enjoys an article for the simple reason they enjoyed it. Their Aha! makes me smile. It's "I feel good" not "I am finally good".
07f09cc2af37.target: 你可能会认为“不指责、不表扬”的心态会剥夺快乐，但实际上并不会——我的快乐清单一直在增长！当有人仅仅因为喜欢而享受一篇文章时，我会感同身受地高兴。他们的顿悟让我微笑。这是“我感觉很好”，而不是“我终于很好了”。

197a6c11fce7.source: Not taking things personally doesn't mean you need to
197a6c11fce7.target: 不要把事情个人化并不意味着你需要

82f39f37eb01.source: it. When something bothers you, it means you're basing some element of self-worth on that opinion. It's a "teaching moment", however frustrating. Writing online gives you many teaching moments.
82f39f37eb01.target: 。当某件事困扰你时，意味着你正在将某些自我价值建立在那条意见上。这是一个“教学时刻”，无论多么令人沮丧。在网上写作会给你很多这样的教学时刻。

983f941504a7.source: I read somewhere the Dalai Lama will cut short interviews with people who aren't making a genuine effort. You can be polite, compassionate, and respect your own time.
983f941504a7.target: 我在某处读到，达赖喇嘛会缩短与没有真正付出努力的人的采访。你可以保持礼貌、富有同情心，同时尊重自己的时间。

d20eeda34add.source: Meditation, CBT, and other techniques helped me immensely with very negative thoughts (I like
d20eeda34add.target: 冥想、CBT（认知行为疗法）和其他技巧极大地帮助我处理了非常消极的想法（我喜欢

b9ecc266ce42.source: ). Subjectively, 90% of issues that used to bother me don't anymore. They were typically a variation of
b9ecc266ce42.target: ）。主观上，曾经困扰我的问题中90%都不再困扰我了。它们通常是某种变体

99690133c66c.source: . (Protip: If XYZ makes you good enough, then losing XYZ means you're no longer good enough. Uh oh. You've got the tiger by the tail and can never let go.)
99690133c66c.target: 。（提示：如果XYZ让你足够好，那么失去XYZ意味着你不再足够好。哦哦。你抓住了老虎的尾巴，再也不能放手了。）

8c5fb7849f7a.source: I'm still working on this one. My internal chatter is usually something like:
8c5fb7849f7a.target: 我仍在努力解决这个问题。我的内心独白通常是这样的：

04b44c1d057c.source: First: Someone read an article about math and liked it enough to apply it? Wow! That's objective #1.
04b44c1d057c.target: 首先：有人读了一篇关于数学的文章并喜欢到足以应用它？哇！这是目标一。

d4a883923c5f.source: More seriously, I want to mitigate a false sense of understanding by including real examples and sanity checks. It's not intuition
d4a883923c5f.target: 更严肃地说，我想通过包含真实的例子和合理性检查来减轻错误的理解。这不是直觉

0993a70c2da3.source: the technical. I'll make mistakes, sure, and thankfully text is easy to update.
0993a70c2da3.target: 技术。我当然会犯错误，但幸好文本很容易更新。

ea0e420f2dde.source: Perhaps I could have pushed things faster while staying motivated. But is there an expiration date on sustainably aligning with your passion? My goal isn't growth for its own sake, it's to uncover insights that wouldn't have been shared otherwise. Hitting a number in 2017 (not 2007) is ok with me if I enjoyed the journey.
ea0e420f2dde.target: 也许我本可以在保持动力的同时更快地推进。但是，可持续地与你的热情保持一致是否有到期日期？我的目标不是为了增长而增长，而是发现那些原本不会被分享的见解。如果我喜欢这个旅程，那么在2017年（而不是2007年）达到某个数字对我来说是可以的。

8d06d11df845.source: Got me on that one. I have to constantly
8d06d11df845.target: 这一点我同意。我必须不断地

191903aef678.source: Things I'd like to work on:

afe14aadfeb9.source: These are things to observe, not beat myself up about. I've tried the beat-up approach and it works for days, not years. When I'm in the zone I do weeks of work in days while having a blast. I'd like to foster that.
afe14aadfeb9.target: 这些都是需要观察的事情，而不是自我鞭挞。我试过自我鞭挞的方法，它有效几天，但无法持续几年。当我进入状态时，我能在几天内完成几周的工作，同时乐在其中。我希望培养这种状态。

90407e347ec8.target: 通讯订阅者提出的问题：

76b418ff27b6.source: I primarily wanted a place for creative expression, and secondarily wanted it to grow large enough to make a living. Better Explained has made more than projects I started with the purpose of making money.
76b418ff27b6.target: 我首先希望有一个创意表达的地方，其次希望它能发展壮大到足以谋生。BetterExplained 的成就超过了那些我初衷是为了赚钱而启动的项目。

c263a29f4b0f.source: Write about something that makes you come alive, for which you can share truly helpful information. What does everyone overlook? What do you wish someone told you? What don't you see anywhere else?
c263a29f4b0f.target: 写一些能让你充满活力、并能分享真正有用信息的东西。有什么是大家都忽略的？你希望别人曾告诉过你什么？你在别处看不到什么？

b5790115e402.source: There are tools to find underserved niches, but for a lifelong project it's hard to get motivated by a report telling you to write about X. It's ok to separate the "make money" and "learn a skill" projects.
b5790115e402.target: 有一些工具可以找到服务不足的细分领域，但对于一个终身项目来说，一份告诉你写关于X的报告很难让你有动力。将“赚钱”和“学习技能”的项目分开是可以的。

c8be6740cd7e.source: My strongest drive is that intuitive learning works: I
c8be6740cd7e.target: 我最强烈的动力是直观学习是有用的：我

e1b0036e2ea3.source: an idea can be understood if we have the right approach. It's a fact of life for me -- you know gravity works because you've seen things fall over and over. I know an Aha! moment will eventually help because I've experienced hundreds of concepts snapping into place with the right analogy or example.
e1b0036e2ea3.target: 一个想法只要方法得当就能被理解。这对我来说是生活的事实——你相信重力是因为你看到东西反复下落。我知道顿悟时刻最终会有帮助，因为我经历过数百个概念通过恰当的类比或例子而豁然开朗。

56bf231b293d.source: Now, finding this Aha! moment isn't easy or even pleasant. But there's an optimism that however long it takes (even years), I'll be thrilled when I finally figure it out. (It's the
56bf231b293d.target: 现在，找到这个顿悟时刻并不容易，甚至不愉快。但有一种乐观的态度：无论需要多长时间（甚至几年），当我最终弄明白时，我会感到无比兴奋。（这就是

51225b415c82.source: philosophy, where difficult ideas become allies after they are understood.)
51225b415c82.target: 哲学：困难的思想在被理解后会变成盟友。）

29f3a5b00eb3.source: I don't have this motivation in other parts of life, but experiencing it in one domain makes me think it's possible. Maybe there's a mindset shift that can work similarly; pulling towards my values seems to be on the right track.
29f3a5b00eb3.target: 我在生活的其他方面没有这种动力，但在一个领域体验到它，让我觉得这是可能的。也许有一种思维转变可以起到类似的作用；朝着我的价值观前进似乎是正确的方向。

f0f1e17079f7.source: I tend to write once I'm both confident about my intuition, and have verified its usefulness in practice problems and simulations. Of course I've had plenty of corrections, suggested improvements, and miss subtleties that true experts would see. But the key insights seem to be generally OK (always happy to fix them, yay for text).
f0f1e17079f7.target: 我倾向于在既对自己的直觉有信心，又通过实践问题和模拟验证了其有用性之后才动笔。当然，我也有过很多修正、改进建议，并遗漏了真正专家才能看到的细微之处。但关键的见解似乎大体上没问题（总是乐于修正，文本的好处）。

33078599cdbe.source: I have certain philosophical differences with a typical math curriculum. For example, I casually introduce
33078599cdbe.target: 我与典型的数学课程存在某些哲学上的分歧。例如，我随意地将

c4b53f3a6ce7.source: as a type of "math spreadsheet". This is a casual description compared to the official "vector space" abstraction, but spreadsheets are familiar and what I wish someone had showed me first. I won't pretend an approach is helpful to me when it's not.
c4b53f3a6ce7.target: 介绍为一种“数学电子表格”。与官方的“向量空间”抽象相比，这是一个随意的描述，但电子表格是熟悉的，也是我希望别人当初先给我展示的东西。当一种方法对我没有帮助时，我不会假装它有用。

7add8c28f8d9.source: Typically 10-20 hours per post, but sometimes 2 hours and sometimes years. Certain ideas can sit in my head (or idea notebook, or idea text file) for months before a satisfying Aha! hits me. I try to capture it as soon as possible, with the fresh enthusiasm of "Whoa! Here's how I finally figured out XYZ".
7add8c28f8d9.target: 通常每篇文章10-20小时，但有时2小时，有时几年。某些想法可以在我的脑中（或想法笔记本，或想法文本文件）里放上几个月，直到一个令人满意的顿悟击中我。我试图尽快抓住它，带着“哇！我终于弄懂了XYZ”的新鲜热情。

c4a0838d9f56.source: It's hard to express how much a role Better Explained has had in my life. This project is more than I could have asked for as a kid looking to publish his college insights online.
c4a0838d9f56.target: 很难表达BetterExplained在我的生活中扮演了多大的角色。这个项目远远超出了我小时候希望在网上发表大学见解时的期望。

3aaa2f8e639e.source: I feel deeply grateful to every Better Explained reader for the encouraging and thoughtful blog comments, emails, and contact form submissions. From marked-up books with countless improvements, to
3aaa2f8e639e.target: 我深深感激每一位BetterExplained的读者，感谢你们在博客评论、电子邮件和联系表单中留下的鼓励和深思熟虑的留言。从满是改进标记的书，到基于这些类比制作的

8abb3fcb32d1.source: based on the analogies, to mind-blowingly

f4f06e568249.source: Thank you for helping the site (and me) develop these past 10 years. Hope you'll join me for a decade more :).
f4f06e568249.target: 感谢你们在过去10年里帮助这个网站（和我）成长。希望你们能再陪我十年 :)。

e3d628881bc8.target: "更多时间"（我缺乏动力，而不是时间）

a10a3acdc6ea.source: Don't try to conceptualize "the square root of a negative number". That's really clunky. Think about rotations instead.
a10a3acdc6ea.target: 不要试图概念化"负数的平方根"。这真的很笨拙。试着从旋转的角度来思考。

e3a1d9e27578.source: Don't try to memorize SOH-CAH-TOA and a mess of equations. Visualize a single diagram and the connections jump out.
e3a1d9e27578.target: 不要试图死记SOH-CAH-TOA和一堆方程。可视化一个简单的图表，其中的联系就会跃然纸上。

6aa24c69deff.source: Don't force yourself through epsilon-delta proofs. Practice breaking things apart, putting them together, and get a feel for the patterns.
6aa24c69deff.target: 不要强迫自己硬啃epsilon-delta证明。练习分解和组合，并感受其中的模式。

783afc3fcb55.source: Don't simply memorize the formula. Internalize the notion of a
783afc3fcb55.target: 不要只是死记公式。内化一个

71bdc7c40654.source: and practice going from a pattern, to the recipe, and back.
71bdc7c40654.target: 并练习从模式到配方，再返回去的过程。

7ecd27b523af.source: Appendix: Technology As The Education Fix
7ecd27b523af.target: 附录：技术作为教育解决方案

1e76cd40dcf5.source: IV times VII is really hard!

5fae85a83222.source: What's a tough concept you finally figured out? (For me, it was the imaginary numbers. I'll never forget that
5fae85a83222.target: 你终于弄懂了哪个困难概念？（对我来说，是虚数(imaginary numbers)。我永远不会忘记那个

373ad83055dd.source: Ok. For that difficult concept, what
373ad83055dd.target: 好。对于那个困难概念，是什么

1127962addbf.source: Rarely is it because we're lacking:

5930132c796a.source: The limiting factor -- the thing holding me back -- is how I approach a concept.
5930132c796a.target: 限制因素——阻碍我的东西——是我理解一个概念的方式。

a340ddc369b8.source: Imagine you're teleported to a Roman classroom. Kids -- heck, the adults -- are struggling with multiplication. (
a340ddc369b8.target: 想象你被传送到一个罗马教室。孩子们——甚至成年人——都在乘法上挣扎。（

bddf581895e7.source: What do you fix: Flip the classroom? Gamify things? Invent a printing press to distribute more worksheets?
bddf581895e7.target: 你会解决什么：翻转课堂？游戏化？发明印刷机来分发更多练习册？

f3c13ed6e3c9.source: Helpful, in time. But the first fix should be a simple discussion:
f3c13ed6e3c9.target: 这些最终会有帮助。但首要的解决方案应该是一个简单的讨论：

c81c3302486b.source: Hey, I'm from the future. Yes, it's pretty nice. But first, we need to fix your concept of a number. Individual lines for digits is cumbersome. Instead, think in groups of ones, tens, hundreds, and so on. Now multiplication is built into your numbers, and arithmetic gets a lot easier. Let me show you...
c81c3302486b.target: 嘿，我是从未来来的。没错，那里相当不错。但首先，我们需要修正你对数字的理解。用单独的线条表示数字太繁琐了。相反，要按个、十、百等分组思考。现在乘法已经内置到数字中，算术变得容易多了。让我给你展示一下……

030c6929803f.source: Boom. The "Roman Numeral Problem" is not fixed with better tech. Just a better understanding.
030c6929803f.target: 砰。“罗马数字问题”不是靠更好的技术解决的，而是靠更好的理解。

7b088d744a1a.source: Ok. Imagine a time traveler (you, 6 months from now) is going to tutor you today. What would they suggest?
7b088d744a1a.target: 好。想象一下，一个时间旅行者（你，六个月后的你）今天要来辅导你。他会建议什么呢？

1dde2c311e89.source: Focus on the problems a time traveler would fix first.
1dde2c311e89.target: 首先关注时间旅行者会解决的问题。

3bf92b9c2e05.source: While drafting this post, a comment came in:
3bf92b9c2e05.target: 在起草这篇文章时，收到一条评论：

623098c72cc6.source: So, I just started learning about imaginary numbers in math class, and I was so confused. I understood the idea, but not the practical application or really what i was. I am a person who needs to understand a concept fully, I have trouble accepting that i=the square root of -1. I was googling it and I only got more confused. Then, I found your article on imaginary numbers, and all of a sudden, I got it. I could visualize it, even though I have no specific examples of their importance, I can understand why and how they could be important. It clicked. It doesn't make me want to go do my worksheet on adding and subtracting them, but in math tomorrow, I will be a much happier camper. -Abby
623098c72cc6.target: 所以，我刚刚在数学课上开始学习虚数(Imaginary Numbers)，感到非常困惑。我理解了概念，但不明白实际应用，也不真正理解它是什么。我是一个需要完全理解概念的人，很难接受 i = 负一的平方根。我在谷歌上搜索，结果更困惑了。然后，我找到了你关于虚数的文章，突然就明白了。我可以把它形象化，即使没有具体的例子说明它们的重要性，我也能理解它们为什么以及如何重要。我顿悟了。这并没有让我想做加减虚数的练习题，但在明天的数学课上，我会开心很多。——Abby

e6f0f3365f1a.source: It drives me crazy to see endless tutorials on imaginary numbers that don't address the
e6f0f3365f1a.target: 看到无数关于虚数的教程没有解释负数怎么会有平方根的

0f76b655e55f.source: of how a negative number can have a square root. You can give me all the videos and interactive quizzes you want, I'm not truly learning until you explain the notion of a rotation.
0f76b655e55f.target: 本质，这让我抓狂。你可以给我所有视频和互动测验，但除非你解释旋转的概念，否则我并没有真正学到东西。

09abbc7a272d.target: 这种优先级错乱随处可见：

d225f7553bd4.source: Fix the plot, then worry about special effects.
d225f7553bd4.target: 先搞定情节，再担心特效。

594612a84bdf.source: Fix the recipe, then worry about decor.
594612a84bdf.target: 先搞定食谱，再担心装饰。

4445766663cc.source: Fix the melody, then worry about the instruments.
4445766663cc.target: 先搞定旋律，再担心乐器。

cc618278de60.source: Fix the analogy, then worry about the presentation format.
cc618278de60.target: 先搞定类比，再担心呈现形式。

1042ef551143.source: Identify what's held you back and fix that first.
1042ef551143.target: 找出什么在阻碍你，然后先解决它。

a82b7ec56fb5.source: Technology helps with certain limitations (access, distribution, cost). But the quality of the source material is still up to us. I'd prefer handwritten letters with Socrates to a HD video conference with Carrot Top.
a82b7ec56fb5.target: 技术可以帮助解决某些限制（获取、分发、成本）。但源材料的质量仍然取决于我们自己。我宁愿读苏格拉底手写的信，也不愿和胡萝卜头开高清视频会议。

52bc9034e4f5.source: Veritasium has a great video on these lines ("This Will Revolutionize Education"):
52bc9034e4f5.target: Veritasium 有一个关于这个主题的好视频（“这将彻底改变教育”）：

cfe40f0406f0.source: If we think the limiting factor in education is still distribution, we'll focus on technical solutions.
cfe40f0406f0.target: 如果我们认为教育的限制因素仍然是分发，我们会专注于技术解决方案。

583adf5900a5.source: But you know what? We've had Shakespeare
583adf5900a5.target: 但你知道吗？莎士比亚的作品

1a56880c57c5.source: for a few decades now. Modern kids must be poetry experts because of free access to quality literature, right?
1a56880c57c5.target: 我们已经有几十年了。现代孩子应该因为免费获取优质文学而成为诗歌专家，对吧？

6919ab841897.source: It's not an access problem any more. It's a motivation, interest, enthusiasm, understanding-what's-actually-going-on problem. Let's fix that first.
6919ab841897.target: 这已经不再是一个获取问题。这是一个动机、兴趣、热情、理解真正发生了什么的问题。让我们先解决这个问题。

68d422162a49.source: this article in Forbes about Vista adoption
68d422162a49.target: 福布斯上关于Vista采纳率的这篇文章

5608dbd7852c.source: Quality is often, but not always, a cause of popularity.
5608dbd7852c.target: 质量常常是流行的一个原因，但并非总是如此。

e3d5e5778d8e.source: And it’s all too easy to overlook Microsoft’s achievements, like making operating system software that works well enough and is priced well enough to attract 96% of the world’s PC users
e3d5e5778d8e.target: 而且我们很容易忽视微软的成就，比如制造出运行良好且价格合理、吸引了全球96% PC用户的操作系统软件。

163cef75492f.source: “Why did the manufacturer/IT department install that particular OS?”
163cef75492f.target: “为什么制造商/IT部门安装了那个特定的操作系统？”

fc4ee89dbf2a.source: Look at a product’s popularity and ask why
fc4ee89dbf2a.target: 审视一个产品的流行度并追问原因

acab33f6b6f8.source: If the computer manufacturer is Apple, they will install an Apple OS which supports their company — their definition of best.
acab33f6b6f8.target: 如果电脑制造商是苹果，他们会安装支持他们公司的苹果操作系统——他们眼中的最佳。

dd691ed17f61.source: If your company already runs Windows, the next computer they give you is likely to run Windows. Your IT admin will give you whatever is cheapest to operate, maintain and train you on — their definition of best.
dd691ed17f61.target: 如果你的公司已经在运行Windows，他们给你的下一台电脑很可能运行Windows。你的IT管理员会给你运营、维护和培训成本最低的系统——他们眼中的最佳。

6651dc5f1c4b.source: If it comes from a PC manufacturer like Dell, they will include whatever sells the most and has the most profit — their definition of “best”. Notice that I
6651dc5f1c4b.target: 如果来自戴尔这样的PC制造商，他们会包含销量最大、利润最高的产品——他们眼中的“最佳”。注意我

447dec1e2289.source: say security, features, reliability or user interface. They will focus on what sells the best, and the reason it sells doesn’t really matter (except, perhaps, to forecast what will sell the best in the future).
447dec1e2289.target: 说的是安全性、特性、可靠性或用户界面。他们会专注于什么卖得最好，而卖得好的原因其实无关紧要（或许除了预测未来什么会卖得最好之外）。

303b91e1c468.source: Arguing that an idea or product has merit because it has popular support is one of the oldest argument tactics around. It’s also one of the most flawed. Does McDonald’s have the
303b91e1c468.target: 认为一个想法或产品因为受到大众支持就具有价值，是最古老的论证手法之一。它也是最错误的论证之一。麦当劳的汉堡包真的是因为销量达到数十亿而美味吗？丰田汽车真的比法拉利更好，只是因为前者更常见吗？布兰妮·斯皮尔斯真的比莫扎特更优秀，只因为你在收音机里听不到莫扎特吗？反例不胜枚举，令人作呕。

fd26b811f477.source: hamburger because they sell billions? Is a Toyota
fd26b811f477.target: 汉堡包是因为他们卖出了数十亿个吗？丰田汽车真的

9394b1b5fdc2.source: than a Ferrari because one is far more common than the other? Is Britney Spears a better musician than Mozart, who you never hear on the radio? The counter-examples continue ad infinitum and ad nauseum.
9394b1b5fdc2.target: 比法拉利更好，只是因为一种比另一种更常见吗？布兰妮·斯皮尔斯真的比莫扎特更优秀，因为你在收音机里听不到莫扎特吗？反例无穷无尽，令人厌烦。

9c03cc8f5518.source: Why does this occur? My guess is that it’s a confusion between cause and effect.
9c03cc8f5518.target: 为什么会这样？我猜这是对因果关系的混淆。

fc9f97dafd3d.source: If two products are functionally identical, then the one with better quality should become more popular over time. The problem with this theoretical situation is, well, that it’s theoretical. Two products are never the same. There’s different marketing, branding, advertising, consumer perception of company, packaging, features, design and price. All of these factors, and a few that I’ve left out, contribute to what the consumer will buy.
fc9f97dafd3d.target: 如果两种产品在功能上完全相同，那么质量更好的产品应该会随着时间的推移而变得更加流行。这个理论情境的问题在于，它只是理论上的。两种产品从来不会完全相同。不同的营销、品牌、广告、消费者对公司的认知、包装、功能、设计和价格——所有这些因素，以及我遗漏的一些因素，都会影响消费者的购买决策。

c9e54523bdeb.source: Even professional journalists can make the mistake of arguing that popularity indicates some inherent quality. Take a look at
c9e54523bdeb.target: 即使是专业记者也可能犯这样的错误，认为流行就代表了某种内在质量。看看这篇文章：

5bf7623171ae.source: The article appears to say that Windows XP spread because of the “choice” of customers. Quick question: do you think most people actually choose their operating system, or just get whatever came on their machine?
5bf7623171ae.target: 这篇文章似乎在说Windows XP之所以普及，是因为顾客的“选择”。快速问题：你认为大多数人真的选择了他们的操作系统，还是只是用他们机器上预装的那个？

9cfc5577bd6d.source: the operating system on your phone? Your TV? For the non-geeks out there, no. You got whatever OS the manufacturer put on there (if you bought it) or whatever your IT department put on there (if your company bought it for you).
9cfc5577bd6d.target: 选择过你手机或电视的操作系统吗？对非极客来说，没有。你用的是制造商预装的操作系统（如果你自己买了设备），或者你公司的IT部门装的那个（如果是公司给你买的）。

63fec724fbe7.target: 所以，真正的问题变成了

3c0fc783e3e3.source: . There’s a long, sordid history on this, but there are a few obvious reasons:
3c0fc783e3e3.target: 。关于这一点，有一段漫长而肮脏的历史，但有几个显而易见的原因：

85bfd4f919c1.source: The question of what operating system is “best” is too general and doesn’t really make sense. Give me specifics: Fastest. Most reliable. Best price/performance. Most easy-to-use. Most compatible. Gimme some details!
85bfd4f919c1.target: “哪个操作系统最好”这个问题太笼统了，其实没有意义。给我具体点：最快的。最可靠的。最佳性价比。最容易使用的。最兼容的。给我一些细节！

d48e39cc81fe.source: Don’t presume that qualities like market share or usage is  a direct reflection of end-user choice. Sure,
d48e39cc81fe.target: 不要认为市场份额或使用率这样的指标直接反映了最终用户的选择。当然，

b7430ebe8cdb.source: because they have the best quality. Or, they can become popular because they have a lot of advertising. Or they have no competitors. Or because their name is first in the alphabet. Or people have inertia and don’t want to switch. There’s a lot of reasons. It’s a fallacy to think that just because something happened/was elected/was decided, it was the “best” decision. Sometimes things just happen.
b7430ebe8cdb.target: 因为它们质量最好而流行。或者，它们可以因为广告多而流行。或者因为它们没有竞争对手。或者因为它们的名字在字母表中排在前面。或者因为人们有惯性，不想更换。原因有很多。认为仅仅因为某事发生了/被选上了/被决定了，就是“最好”的决定，这是一种谬误。有时候事情就这么发生了。

0e8eb45da44b.source: In the case of PCs, popularity is a reflection of the choice by the providers (manufacturers) and the people making the purchasing decisions (IT departments). In most cases, users are just along for the ride. The vast, vast majority of operating system sales are made to manufacturers and corporations. Not many people buy Windows off the shelf at Best Buy.
0e8eb45da44b.target: 在个人电脑的情况下，流行反映了供应商（制造商）和做购买决策的人（IT部门）的选择。在大多数情况下，用户只是随波逐流。绝大多数操作系统销售都是面向制造商和企业的。没有多少人会在百思买货架上买Windows。

26a62f0bc193.source: Rewording the article to say “Windows is the best platform for users because it has the most market share, the most applications and is compatible with the most devices” makes sense. Claiming it is the best simply because it attracted a lot of people doesn’t.
26a62f0bc193.target: 将文章改写成“Windows对用户来说是最好的平台，因为它拥有最大的市场份额、最多的应用程序以及与最多设备的兼容性”是有道理的。声称它最好仅仅因为它吸引了很多人，则不然。

ddfd065171c2.source: Of course, this reasoning may not stop screaming fans from declaring their system the “best” because it is widely used. It will just prevent you from believing them.
ddfd065171c2.target: 当然，这种推理可能无法阻止狂热的粉丝们因为他们的系统被广泛使用而宣称它是“最好的”。但它可以防止你相信他们。

4c8e7f4cd83c.source: Show how your product is useful
4c8e7f4cd83c.target: 展示你的产品如何有用

e5240e0132da.source: Gives a sense of the product: “Ok, it’s some sort of spreadsheet/calculation tool”
e5240e0132da.target: 对产品有个大致概念：“嗯，某种电子表格/计算工具”

a9c9fbac4f42.source: Uses all the existing Excel marketing. Everyone knows what a spreadsheet is because Microsoft and others have spent decades educating and selling them to people. Use the existing lingo.
a9c9fbac4f42.target: 利用现有的Excel营销。大家都知道电子表格是什么，因为微软和其他公司花了数十年来教育和推广它们。使用现有的术语。

5ad75d31fec8.source: Gives a concrete measure of comparison: Excel must be difficult to use in some way, let’s see how this stacks up.
5ad75d31fec8.target: 提供了一个具体的比较标准：Excel在某种程度上一定难以使用，让我们看看这个相比之下如何。

2fbcff5589cb.source: Speak in terms of the known

cef3feebca9f.source: Make it easy on the reviewers

8fe44da3ff70.source: Put a name behind the site

92f5ec6a2049.source: Make it easy to get feedback

91ca1b0cc0cd.source: Don’t take my word for it

558e16925b3a.source: I’m no expert, but creating your own project invariably teaches you a few things about presenting your site. Here are a few lessons that helped me launch
558e16925b3a.target: 我并非专家，但创建自己的项目无疑会教你一些展示网站的技巧。以下是一些帮助我启动项目的经验

5f6e80b4d80e.target: ，一个实时的、可共享的计算器。

f58f33097f07.source: Don’t push out your initial version to the public right away. Create a version, iterate, create it again, iterate, and create it again. Show your friends, family, coworkers, neighbors… and then show the world.
f58f33097f07.target: 不要立即向公众发布你的初始版本。创建一个版本，迭代，再创建，再迭代。展示给你的朋友、家人、同事、邻居……然后再展示给全世界。

c150299f8239.source: I’m not a designer, but the site has improved markedly over the course of various iterations. Take a look at the progression:
c150299f8239.target: 我不是设计师，但经过多次迭代，网站明显改善了。看看这个进化过程：

d2f0b39cbccb.source: Of course, if you’re like me you can get caught in a constant trap of tweaking. It’s a learned skill, but have a sense of when it is “great” for a first-time visitor. It’s hard to look at your project with fresh eyes and imagine what a new visitor sees.
d2f0b39cbccb.target: 当然，如果你像我一样，可能会陷入不断调整的陷阱。这是一种需要学习的技能，但要懂得何时对首次访问者来说已经“很好”。很难用全新的眼光看待你的项目，并想象新访客看到了什么。

1635a0d9ae0b.source: There’s a balance between refininement and “just get it done”, but it’s hard to impress first-time visitors without getting some feedback first. Don’t tell strangers about your site without first testing with your friends.
1635a0d9ae0b.target: 在“精雕细琢”和“尽快完成”之间需要平衡，但如果没有事先获得反馈，就很难给首次访问者留下深刻印象。不要在没有先与朋友测试的情况下，向陌生人介绍你的网站。

492ce20ecc42.source: Site visitors may not read your text, but they can’t avoid a screenshot.
492ce20ecc42.target: 网站访问者可能不会阅读你的文字，但他们无法忽略截图。

bb410c0e2b5a.source: . I could just have written text about this, and tried to explain it. After getting feedback (see above), I used quick text (only a line or two) and screenshots to show the capabilities:
bb410c0e2b5a.target: . 我本可以只写文字来解释。但获得反馈（见上文）后，我使用了简短文字（仅一两行）和截图来展示功能：

014ce89a45f6.source: Is this the only way, the best way? No way. But I know it sends a clear, succinct message and is much more effective than a plain-text description.
014ce89a45f6.target: 这是唯一的方式、最好的方式吗？绝对不是。但我知道它传达了一个清晰简洁的信息，并且比纯文本描述有效得多。

206ab79cc219.source: If you have a new idea, relate it to existing ideas. Rather than saying “This tool gets answers fast!”, which could be true but doesn’t tell us what it does, say: “Easier than Excel.”
206ab79cc219.target: 如果你有一个新想法，将其与现有想法联系起来。与其说“这个工具能快速得到答案！”（这可能是真的，但没有说明它做什么），不如说：“比Excel更容易。”

aee51dc339d3.source: The reviewers are the ones who help spread the word. The points above make it easy for them: give people screenshots, taglines, and feature comparisons so reviewers have something to talk about.
aee51dc339d3.target: 评论者是帮助传播信息的人。上面的要点让他们更轻松：提供截图、标语和功能对比，让评论者有话题可谈。

6bedc06fae6b.source: the text and screenshots as a starting point to discuss what was possible with their readers. Make your site reviewable. Have tours,
6bedc06fae6b.target: 以文本和截图作为起点，与读者讨论可能的事情。让你的网站易于评价。提供导览、

1769988a8107.source: s, easy screenshots and quoteable taglines.
1769988a8107.target: s、易获取的截图和可引用的标语。

1f72550db2e8.source: suggested I put up a quick

1114883ff64c.source: . This ended up being a big deal – there are lots of anonymous sites and services out there. Being able to say “InstaCalc was created by Kalid Azad,
1114883ff64c.target: 。这最终变得很重要——网上有很多匿名网站和服务。能够说“InstaCalc是由Kalid Azad创建的，

877e0a2bfaef.source: …” in your review makes the article more substantial and adds credibility.
877e0a2bfaef.target: ……”在你的评论中使文章更充实，增加了可信度。

bcb400512e8a.source: I have three methods of feedback: an anonymous
bcb400512e8a.target: 我有三种反馈方式：一个匿名

2d89c491980c.source: . 95% of my feedback comes from the first two methods – most people can’t be bothered to send email directly, which is way more formal than a simple one-line comment in a blog or form.
2d89c491980c.target: 。95%的反馈来自前两种方式——大多数人懒得直接发邮件，这比在博客或表单中写一行评论要正式得多。

b1d773e1455d.source: Most people just want to fire off a quick note, so let them do it without a hassle. The contact form is fully anonymous – there are no required fields.
b1d773e1455d.target: 大多数人只想快速发一条消息，所以让他们轻松完成。联系表单是完全匿名的——没有必填字段。

d0f6413c628a.source: Like most things in life, experiment and see what works for you. Some products may be better served by secretive campaigns with less detail up front. Others might want anonymity (like the
d0f6413c628a.target: 像生活中的大多数事情一样，尝试一下，看看什么适合你。有些产品可能更适合前期细节较少的秘密活动。其他人可能想要匿名（比如

3a3a5df402bc.source: ). Take these lessons as nuggets of information gleaned from a single example, and use them if they make sense.
3a3a5df402bc.target: ）。将这些经验视为从单个例子中收集到的信息金块，如果合理就加以利用。

17061ef108e4.source: Here's a talk on "Math And Analogies" I did for the

40edeff246a7.target: (CoCA)所做的一次关于“数学与类比”的演讲。

e64883969cfd.source: What's this? Well, it's a number. Probably written down a few thousand years ago. Great. And people, I suppose, could have read it back then if they spent enough time. And if we want, we can figure it out by spending time on it too (sigh).
e64883969cfd.target: 这是什么？嗯，这是一个数字。大概几千年前被人写下来的。很好。我想，如果当时的人花足够的时间，他们应该是能读懂的。而如果我们想，我们也可以花时间弄明白（叹气）。

17955ccdcce9.source: So let's see. We have M, that's 1000. Then we have CM, that's 100 less than 1000, or 900. Then we have XC, which is 10 less than 100, or 90. Then we have VII, which is two more than 5, or 7. So the number is 1997.
17955ccdcce9.target: 让我们来看看。M代表1000。CM表示比1000少100，即900。XC表示比100少10，即90。VII表示比5多2，即7。所以这个数字是1997。

03a89f3ce0b1.source: OK, that wasn't fun. The question: why was that hard? Why was that difficult? Why was one representation so much easier? Well, there's a few answers.
03a89f3ce0b1.target: 好吧，这并不有趣。问题是：为什么这么难？为什么这么困难？为什么一种表示法会简单这么多？有几个答案。

31eb74ebdf33.source: Sorry guys, our brains just can't handle Roman numerals. I mean, computers can use them, why can't we? We're just not smart enough. And we're just bad at math.
31eb74ebdf33.target: 抱歉，我们的大脑就是处理不了罗马数字。我的意思是，计算机能用它们，为什么我们不能？我们只是不够聪明。而且我们不擅长数学。

1359de700fb2.source: That's one answer, and maybe it's possible that humans are incapable of understanding numbers.
1359de700fb2.target: 这是一个答案，也许人类确实没有能力理解数字。

c8c25de1c520.source: But I think the better answer is that those numbers were poorly designed. Roman numerals aren't a great representation of numbers. We're trying to keep track of numbers in terms of two more than five, and one less than ten. We can't just say 9, we need to say "one less than ten", and so on. It makes things really complicated and there's not really a good reason for it. The result is that it's cumbersome, hard to use, and our brains are doing extra work they don't have to.
c8c25de1c520.target: 但我认为更好的答案是那些数字设计得很糟糕。罗马数字不是数字的良好表示法。我们试图用“比五多二”、“比十少一”的方式来记录数字。我们不能直接说9，而要说“比十少一”，诸如此类。这让事情变得非常复杂，而且没有充分的理由。结果就是它笨重、难用，我们的大脑做了不必要的额外工作。

805aacc053d3.source: My goal for learning is to find the analogies that help things make sense. The analogy of numbers being in terms of "two more than five", "one less than ten" isn't a great system. Decimals are much better and that's what we use today.
805aacc053d3.target: 我学习的目标是找到能帮助理解的类比。将数字表示为“比五多二”、“比十少一”的类比不是一个好体系。十进制要好得多，这也是我们今天使用的体系。

721e8af556e0.source: The idea is that an analogy is like a raft you use to cross a river. The river is the problem, the concept, something that you want to understand, and the raft is how you approach it.
721e8af556e0.target: 其理念是，类比就像你用来过河的筏子。河流就是问题、概念，是你要理解的东西，而筏子就是你处理它的方式。

9fd15eeb4494.source: If you don't have an analogy at all, yes, maybe you're strong enough to swim to the other side by pure brute force, but usually you have a mental model that helps you with the problem. The problem is some mental models aren't as good as others. The idea of Roman numerals, while it "works", is not as good as decimals. We're taking a weaker raft to cross the river.
9fd15eeb4494.target: 如果你完全没有类比，也许你可以凭借纯粹的蛮力游到对岸，但通常你会有一个心智模型来帮助你解决问题。问题是，有些心智模型不如其他模型好。罗马数字的概念虽然“有效”，但不如十进制好。我们是在用更弱的筏子过河。

ec7506c18898.source: Let's take a look at a few mental models we've tried so far.
ec7506c18898.target: 让我们看看我们目前尝试过的一些心智模型。

5ccec5438383.source: Rocks were the first mental model for counting. The word calculus (calculate) comes from "pebble", and the idea that numbers are basically rocks being counted is a powerful analogy. It sounds simple, but it's powerful.
5ccec5438383.target: 石头是计数的第一个心智模型。“微积分”(calculus)这个词源自“鹅卵石”(pebble)，而数字基本上就是被计数的石头，这个类比非常有力。听起来很简单，但很强大。

1bae9d2c934d.source: The reason is we've taken out all the differences. There's a bunch of rocks in this photo. Different sizes, colors, weights, everything -- but we're counting them as rocks. And that's the big idea: we take things that are different in countless ways, but find the unifying principle behind them. And now we're counting that.
1bae9d2c934d.target: 原因是我们忽略了所有差异。这张照片里有一堆石头。大小、颜色、重量各不相同——但我们把它们当作石头来计数。这就是关键想法：我们把无数不同的事物，找到它们背后的统一原则，然后对那个统一体进行计数。

0114c23c5ff0.source: So the first concept (mental model) is that numbers are like rocks. Numbers are these physical things that unify disparate items and we can count them together and it works pretty well. One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten. With enough time we can count a herd of sheep. We can count money, it works pretty well.
0114c23c5ff0.target: 所以第一个概念(心智模型)是数字就像石头。数字是这些物理的东西，统一了不同的物品，我们可以一起计数，效果不错。一、二、三、四、五、六、七、八、九、十。有足够的时间，我们可以数一群羊。我们可以数钱，效果很好。

d0bd227599eb.source: Well, it's zero. Zero rocks, zero sheep, zero billion dollars are right there. Zero of the most beautiful painting in the world is right there.
d0bd227599eb.target: 嗯，是零。零块石头，零只羊，零亿美元就在那里。世界上最美丽的画作的零幅就在那里。

3cdb79ebd530.source: Zero is a really weird concept. And when you've tied your concept of a number to physical items, concepts like zero become difficult.
3cdb79ebd530.target: 零是一个非常奇怪的概念。当你把数字的概念与实物挂钩时，像零这样的概念就变得困难了。

95cff6a5b24e.source: Now we might work through it and say, "OK, let's allow for nothingness". So we can have "nothingness" as well as "somethingness".
95cff6a5b24e.target: 现在我们可以解决它，说：“好吧，让我们允许‘无’的存在。”所以我们既可以有“有”，也可以有“无”。

a70c4930ce02.source: OK, how about this? What's less than nothing? What's a negative rock?
a70c4930ce02.target: 好吧，那这个呢？什么比无还少？什么是负数石头？

ae5cefe1718c.source: Is it a rock that you owe? Well, if you're holding it, it's not negative, right? Is negative money something I owe you? Again, if I gave it to you, it might be negative to me, but it sure seems positive to you. There's a lot of things that get messed up when we're trying to use a very physical analogy. So the analogy, the raft is starting to break down. It's not helping us understand things.
ae5cefe1718c.target: 是你欠的石头吗？嗯，如果你拿着它，它就不是负数，对吧？负数货币是我欠你的吗？同样，如果我把它给你，对我来说可能是负数，但对你来说显然是正数。当我们试图使用一个非常物理的类比时，很多东西都会乱套。所以这个类比，这个筏子开始出问题了。它不能帮助我们理解事物。

f0ca2379d48a.source: In fact, even in 1759 people thought negative numbers were confusing. They "darken the very whole doctrines of the equations", right? They just didn't make sense. It was a weird concept and the problem was our analogy just couldn't handle it.
f0ca2379d48a.target: 事实上，即使在1759年，人们也认为负数令人困惑。它们“模糊了整个方程的理论”，对吧？它们就是没有意义。这是一个奇怪的概念，问题在于我们的类比无法处理它。

5a87a99fa9b4.source: But of course, any modern third grader can tell you we have the concept of a number line. Moving our mental model to a number line let negatives and zero be part of the picture.
5a87a99fa9b4.target: 但当然，任何现代三年级学生都能告诉你，我们有数轴的概念。将我们的心智模型转移到数轴上，负数和零就成了图景的一部分。

323d9fa0c4f2.source: Instead of zero as a void, it became the neutral point, the center, the place that every other number moves away from. That's a really cool concept -- positive and negative numbers aren't that different. They're left and right directions away from the same starting point.
323d9fa0c4f2.target: 零不再是虚空，而是中性点、中心点，所有其他数字都远离它。这是一个非常酷的概念——正数和负数并没有那么大的不同。它们是离开同一个起始点的左右方向。

3178cd7e968a.source: Something that was really, really difficult became easy with the right analogy. Let's keep going.
3178cd7e968a.target: 以前非常非常困难的事情，有了正确的类比就变得容易了。我们继续。

b6005ca1cad4.source: How about something like this? i squared equals -1. This is super confusing. Our raft is starting to shake.
b6005ca1cad4.target: 像这样的东西呢？i 的平方等于 -1。这超级令人困惑。我们的筏子开始摇晃了。

3598d912c66a.source: Is there any number that when you square it becomes a negative? Zero squared is still zero. A positive number, when squared, is still positive. A negative number, when squared, becomes positive.
3598d912c66a.target: 有没有任何数字，平方后变成负数？零的平方还是零。正数的平方仍然是正数。负数的平方变成正数。

a0fe4685d300.source: So, it seems like there's no solution. But again, this is like trying to find that negative rock. We're trying to apply the analogy in a way that limits our thinking. The analogy isn't the ultimate truth, right?
a0fe4685d300.target: 所以，似乎没有解。但同样，这就像试图找到那块负数石头。我们试图以限制我们思维方式的方式应用类比。类比并不是终极真理，对吧？

d8632da8fb7a.source: There might be a different way of thinking that makes it make sense, but we're stuck in this old system.
d8632da8fb7a.target: 可能有一种不同的思考方式能使其合理，但我们被困在了这个旧系统中。

ecd0b9fc5c83.source: Here's an idea: If we have a number line going left and right, why not another number line going north and south?
ecd0b9fc5c83.target: 这里有一个想法：如果我们有一条左右延伸的数轴，为什么不能再加上一条南北延伸的数轴呢？

700a04b7ddc8.source: We have East/West, why not North/South too? Let's allow this extra dimension where numbers can move up and down, left or right.
700a04b7ddc8.target: 我们有东西方向，为什么不能也有南北方向呢？让我们允许这个额外的维度，数字可以在其中上下左右移动。

6a5a1bd4e7ff.source: The imaginary dimension lets us get to negatives in two steps. Saying "i squared is negative one" is really saying: I'm starting at 1. We multiply by i, multiply by i again, and get to -1. So i represents a 90-degree rotation, and rotating twice points you backwards. That's what "i squared equals -1" means.
6a5a1bd4e7ff.target: 虚数维度让我们可以通过两步到达负数。说“i的平方等于负一”其实是在说：我从1开始。乘以i，再乘以i，得到-1。所以i代表90度旋转，旋转两次就会指向后方。这就是“i的平方等于-1”的含义。

3f6b611e8c5c.source: Finding the right analogy makes something that seems baffling click, all because we're using the right metaphor. We're thinking about it the right way. So, we've gone from rocks to lines to directions and that seems pretty good.
3f6b611e8c5c.target: 找到合适的类比，会让原本令人困惑的东西豁然开朗，这完全是因为我们使用了恰当的比喻。我们以正确的方式思考它。所以，我们从石头到线段再到方向，这似乎很不错。

7dd3aae2581c.source: Let's put this into action a little bit. Here's a very famous equation. Euler's Identity is one of the most beautiful in math.
7dd3aae2581c.target: 让我们稍微实践一下。这里有一个非常著名的方程。欧拉恒等式是数学中最美的方程之一。

4fde783813da.source: If you ask a mathematician or a physicist, this is usually considered the most beautiful identity in all of mathematics. It relates to so many different concepts.
4fde783813da.target: 如果你问数学家或物理学家，这通常被认为是整个数学中最美的恒等式。它与许多不同的概念相关。

48326b770ca8.source: The problem is it's really confusing and again, mathematicians thought it was just crazy when it came out. e to the i times pi equals negative one. We're taking these crazy constants: pi is irrational. i is this weird dimension, and e is another irrational number. All of these combined to equal -1 in such a clean way. It's wild.
48326b770ca8.target: 问题在于它非常令人困惑，而且再次，数学家们最初认为它很疯狂。e的i乘以pi次方等于负一。我们把这些疯狂常数组合在一起：pi是无理数，i是这个古怪的维度，e是另一个无理数。所有这些如此简洁地组合起来等于-1。这太不可思议了。

5e64cba153fa.source: And so people thought that OK, there are proofs, but it can't be understood. Again, that's coming from out existing analogies. If we have poor analogies for what numbers are, then yes, concepts like this are difficult, but with the right analogies they can make sense.
5e64cba153fa.target: 所以人们认为，好吧，有证明，但无法理解。再次，这源于我们原有的类比。如果我们对数字的类比很差，那么像这样的概念确实困难，但有了恰当的类比，它们就能被理解。

6da582388c13.source: So let's take a crack at it. I like to mentally colorize equations. I want to understand what every single part of the equation is trying to say.
6da582388c13.target: 那么让我们尝试一下。我喜欢在脑海中给方程上色。我想理解方程中每一部分想要表达什么。

5387d235b2cd.source: Normally we look at equations as a mix of symbols, but if we can identify what each part is doing, it becomes a sentence. It becomes a story of what's actually happening. So let's start.
5387d235b2cd.target: 通常我们把方程看作符号的混合，但如果我们能识别出每一部分的作用，它就变成了一个句子。它变成了实际发生的故事。那么让我们开始。

f84a2a9fb73f.source: e represents growth. It could be an entire separate video, but e is the concept of continuous growth, and i is the concept of going sideways (not the normal direction).
f84a2a9fb73f.target: e代表增长。这可以单独做一个视频，但e是持续增长的概念，而i是横向（非正常方向）的概念。

c8f538f38523.source: So the concept is that we have growth, but instead of growing like we want, we're growing sideways. And how long do we do this for?
c8f538f38523.target: 所以概念是我们有增长，但不是像我们想要的那样增长，而是横向增长。我们这样做多久？

8c42a2ad6b1a.source: Pi is halfway around a circle. A unit circle has two pi as its full circumference. So a single pi is just halfway around the circle.
8c42a2ad6b1a.target: Pi是半个圆。一个单位圆的周长是2pi。所以单个pi就是刚好半个圆。

010eb7673f82.source: We have the concept of growth going sideways that is lasting for half a circle. And if we plot it out, it looks like this:
010eb7673f82.target: 我们有一个横向增长的概念，持续了半个圆。如果把它画出来，就像这样：

c53a94e03db1.source: We have growth, but it's going sideways, and we have enough fuel in the growth engine to point us backwards. So the equation is saying that this system of growth, pushing sideways, with enough fuel to last for half a circle, will point you backwards to negative one.
c53a94e03db1.target: 我们有增长，但它是横向的，并且在增长引擎中有足够的燃料让我们指向后方。所以这个方程是在说，这个增长系统，横向推动，有足够的燃料持续半个圆，会让你指向后方到负一。

b297613c9fdd.source: 1 was the implied starting point. Start growing sideways with enough fuel to go half a circle and you'll end up backwards. So the key is we understood a concept that was baffling using a very simple diagram and analogy.
b297613c9fdd.target: 1是隐含的起点。开始横向增长，有足够的燃料走半个圆，你会最终指向后方。所以关键是我们通过一个非常简单的图表和类比理解了一个令人困惑的概念。

4f4b225b0537.source: Now to really test it, let's try a few scenarios.
4f4b225b0537.target: 现在为了真正测试它，让我们尝试几个场景。

c5966e8f4f5c.source: What if we don't put in any fuel?
c5966e8f4f5c.target: 如果我们不添加任何燃料呢？

b49d3eac880a.source: What if we want to grow sideways, but we decided not to grow it all. We have the engine ready to go, but we don't fuel it up. If we put zero here instead, then we would stay at the starting point of 1.
b49d3eac880a.target: 如果我们想横向增长，但决定完全不增长呢？我们有引擎准备好，但没有加燃料。如果我们在这里放零，那么我们将保持在起点1。

86677a4d4618.source: Mathematically, we can say anything to the zeroth power is 1, which is true, but it's more helpful to think "I have this engine setup but didn't put any fuel into it, I never used it." Then you'll stay at your starting point.
86677a4d4618.target: 数学上，我们可以说任何数的零次幂等于1，这是对的，但更有帮助的思考是“我有这个引擎设置但没有加任何燃料，我从未使用过它。”那么你将停留在你的起点。

6397c3a0f9e2.source: If I put in twice as much fuel (2 * pi), I should circle all the way around. And again, we can mathematically say that if we square both sides, the result should be 1, but I like to think of it as using twice the fuel and going all the way around.
6397c3a0f9e2.target: 如果投入两倍的燃料（2 * pi），我就能绕一整圈。同样，从数学上我们可以说，如果两边平方，结果会是1，但我更喜欢将其视为使用两倍的燃料并绕一整圈。

37e8ea2c79b3.source: This is how to take an analogy and put it to work, helping you understand.
37e8ea2c79b3.target: 这就是如何将一个类比付诸实践，帮助你理解。

527eeacc135f.source: The main takeaway for me is that good analogies make math a joy. An idea that's baffling can become simple, even enjoyable, if we see it the right way. That's the heart of my philosophy. If I'm confused by something, I don't think my CPU isn't good enough. I just think I had the wrong analogy.
527eeacc135f.target: 对我来说，主要的收获是好的类比让数学变得愉悦。一个令人困惑的想法，如果我们以正确的方式看待，就会变得简单甚至有趣。这就是我哲学的核心。如果我对某事感到困惑，我不会认为自己的CPU不够好，而只是觉得我用了错误的类比。

b170464dcd46.source: It’s easy to forget math is a language for
b170464dcd46.target: 人们很容易忘记数学是一种语言，用于

4bda0230d622.source: . As words, “two and three is equal to five” is cumbersome. Replacing numbers and operations with symbols helps: “2 + 3 is equal to 5”.
4bda0230d622.target: 用文字表达，“二加三等于五”很冗长。用符号代替数字和运算会有帮助：“2 + 3 is equal to 5”。

74b5a40ae30a.source: But we can do better. In 1557, Robert Recorde
74b5a40ae30a.target: 但我们还能做得更好。1557年，Robert Recorde

05a907e55cdd.source: the equals sign, written with two parallel lines (=), because “noe 2 thynges, can be moare equalle”.
05a907e55cdd.target: 发明了等号，用两条平行线（=）表示，因为“没有两样东西能比这更相等了”。

659687e31ede.source: “2 + 3 = 5” is much easier to read. Unfortuantely, the meaning of “equals” changes with the context — just ask programmers who have to distinguish =, == and ===.
659687e31ede.target: “2 + 3 = 5”更容易阅读。不幸的是，“等于”的含义随上下文而变化——问问那些必须区分 =、== 和 === 的程序员就知道了。

5747f89c04a7.source: A “equals” B is a generic conclusion: what
5747f89c04a7.target: A“等于”B是一个泛泛的结论：我们

cb2c086b57a8.source: I see “2 + 3 = 5” as “2 + 3 can be simplified to 5”. The equals sign transitions a complex form on the left to an equivalent, simpler form on the right.
cb2c086b57a8.target: 我将“2 + 3 = 5”理解为“2 + 3 可以简化为 5”。等号将左侧的复杂形式转换为右侧等价的、更简单的形式。

8b0a8b90fd04.source: Statements like “speed = 50” mean “the speed is 50, for this scenario”. It’s only good for the problem at hand, and there’s no need to remember this “fact”.
8b0a8b90fd04.target: 像“speed = 50”这样的语句意思是“在这个场景下，速度为50”。它仅适用于当前问题，不需要记住这个“事实”。

db74540a8cb0.source: like $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$, where a, b, and c are the sides of a right triangle.
db74540a8cb0.target: 像 $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$，其中 a、b、c 是直角三角形的边。

6afa9b8a0463.source: I read this equals sign as “must always be equal to” or “can be seen as” because it states a permanent relationship, not a coincidence. The arithmetic of $3^2 + 4^2 = 5^2$ is a simplification; the geometry of $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$ is a deep mathematical truth.
6afa9b8a0463.target: 我将这个等号理解为“必须始终等于”或“可以被视为”，因为它陈述的是一个永久关系，而非巧合。$3^2 + 4^2 = 5^2$ 的算术是一个简化；$a^2 + b^2 = c^2$ 的几何则是一个深刻的数学真理。

0814f6ae0531.source: which can be seen as a type of geometric rearrangement, combinatorics, averaging, or even list-making.
0814f6ae0531.target: 可以被视为一种几何重新排列、组合数学、平均甚至列表制作。

df50610fb513.source: are definitions of our choosing; the left hand side is a shortcut for the right hand side. It’s similar to temporary assignment, but reserved for “facts” that won’t change between scenarios (e always has the same value in every equation, but “speed” can change).
df50610fb513.target: 是我们选择的定义；左侧是右侧的简写。它类似于临时赋值，但保留给在不同场景下不会改变的“事实”（e 在每个方程中总是相同的值，但“speed”可以变化）。

dc62de655eab.source: Here’s a tricky one. We might write
dc62de655eab.target: 这是一个棘手的例子。我们可能会写

27c24f67c375.source: to be true. I read this as “x + y should be 5, if possible” and “x – y should be 3, if possible”. If we satisfy the constraints (x=4, y=1), great!
27c24f67c375.target: 是真实的。我把这个理解为“x + y 应该等于 5，如果可能的话”，而“x – y 应该等于 3，如果可能的话”。如果我们满足约束 (x=4, y=1)，那就太好了！

222708668468.source: If we can’t meet both goals (x + y = 5; 2x + 2y = 9) then the equations could be true individually but not together.
222708668468.target: 如果我们不能同时满足两个目标 (x + y = 5; 2x + 2y = 9)，那么这些方程单独可能成立，但无法同时成立。

e71d12c05d90.source: Untangling the equals sign helped me decode
e71d12c05d90.target: 理清等号(equals sign)的含义帮助我理解了

35d89879686e.source: A strange beast, indeed. What type of “equals” is it?
35d89879686e.target: 真是个奇怪的家伙。这究竟是哪种“等于(equals)”？

693dc3a3107e.source: A pedant might say it’s just a simplification and break out the calulus to show it. This isn’t enlightening: there’s a fundamental relationship to discover.
693dc3a3107e.target: 学究可能会说这只是一个化简，然后搬出微积分(Calculus)来证明。但这没有启发性：这里有一个需要发现的基本关系。

ef5bba38fc52.source: e^i*pi refers to the same destination as -1. Two fingers pointing at the same moon.
ef5bba38fc52.target: e^i*pi 指向与 -1 相同的目的地。两根手指指向同一个月亮。

392c2f0f9832.source: They are both ways to describe “the other side of the unit circle, 180 degrees away”. -1 walks there, trodding straight through the grass, while e^i*pi takes the scenic route and rotates through the imaginary dimension. This works for any point on the circle: rotate there, or move in straight lines.
392c2f0f9832.target: 它们都是描述“单位圆的另一边，180度之外”的方式。-1 直接穿过草地走过去，而 e^i*pi 则走风景路线，通过虚数维度旋转。这适用于圆上的任何点：旋转过去，或者直线移动。

2d9adf26ff6b.source: Two paths with the same destination:
2d9adf26ff6b.target: 两条路径通向同一个目的地：

7572162b0588.source: what their equality means. Move beyond a generic equals and find the deeper, specific connection (“simplifies to”, “has been chosen to be”, “refers to the same concept as”).

dc8286f55a69.source: I became fascinated with intuitive learning after a tough math class at Princeton University. After cramming for days, the formulas finally clicked -- "Argh! Why couldn't they explain it like that in the first place?" I had to share my insights to save other people my frustration.
dc8286f55a69.target: 在普林斯顿大学上了一门艰难的数学课后，我对直观学习产生了浓厚兴趣。经过几天的死记硬背，公式终于豁然开朗——‘啊！为什么他们一开始不这么解释呢？’我必须分享我的见解，以免他人重蹈我的挫败。

e8acc78ada42.source: That "aha!" moment spawned BetterExplained.com, whose articles have been read by millions worldwide. It's regarded as one of the best places to really understand math. Memorization is fragile, but insights stick.
e8acc78ada42.target: 那个“啊哈！”时刻催生了BetterExplained.com网站，文章被全球数百万人阅读。它被誉为真正理解数学的最佳地方之一。记忆是脆弱的，但见解却能扎根。
