ac6ff36a7c6a.source: Everyone’s heard of the stock market — but few know why it works. Were you aware that each stock has two prices? That you can’t buy and sell for the same amount? That a “stock market” works better and is more open than a “stock store”?
ac6ff36a7c6a.target: 每个人都听说过股票市场——但很少有人知道它为什么有效。你是否知道每只股票有两个价格？你不能以同样的金额买入和卖出？一个“股票市场”比一个“股票商店”运作得更好、更开放？

0e31da2be058.source: If you’re like most of us, probably not. Here’s why stock markets rock:
0e31da2be058.target: 如果你和我们大多数人一样，可能不知道。以下是股票市场为何如此出色的原因：

51fb05a02c95.source: Most explanations jump into the minor details — not here. Today we’ll see why the stock market works as it does.
51fb05a02c95.target: 大多数解释都会深入细节——但这里不会。今天我们将看到股票市场为什么以它现有的方式运作。

10741f0b7d40.source: I’m told iPhones are popular with the 18-35 demographic. A market research firm asked me to find a good selling price, so I’ll pass the question onto you:
10741f0b7d40.target: 我听说 iPhone 在 18-35 岁人群中很受欢迎。一家市场研究公司让我找到一个好的售价，所以我把问题抛给你：

3bfa06ebd43e.source: Me: You, the coveted 18-35 year old demographic, want an iPhone. What’s it worth?
3bfa06ebd43e.target: 我：你，令人垂涎的 18-35 岁人群，想要一部 iPhone。它值多少钱？

82b8dd8ab901.source: You: Dude, just get the price. Duh.
82b8dd8ab901.target: 你：老兄，直接告诉我价格吧。废话。

e167884653a3.source: Ok hotshot, riddle me this: what
e167884653a3.target: 好吧，大人物，回答我这个问题：什么

74f3047fb642.source: So which price is the “real one”? Both.
74f3047fb642.target: 那么哪个价格才是“真正的价格”呢？两者都是。

5a8f6fb831bd.source: You see, buyers and sellers each have prices in mind. When prices match, whablamo, there’s a transaction (no match,
5a8f6fb831bd.target: 你看，买家和卖家各自心中都有价格。当价格匹配时，哐当一下，交易就发生了（如果没有匹配，

53aacfd78806.source: is key to understanding any market, not just stocks. Everything has a bid and an ask, and each shopping model has a different way of handling them. This leads to different advantages for buyers and sellers.
53aacfd78806.target: 是理解任何市场的关键，不仅仅是股票市场。每样东西都有买价(Bid)和卖价(Ask)，每种购物模式处理它们的方式不同。这给买卖双方带来了不同的优势。

3e4e5724f058.source: Suppose we want to buy an iPhone from Amazon. You see the selling price of \$200 (Amazon’s ask), and personally decide if it’s worth it (i.e. less than or equal to your bid):
3e4e5724f058.target: 假设我们想从亚马逊购买一部iPhone。你看到售价是\$200（亚马逊的要价），然后自己决定是否值得（即小于或等于你的买价）：

8e71e8fdd9e9.source: In the store model, Amazon shows a public asking price (\$200). Each buyer has a secret bidding price, some more than others. Buyers willing to bid \$200 or more purchase it; the rest hold off  (\$199 and below).
8e71e8fdd9e9.target: 在商店模式中，亚马逊公布一个公开要价（\$200）。每个买家都有一个秘密的出价，有些人的出价更高。愿意出价\$200或以上的买家购买它；其余的则暂时不买（\$199及以下）。

0e55f10feedb.source: Amazon picks a price that attracts the most bidders yet still keeps a profit. In the store model:
0e55f10feedb.target: 亚马逊选择一个能吸引最多买家但仍能盈利的价格。在商店模式中：

3d920f3155a0.source: Even though buyers are “in control”, they may have to search around to find a store that meets their bid (if any). That’s inefficient.
3d920f3155a0.target: 尽管买家“掌控局面”，但他们可能需要四处寻找符合自己出价的商店（如果有的话）。这效率低下。

7082d51e3834.source: Now suppose we want to sell our new, unopened gadget (you, the 18-35 demographic, are fickle like that; the survey said so). Sure, we could try to sell it on Amazon — now we’re our own store and need a price we
7082d51e3834.target: 现在假设我们要卖掉我们崭新未拆封的小玩意（你，18-35岁的人群，就是那么善变；调查是这么说的）。当然，我们可以试着在亚马逊上卖——现在我们自己就是商店，需要一个我们

8126c7dbe4c4.source: people will pay. We’re in the same boat as Amazon, and could set the price too low. That’s no fun.
8126c7dbe4c4.target: 人们愿意支付的价格。我们和亚马逊处境相同，可能把价格定得太低。那就不妙了。

2cf9929060de.source: Instead, we auction off the new iPhone on eBay to maximize profits:
2cf9929060de.target: 相反，我们在eBay上拍卖这部新iPhone以最大化利润：

329cf664abbf.source: In the eBay model, buyers have public bids and compete for the product. The seller keeps their minimum price secret and hopes to make a profit by having someone “overpay”. In the auction model:
329cf664abbf.target: 在eBay模式中，买家有公开出价，并为产品竞争。卖家保密自己的最低价格，希望通过有人“多付”来盈利。在拍卖模式中：

6abc5d53ee2f.source: eBay is great for sellers — you have the chance of making extra profit. For buyers, it’s not so great: you can lose auctions by \$1 (paying 201 when 202 was the highest bid), even though the seller would have been happy with 201. You could enter multiple auctions with \$201 but risk getting two iPhones.
6abc5d53ee2f.target: eBay对卖家很有利——你有机会获得额外利润。对买家来说，就没那么好了：你可能因为差1美元而输掉拍卖（支付201美元，而202美元是最高出价），尽管卖家本可以对201美元感到满意。你可能参加多个出价201美元的拍卖，但冒着买到两部iPhone的风险。

3b354d947117.source: In want ads, the asks are transparent while the bids (your value) are hidden. When haggling, both prices are hidden which can lead to a stressful situation.
3b354d947117.target: 在分类广告中，要价是透明的，而出价（你的估值）是隐藏的。在讨价还价中，双方价格都隐藏，可能导致紧张的局面。

f3363d4c0ef4.source: Each model has similar concepts, namely:
f3363d4c0ef4.target: 每种模式都有相似的概念，即：

6eac9d5c85f4.source: refers to how effectively you can trade; how easily cash can
6eac9d5c85f4.target: 指的是交易的效率；现金如何轻松地

d2707f2ba242.source: . When buyers and sellers have to argue or haggle, trading freezes up. In particular, there’s a common problem in the market above:
d2707f2ba242.target: 。当买卖双方必须争论或讨价还价时，交易就会冻结。特别是，上述市场存在一个常见问题：

5233693a376f.source: When buyers and sellers need to search to find each other, and haggle when they get there, trading slows down.
5233693a376f.target: 当买家和卖家需要互相搜索才能找到对方，并讨价还价时，交易速度就会减慢。

6795bff56335.source: But hope is not lost! Surprisingly, the very symbol of capitalism is an “open source” model:
6795bff56335.target: 但希望犹存！令人惊讶的是，资本主义的象征本身就是一个“开源”模式：

0969738a6b4d.source: And here’s what it looks like:

efc4fe08da23.source: Every iPhone seller lists their asking price (210, 205, 201, 200). Every iPhone buyer lists their buying price (190, 195, 199, 200). When prices match, a transaction happens: the buyer who wants to pay 200 gets matched with the seller who wants 200. They’re happy.
efc4fe08da23.target: 每个iPhone卖家列出他们的要价（210、205、201、200）。每个iPhone买家列出他们的出价（190、195、199、200）。当价格匹配时，交易发生：愿意支付200的买家与要价200的卖家匹配。他们都很满意。

b5cb97fdc529.source: Eventually the matches cease and we come to a standstill.
b5cb97fdc529.target: 最终匹配停止，我们陷入僵局。

e01f90cae477.source: Trades don’t last forever: there’s a standoff and an awkward pause. The lowest sellers want \$201, and the highest bidder wants \$199; this \$2 gap is called
e01f90cae477.target: 交易不会永远持续：出现僵持和尴尬的停顿。最低的卖家要价\$201，最高的买家出价\$199；这\$2的差距称为

48f5e6ad19d1.source: Now what happens? Buyers and sellers can do:
48f5e6ad19d1.target: 接下来会发生什么？买家和卖家可以：

53da836ebee6.source: When you place a limit order (“Buy an iPhone for 195″), your order gets added to the bid queue (similar for asks).
53da836ebee6.target: 当你下达限价单（“以195买入iPhone”）时，你的订单会添加到出价队列（要价类似）。

e299142e1fb0.source: (“buy it now!” or “sell it now!”), then you use a market order. You’ll get the best price available:
e299142e1fb0.target: （“立即买入！”或“立即卖出！”），那么你就要使用市价单。你会得到当前最佳价格：

a28937ecc586.source: Now this is interesting. Notice how market orders
a28937ecc586.target: 这很有趣。注意市价单如何

bf85e878cf95.source: . When people place market orders, the stock price fluctuates. Yes, it’s “just” supply and demand, but it’s pretty cool to know it’s happening real-time in the stock market.
bf85e878cf95.target: 。当人们下市价单时，股票价格会波动。是的，这“只是”供求关系，但知道它在股市中实时发生还是很酷的。

61b3c19322cb.source: If there’s a lot of buyers, they’ll “use up” the ask queue and the price will rise. If there’s a lot of sellers, they’ll “use up” the bid queue and the price will fall.
61b3c19322cb.target: 如果有大量买家，他们会“消耗”要价队列，价格就会上涨。如果有大量卖家，他们会“消耗”出价队列，价格就会下跌。

9fc5452f84a1.source: This explains why it’s hard to buy and sell for the same price. If you buy for 201, and no new bids come in, you’ll only be able to sell for 199.
9fc5452f84a1.target: 这就解释了为什么很难用同一价格买入和卖出。如果你以201买入，且没有新的出价，你只能以199卖出。

94835a2c9b8d.source: In the real world, the list looks like this:
94835a2c9b8d.target: 在现实中，列表看起来是这样的：

e192a2811f61.source: You see the bids, asks, quantities, and names. Here the bid is 204.91 (max someone will pay) and the ask is 204.92 (min someone will sell). When a buyer or seller gets restless, they may decide to immediately buy/sell, which moves the price. This detailed data is called a Level II quote.
e192a2811f61.target: 你会看到出价、要价、数量和名称。这里的出价是204.91（某人愿意支付的最高价），要价是204.92（某人愿意卖出的最低价）。当买方或卖方变得焦躁时，他们可能决定立即买入/卖出，从而改变价格。这种详细数据称为二级报价。

3930a6e05464.source: are the two major American exchanges. There are differences, but at the core they provide:
3930a6e05464.target: 是美国两大交易所。它们有差异，但核心都提供：

7e1effb26be9.source: Well, often they don’t. In the

1e8bbec2dd83.source: 88% of the trades happen between the public without needing the specialist (remember those guys waving papers and screaming at each other? I wouldn’t want to get involved with them either).
1e8bbec2dd83.target: 88%的交易在公众之间发生，不需要专家（还记得那些挥舞着纸片互相喊叫的家伙吗？我也不想和他们扯上关系）。

20e047602908.source: But sometimes they are needed. The market makers literally “create a market” by providing liquidity: they must always offer stocks for you to buy from them, or sell to them. With many market makers competing for your business, popular stocks get a narrower spread.
20e047602908.target: 但有时它们是必要的。做市商通过提供流动性来“创造市场”：他们必须始终提供股票供你买入或卖给他们。由于许多做市商竞争您的业务，热门股票的价差会更窄。

510ad9d10a05.source: But how do market makers make money?

35a8868e10c9.source: Well, it’s a bit like a currency exchange at a bank, where’s there’s a different rate for buying and selling. If a steady stream of buyers and sellers transacts at the booth, the exchange pockets the price difference.
35a8868e10c9.target: 嗯，这有点像银行的外汇兑换，买入和卖出有不同的汇率。如果稳定的买卖双方在兑换点交易，兑换商就能赚取差价。

3be0b306af0d.source: Let’s say Sue has an iPhone to sell, and Bob wants to buy an iPhone. They both want to make an immediate (market) order that's guaranteed to execute. It might go like this:
3be0b306af0d.target: 假设苏有一部iPhone要卖，鲍勃想买一部iPhone。他们都想下一个立即成交的市价单。过程可能是这样的：

0df0ba1b2969.source: See what happened? The market maker bought an iPhone for 199 and sold it for 201: they pocketed the spread of \$2. However, Sue and Bob got better prices than they would have otherwise.
0df0ba1b2969.target: 看到了吗？做市商以199美元买入iPhone，以201美元卖出：他们赚取了2美元的价差。然而，苏和鲍勃得到了比他们原本更好的价格。

8ed2134cde5b.source: Market makers are just another market participant, but they are obligated to always list their bid and ask price. But, they are taking some risk: in a volatile market, they may pick up too many shares that they can't sell later. Then, they'd "widen the spread" by listing prices that would help guarantee a profit: perhaps offering to buy at 195 and sell at 205, expecting a steady stream of market orders.
8ed2134cde5b.target: 做市商只是另一种市场参与者，但他们有义务始终列出自己的买入价和卖出价。不过，他们承担了一些风险：在波动的市场中，他们可能积累太多后来无法卖出的股票。这时，他们会通过列出能保证利润的价格来“扩大价差”：比如，提供195美元的买入价和205美元的卖出价，期望有稳定的市价单流。

d082d406a382.source: As a regular investor, you can avoid paying the spread by only putting in limit orders. But you aren't guaranteed to make a trade.
d082d406a382.target: 作为普通投资者，你可以只使用限价单来避免支付价差。但你不能保证一定能成交。

a67976d788b5.source: of Microsoft. People naively put this wealth as “shares times price”, but you know that doesn’t really work. If he tried to sell all his shares, he’d use up the bids.
a67976d788b5.target: 。人们天真地将这笔财富视为“股票数量乘以价格”，但你知道这实际上行不通。如果他试图卖出所有股票，他会消耗掉所有买入订单。

1df47b8a46da.source: Each block of shares would be sold for a lower and lower value — and potential buyers would panic and reduce their bids, thinking something was amiss. Sellers would fear the worst and lower their asks to compete. Pandemonium would ensue. So the actual liquidation value of his shares is really some fraction of the reported amount. But it’s still nothing to sneeze at.
1df47b8a46da.target: 每一批股票都会以越来越低的价格卖出——潜在买家会恐慌并降低他们的买入价，认为出了问题。卖家会担心最坏情况，降低他们的卖出价来竞争。混乱将随之而来。因此，他股票的实际清算价值只是报告金额的一部分。但这仍然不容小觑。

41b354a38139.source: Similarly, large institutions must spread their stock trades over time so they don’t disrupt the market (and evaporate their profits).
41b354a38139.target: 同样，大型机构必须在时间上分散股票交易，以免扰乱市场（并蒸发他们的利润）。

d5bdb73da3fe.source: The market has built-in shock absorbers: as you sell more, the price you get is smaller and smaller, so you sell less. As you buy more, the price you pay gets higher and higher, so you buy less. So it makes sense to take things slow. Nifty.
d5bdb73da3fe.target: 市场有内置的减震器：随着你卖出更多，你得到的价格越来越低，所以你会减少卖出。随着你买入更多，你支付的价格越来越高，所以你会减少买入。因此，慢慢来是有道理的。不错。

11944307af94.source: I’ve simplified a lot of things and only scratched the glossed-over surface. Each market has its own rules to create a trading-friendly environment. Read more here:
11944307af94.target: 我简化了很多东西，只触及了表面的皮毛。每个市场都有自己创建友好交易环境的规则。更多内容请见：

2e0b8088a817.source: But, my goal wasn’t to fill your head with details. I want to share insight:
2e0b8088a817.target: 但我的目标不是用细节填充你的大脑。我想分享一个见解：

c5e73f034764.source: Want a stock tip? Don’t listen to stock tips. (Stolen from a Charles Schwab ad.) This article is about looking at a system as
c5e73f034764.target: 想要股票建议？不要听信股票建议。（引自嘉信理财的一则广告。）这篇文章是将一个系统视为

630f6cdf1567.source: to solve the larger problem of trading goods. Happy investing.
630f6cdf1567.target: 来解决商品交易这一更大问题。祝你投资愉快。

bf57dd6619c1.source: We have "number words" for each type of quantity ("one, two, three... five hundred seventy nine")
bf57dd6619c1.target: 对于每种数量，我们都有对应的"数词"（如"一、二、三……五百七十九"）

6cfcc2bad650.source: The "number words" can be written with symbols, not regular letters, like lines in the sand. The unary (tally) system has a line for each object.
6cfcc2bad650.target: 这些"数词"可以用符号书写，而不是普通的字母，就像沙地上的线条。一元（计数）系统每个对象对应一条线。

2218ab2367b1.source: Shortcuts exist for large counts (Roman numerals: V = five, X = ten, C = hundred)
2218ab2367b1.target: 大数有快捷表示法（罗马数字：V=五，X=十，C=百）

1fd8edce4d7c.source: We even have a shortcut to represent emptiness: 0
1fd8edce4d7c.target: 我们甚至有一个表示空无的快捷符号：0

05acdf02725b.source: The position of a symbol is a shortcut for
05acdf02725b.target: 符号的位置是一种快捷方式，用于表示

7fee5e9b6a56.source: Numbers can have incredibly small, fractional differences: 1.1, 1.01, 1.001...
7fee5e9b6a56.target: 数字可以有极其微小的分数差异：1.1, 1.01, 1.001...

93e7c4aae866.source: Numbers can be negative, less than nothing (Wha?). This represents "opposite" or "reverse", e.g., negative height is underground, negative savings is debt.
93e7c4aae866.target: 数字可以是负数，小于零（什么？）。这表示“相反”或“反向”，例如，负高度表示地下，负储蓄表示债务。

f5b0d66d0bdb.source: (or more). This isn't yet commonplace, so it's called "Math" (scary M).
f5b0d66d0bdb.target: （或更多）。这还不常见，因此被称为“数学”（可怕的M）。

312665b2f618.source: , yet still not zero. This is also called "Math".
312665b2f618.target: ，但仍然不是零。这也被称为“数学”。

4474145c8fc9.source: I cringe when hearing "Math teaches you to think".
4474145c8fc9.target: 听到“数学教你思考”时，我会退缩。

d76dc79865b6.source: It's a well-meaning but ineffective appeal that only satisfies existing fans (see: "Reading takes you anywhere!"). What activity, from crossword puzzles to memorizing song lyrics, doesn't help you think?
d76dc79865b6.target: 这是一种善意但无效的呼吁，只能满足现有的粉丝（参见：“阅读带你去任何地方！”）。从填字游戏到记歌词，哪种活动不能帮助你思考？

184832eef7ef.source: Math seems different, and here's why: it's a specific, powerful vocabulary for ideas.
184832eef7ef.target: 数学似乎不同，原因如下：它是一种特定且强大的思想词汇。

da6777348d8d.source: Imagine a cook who only knows the terms "yummy" and "yucky". He makes a bad meal. What's wrong? Hrm. There's no way to describe it! Too mild? Salty? Sweet? Sour? Cold? These specific critiques become hazy variations of the "yucky" bucket. He probably wouldn't think "Needs more
da6777348d8d.target: 想象一个只知道“好吃”和“难吃”两个词的厨师。他做了一顿糟糕的饭菜。哪里出了问题？嗯。无法描述！太淡？太咸？太甜？太酸？太冷？这些具体的批评变成了“难吃”桶中模糊的变体。他可能不会想到“需要更多

fae2d8aa2c62.source: Words are handholds that latch onto thoughts. You (yes, you!) think with extreme mathematical sophistication. Your common-sense understanding of quantity includes concepts refined over millennia: base-10 notation, zero, decimals, negatives.
fae2d8aa2c62.target: 词语是抓住思想的抓手。你（没错，就是你！）以极其复杂的数学思维思考。你对数量的常识理解包含了数千年来精炼的概念：十进制记数法、零、小数、负数。

2626de1d61bf.source: What we call "Math" are just the ideas we haven't yet internalized.
2626de1d61bf.target: 我们所谓的“数学”只是我们尚未内化的想法。

d65ab605b8fd.source: Let's explore our idea of quantity. It's a funny notion, and some languages only have words for one, two and many. They never thought to subdivide "many", and you never thought to
d65ab605b8fd.target: 让我们探索我们对数量的概念。这是一个有趣的概念，有些语言只有表示一、二和多的词语。他们从未想过细分“多”，你也从未想过

b080fb053357.source: to your East and West hands.
b080fb053357.target: 给你的东方手和西方手。

6da137ea6e68.source: Our concept of numbers shapes our world. Why do ancient years go from BC to AD? We needed separate labels for "before" and "after", which weren't on a single scale.
6da137ea6e68.target: 我们的数字概念塑造了我们的世界。为什么古代年份从公元前到公元？我们需要为“之前”和“之后”设置单独的标签，它们不在同一个尺度上。

75b2b9a1cb64.source: Why did the stock market set prices in increments of 1/8 until 2000 AD? We were based on centuries-old systems. Ask a modern trader if they'd rather go back.
75b2b9a1cb64.target: 为什么直到公元2000年，股票市场都以1/8的增量设定价格？我们是基于几个世纪前的系统。问问现代交易员是否愿意回到过去。

496401b0b6b8.source: Why is the decimal system useful for categorization? You can always find room for a decimal between two other ones, and progressively classify an item (1, 1.3, 1.38, 1.386).
496401b0b6b8.target: 为什么十进制系统对分类有用？你总能在两个数字之间找到一个小数位，并逐步分类一个项目（1, 1.3, 1.38, 1.386）。

306ac4950cb9.source: Why do we accept the idea of a vacuum, empty space? Because you understand the notion of zero. (Maybe true vacuums don't exist, but you get the theory.)
306ac4950cb9.target: 为什么我们接受真空、空空间的概念？因为你理解零的概念。（也许真正的真空不存在，但你明白这个理论。）

aecd27b1b8e1.source: Why is anti-matter or anti-gravity palatable? Because you accept that positives could have negatives that act in opposite ways.
aecd27b1b8e1.target: 为什么反物质或反重力是可接受的？因为你接受正数可以有以相反方式作用的负数。

a7749acb2859.source: How could the universe come from nothing? Well, how can 0 be split into 1 and -1?
a7749acb2859.target: 宇宙如何从无中产生？嗯，0如何被拆分成1和-1？

98ea8704f006.source: Our math vocabulary shapes what we're capable of thinking about. Multiplication and division, which eluded geniuses a few thousand years ago, are now homework for grade schoolers. All because we have better ways to think about numbers.
98ea8704f006.target: 我们的数学词汇塑造了我们能够思考的内容。几千年前连天才都难以掌握的乘法和除法，现在是小学生的家庭作业。这一切都是因为我们有了更好的思考数字的方式。

93ed9d173cd2.source: : quantity. Imagine improving our vocabulary for structure, shape, change, and chance. (Oh, I mean, the important-sounding Algebra, Geometry, Calculus and Statistics.)
93ed9d173cd2.target: : 数量有不错的了解。想象一下，如果我们的词汇扩展到结构、形状、变化和概率（哦，我指的是听起来很重要的代数、几何、微积分和统计学）。

24610b2e0f1a.source: Caveman Chef Og doesn't think he needs more than yummy/yucky. But you know it'd blow his mind, and his cooking, to understand sweet/sour/salty/spicy/tangy.
24610b2e0f1a.target: 穴居人厨师奥格认为自己只需要知道好吃/难吃就够了。但你知道，如果他懂得甜/酸/咸/辣/辛，那将颠覆他的认知，也提升他的厨艺。

91ae02613017.source: We're still cavemen when thinking about new ideas, and that's why we study math.
91ae02613017.target: 在思考新概念时，我们仍然像穴居人一样，而这正是我们学习数学的原因。

17ec77c4499e.source: 1 tail = 10 choices (exactly one flip was tails)
17ec77c4499e.target: 1个反面 = 10种选择（恰好一次为反面）

ab3df12be486.target: 心智模型：与(AND) vs 或(OR)

bcff69a5abd6.source: You flip a coin 10 times. How many ways can you get at least 7 heads?
bcff69a5abd6.target: 你抛硬币10次。有多少种方式可以得到至少7次正面？

a268048d700d.source: When working on a combination problem, we usually multiply. But sometimes addition shows up -- how do we know which is which?
a268048d700d.target: 在处理组合问题时，我们通常做乘法。但有时加法也会出现——我们如何区分何时用哪种？

59349eee190d.source: Here's a few mental models I use to keep them straight.
59349eee190d.target: 这里有几个我用来区分它们的思维模型。

365ab8d8223f.source: Let's take a simple situation: You have 4 shirts and 8 pants, how many outfits can you make?
365ab8d8223f.target: 让我们看一个简单的场景：你有4件衬衫和8条裤子，你能搭配出多少套衣服？

984fcdb69d03.source: In essence, you are picking a spot on this grid:
984fcdb69d03.target: 本质上，你是在这个网格上选择一个点：

30a90078180a.source: Shirts and Pants exist in separate dimensions, whose area represents distinct solutions. We can pick any spot
30a90078180a.target: 衬衫和裤子存在于不同的维度，其面积代表不同的解决方案。我们可以选择任意一点

9c5d0e4c2bf6.source: Now, suppose we had 4 shirts and 8 pants and had to pick a single item to sell. Here, they lie along the same "clothing item" dimension:
9c5d0e4c2bf6.target: 现在，假设我们有4件衬衫和8条裤子，但必须挑选一件物品出售。在这里，它们处于同一个“衣物”维度：

99cee1d0ce9a.source: We can randomly pick any point
99cee1d0ce9a.target: 我们可以随机选择任意一点

8bbc845d257e.source: Think "different dimensions vs. same dimesion" or "grid vs. line".
8bbc845d257e.target: 可以理解为“不同维度 vs. 相同维度”或“网格 vs. 直线”。

119b13cc6e16.source: Another interpretation is AND (multiplication) vs. OR (addition).
119b13cc6e16.target: 另一种解释是“与(AND)” (乘法) vs. “或(OR)” (加法)。

058559269428.source: Let's say we must pick one of 4 shirts AND one of 8 pants. We need both to stay out of trouble. The scenario is:
058559269428.target: 假设我们必须选择一件衬衫与(AND)一条裤子。我们需要两者才能避免麻烦。场景如下：

05756beb7b3a.source: What if McDonald's softens their regulations and allows a shirt OR pants? (But not both -- yikes.) Then, we have:
05756beb7b3a.target: 如果麦当劳放宽规定，允许一件衬衫或(OR)一条裤子呢？(但不能两者都选——哎呀。)那么我们有：

97f1e4ecee74.source: Writing out the scenario is often easier to think through, especially with numerous dimensions (shirts, pants, hats, shoes).
97f1e4ecee74.target: 写出场景通常更容易思考，特别是当有多个维度时(衬衫、裤子、帽子、鞋子)。

15203b54c27a.source: As you internalize the analogies, you'll quickly recognize whether multiplication or addition is needed.
15203b54c27a.target: 当你内化了这些类比，你会很快识别出是需要乘法还是加法。

05dba6c888ea.source: Let's go meta for a minute. The

a47fcbcf73a2.source: How can we think about this?
a47fcbcf73a2.target: 我们该如何思考这个问题？

e580f9b6a95f.source: The numerator ($n!$) is the max volume assuming each of the $n$ choices has its own dimension. The number of rearrangements of 8 people is 8 * 7 * 6 * 5 * 4 * 3 * 2 * 1.
e580f9b6a95f.target: 分子($n!$)是假设每个$n$个选择都有其自身维度时的最大体积。8个人的排列数是8 * 7 * 6 * 5 * 4 * 3 * 2 * 1。

04fdf22b777b.source: But suppose we only care about the first 3 decisions -- picking a Gold, Silver and Bronze among 8 contestants. In this case, we shrink our solution space by dividing out the 5 dimensions we aren't using (which has 5! options on its own). We are left with 8! / 5! = 8 * 7 * 6 = 336 choices, with the general formula $\frac{n!}{(n-k)!}$.
04fdf22b777b.target: 但假设我们只关心前3个决策——在8名参赛者中挑选金牌、银牌和铜牌。在这种情况下，我们通过除以未使用的5个维度(它们本身有5!种选择)来缩小解空间。我们得到8! / 5! = 8 * 7 * 6 = 336种选择，一般公式为$\frac{n!}{(n-k)!}$。

000b9ee702b4.source: (If multiplication creates dimensions, then division should remove them.)
000b9ee702b4.target: (如果乘法创造维度，那么除法应该移除它们。)

c08976735fd0.source: Now, let's say the medals are identical: we're giving a tin can to 3 out of 8 people. We need to further remove dimensions, because we have 3! = 3 * 2 * 1 = 6 redundancies for each permutation in our solution space. We again shrink our solution space:
c08976735fd0.target: 现在，假设奖牌是相同的：我们给8人中的3人颁发一个罐头。我们需要进一步移除维度，因为我们的解空间中每个排列有3! = 3 * 2 * 1 = 6种冗余。我们再次缩小解空间：

fbba017c88c4.source: (I imagine the solution space volume getting denser.)
fbba017c88c4.target: (我想象解空间体积变得更密集。)

475029b69493.source: Ah! That's what's happening with the combination and permutation formula. We create the max volume and shrink it by the dimensions we are not using. Mentally translate the scenario into a version that makes sense to you.
475029b69493.target: 啊！这就是组合与排列公式的运作方式。我们创建最大体积，然后按未使用的维度收缩。在脑海中将场景翻译成对你有意义的版本。

97053c778c2c.source: Here's how I think through a few sample problems.
97053c778c2c.target: 以下是我思考几个示例问题的方式。

4cdc2f6f93c6.source: First off, the total number of possibilities is 2^10 = 1024. Intuitively, I see each flip as a decision along a different dimension, not the same number line. This means we have 2 * 2 * 2 *... possibilities, not 2 + 2+ 2 + ... possibilities.
4cdc2f6f93c6.target: 首先，总可能性数量是2^10 = 1024。直觉上，我把每次抛掷看作沿不同维度的一次决策，而不是同一个数轴。这意味着我们有2 * 2 * 2 *...种可能性，而不是2 + 2 + 2 +...种可能性。

46f45f721d15.source: Geometrically, this would be a 10-dimensional "choice space", or, written out:
46f45f721d15.target: 几何上，这将是一个10维的“选择空间”，或者写成：

9fc596b117e5.source: Ok. Now, how can we get at least 7 heads? That means we had 0 tails [10 heads], 1 tails [9 heads], 2 tails [8 heads], or 3 tails [7 heads].
9fc596b117e5.target: 好的。现在，我们如何得到至少7次正面？这意味着有0次反面[10次正面]、1次反面[9次正面]、2次反面[8次正面]或3次反面[7次正面]。

370f7f2eaa0e.source: Switching to the written description, this becomes:
370f7f2eaa0e.target: 转换为文字描述，这变成：

3be4ae7f0fa1.source: Given our 10 flips, the number of outcomes are:
3be4ae7f0fa1.target: 给定我们的10次抛掷，结果的数量是：

6e075dfedb69.source: And for kicks, the chance of seeing this happen is:
6e075dfedb69.target: 为了好玩，看到这种情况发生的概率是：

120ba44bea4b.source: Multiplication goes beyond "repeated addition". It's a general notion of combining for which I'm still discovering interpretations. Let's not get tied into a single meaning.
120ba44bea4b.target: 乘法超越了“重复加法”。它是一种通用的组合概念，我仍在发现其解释。不要局限于单一含义。

9ccc98bffe9f.source: Turning AND/OR statements into arithmetic maps nicely to Boolean logic.
9ccc98bffe9f.target: 将AND/OR语句转化为算术运算，能很好地映射到布尔逻辑。

db0031270205.source: If A and B are variables with the values 1 or 0, we can write:
db0031270205.target: 如果A和B是值为1或0的变量，我们可以写成：

4f8bc10cb5e2.source: In most languages, a positive number evaluates to "true", so A + B = 2 is true. Note that this OR is an "inclusive OR" that allows both values to be true. To force an exclusive OR, we could take the remainder after dividing by two:
4f8bc10cb5e2.target: 在大多数语言中，正数会被评估为“真”，所以A + B = 2为真。注意这个OR是“包含性OR”，允许两个值都为真。要强制实现排他性OR，我们可以取除以2后的余数：

abeff2a07be5.source: Most programming languages have separate operators for AND (
abeff2a07be5.target: 大多数编程语言有独立的运算符用于AND（

f041519f254c.source: ), but it's nice seeing how logic works with regular arithmetic.
f041519f254c.target: ），但看到逻辑如何用常规算术工作还是不错的。

40391ef12b42.source: Additionally, "if/then/else" statements can be converted to arithmetic.
40391ef12b42.target: 此外，“if/then/else”语句可以转换为算术运算。

03693f738ec7.source: is a variable (1 or 0) that determines a result, instead of:
03693f738ec7.target: 是一个变量（1或0），它决定了一个结果，而不是：

a18f2709c721.source: we can use the single statement:
a18f2709c721.target: 我们可以使用单一语句：

d9de6f58221f.source: This version avoids the needs for branching, which is expensive for a CPU, and is a formula we can optimize with Calculus (used in machine learning algorithms).
d9de6f58221f.target: 这个版本避免了分支的需求（分支对CPU来说代价高昂），并且是一个我们可以用微积分优化的公式（用于机器学习算法）。

8c033c91d57c.source: to be zero may be nonzero in a different dimension (just like i might appear to be 0 to us, but isn’t)
8c033c91d57c.target: 在另一个维度中可能非零（就像虚数单位 i 在我们看来可能是0，但实际上不是那样）

063d0e049a26.source: Realize x=0 is not reachable from our accuracy; a “small but nonzero” x is always available at a greater level of accuracy
063d0e049a26.target: 意识到从我们的精度无法达到 x=0；在更高的精度下，总有一个“小但不为零”的 x 可用

994a590594ca.source: Replace sin(x) by a straight line as a simpler model
994a590594ca.target: 用一条直线替代 sin(x) 作为更简单的模型

bc3ee3c28327.source: “Do the math” with the simpler model (x / x = 1)
bc3ee3c28327.target: 用这个更简单的模型“进行计算”(x / x = 1)

d44aa0db89bf.source: Bring the result (1) back into our accuracy (stays 1)
d44aa0db89bf.target: 将结果(1)带回我们的精度（保持为1）

6a3b452955de.source: Zero is relative: something can be zero to us, and non-zero somewhere else
6a3b452955de.target: 零是相对的：对我们来说可以是零，而在其他地方则非零

3dfad03a6056.source: Infinitesimals (“another dimension”) and limits (“beyond our accuracy”) resolve the dilemma of “zero and nonzero”
3dfad03a6056.target: 无穷小（“另一个维度”）和极限（“超出我们的精度”）解决了“零与非零”的困境

e4f566552fdd.source: We create simpler models in the more accurate dimension, do the math, and bring the result to our world
e4f566552fdd.target: 我们在更精确的维度中创建更简单的模型，进行运算，然后将结果带回我们的世界

c0520ca1cc70.source: The final result is perfectly accurate for us
c0520ca1cc70.target: 最终结果对我们来说是完美精确的

319366a9faf0.source: That trick doesn’t work, does it?

966db1c546a4.source: Caveats: The Trick Doesn’t Always Work
966db1c546a4.target: 注意事项：技巧并非总是有效

c5b71454ae0e.source: So many math courses jump into limits, infinitesimals and Very Small Numbers (TM) without any context. But why do we care?
c5b71454ae0e.target: 许多数学课程直接跳入极限、无穷小和非常小的数，而不提供任何背景。但为什么我们要关心呢？

d48aaa2e6562.source: Math helps us model the world. We can break a complex idea (a wiggly curve) into simpler parts (rectangles):
d48aaa2e6562.target: 数学帮助我们建模世界。我们可以将一个复杂的思想（一条弯曲的曲线）分解成更简单的部分（矩形）：

8bc9f17da067.source: where I’ll make the curve. The precision is totally beyond your reach — I’m at the sub-atomic level, and you’re a caveman who can barely walk and chew gum. It’s like getting to the imaginary plane from the real one — you just can’t do it. To you, the rectangular shape I made at the sub-atomic level is the most perfect curve you’ve ever seen.”
8bc9f17da067.target: 我将做出曲线。其精度完全超出你的触及范围——我处在亚原子级别，而你是一个几乎连走路和嚼口香糖都勉强的穴居人。这就像从实数平面到达虚数平面一样——你根本做不到。对你来说，我在亚原子级别做出的矩形形状是你所见过的最完美的曲线。”

433f07a4a4ed.source: Limits stay in our dimension, but with ‘just enough’ accuracy to maintain the illusion of a perfect model. Infinitesimals build the model in another dimension, and it looks perfectly accurate in ours.
433f07a4a4ed.target: 极限停留在我们的维度中，但拥有“恰到好处”的精度以维持完美模型的假象。无穷小在另一个维度中构建模型，而在我们的维度中它看起来完美精确。

bae8a787e474.source: The trick to both approaches is that the simpler model was built beyond our level of accuracy. We might
bae8a787e474.target: 两种方法的关键都在于：更简单的模型是在超出我们精度的层面上构建的。我们可能

7300f0570f8c.source: the model is jagged, but we can’t tell the difference — any test we do shows the model and the real item as the same.
7300f0570f8c.target: 模型是锯齿状的，但我们无法分辨差异——我们进行的任何测试都显示模型和真实事物是一样的。

4b1480033bd7.source: Oh, but it does. We’re tricked by “imperfect but useful” models all the time:
4b1480033bd7.target: 哦，但它确实如此。我们经常被“不完美但有用”的模型所欺骗：

3db3f0f7cd9b.source: Audio files don’t contain all the information of the original signal. But can you tell the difference between a high-quality mp3 and a person talking in the other room?
3db3f0f7cd9b.target: 音频文件并不包含原始信号的所有信息。但你能分辨出高质量MP3和隔壁房间的人说话之间的区别吗？

05b078251dfb.source: Computer printouts are made from individual dots too small to see. Can you tell a handwritten note from a high-quality printout of the same?
05b078251dfb.target: 计算机打印输出由肉眼无法分辨的单个小点组成。你能区分手写笔记和同一内容的高质量打印件吗？

4b6379da6fa4.source: Video shows still images at 24 times per second. This “imperfect” model is fast enough to trick our brain into seeing fluid motion.
4b6379da6fa4.target: 视频以每秒24帧的速度显示静止图像。这个“不完美”的模型速度足够快，能欺骗我们的大脑，让我们看到流畅的运动。

674ae762a94a.source: On and on it goes. We resist because of our
674ae762a94a.target: 这样的例子不胜枚举。我们之所以抗拒，是因为我们的

daff3ee7819c.source: . But audio and video engineers know they don’t need a perfect reproduction, just quality
daff3ee7819c.target: 。但音频和视频工程师知道他们不需要完美的复制，只需要足够好的质量

3a0f1fdeb940.source: to trick us into thinking it’s the original.
3a0f1fdeb940.target: 来欺骗我们，让我们以为它是原版。

ea0ee230a922.source: Calculus lets us make these technically imperfect but “accurate enough” models in math.
ea0ee230a922.target: 微积分让我们在数学中制造出这些技术上不完美但“足够精确”的模型。

9bc45f3b4adc.source: We need to be careful when reasoning with the simplified model. We need to “do our work” at the level of higher accuracy, and bring the
9bc45f3b4adc.target: 在使用简化模型进行推理时，我们需要小心。我们需要在更高精度的层面“完成工作”，然后将

ceb2f47e2d21.source: back to our world. We’ll lose information if we don’t.
ceb2f47e2d21.target: 带回我们的世界。如果我们不这样做，就会丢失信息。

127e436d4c22.source: Suppose an imaginary number (i) visits the real number line. Everyone thinks he’s zero: after all, Re(i) = 0. But i does a trick! “Square me!” he says, and they do: “i * i = -1″ and the other numbers are astonished.
127e436d4c22.target: 假设一个虚数(i)访问实数轴。大家都以为它是零：毕竟，Re(i) = 0。但i耍了个花招！“把我平方！”它说，于是大家照做：“i * i = -1”，其他数字都震惊了。

f0c0a6d3bc2f.source: To the real numbers, it appeared that “0 * 0 = -1″, a giant paradox.
f0c0a6d3bc2f.target: 对实数而言，看起来就像是“0 * 0 = -1”，一个巨大的悖论。

c4b5986d08f7.source: But their confusion arose from their perspective — they only
c4b5986d08f7.target: 但它们的困惑源于它们的视角——它们只

87b4e4eb6a49.source: it was “0 * 0 = -1″. Yes, Re(i) * Re(i) = 0, but that wasn’t the operation! We want Re(i * i), which is different entirely! We square i in its own dimension, and bring
87b4e4eb6a49.target: （以为是）“0 * 0 = -1”。是的，Re(i) * Re(i) = 0，但那不是实际操作！我们想要的是Re(i * i)，这完全不同！我们在它自己的维度中将i平方，然后把

e3f328096b60.source: result back to ours. We need to square i, the imaginary number, and not 0, our
e3f328096b60.target: 结果带回我们的维度。我们需要平方的是虚数i，而不是0——我们对i的

c9491ff50169.source: Beware similar mistakes in calculus: we deal with tiny numbers that
c9491ff50169.target: 注意微积分中类似的错误：我们处理的是对我们而言很小的数字，

21083edf25d2.source: to us, but we can’t do math assuming they are (just like treating i like 0). No, we need to “do the math” in the other dimension and convert the results back.
21083edf25d2.target: 但不能假设它们就是零来进行数学运算（就像将 i 视为 0 一样）。不，我们需要在另一个维度“进行数学运算”，然后将结果转换回来。

fd0df99c8187.source: Limits and infinitesimals have different perspectives on how this conversion is done:
fd0df99c8187.target: 极限和无穷小对于如何进行这种转换有不同的观点：

2dbe580eb3f3.source: “Do the math” at a level of precision just beyond your detection (millimeters), and bring it back to numbers on your scale (inches)
2dbe580eb3f3.target: 在刚好超出你检测能力的精度级别（毫米）上“进行数学运算”，然后将结果带回你尺度上的数字（英寸）

c0ea92e50051.source: “Do the math” in a different dimension, and bring it back to the “standard” one (just like taking the real part of a complex number; you take the “standard” part of a hyperreal number — more later)
c0ea92e50051.target: 在另一个维度上“进行数学运算”，然后将结果带回“标准”维度（就像取复数的实部一样；你取超实数的“标准”部分——稍后会详述）

333514317238.source: Nobody ever told me: Calculus lets you work at a better level of accuracy, with a simpler model, and bring the results back to our world.
333514317238.target: 从来没有人告诉过我：微积分让你能够在更高精度的层次上工作，使用更简单的模型，然后将结果带回我们的世界。

349e3a3bcf49.source: Let’s try a conceptual example. Suppose we want to know what happens to sin(x) / x at zero. Now, if we just plug in x = 0 we get a nonsensical result: sin(0) = 0, so we get 0 / 0 which could be anything.
349e3a3bcf49.target: 让我们尝试一个概念性的例子。假设我们想知道 sin(x) / x 在零处的行为。现在，如果我们直接代入 x = 0，会得到一个无意义的结果：sin(0) = 0，所以我们得到 0 / 0，它可以是任何值。

3a4d094c0795.source: Let’s step back: what does “x = 0″ mean in our world? Well, if we’re allowing the existence of a greater level of accuracy, we know this:
3a4d094c0795.target: 让我们退一步思考：在我们的世界中，“x = 0”意味着什么？好吧，如果我们允许存在一个更高精度的层次，我们知道：

e82488b0eeae.source: We’re going to say that x can be really, really close to zero at this greater level of accuracy, but not “true zero”. Intuitively, you can think of x as 0.0000…00001, where the “…” is enough zeros for you to no longer detect the number.
e82488b0eeae.target: 我们要说的是，在这个更高精度的层次上，x 可以非常非常接近零，但不是“真正的零”。直观上，你可以把 x 想象成 0.0000…00001，其中“…”是足够多的零，让你无法再检测到该数字。

8b8716e851f0.source: (In limit terms, we say x = 0 + d (delta, a small change that keeps us within our error margin) and in infinitesimal terms, we say x = 0 + h, where h is a tiny hyperreal number, known as an infinitesimal)
8b8716e851f0.target: （在极限术语中，我们说 x = 0 + d（delta，一个让我们保持在误差范围内的小改变）；在无穷小术语中，我们说 x = 0 + h，其中 h 是一个很小的超实数，称为无穷小）

aef99edd4660.source: Ok, we have x at “zero to us, but not really”. Now we need a simpler model of sin(x). Why? Well, sine is a crazy repeating curve, and it’s hard to know what’s happening. But it turns out that a
aef99edd4660.target: 好的，我们有了“对我们来说是零，但实际上不是”的 x。现在我们需要一个 sin(x) 的更简单模型。为什么？因为正弦是一个疯狂的重复曲线，很难知道发生了什么。但事实证明，一个

8ea78b878e49.source: is a darn good model of a curve
8ea78b878e49.target: 是曲线的一个极好的模型

db9e9387e141.source: Just like we can break a filled shape into tiny rectangles to make it simpler, we can dissect a curve into a series of line segments. Around 0, sin(x) looks like the line “x”. So, we switch sin(x) with the line “x”. What’s the new ratio?
db9e9387e141.target: 就像我们可以将一个填充形状分解成 tiny 矩形以使其更简单一样，我们可以将曲线分解成一系列线段。在 0 附近，sin(x) 看起来像直线“x”。所以，我们用直线“x”替换 sin(x)。新的比值是多少？

6ddd7670b693.source: Well, "x/x" is 1. Remember, we aren’t really dividing by zero because in this super-accurate world: x is tiny but non-zero (0 + d, or 0 + h). When we “take the limit or “take the standard part” it means we do the math (x / x = 1) and then find the closest number in our world (1 goes to 1).
6ddd7670b693.target: 嗯，“x/x”是 1。记住，我们并不是真正地除以零，因为在这个超精确的世界里：x 很小但非零（0 + d，或 0 + h）。当我们“取极限”或“取标准部分”时，意味着我们进行数学运算（x / x = 1），然后找到我们世界中最接近的数字（1 变成 1）。

94092eca6a51.source: So, 1 is what we get when sin(x) / x approaches zero — that is, we make x as small as possible so it becomes 0 to us. If x became pure, true zero, then the ratio would be undefined (and it is at the infinitesimal level!). But we’re never sure if we’re at perfect zero — something like 0.0000…0001 looks like zero to us.
94092eca6a51.target: 所以，1 就是我们得到的当 sin(x) / x 趋近于零时的值——也就是说，我们让 x 尽可能小，使得它对我们来说变成 0。如果 x 变成了纯粹的、真正的零，那么比值就是未定义的（在无穷小层次上确实是未定义的！）。但我们永远无法确定是否处于完美零——像 0.0000…0001 这样的东西对我们来说看起来就是零。

9509dc83e547.source: So, "sin(x)/x" looks like "x/x = 1" as far as we can tell. Intuitively, the result makes sense once we
9509dc83e547.target: 所以，据我们所知，“sin(x)/x”看起来就像“x/x = 1”。直观上，一旦我们，结果就有意义了。

1eb2a490f44d.source: Today’s goal isn’t to solve limit problems, it’s to understand the process of solving them. To solve this example:
1eb2a490f44d.target: 今天的目标不是解决极限问题，而是理解解决它们的过程。为了解这个例子：

fd5c464755fe.source: Here’s how I see the process:
fd5c464755fe.target: 以下是我看待这个过程的方式：

feffc184e28b.source: In later articles, we’ll learn the details of setting up and solving the models.
feffc184e28b.target: 在后续文章中，我们将学习建立和求解模型的细节。

6f39a0fdb5a1.source: Some functions are really “jumpy” — and they might differ on an infinitesimal-by-infinitesimal level. That means we can’t reliably bring them back to our world. It looks like the function is unstable at microscopic level and doesn’t behave “smoothly”.
6f39a0fdb5a1.target: 有些函数非常“跳跃”——它们可能在无穷小层面上有差异。这意味着我们无法可靠地将它们带回我们的世界。看起来该函数在微观层面上不稳定，行为不“光滑”。

f1092209855e.source: The rigorous part of limits is figuring out which functions behave well enough that simple yet accurate models can be made. Fortunately, most of the natural functions in the world (x, x
f1092209855e.target: 极限的严格部分在于判断哪些函数表现得足够好，从而可以构建简单而精确的模型。幸运的是，自然界中的大多数常见函数（如 x、x

fa10e123a5b1.source: Logically, both approaches solve the problem of “zero and nonzero”. I like infinitesimals because they allow “another dimension” which seems a cleaner separation than “always just outside your reach”. Infinitesimals were the foundation of the intuition of calculus, and appear inside physics and other subjects that use it.
fa10e123a5b1.target: 逻辑上，两种方法都解决了“零与非零”的问题。我喜欢无穷小，因为它们允许“另一个维度”，这似乎比“总是在你触及范围之外”更清晰。无穷小是微积分直觉的基础，并出现在物理学及其他使用它的学科中。

10066e72c168.source: This isn’t an analysis class, but the math robots can be assured that infinitesimals have a rigorous foundation. I use them because they click for me.
10066e72c168.target: 这不是一门分析课，但数学机器人可以放心，无穷小有严格的基础。我使用它们是因为它们适合我。

cd890531fb0b.source: Phew! Some of these ideas are tricky, and I feel like I’m talking from both sides of my mouth: we want to be simpler, yet still perfectly accurate?
cd890531fb0b.target: 呼！其中一些想法很棘手，我感觉自己在自相矛盾：我们想更简单，却又希望完全精确？

aad02ed526fe.source: This famous dilemma about “being zero sometimes, and non-zero others” is a famous critique of calculus. It was mostly ignored since the results worked out, but in the 1800s limits were introduced to really resolve the dilemma. We learn limits today, but without understanding the nature of the problem they were trying to solve!
aad02ed526fe.target: 关于“有时是零，有时不是零”的这个著名困境，是微积分的一个著名批评。由于结果有效，它大多被忽略，但在19世纪，引入了极限来真正解决这个困境。我们今天学习极限，但却不理解它们试图解决的问题的本质！

004a69e5294b.source: My goal isn’t to do math, it’s to understand it. And a huge part of grokking calculus is realizing that simple models created beyond our accuracy can look “just fine” in our dimension. Later on we’ll learn the rules to build and use these models. Happy math.
004a69e5294b.target: 我的目标不是做数学，而是理解数学。而掌握微积分的一个巨大部分是认识到，超越我们精度创建的简单模型在我们的维度中看起来“刚刚好”。稍后我们将学习构建和使用这些模型的规则。祝数学愉快。

97110d90d2d6.source: I had a book club discussion with
97110d90d2d6.target: 我和一个读书俱乐部讨论过

7321da36ba83.source: It's a wonderful book, a mix of philosophy and narrative storytelling. The central thesis is that an ineffable sense of Quality (capital Q) underlies art and science/technology, and by following it, we can unlock joy, creativity, and technical excellence.
7321da36ba83.target: 这是一本精彩的书，融合了哲学和叙事故事。其核心论点是：一种不可言喻的良质（大写Q）是艺术和科学/技术的基础，遵循它，我们可以解锁快乐、创造力和技术卓越。

6a774fb753a3.source: In terms of process, I read the paperback book, kept notes/highlights in the margins, transferred them a the Google doc, then summarized a few practical takeaways:
6a774fb753a3.target: 在过程上，我读了纸质书，在页边做了笔记/划线，将它们转移到Google文档，然后总结了一些实用的要点：

d10f6fe287c6.source: The distinction between Science and Art is a human one, the knife we pick. (“The universe can be divided into Bananas and Non-Bananas”). This may be the source of our biases and frustrations.
d10f6fe287c6.target: 科学与艺术之间的区别是人造的，是我们选择的刀子。（“宇宙可分为香蕉和非香蕉”。）这可能是我们偏见和挫折的根源。

5cd283194013.source: There exist “gumption traps” that can drain our enthusiasm (which is the source of real understanding, enjoyment, mastery).
5cd283194013.target: 存在一些“勇气陷阱”会耗尽我们的热情（而热情正是真正理解、享受和精通的源泉）。

ccee3cd2f365.source: The power of our a priori concepts (our mental models) which is what we deal with, not our sense data directly.
ccee3cd2f365.target: 我们先验概念（心智模型）的力量，我们与之打交道的是这些概念，而非直接处理感官数据。

dff069a34f84.source: Writing, experience things directly. Writing about one side of one coin, the back of your thumb. It’s your experience now.
dff069a34f84.target: 写作，直接体验事物。描写一枚硬币的一面，你拇指的背面。现在这是你的经历。

5b04e7da7cab.source: Work through things at a speed / pace consistent with your nature (walking up the mountain). To live only for some future goal is shallow.
5b04e7da7cab.target: 以符合自己天性的速度/节奏处理事情（如同登山）。只为某个未来目标而活是肤浅的。

4b708b553b49.source: The multi-step process of taking notes, transferring them, and summarizing them to myself was quite enjoyable, and I got much more from the text than I would have otherwise.
4b708b553b49.target: 记笔记、整理笔记并为自己总结的多步骤过程非常愉快，我从中获得的收获比原本多得多。

2eab0620f0a9.source: Overall, the book is a well-written, thoughtful meditation on merging technical and artistic quality within the human experience. This is the balance I'm striving to hit with the lessons on the site, finding ways to unify technical understanding with artistic intuition and the wonder of discovery (that Aha! moment).
2eab0620f0a9.target: 总体而言，这本书文笔优美、思想深刻，探讨了如何在人类体验中融合技术与艺术品质。这正是我在本网站课程中力求达到的平衡——寻找将技术理解与艺术直觉以及发现的惊奇（那种“啊哈！”时刻）统一起来的方法。

45792e24443f.source: It's an endless game to find a better way to explain difficult concepts, and the book reminds me to enjoy the journey for its own sake.
45792e24443f.target: 寻找解释复杂概念的更好方法是一场无尽的游戏，而这本书提醒我要为了旅程本身而享受它。

04341e97da16.target: 为什么 e 很特别？

086d3bcdb182.source: : the unit quantity (1.0), continuously growing the unit rate (100%), for unit time (1 period).
086d3bcdb182.target: ：单位数量 (1.0)，以单位速率 (100%) 持续增长，持续单位时间 (1 个周期)。

3fbc21f8947e.source: All circles are the unit circle, scaled up. All continuously growing systems are e^{rt}, scaled to some rate and time.
3fbc21f8947e.target: 所有圆都是单位圆按比例放大。所有持续增长的系统都是 e^{rt}，按某个速率和时间缩放。

da4a610658f5.source: Use e^{rt} for things that change constantly (radioactive decay, populations). For growth based on discrete intervals (interest payments, combinatorics), (1 + rate)^{time} is a better model.
da4a610658f5.target: 对持续变化的事物（放射性衰变、人口）使用 e^{rt}。对于基于离散间隔的增长（利息支付、组合数学），(1 + rate)^{time} 是更好的模型。

b45b8e5e3605.source: A number pointing sideways (North/South) isntead of the typical East/West number line. ("Imaginary" was a derogatory name from critics, Gauss wanted them called "lateral" numbers.)
b45b8e5e3605.target: 一个指向侧面（北/南）而非典型东西方向数线的数。（“虚数”是批评者起的贬义名称，高斯希望称其为“侧向数”。）

3e647f3c6161.source: i, by itself, points North. Multiplying by i rotates you 90 degrees. 2 rotations points you backwards (i * i = -1), 4 rotations spins you around fully (i^4 = 1).
3e647f3c6161.target: i 本身指向北方。乘以 i 会使你旋转 90 度。旋转两次使你向后（i * i = -1），旋转四次则让你完整转一圈（i^4 = 1）。

e0a4c26e1e15.source: The second dimension is useful, right? Imaginary numbers make 2d rotation problems simple.
e0a4c26e1e15.target: 第二维度很有用，对吧？虚数让二维旋转问题变得简单。
